target_raw:
Takāanuman, Ḫappu-ašu and his wife Manamana owe Innāya 21.5 shekels fine liti silver.
They will pay four weeks from the ḫamuštum of Enna-Suen and Ili-ālim.
If they do not pay, they will add 1 shekel silver per month for this silver as interest.
Month: Teʾinātim.
Eponymate: Kapādīya.
The silver is bound to whomever is legally responsible and solvent.
Witnesses: iš-me-li-im, Arḫanuman.

1/2 mina 5 shekels of silver, 6 1/4 shekels of gold belonging to Aššur-nādā's daughter, 1/3 mina 5 shekels of silver including that of my sacrifice, Arši-ah brought.
1/3 mina 1 shekel of silver were (separate) shipments-all this belongs to Aššur-nādā.
For all this Puzur-Anna has received in silver: 3 minas of refined silver from Ahu-waqar on behalf of Aššur-nādā.
With respect to the 3 minas of silver Aššur-nādā must not raise claims against Ahu-waqar.
If he raises claim, Puzur-Anna must clear him.
Should Ahu-waqar owe anything else to Aššur-nādā, he may sue him.
Month Qarra' atum.
Eponymate: Ṭab-Aššur.
Witnessed by Šumi-abiya, witnessed by Amaya.

1/2 mina 5 shekels of silver, 6 1/4 shekels of gold belonging to Aššur-nādā's daughter, 1/3 mina 5 shekels of silver including that of my sacrifice, Arši-ah brought.
1/3 mina 1 shekel of silver were (separate) shipments-all this belongs to Aššur-nādā.
For all this Puzur-Anna has received in silver: 3 minas of refined silver from Ahu-waqar on behalf of Aššur-nādā.
With respect to the 3 minas of silver Aššur-nādā must not raise claims against Ahu-waqar.
If he raises claim, Puzur-Anna must clear him.
Should Ahu-waqar owe anything else to Aššur-nādā, he may sue him.
Month Qarra' atum.
Eponymate: Ṭab-Aššur.
Witnessed by Šumi-abiya, witnessed by Amaya.
label: Cuneiform Tablet KUG 16

source_raw: 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-na a-lim{ki} a-na en-na-sú-en6 ša a-na bé-ú-lá-tim a-dí-nu-šu-ni um-ma en-na-sú-en6-ma ša ki-ma ku-a-tí i-na a-lim{ki} ṭup-pí il5-pu-tù šu-ma ša ki-ma i-a-ti i-na a-lim{ki} ṭup-pu-šu il5-pu-tù 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR a-na ša ki-ma i-a-ti i-ša-qal i-ša-<gap> ṭup-pu-šu a-ni-um i-mu-at šu-ma ṭup-pu-šu ša ki-ma i-a-ti lá il5-pu-tù 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-na a-lim{ki} šál-ma-am i-na-sú-en6 i-ša-qal IGI ša-lim-a-ḫi-im IGI pu-šu-ke-en6 IGI ḫu-ra-ṣa-nim
target_raw:
Regarding the 1/2 mina refined silver in the city which I gave to Enna-Suen for management, Enna-Suen said, "Your representatives drew up my tablet in the city."
If my representatives wrote up his tablet in the city, he will pay the 1/2 mina silver to my representatives.
In that eventuality (?) this tabelt of his will be dead.
If my representatives did not draw up his tablet, Enna-Suen will pay 1/2 mina silver in the city in full.
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn, Šalim-aḫum, Ḫuraṣānum.
source: translate Akkadian to English: 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-na a-lim{ki} a-na en-na-sú-en6 ša a-na bé-ú-lá-tim a-dí-nu-šu-ni um-ma en-na-sú-en6-ma ša ki-ma ku-a-tí i-na a-lim{ki} ṭup-pí il5-pu-tù šu-ma ša ki-ma i-a-ti i-na a-lim{ki} ṭup-pu-šu il5-pu-tù 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR a-na ša ki-ma i-a-ti i-ša-qal i-ša-<gap> ṭup-pu-šu a-ni-um i-mu-at šu-ma ṭup-pu-šu ša ki-ma i-a-ti lá il5-pu-tù 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-na a-lim{ki} šál-ma-am i-na-sú-en6 i-ša-qal IGI ša-lim-a-ḫi-im IGI pu-šu-ke-en6 IGI ḫu-ra-ṣa-nim
target:

15 Seqel Silber, (stehen) bei Abūšalim, dem Sohne des Aššurtāb; 6½ Seqel Silber (stehen) bei Aššurmalik, dem Schmied; 2 Seqel Silber: Aššurtāb, der Transporteur; ⅔ Seqel Silber: Ḫaltika; 5 Seqel Silber: Daknabura ; 4 Seqel Silber: Lībūrbēlī; 2½ Seqel Silber: der Angestellte des Ḫalitka; 1⅓ Seqel Silber: der…...... des Öles (?); 2½ Seqel Silber: Šiwašmi; 3 Seqel Silber: Kakuzānum.

The 38 shekels of silver that Amur-ilī had as a claim against Adad-bām, Aššur -taklāku has taken (that).

source_raw: 1 GÚ 20 ma-na AN.NA É pí-lá-aḫ-IŠTAR a-na a-mur-DINGIR a-dí-in 2 GÚ 9 ma-na AN.NA ša šé-ep DINGIR-na-da as-ni-iq-šu-um 11 ma-na 10 GÍN AN.NA ni-ip-lá-tim <gap> ma-na <gap> GÍN AN.NA da-tám KÙ.BI 25.83333 ma-na 3 GÍN ŠÀ.BA 25 ma-na LÁ 13 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR iš-qúl ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i li-bi a-mur-DINGIR
target_raw:
Zwei talent zwei Minen Zinn <vom> Hause des Pilaḫištar habe ich dem Āmurilī gegeben.
(Weitere) zwei Talent neun Minen Zinn von der Karawane des Ilīnādā habe ich ihm überreicht.
Elf Minen zehn Seqel Zinn sind Ausgleichszahlungen; <x Minen x Seqel> Zinn sind Wegzoll.
Die mir (dafür zustehende) [Sil]ber(menge beträgt) 25⅚ Minen 3 Seqel.
Davon hat er 24 Minen 48 Seqel Silber bezahlt.
Den Rest des Silbers, eine Mine fünf Seqel Silber ist Āmurilī (mir) schuldig.

target_raw:
Amur-Aššur s. Aššur-taklāku owes the merchant 1 talent fine copper.
He will pay nine weeks from the month of Ṣipʾum, eponymate of Išme-Aššur.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 minas per talent per month.
Witnesses: Mannu-bālum-Aššur s. Ṣilli-Adad, Aribtua s. Amur-Aššur, Puzur-Aššur s. Šū-Bēlum.

source_raw: 1 ma-na 10 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pu-um iš-tí a-al-DU10 DUMU a-šur-na-da iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša e-lá-lí ù {d}UTU-ba-ni a-na 20 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qú-ul ki-ma a-wa-at kà-ri-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb ITU.1.KAM be-el-tí-É.GAL-lim li-mu-um i-dí-a-šur DUMU ku-bi-dí IGI ku-zi-zi-a IGI ku-na-a IGI PUZUR4-a-šur DUMU DINGIR-ku-ru-ub IGI a-šur-i-dí DUMU i-li-a IGI ku-uš-im
target_raw:
Al-ṭāb s. Aššur-nādā owes 1 mina 10 shekels refined silver.
He will pay 20 ḫamuštum weeks after the ḫamuštum of Elāli and Šamaš-bāni.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add interest according to the rate of the colony.

Iddin-Abum s. Aššur-ṭāb gave 1 mina 15 shekels silver to Šalim-aḫum s. Dān-Aššur for management.
He will pay at his ascension from the city.
Iddin-abum s. Aššur-ṭāb will give to Šalim-aḫum s. Dān-Aššur separately 1 mina silver, his excise and exit tax added and he will pay 2 minas silver at his ascension from the city.
In total, Šalim-aḫum will pay 3 minas silver to Iddin-abum at his ascension from the city, whether in Kanesh or Ḫattum wherever he meets him.
Also, Šalim-aḫum will pay 1 mina ailver to Iddin-abum so that Iddin-abum s. Aššur-ṭāb will pay 2 minas silver to Šalim-aḫum s. Dān-Aššur at his ascension from the city.
Witnesses: Iliya s. Attay, Al-aḫum s. Šū-Laban, Pūšu-kēn s. Buzāzu.
He gave 2 minas refined silver to Ḫudurla s. Aššur-mālik as cash and Merali s. Ennum-Aššur will pay it to Idi-Aššur s. Aššur-ṭāb.
If Merali does not pay the silver in Waḫšušana to Idī-abum's representative Abu-waqar, Ḫurdurla will pay Iddin-abum s. Aššur-ṭāb 3 minas silver at his ascension from the city.
Witnesses: Mannum-bālum-Aššur s. Ennum-Aššur, Aššur-bāni s. Bēlum-nādā, Lediš-Ištar his brother.
Witnesses: Iliya s. Attaya, Amur-Šamaš s. Bēlānim, Pūšu-kēn s. Buzazu.
Witnesses: Šū-Bēlim s. Išim-Suen, Ilmu-damiq s. Šū-Bēlim, Puzur-Anna s. Ilī-nādā.
Month: Teʾinātim.
Eponymate: Ṭāb-Aššur s. Uzuwa.
Šalim-Ištar s. Adad-bāni owes Iddin-abum s. Aššur-ṭāb 1 mina refined silver.
He will pay at his ascension from the city.
Witnesses: Šū-Bēlum s. Idī-Suen, Šalim-aḫum s. Dan+Aššur, ...

source_raw: 1 ma-na 6.5 GÍN KÙ.GI pá-ša-lúm 5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ik-ri-bi-a 2.5 ma-na ša ni-qí-im a-na um-mì-a-ni-a 2 ma-na KÙ ša šu-be-lim ù i-a-tí ša ba-ri-ni 4 ma-na KÙ ša É ì-lí-dan 0.33333 ma-na ša ik-ri-bi4-šu-nu i pá-ni né-pí-ší-im na-dí 1 ma-na ša-wi-ru mì-ma a-nim ni-is-ḫa-sú DIRI ša-du-a-sú ša-bu 0.5 ma-na LÁ 1.5 GÍN ta-ad-mì-iq-tum ša i-li-a 10 GÍN šé-bu-ul-tum ša IŠTAR-um-mì 2 GÍN a-gi5-a-a 2 GÍN me-me 2 GÍN ì-lí-a 2 GÍN É ì-lí-dan 2 GÍN ší-ma-at-IŠTAR 0.5 GÍN la-qé-ep 1.5 GÍN {d}IŠKUR.SIG5 1.5 GÍN ší-la-ma-sí 1.5 GÍN mu-šé-ni-iq-tum 3 GÍN ša la-ma-sí a-na la-ma-sà-tim 10 GÍN KÙ ik-ri-bu ša be-lim a-na bu-ṣí-a a-dí 10 GÍN ik-ri-bu ša be-lim 5 GÍN ik-ri-bu ša {d}UTU a-na a-šùr-dan a-dí mì-ma a-nim a-na a-šùr-dan áp-qí-id IGI a-šùr-ba-ni IGI šu-li DUMU me-ra-lí
target_raw:
1 mina 6 1/2 shekels pašallum-gold and 5 minas silver for my votive fund, 2 1/2 minas for nāiqum to my investors, 2 minas silver belonging to Šū-Bēlum and myself, between us, 4 minas silver belonging to the house of Ilī-dān, 1/3 mina for their votive fund is deposited at the top of the sack, 1 mina bracelets--all this , its excise added and shipping paid, 28 1/2 shekels as tadmiqtum of Iliya, 10 shekels shipping charge of Ištar-ummī, 2 shekels for Agiya, 2 shekels for Meme, 2 shekels for Iliya, 2 shekels for the house of Ilī-dān, 2 shekels for Šimat-Ištar, 1/2 shekel for Lā-qēpum, 1 1/2 shekels for Adad-damiq, 1 1/2 shekels Šī-Lamassī, 1 1/2 shekels for the m., 3 shekels beloning to Lamassī to (go to) Lammassatum, 10 shekels silver for the votive offering of Bēlum I gave to Buṣiya, 10 shekels votive for Bēlum, 5 shekels for the votive offering of Šamaš I gave to Aššur-dān-- all this I entrusted to Aššur-dān.
Witnesses: Aššur-bāni, Šuli s. Merali.

source_raw: 1 ma-na 7.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-ṣé-er ma-wa-áš-ḫi a-šur-na-da i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša na-ra-am-sú a-na ḫa-ar-pè ta-ša-qal šu-ma lá ta-áš-qú-ul 3 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.1.KAM a-na 1 ma-na-em tù-ṣa-áb IGI me-ṣí-li IGI šar-ma-ma IGI ḫa-li-it-kà IGI ku-šu-ma-an
target_raw:
Mawašhi owes 1 mina 7 1/2 shekels of silver to Aššur-nādā.
She will pay before harvest from the ḫamuštum week of Naram-Suen.
If she does not paid (in time), she will add interest at the rate 3 shekels per month per mina.
Witnessed by Mezini, witnessed by Šarmama, witnessed by Halitka, witnessed by Kušuman.

source_raw: 1 ma-na AN.NA iš-tù ḫa-ḫi-im ú-šé-bi-lam-ma ší-im-šu 7.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR tí-ib-nam iš-ú-mu-nim-ma ḫa-ra-an sú-qí-nim a-ta-lá-ak-ma tí-ib-nam šu-wa-tí a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku il5-qé-ma a-na am-tim ší-im-šu lá i-dí-in
target_raw:
I sent 1 mina tin to you from Ḫaḫḫum and its price was 7 1/2 shekels silver.
They purchased straw and I left for the 'narrow track.'
Aššur-taklāku took that straw but he must not give its exchange value to the amtum-wife.

source_raw: 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-ṣé-er e-lá-a ù a-šur-ma-lik PÚZUR-a-šur i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša PÚZUR-a-šùr a-na 7 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qú-lu šu-ma lá iš-qú-lu 1.5 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣú-bu ITU.KAM té-i-na-tim li-mu-um a-šur-i-mì-tí ma-lá-ḫu-um KÙ.BABBAR i qá-qá-ad šál-mì-šu-nu ù ke-ni-šu-nu ra-ki-is IGI i-ku-pì-a IGI zu-zu-ba-ar
target_raw:
Elā(ya) and Aššur-mālik owe Puzur-Aššur 1 mina silver.
They will pay 7 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Puzur-Aššur.
If they do not pay (on time), they will add interest at a rate of 1 1/2 shekels per month
Month: Teʾintātim.
Eponymate: Aššur-imittī the boatman.
(The obligation to pay back) the silver is bound to whichever one is financially solvent and legally responsible.
Witnesses: Ikuppiya, Zuzubar.

1 sack, 10 minas 49 1/6 shekels silver Aššur-imittī s. Ennānum, 1 bundle of 1 mina 5 shekels silver, the price of the house for my representative, I entrusted all this to Aššur-nādā s. Sutiya and Balṭušar.
Witnesses: Innāya s. Elāli, Šū-Ištar s. Luzina, Irma-Aššur.
1 sack, 6 minas 5 shekels silver beloning to Aššur-imittī s. Ennānum, 1 sack of 12 minas 18 5/6 shekels belonging to Šalim-aḫum, 1 sack 10 minas silver beloning to the sons of Lā-qēpum for Aššur-imittī, I entrusted all this to Šuli-s. Dādum.
Witnesses: Libbaya s. Uzuwa, Kula brother of Šukkutum, Buzuwa s. Aššur-ṭāb.
1 sack of 6 47 shekels silver belonging to Aššur-imittī s. Ennānum, 1/2 mina silver belonging to Ilī-ālum s. Ilpi-uṣur, I took from Tūrāya for him.
x shekels silver from his textiles from Sumānum, 2 shekels of his 'offerings' of the son of Buzutaya, I entrusted all this to Uṣur-ša-Aššur.
Witnesses: Dān-Aššur, Irma-Aššur.
23 shekels silver of its totality of Tuklanum which Šū-Ḫubur paid, Irma-Aššur brings to you.
Witnesses: Uṣur-ša-Aššur, Dān-Aššur.
1 sack, 6 minas 48 1/3 shekels silver beloning to Aššur-imittī s. Ennānum, I ... 5 shekels and I entrusted all this to Kurub-Ištar.
Witnesses: Šū-Bēlum s. Amur-ilī, Aššur-imittī s. Ikuppī-Ištar.
I checked the package of Aššur-imittī and Ilī-ālum took 1 talent.
I took the 2 minas surplus.
Witnesses: Kurub-Ištar, Nāb-Suen s. Puzur-Ištar, Ennum-Bēlum s. Šū-Laban.
I gave 1 talent 2 minas 25 shekels tin and 2 textiles belonging to Aššur-imittī to Aššur-šamšī s. Lā-qēpum.
Witnesses: Ikūnum s. Samaya, Dān-Aššur s. Šalim-aḫum

source_raw: 1 ṭup-pu-um ša 5 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫu-bu-ul bu-ur-IŠTAR 1 ṭup-pu-um ša 2.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ú-ṣú-ra-nim DUMU šu-lá-ba-an a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku DUMU šu-{d}EN.LÍL a-na i-tur4-DINGIR e-zi-ib-ma 7.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR me-eḫ-ra-tim i-tur4-DINGIR a-na a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku i-dí-in iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša a-šur-na-da ù e-me-me 1.5 GÍN.TA i ITU a-na 1 ma-na-em ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣú-bu šu-ma lá iš-ta-aq-lu-nim KÙ.BABBAR ú ṣí-ba-sú a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku i-ša-qal IGI pu-šu-ke-en6 DUMU sú-e-a IGI a-šur-i-mì-tí DUMU i-ku-pì-IŠTAR
target_raw:
Aššur-taklāku son of Šū-Enlil left with Itūr-ilī one tablet stating 5 minas 5 shekels silver from the debt of Bur-Ištar; (and) one tablet stating 2 1/2 minas silver (owed by) Ušurānum son of Šū-Laban and Itūr-ilī gave Aššur-taklāku 7 1/2 minas silver as exchange value.
From the ḫamuštum-week of Aššur-nādā and Ememe, they will add 1 1/2 shekels per month per mina as interest.
If they do not pay (on time), Aššur-taklāku will pay the silver and its interest.
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn son of Sueyya and Aššur-imittī son of Ikuppī-Ištar.

target_raw:
The son of Ṣilliya and the brother of Dadanīya owe 1 mina 27 shekels refined silver.
They will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-rēʾī and Ennamānim.
Thereof, the 27 shekels checked (silver) and the 9 shekels (silver) the price of šurum textile, he deducted to me(?) 5 shekels silver, the interest on 5 textiles, and he owes the rest of the silver and its interest.
He will 'check' the silver from the ḫamuštum of Narām-Suen and Ḫanānum brother of Dagāniya.
7 1/2 shekels silver from my lot and interest on 1/2 mina--4 1/2 shekels, comes to 12! shekels.
You owe (it).
Ilaliaḫšu and Peruwa are guarantors.

target_raw:
Dadaya owes Ikuppiya 10 minas refined silver.
He will pay six weeks from the ḫamuštum of in-na-a-šur
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
Month: Teʾinātim.
Eponymate: Ilī-dān.
Witnesses: Aššur-dūrī, Abu-šalim.

target_raw:
Enli-bāni s. Ilī-bāni owes Imdi-ilum 10 mina refined silver.
He will pay 60 weeks from the ḫamštum of Aššur-bēl-awātim s. Panaka and Aššur-ṭāb s. Qarwiya
If he does not pay at the fulfillment of his term, he will 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest.
Witnesses: Enna-Bēlim s. Šū-Laban, Uṣur-ša-Ištar s. Aššur-imittī.
Idī-abim s. Idī-Ištar owes Aššur-imittī and Imdi-ilum 22 1/2 minas silver.
He will pay 55 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-taklāku and Itūr-ilī.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels.
Month: Ṣip'um.
Year: [broken].
Witnesses: [broken], Iddin-[...], [broken](?).
Ušur-ša-Ištar owes 34 (1/2?) shekels silver.
Witnesses: Enna-Bēlum, [1 broken name].
Thereof, the 'third' of the caravan of Ennam-Anum is with/owed by Imdi-ilum.
Ištar-pilaḫ s. Anninum owes 1 mina 23 3/4 shekels silver.
He will pay at the time of his departure.
Witnesses: Aḫ-šila his transporter, Aššur-dān s. Ikuppiya, Iddin-Adad s. Ṣilli-Adad.
I gave your representatives' tin to Aššur-nādā s. Aššur-idī.
Kuzizīya owes Imdi-ilum 21 shekels extremely fine
He will pay 13 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Pūšu-kēn and Kurub-Ištar.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1/2 shekels.
Month: Kuzallu.
Year: Al-aḫum s. Inaḫ-ilum.
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn, Ḫuraṣānum s. Šū-Anum, Aššur-rē'ī s. Idīda.
Imdi-ilum gave Iddin-Aššur 1 mina silver.
He will pay promptly at his coming up to Kanesh.
Witnesses: Enlil-bāni s. Ilī-bāni, Amur-ilī s. Ibbī-Suen.
I gave 1/2 mina refined silver to Ennam-Aššur s. Šalim-aḫum.
Dān-Aššur will pay me the silver.
He (Imdi-ilum?) gave it as a capital loan (be'alum).
They will pay x ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum of Enna-Bēlum and Aššur-ṣulūlī.
If they do not pay (on time) they will add x shekels (as interest).

source_raw: 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er šu-na-bar a-šùr-na-da i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša da-da-nim ù a-šùr-ba-ni a-na 44 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá ìš-qú-ul 1.5 GÍN.TA i ITU.KAM a 1 ma-na-em ú-ṣa-áb a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku qá-ta-tum IGI a-mur-a-šur DUMU DINGIR.SIPA IGI en-nam-a-šur DUMU i-li-IŠTAR
target_raw:
Šū-Nabar owes Aššur-nādā 10 minas refined silver.
He will pay 44 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum week of Dadāanum and Aššur-bāni.
If he does not pay, he will add 1 1/2 shekels per month per mina.
Aššur-taklāku is the guarantor.
Witnesses: Amur-Aššur s. Ilī-rēʾī, Ennam-Aššur s. Ilī-Ištar.

source_raw: 10.66666 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er šu-ḫu-ur-pì-a <gap> ù ku-ur-ku-ra a-šùr-na-da i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša šu-a-nim ù ì-lí-iš-tí-kál a-na 3 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qú-lu šu-ma i-na u4-mì-šu-nu ma-al-ú-tim lá iš-qú-lu 3 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM a-na 1 ma-na-im ú-ṣú-pu KÙ.BABBAR i qá-qá-ad šál-mì-šu-nu ù ke-ni-šu-nu ra-ki-is IGI a-šur-ma-lik IGI il5-ba-ni
target_raw:
Šuhurpia, Ma[ ... ] and Kurkura owe 10 2/3 minas 5 shekels of silver to Aššur-nādā.
They will pay 3 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum week of Šu-Anum and Iliš-tikal.
If they have not paid within their completed term they will add interest at the rate 3 shekels per month per mina.
They are jointly responsible for the silver.
Witnesses: Aššur-malik, Ilī-bānī.

target_raw:
10 2/3 mina silver is with Amur-Aššur s. Išme-ilī.
From the ḫamuštum of Ḫa-bi₄-a-aḫ-šu and Lāqēp he has 30 weeks to pay.
3 1/2 mina 9 shekels silver is with Puzur-ilī s. Uṣur-ša-Ištar.
Puzur-Ištar s. Ipìlís and Mannum-bālum-Aššur s. Dunnīya owe 3 mina 13 1/2 shekels silver.
From the ḫamuštum of Aššur-bēl-awātim they have one month to pay.
3 1/2 minas silver is with Dudu s. Ištar-bāni.
From the ḫamuštum of Aššur-imittī he has 20 weeks to pay.
1/3 mina 2/3 shekel silver is will Iziz-Mīšar.
2 1/3 minas 5 shekels silver is with Aššur-nādā and 16 shekels is separately with him?
From the ḫamuštum of Aššur-imittī he has one month to pay.
2 minas is with Ḫu-bi-iš-nu-ma-an and Šili-uman . . . Nipas will pay.
1/3 mina capital is with Al-abim.
3 minas silver is with Ki-dí-im.
From the ḫamuštum of Ḫanānum and Šalim-Aššur he has 40 weeks to pay.
1 mina - 1 2/3 shekels is with A-bi₄-lá-ma-sí, I-tù-a-ri-ší will pay.
10 shekels is with Ennamānim s. Uta-aḫum.
2/3 minas 4 shekels silver is with Nāb-Suen s. Gal-ga-ri-im in the city will pay.

Aššur-imittī his brother, Zizizi his wife, and Uṣur-ša-Aššur his son repayed us 11 minas 17 shekels refined silver, from the funds of Atata son of Mannum-bālum-Aššur.
As for the silver they will pay, if they do not pay, they will pay 1 mina (extra) for each mina.
(Month:) Ab-šarrānē.
Eponymate: Enna-Suen son of Iddin-abum.
In the presence of Attaya son of Buziya, Pūšu-kēn son of Šarawa, and Aššur-mutabbil son of Iddin-abum.

source_raw: 11 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR li-tí SIG5 i-ṣé-er ḫa-ar-šu-nu-ma-an AḪ.ME ša {d}UTU pu-šu-ke-en6 i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša šu-ku-bi-im ù kur-ub-IŠTAR a-na 13 ḫa-am-ša-tim a-na ša pár-kà ša i-lá-kà-ni i-ša-qal šu-ma i-na u4-me-šu ma-al-ú-tim <gap> lá iš-qúl 2.5 GÍN.TA a ma-na-im ú-ṣa-ab ITU.KAM ša ke-na-tim li-mu-um ša qá-tí i-dí-a-bi-im IGI a-de8-lá-at IGI en-na-nim IGI ḫa-al-ki-a-šu IGI ar-zi-ap
target_raw:
Ḫaršunuma, the priest of Šamaš, owes Pūšu-kēn 11 minas of fine liti silver.
He will pay 13 ḫamuštum-weeks from the ḫamuštum-week of Šū-Kūbum and Kurub-Ištar at the festival of Parka which is coming.
If he does not pay when the term is full, he will add 2 1/2 shekels per mina.
Eponymate: The one following Idī-abim.
Witnesses: Adelat, Ennānum, Ḫalikiaššu, Arziap.

source_raw: 11 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am a-na a-šur-dan a-na be-a-lim a-šùr-SIPA i-dí-in ITU.KAM a-lá-na-tím li-mu-um šu-da-a DUMU en-na-nim a-na 13 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM a-na 1 ma-na-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb 5 pá-šu-re ša-a-ma ù ú-ta ù 5 pá-šu-re ša-a-ma 1 BANEŠ LÀL 1 BANEŠ ḫi-im-tám iš-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pì-šu-ma ḫa-muš-tim ša i-dí-a-šur ú a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku IGI lá-li-im IGI ku-ra
target_raw:
Aššur-rēʾī gave 11 minas refined silver to Aššur-dān as a beʾalum loan.
He will pay 13 ḫamuštum weeks (from) the month Allanātim, eponymate of Šudaya s. Ennānum.
If he does not pay, he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
Five tables ... and grain ... and 5 tables ... 1 šūt measure of honey, 1 šūt measure of butter(?) is with his silver.
Ḫamuštum of Idī-Aššur and Aššur-taklāku.
Witnesses: Lālum, Kura.

source_raw: 11 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er ku-na-a PUZUR4-sú-en6 i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša en-na-nim ù ša-lim-a-šur a-na 40 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qú-ul 1.5 GÍN.TA i ITU.KAM a-na 1 ma-na-im ú-ṣa-áb SAG ITU.KAM ku-zal-li li-mu-um ib-ni-{d}IŠKUR DUMU ba-qú-nim IGI PUZUR4-sa-du-e IGI ú-ṣú-ur-ša-a-šur DUMU a-gu5-a IGI a-šur-iš-ta-ki-il5
target_raw:
Kunāya owes Puzur-Suen 11 minas refined silver.
He will pay 40 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Ennānum and Šalim-Aššur.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
From the head of the month Kuzalli, eponymate of Ibni-Adad s. Baqqunum.
Witnesses: Puzur-šaduʾe, Uṣur-ša-Aššur s. Agua, Aššuriš-takil.

source_raw: 15 ma-na AN.NA za-ku-am i-ṣé-er a-šùr-DUG DAM.GÀR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša im-dí-lim ù {d}UTU-ba-ni ITU.KAM ma-ḫu-ur-ì-lí li-mu-um ša qá-tí i-dí-a-bi-im DUMU na-ar-bi4-tim a-na 16 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma la iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA i ITU.KAM a-1 ma-na-im ú-ṣa-áb i-nu-mì zu-zu-wa iš-tù a-lim{ki} e-ta-li-a-ni a-šùr-DUG ú-lá i-qá-bi4 um-ma šu-ut-ma u4-mu-a li-im-lu-ú AN.NA a-na DAM.GÀR a i-da-an KÙ.BABBAR GÁN-a-lam5 a-šu-mì DAM.GÀR a-na-nu-um ù a-lá-num e-tí-iq IGI a-ḫu-wa-qar IGI ir-ma-a-šùr
target_raw:
Aššur-ṭab owes the merchant 15 minas clear tin.
He will pay 16 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum of Imdilim and Šamaš-bāni, month of Maḫur-ilī, eponymate after Iddin-abim s. Narbitum.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add (interest) at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
Witnesses: Aḫu-waqar, Irama-Aššūr

source_raw: 15 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša i-ku-num a-na a-mur-{d}UTU i-ḫi-ib-lu-ma pu-šu-ke-en6 qá-ta-tù-ni ṭup-pu-um ša ku-nu-uk pu-šu-ke-en6 ú i-ku-nim É im-dí-lim i-na ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim ú nu-du-a-tim ša im-dí-lim i-ba-ší i-na qá-ti PUZUR4-<gap> me-er-i-šu sà-ak-li-a DUMU šu-IŠTAR DUMU na-ap-ra-kà ú ma-nu-um-ki-a-šur DUMU šu-be-lim e-mu-ru iš-ni-ú-ma il5-ba-ni sú-en6-SIPA ù a-šur-ni-šu me-er-ú a-mur-IŠTAR DUMU a-mur-DINGIR e-mu-ru-ma i nu-du-a-tim ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim iš-tù ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim pá-tí-a-tim ṭup-pè-e ḫa-ru-mu-tim ša a-bi4-šu iš-ta-kà-šu
target_raw:
As for the 15 minas silver by which Ikūnum is in debt to Amur-Šamaš and for which Pūšu-kēn is the guarantor, the tablet with the seals of Pūšu-kēn and Ikūnum is available in the house of Imdī-ilum among the t. and n. of Imdī-ilum.
From the (account?) of Puzur-..., Saklia son of Šū-Ištar son of Napraka and Mannum-kī-Ištar son of Šū-Bēlum saw?.
They (repeated?) and Il-bāni, Suen-rēʾī, and Aššur-nīšu, the sons of Amur-Ištar son of Amur-ilī, saw and placed it among the n. (and) t. from the open t. (and) sealed tablets of his father.

He will pay forty five (weeks) from the ḫamuštum of li-ba-a-a and Dudu.
He will pay twenty seven (weeks) from the ḫamuštum of Lāqēp and ḫa-bi-a-aḫ-šu.
He will pay thirty (weeks) from the hamuštum of Ilī-bāni and Amurrum-bāni.

Aššur-mālik s. Aššur-bāni owes 7 1/2 minas refined silver.
He will pay forty (weeks) from the ḫamuštum of Šamaš-bāni and Elāli.
He will pay twenty (weeks) from the ḫamuštum of Lāqēp and ḫa-bi-a-aḫ-šu.

source_raw: 16 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-ṣé-er en-um-a-šùr ù be-ra-dí DAM.GÀR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša im-dí-lim ù a-ni-nim a-na ITU.2.KAM i-ša-qú-lu KÙ.BABBAR i qá-qá-ad šál-mì-šu-nu ra-ki-is šu-ma i-na u4-mì-šu-nu ma-al-ú-tim lá iš-qú-lu ki-ma a-wa-at kà-ri-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣú-bu ITU.KAM ma-ḫu-ur-DINGIR li-mu-um DUG-a-šur IGI šu-ku-bi4-im IGI a-šur-lá-ma-sí
target_raw:
Ennam-Aššur and Beradi owe the merchant 16 shekels silver.
They will pay 2 months after the ḫamuštum of Imdi-ilum and Anninum.
The silver is bound to whichever person is solvent.
If they do not pay at full term, they will add interest at the rate of the colony.
Month: Maḫḫur-ilī
Eponymate: Ṭāb-Aššur.
Witnesses: Šū-Kūbum, Aššur-kamassī.

2 Minen 3½ Seqel gutes . . . . Silber hat zu Lasten des Allu der (jeweilige) Gläubiger gut.
Vom Amtstage des Ili’a wird er 1 Mine 1¾ Seqel wird er bis zum siebenten Amtstag zahlen.

Vor Takniš, vor Aššurmalik, vor Šamašbāni.

source_raw: 3 ma-na 15 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ù 25 na-ru-uq ŠÀ 7 na-ru-uq ar-ša-tum ší-mì-šu pé-ru-wa a-na šu-pì-ib-ra a-bi-šu ù um-mì-šu i-dí-in iš-tù u4-mì-im a-ni-im a-na pé-ru-wa a-bu-šu ù um-mu-šu ù a-ḫu-šu lá i-tù-ru-šu-um šu-ma i-tù-ru-šu-um 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR a-na pé-ru-wa i-ša-qú-lu šu-ma pé-ru-wa a-na a-bi4-šu um-mì-šu i-tù-wa-ar 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-ša-qal šu-ma a-ḫu-um a-na a-ḫi-im i-tù-ar i-na i-dí-ni-im i-du-ku-šu IGI ta-li-a IGI ḫa-al-ki-a-šu IGI pè-ru-wa IGI lá-ba-ar-ša IGI wa-áš-ḫu-pá IGI ku-nu-wa-an
target_raw:
Peruwa gab an Šuppibra, seinem Vater und seiner Mutter, 3 Mine 15 Seqel Silber und 25 Sack (Gereide), davon 7 Sack Weizen, als seinen Preis.
Von heute an werden sein Vater, seine Mutter, und seine Brüder auf Peruwa nicht zurückkommen.
Wenn sie auf ihn zurückkommen, werden sie Peruwa 10 Mine Silber zahlen.
Wenn Peruwa auf seinen Vater (und) seine Mutter zurückkommt, wird er 10 Mine Silber zahlen.
Wenn einer auf den anderen zurückkommt, wird man ihn außerhalb der Ortsgemeinschaft töten.
Vor Talia, vor Ḫalkiašu, vor Peruwa, vor Labarša, vor Wašhupa, vor Kunuwan.

Pilaḫ-Ištar s. Ikuppiya owes Puzur-Aššur s. Šū-Laban 3 minas refined silver.
He will pay 15 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Al-aḫum and Ennam-Anim.
Month: ša kēnātum.
Eponymate: after Puzur-Niraḫ.
If he does not pay the silver at the fulfillment of his term, he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month at the colony rate as interest.

Ṣilliya owes Adi-mātum 3 minas refined silver.
From the hamuštum of Aššur-bēl-awātim and Amur-Ištar, the beginning of Month III, he will pay 1 mina silver per year.

Witnesses: Kaspum-rabi(?), Dān-Aššur.

Elama took 1 talent 20 minas tin.
Aššur-nādā took 1 talent 22 minas tin.
Elama, Aššur-nādā and Aššur-dān sent 1 talent 20 minas tin the share of Karwaya to Durḫumit to Karwaya.

target_raw:
Iliš-tīkal gave 4 minas refined silver to Enlil-bāni.
From the hamuštum-week of Aššur-imittī and Aššur-rabi, he will pay 8 minas in 4 years.
Month: Narmak Aššur.
Eponymate: The one after the year of Šudāya.
As much as he takes he will receive to the name of his narruqum.
As much as Enlil-bāni 'treads' and Ilš-tīkal, as much as (there is?) to him, he will receive with the result that his joint stock fund will enter into the house of Iliš-tīkal.
Witnesses: Lālum, Ennānum.

Seals: Aššur-imittī, Pilaḫ-Ištar

target_raw:
The 4 packets constituting 1 talent silver, its excise added, its shipping charges satisfied are mine.
The 1 packet constituting 15 minas silver is the ikrībū of Aššur.
The one bundle constituting 1/2 mina 8 1/4 shekels, it's excise added and the excise of its excise belongs to my ikrībū.
I placed the packet constituting 14 2/3 minas silver of Aššur-idī (with its?) shipping charges among my things.
I placed the packet constituting 10 minas silver belonging to Pūšu-kēn and Enna-Suen, with its excise and shipping charges, among my things.
The bundle constituting 15 shekels silver is for Kulumaya.
The bundle, 10 shekels is for Anina son of x-x, x shekels is for Ennum-Aššur son of Anninum, 2 shekels is for Aššur-dān son of Ikūn-pīya.
I ...them all this.
11 talḫadiʾātum textiles, 4 ..., 2 būritum-textiles?, 1 menunuʾānum-textile, 1 sack under my seals, (and) a warḫālum.
I entrusted all this to Dān-Aššur.
Witnesses: Puzur-Aššur son of Damutia and Ennu-Bēlum son of Šū-Laban.

source_raw: 4.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR li-tí i-ṣé-er a-lu-lu-wa i-dí-IŠTAR i-šu a-lu-lu-wa iš-tí i-dí-IŠTAR uk-ta-al mì-ma ta-ak-ší-tám ša ú-kà-šu-ú i-dí-IŠTAR ší-ta qá-té-en i-lá-qé iš-té-et qá-tám a-lu-lu-wa i-lá-qé šu-ma i pá-ni i-dí-IŠTAR pu-úz-ra-am e-ta-ḫa-az a-na 1 ma-na 6 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb šu-ma ba-lúm i-dí-IŠTAR a-na ig-ri i-ta-lá-ak i-ša-ri-iq-šu-um a-na É i-dí-IŠTAR e-ra-áb ú ú-ṣí ba-lúm i-dí-IŠTAR a-na ig-ri ú-lá i-lá-ak IGI ḫa-pu-a-lá IGI i-lá-li-a-aḫ-su IGI a-mu-ra IGI ta-lu-wa-na IGI ku-lá IGI e-lá-lí IGI ta-ta-li
target_raw:
Aluluwa owes 4 1/2 minas of lītu-silver to Iddin-Ištar;
Aluluwa is held (in employment) by Iddin-Ištar.
Whatever income he may achieve, Iddin-Ištar will take two-thirds, and Aluluwa will take one-third.
If he goes into hiding from Iddin-Ištar, he will add interest at the rate 6 shekels per mina per month.
If without Iddin-Ištar's permission he takes up work for hire, it will be taken away from him.
He may enter and leave Iddin-Ištar's house.
Without Iddin-Ištar's permission he must not take up work for hire.
Witnessed by Habuala, by Ilaliahšu, by Ammura, by Taluwana, by Kula, by Elāli, by Tatali.
source: translate Akkadian to English: 4.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR li-tí i-ṣé-er a-lu-lu-wa i-dí-IŠTAR i-šu a-lu-lu-wa iš-tí i-dí-IŠTAR uk-ta-al mì-ma ta-ak-ší-tám ša ú-kà-šu-ú i-dí-IŠTAR ší-ta qá-té-en i-lá-qé iš-té-et qá-tám a-lu-lu-wa i-lá-qé šu-ma i pá-ni i-dí-IŠTAR pu-úz-ra-am e-ta-ḫa-az a-na 1 ma-na 6 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb šu-ma ba-lúm i-dí-IŠTAR a-na ig-ri i-ta-lá-ak i-ša-ri-iq-šu-um a-na É i-dí-IŠTAR e-ra-áb ú ú-ṣí ba-lúm i-dí-IŠTAR a-na ig-ri ú-lá i-lá-ak IGI ḫa-pu-a-lá IGI i-lá-li-a-aḫ-su IGI a-mu-ra IGI ta-lu-wa-na IGI ku-lá IGI e-lá-lí IGI ta-ta-li
target:
Aluluwa owes 4 1/2 minas of lītu-silver to Iddin-Ištar;
Aluluwa is held (in employment) by Iddin-Ištar.
Whatever income he may achieve, Iddin-Ištar will take two-thirds, and Aluluwa will take one-third.
If he goes into hiding from Iddin-Ištar, he will add interest at the rate 6 shekels per mina per month.
If without Iddin-Ištar's permission he takes up work for hire, it will be taken away from him.
He may enter and leave Iddin-Ištar's house.
Without Iddin-Ištar's permission he must not take up work for hire.
Witnessed by Habuala, by Ilaliahšu, by Ammura, by Taluwana, by Kula, by Elāli, by Tatali.
label: Cuneiform Tablet ICK 1 83

Luwatuman and Tamuria owe Imdi-ilum 4 1/2 minas refined silver.
They will pay 13 ḫamuštum weeks from the week of Ili-ālim and Imdi-ilum.

target_raw:
Aḫ-šalim s. Buzutaya and Aššur-taklāku s. Al-aḫim owe Enlil-bāni 42 minas 1 shekel refined silver.
They will pay 46 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Luzina.
If they do not pay, they will add interest at the rate of 1 shekel per mina per month.
Month: bēlet-ekallim
Year: ša qātī Akūtum
Aḫ-šalim s. Buzutaya and Aššur-taklāku s. Al-aḫim owe Enlil-bāni 40 mina refined silver.
They will pay 35 weeks from the ḫamuštum ša Luzina.
If they do not pay they will add interest at the rate of 1 shekel per mina per month.
Dadaya s. Aššur-imittī owes Enlil-bāni 6 1/2 mina refined silver.
From the month of Ša sarrātim, eponymate after Maṣi-ilī -city of Assur-to come up/travel up to-toward-Kaneš-to enter-5 months-to go-silver-pay
If he does not pay he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
Šū-Suen s. Iliya owes Enlil-bāni 3 mina refined silver.
He will pay from the ḫamuštum ša kaššim ša qātī Ennanim, month Allānatim, year ša qātī Akūtum.
He will add 1 shekel per mina per month as interest.
Nāb-Suen owes Enlil-bāni 1 mina refined silver.
He will pay 11 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Ennanim.
If he does not pay he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels.
Month: Kuzalli
Year: Akūtum
Nūr-kī-ilī owes Enlil-bāni 1 mina refined silver.
He will pay from the ḫamuštum of Enna-Suen.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels.
Aššur-taklāku s. Burkia owes Enlil-bāni 1 1/2 mina refined silver.
He will pay 15 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Kurub-Ištar s. Al-aḫim.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels 1 mina.
Month: ša sarrātim
Year: Enna-Suen s. Šū-Aššur
Lā-qēpim s. Šū-Aššur owes Enlil-bāni 1/2 mina refined silver.
He will pay 6 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-taklāku s. Al-aḫim, month Teʾinātim, year Maṣi-ilī.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 2/3 shekel 15 grain.

target_raw:
Tuḫniš and the sons of Dalaš owe Aššur-rē'ī 42 minas refined silver.
They will pay 20 weeks from the
If they do not pay at the fulfillment of their term, they will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest according to the colony rate.
The silver is bound to (whomever) is financially solvent and legally responsible.
Witnesses: Puzur-Aššur, Aḫanirši, Ikūnum.
The house of Dakniš is his guarantee.

target_raw:
Witnesses: Amur-Aššur s. Šū-Ištar, Ibkum
Duḫniš and Dakniš owe 42 minas refined silver.
He will pay twenty weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-rēʾī s. ma-a-šu and Šalim-Aššur.
Anaḫ-ilī owes 20 minas refined silver.
He will pay five weeks from the ḫamuštum of Erra-idī and kà-áb-ri-a.
Amur-Aššur s. Puzur-Aššur owes 10 minas refined silver.
He will pay ten weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-bāni and en-ma-a-a.
Elāli owes 12 minas refined silver.
He will pay seven weeks from the ḫamuštum of Amur-Ištar and Puzur-Ištar.
Zuzu s. Kula 2 minas refined silver.
He will pay fourteen weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-bāni and en-ma-a-a.

Duduli and Huda his wife, Ḫištaḫšu and Anali, Šuppiuman and Talḫama his wife, Peruwa and kà-na-na his wife, Išpudaḫšu, Muanani and his wife owe Aššur-mālik 5 minas quality silver.
They will pay at the festival of Anna.
If they do not pay at the fulfillment of their term, they will add 3 shekels per mina as interest for twenty months.
Month: Maḫḫur-ilī.
Eponymate: Buzutaya.
The silver is bound to whomever is financially solvent and legally responsible.
Wintesses: Ašu, Barwamada, Abīya, Bēlī-tāb.

Iddin-Ištar son of Aššur-nādā owes 5 5/6minas of refined silver to Huraṣānum.
He will pay 13 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum of Šū-Nunu and Puzur-Aššur.

Eponymate: Al-aḫum s. Inaḫ-ilī.
Witnessed by Abu-šalim son of Iddin-Irra, witnessed by Ennam-Aššur son of Karria.

Šerdu s. Nariya owes Ikūnum 6 minas 15 shekels refined silver.
He will pay in the ḫamuštum following the ḫamuštum of Puzur-Ištar.

Isaya and Erra-dān owe 6 minas refined silver to Lā-qēpum.
They will pay 33 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Agua and Lā-qēpum.
If they do not pay at the fulfillment of their term, they will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
Witnesses: Naram-Suen, Nūr-Ištar.
The silver is bound to the head of their trustworthiness.

target_raw:
I have invested 6 1/2 minas 9 2/3 shekels of silver transported by me in the enterprise of Aššur-taklāku son of Šū-Illil;
I shall receive it from the fund of the enterprise.
2 5/6 minas of silver from the fund, 1 mina 9 shekels from the dividends, 1 donkey belonging to me, 1 donkey belonging to our father, 1 donkey belonging to Alāhum, we shall take from Šū-Nūnu's enterprise.
Aššur-taklāku will take 1 1/3 mina of silver from the fund, 2/3 mina 2 2/3 shekels from the dividends of the enterprise of Šū-Nūnu.
12 minas of copper out of 80 minas each, in Burušhaddum belonging to Aššur-nādā, 6 minas belonging to our father remain.
When there was an opportunity to invest 20 textiles, I deposited 1 1/2 black textiles; the
When there was an opportunity to invest 15
Aššur-taklāku deposited 1
The silver for this accrues to me.
3 shekels of silver in the 5 percent tax on textiles in Burušhaddum; 4 shekels for the tithe in textiles from Kasia's enterprise are with Aššur-taklāku.
There is a second tablet.

target_raw:
Idī-Suen purchased 7 talents 30 minas copper, the share of Pūšu-kēn from Ennam-Aššur for 1 1/3 minas (silver).
The silver of Ennam-Aššur is satisfied.
Thereof, the investors of Puzur-Ištar retained 1 mina silver.
They settlted the surplus of the copper to Ennam-Aššur.
Ennam-Aššūr entrusted to Idī-Suen 1 talent 5 minas tin under the seal of Pūšu-kēn, 2
Witnesses: Ennum-Aššur, Dāyā.

source_raw: 9.66666 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pu-um ša kà-ri-a a-na a-šur-na-da a-na e-bu-ṭim i-dí-nu lu-qú-tum iš-tù a-lim{ki} e-li-a-ma a-šur-DUG ša KÙ.BABBAR a-nim lu-qú-sú il5-qé šu-ma ma-ma-an a-na a-šur-na-da i-tù-a-ar a-šur-DUG ú-ba-áb-šu IGI a-šur-dan IGI im-dí-DINGIR IGI bu-ṣí-a
target_raw:
Regarding the 9 2/3 minas of refined silver which Karria has given to Aššur-nādā as an investment loan--the merchandise has arrived up here from the City, and Aššur-ṭāb has taken (his share of) the merchandise corresponding to this amount in silver.
If anybody should raise a claim against Aššur-nādā, Aššur-ṭāb will clear him.
Witnessed by Aššur-dān, witnessed by Imdī-ilum, witnessed by Buzia.

... silver ... and x minas 2 shekels silver belonging to Buzutaya, we returned to him.
From the 1 talent 4 minas tin, 6 minas silver belonging to Ṣilli-Ištar, from 2 talents 1 mina tin, 5 minas 48 2/3 shekels silver belonging to Itur-Suen; from 2 talents of tin from Ilī-ālum, 8 minas 27 shekels, within it 16 shekels for the driver we deducted.
17 1/2 shekels Ikūnum, 21 shekels, the price of a donkey, to Mannum-bālum-Aššur, 4 minas 49 3/4 shekels to Amur-Ištar, 1 mina to Šalim-Aššur, ... from the goods of Buzutaya and of Ilī-ālum we deducted.
In total, Pūšu-kēn owes 39 minas 25 shekels.
49 kutānum textiles in the house of Ennānum, 5 minas hand tin with Aššur-imittī, 3 kutānum textiles belonging to the merchant are in his account/share.
Pūšu-kēn left all this.

To ... Ennam-Aššur, Usānum, and Addu, from Ennum-Aššur.
I took 40 minas weri-copper of the dead man, a fifth of your share.
Amur-Ištar is written down(subj.?) (for?) 180 minas as the price of the fine weri-copper at the colony office.
Thereof, I myself took 78 minas 15 shekels.
Usānum took 100 (minas) weri-copper at the colony office, the price of the fine copper.
You are satisfied with regard to your weri-copper.
150 minas weri-copper, as much of your share in the colony office which I took, I gave to Usānum and Adu.
Addu took 18 minas from the 250 minas.

source_raw: IGI i-dí-{d}IM DUMU a-šùr-DU10 IGI ú-ṣú-ra-nim DUMU ku-da-a IGI en-na-sú-en6 DUMU a-šur-DU10-me-er-e lá-li-im aṣ-ba-at-ma um-ma a-na-ku-ma DUMU me-tim a-na-ku ù a-tù-nu me-er-ú me-tim-ma a-bi ṭup-pá-am e-zi-ba-am ṭup-pu-um ku-nu-uk a-bi-ku-nu lá ku-nu-uk a-bi-ku-nu ku-nu-ki ú-dí-a um-ma me-er-ú lá-li-im-ma ṭup-pá-am šé-ṣí-ma ù ku-nu-ki lu nu-dí um-ma a-na-ku-ma ku-nu-ki ú-dí-a-ma ù ṭup-pá-am la-sí-a-ku-nu-tí šu-nu-ú ṭup-pá-am ku-nu-ki ú-du-a-am ú-lá i-mu-ú um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-ma ša-ni-iš a-lim{ki} ù ru-ba-am ta-ma-ú kà-i-lá ki-ma ku-nu-ki ú-du-a-am lá i-mu-ú-ni ITU.KAM ku-zal-li li-mu-um i-na-a DUMU a-mur-ra-a iš-tù ṭup-pá-am ú-la ú-kà-li-mu šu-nu-ú ú-du-a-am lá i-mu-ú-ni
target_raw:
The sons of Lālum seized me:
I said: "I am the son of a dead man, and you are sons of a dead man. My father left me a tablet. As for the tablet, make known whether the seals are the seasl of your father or not."
The sons of Lālum said: "Bring out the tablet so that we can idetnify the seals."
I said: "Identify the seals so that I can realease the tablet to you."
They did not want to identify the seals on the tablet.
I said: "Lo, you must swear by the oath of the city and the prince. Make offerings."
They did not want to identify that they were the seals (in question).
Month: Kuzalli.
After they did not k'l the tablet, they did not want to identify.

Seal of Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur son of Šū-Nunu, seal of Šū-Laban son of Āl-ṭāb, seal of Pilaḫ-Ištar son of Aššur-bāni, seal of Anina son of Uṣur-ša-Aššur, seal of Idī-abum son of Aššur-mālik, seal of Sueyya son of Pūšu-kēn, seal of Buzāzu son of Pūšu-kēn, seal of Ikūppaša son of Pūšu-kēn, seal of Aḫaḫa daugher of Pūšu-kēn.
The sons of Pūšu-kēn and the daughter of Pūšu-kēn, the ugbābtum-priestess, will not return to (did not return to?) their brother Aššur mutabbil for the silver--the 40 minas silver which Ikūppiya son of Attaya owed to Pūšu-kēn in Kaneš and Aššur-mutabbil and which Bēlānum the attorney received.
Now, right at his ascent to Kanesh, the sons of Pūš-kēn and the daughter of Pūšu-kēn, the ugbābtum-priestess, will give the tablet stating the debt of Ikūppiya son of Attaya to Aššur-mutabbil.
According to the verdict of the city, the sons of Pūšu-kēn and the daughter of Pūšu-kēn are paid (their) silver in full.
Iddin-abum son of Aššur-mālik stood for Aḫaḫa.

Seal of Aššur-dān, seal of Buzia, seal of Imdī-ilum, seal of Aššur-ṭāb.
Concerning the 9 2/3 minas of refined silver which Karria gave to Aššur-nādā as an investment loan: The silver and the purchases made with it has left the City, and Aššur-ṭāb has taken his merchandise.
Should anyone raise a claim against Aššur-nādā, Aššur-ṭāb will clear him.

Regarding the 2 minas refined silver which Uṣur-ša-Ištar owes to Aššur-rēʾī.
He will pay 1 mina a year from the ḫamuštum of Buṣiya and Šalim-Aššur, month of Qarrātum, eponymat of Šudāya s. Ennānum.
He will pay 1 mina in the second year.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month
Aššur-rēʾī has paid(?) 1 mina 45 shekels silver of the caravan of Aššur-mālik
This memorandum is a copy of the sealed tablet of the debt Uṣur-ša-Ištar.

Seals: Arši-aḫ s. Gabrīya, Puzur-ilī s. Ikuppiya, Ṣilli-Adad s. Naplis.

source_raw: KIŠIB be-ga-a DUMU IŠTAR-pá-li-il5 KIŠIB a-ni-na DUMU a-šur-bé-el-a-wa-tim KIŠIB a-lá-ḫi-im DUMU šál-ma-a-šur KIŠIB a-ḫi-a DUMU lá-li-im 45 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er a-ḫi-a DUMU lá-li-im li-pí-it-IŠTAR a-ḫi-šu i-šu iš-tù ḫa-mu-uš-tim ša en-um-a-šur ú ša-lim-a-ḫi-im a-na 50 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-im i ITU.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb ITU.KAM ṣí-ip-um li-mu-um a-šur-i-mì-tí ma-lá-ḫu-um a-na-num i-na KÙ.BABBAR a-lá-num i-na lu-qú-tim qá-tí ša-ak-na-at IGI i-ku-pì-a DUMU ili5-ma-šar IGI a-šùr-ni-šu DUMU a-mur-IŠTAR IGI šu-be-lim DUMU PUZUR4-{d}IM
target_raw:
Aḫīya s. Lālim owes Lipit-Ištar his brother 45 minas refined silver.
He will pay 50 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Ennum-Aššur and Šalim-aḫim.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
Month: ṣipʾum
Eponymate: Aššur-imittī malāḫum
After he will place that silver from the merchandise in custody of Annanum.

Witnesses: Bēlum-bāni s. Aššur-bēl-awātim, Šar-Suen s. Ma-ni-a, Wer-rēṣi the scribe, Nimar-Ištar s. Puzur-Suen.

He will pay 2 months from the of Šumma-libbi-Aššur, month of Ša kēnātim, eponymate after Samaya.

Seals: Bezīya s. Anaḫ-ilī, Ilī-rēʾī s. Šū-Kūbum, Aššur-bani s. Kurbāya.
Aššur-bāni owes 4 minas refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Kuzallu, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Šū-Laban s. Dān-ilī, Bezīya s. Anaḫ-ilī, Nimar-Adad s. Enna-Suen, Amur-Ištar s. Išar-kit-Ištar.
Amur-Ištar received 4 minas silver, the price of the houses(/my house?).
Seals: Lipit-Ištar, Ṭāb-Aššur s. Enlil-bāni, Kuzīya s. Buzizi, Imlikaya.
Imlikaya owes 2 minas 41 1/3 shekels refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Aššur-bāni s. Kurbāya, Ilī-rēʾī s. Šū-Kūbum, Imlikaya.
Imlikaya owes 3 minas silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Iluku s. Šū-Ištar, Ištar-bāni s. Asquidim.
Ištar-bāni owes 18 shekels refined silver.
Month: Kuzallu.
Eponymate: Buzuzu.
Seals: Uṣur-ša-Aššur s. Agua, Aššur-mālik s. Puzur-Ištar, Puzur-Aššur.
Aššur-mālik owes 3 minas refined silver.
Month: Ṣipʾum.
Eponymate: Aššur-damiq.
Seals: Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum, Takil-Aššur s. Ennānum.
Takil-Aššur s. Ennānum owes 5 talents 15 minas poor quality copper.
They will add 1 1/2 minas (per talent per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ilī-dān.
Seals: Šalim-Aššur s. Ipḫurum, Atata s. Akutum, Ilī-ašranni s. Šū-Bēlum.
Suen-pilaḫ s. Šū-Bēlum owes the merchant 11 minas refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekel (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Ab šarrānē, eponymate after Ilī-dān.
Seals: Idī-ilum s. Šar-ilī, Abiya s. Aššur-imittī, Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum.
Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum owes 3 minas refined silver.
He will pay 30 shekels in one year.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest.
Month: Allānātum.
Eponymate: Buzutaya.
Seperately, 1 mina silver is his beʾulātum loan.
Seals: Aššur-bāni s. Kurubāya, Suen-pilaḫ s. Šū-Bēlum, Kuzīya s, Buzizi.
Aššur-bāni s. Kurbāya owes 2 minas 6 shekels refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Ša kēnātim, eponymate after Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Mannum-bālum-Aššur, Kudātum, Aššur-imittī, Aššur-mālik.
Al-abum owes 33 shekels silver.
Guarantor: Kudātum.
He will pay after he returns to the City.

Seals: Bezīya s. Anaḫ-ilī, Ilī-rēʾī s. Šū-Kūbum, Aššur-bani s. Kurbāya.
Aššur-bāni owes 4 minas refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Kuzallu, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Šū-Laban s. Dān-ilī, Bezīya s. Anaḫ-ilī, Nimar-Adad s. Enna-Suen, Amur-Ištar s. Išar-kit-Ištar.
Amur-Ištar received 4 minas silver, the price of the houses(/my house?).
Seals: Lipit-Ištar, Ṭāb-Aššur s. Enlil-bāni, Kuzīya s. Buzizi, Imlikaya.
Imlikaya owes 2 minas 41 1/3 shekels refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Aššur-bāni s. Kurbāya, Ilī-rēʾī s. Šū-Kūbum, Imlikaya.
Imlikaya owes 3 minas silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Iluku s. Šū-Ištar, Ištar-bāni s. Asquidim.
Ištar-bāni owes 18 shekels refined silver.
Month: Kuzallu.
Eponymate: Buzuzu.
Seals: Uṣur-ša-Aššur s. Agua, Aššur-mālik s. Puzur-Ištar, Puzur-Aššur.
Aššur-mālik owes 3 minas refined silver.
Month: Ṣipʾum.
Eponymate: Aššur-damiq.
Seals: Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum, Takil-Aššur s. Ennānum.
Takil-Aššur s. Ennānum owes 5 talents 15 minas poor quality copper.
They will add 1 1/2 minas (per talent per month) as interest (from) the month of Qarrātum, eponymate of Ilī-dān.
Seals: Šalim-Aššur s. Ipḫurum, Atata s. Akutum, Ilī-ašranni s. Šū-Bēlum.
Suen-pilaḫ s. Šū-Bēlum owes the merchant 11 minas refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekel (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Ab šarrānē, eponymate after Ilī-dān.
Seals: Idī-ilum s. Šar-ilī, Abiya s. Aššur-imittī, Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum.
Abu-šalim s. Asqūdum owes 3 minas refined silver.
He will pay 30 shekels in one year.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest.
Month: Allānātum.
Eponymate: Buzutaya.
Seperately, 1 mina silver is his beʾulātum loan.
Seals: Aššur-bāni s. Kurubāya, Suen-pilaḫ s. Šū-Bēlum, Kuzīya s, Buzizi.
Aššur-bāni s. Kurbāya owes 2 minas 6 shekels refined silver.
He will add 1 1/2 shekels (per mina per month) as interest (from) the month of Ša kēnātim, eponymate after Ṣilūlu.
Seals: Mannum-bālum-Aššur, Kudātum, Aššur-imittī, Aššur-mālik.
Al-abum owes 33 shekels silver.
Guarantor: Kudātum.
He will pay after he returns to the City.
label: Cuneiform Tablet Landsberger Kayseri 1

Regarding the debt of Abu-šalim.
Abu-šalim owes Aššur-rēʾī 1 1/2 minas 7 1/3 shekels refined silver.
He will pay 3 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-Šamši and Amur-Šamas.

target_raw:
Seals of Dadaya s. Aššur-imittī, Šū-Hubur s. iddin-abum, Kukulāanum s. Kutaya.
Kukulānum owes Enlil-bāni 5 minas refined silver.
From the 77 pirikannum textiles placed in the account of Enlil-bāni...
He will pay 6 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum week of Aššur-imittī, month Ša sarrātim, eponymate after the one of Ilī-ālim.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
As for the pirikannum textiles, those belonging to Kukkulānum will go to Anatolia, to Waḫšušana.
The silver from their sale, Enlil-bāni will take ithat silver abroad to Kanesh under the name of Kukulānum and if it exceeds (the 5 mians silver) the Kukulānum will take (the excess), and if it falls short, Kukulānum will stand (for the remainder).
Seals of Kukkulānum s. Kutaya, Lā-qēpum s. Puzur-sadūʾē, Šū-Enlil s. Mannum-kī-Aššur.
Enlil-bāni owes Enlil-bāni 1 1/2 minas refined silver.
Month: Ša Kēnātim.
Eponymate: the one after Ennam-Aššur.
He will pay in four months.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina (per month) as interest.
Seals of Aḫu-waqar s. Šū-Ištar, Ilī-nādā s. Aššur-nādā, Kulumaya s. Aššur-imittī, Buzāzu s. Pūšu-kēn.
Buzazu owes Enlil-bāni 1 mina refined silver (In addition to 1 mina 1/2 shekel he fulfilled- he declared. the silver).
From the ḫamuštum of liptānum, month Qarrātum, eponymate of Sama, he will add 1 shekel as interest per mina (per month).
Kulumaya is the guarantor.
(The silver) is bound to whichever one among them is solvent.
Seals of Qatātum s. Idī-Ištar, Aššur-rābi s. Lā-qēpum, Nanaya s. Ḫanānum, Puzur-Aššur s. Amur-Aššur.
Puzur-Aššur holds 1 mina refined silver, the beʾulātum loan of Enlil-bāni.
He is held by the silver.
This is a copy of the sealed tablets which have gone abroad.

source_raw: KIŠIB e-na-sú-in DUMU ì-lí-a-lim KIŠIB a-šùr-DU10 DUMU ki-i-ki sú-in-na-da DUMU en-um-a-šùr ú iš-ma-a-šur DUMU ḫi-na-a tap-pá-ú-ni šu-IŠTAR DUMU a-šùr-ba-ni en-nam-a-šùr DUMU ša-lim-a-ḫi-im ù ma-nu-um-ki-a-šùr DUMU a-ḫu-wa-qar i-na mì-ig-ra-tí-šu-nu i-na wa-aḫ-šu-ša-na i-bu-tù-ni-a-tí-ma a-mu-tám ša ma-nu-um-ki-a-šur ša a-na GAL sí-kà-tim i-ni-id-nu lu URUDU úṣ-a-am É šu-IŠTAR i-ba-ší lu i-ṣú-ur-tum lu URUDU i-na É.GAL-lim a-ḫu-ur lu URUDU i-na É.GAL-lim ú-ṣí-am 3-šu-nu a-na KÙ.BABBAR ù-ta-ru-ma KÙ.BABBAR a-na kà-ni-iš i-lá-ak-ma šu-IŠTAR en-nam-a-šur ú ma-nu-um-ki-a-šur a-na ṭup-pé-šu-nu ú da-na-tí-šu-nu e-ta-ú ú-lá ra-áb-šu a-dí e-ta-ú-ni a-ḫu-um i pá-ni a-ḫi-im mì-ma ú-lá i-lá-qé a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um kà-ni-iš i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma IGI GÍR ša a-šur ší-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in
target_raw:
Seal of Ennam-Suen, son of Ilī-ālum, seal of Aššur-ṭāb, son of Ki'ki;
Suen-nādā, son of Ennam-Aššur, and Išme-Aššur son of Ḫinnaya, were our partners.
Šu-Ištar, son of Aššur-bāni, Ennam-Aššur, son of Šalim-aĭhum, and Mannum-kī-Aššur, son of Aḫu-waqar, by mutual greement seized us in Waḫšušana
and as for the meteoric iron of Mannum-kī-Aššur that had not been given to rabi sikkatim, whether copper had come forth (and) is in Šu-Ištar's house, or there is an iṣurtu-document, or copper is still due from the palace, or copper has come forth from the palace, the three of them will convert it to silver and that silver will go to Kanesh and then Šu-Ištar, Ennam-Aššur and Mannum-kī-Aššur will discuss on the basis of their tablets and valid deeds without raising claims.
Until they have discussed, nobody shall take anything before the other.
For this matter the colony at Kanesh gave us permission, and before Aššur's dagger we gave our testimony.

target_raw:
Ennānum s. Azua owes Aššur-rēʾī 3 minas 15 shekels refined silver.
He will pay 1 mina 5 shekels one year from the hamuštum week of Ilī-ālum and Uṣur-ša-Aššur, month Ab-šarrānē, year of Buzuzu.
He will pay 1 mina 5 shekels at the second year.
He will pay 1 mina 5 shekels in the third year.
If he does not pay at the fulfillment of his term, he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
This memorandum is a copy of the sealed tablet of the debt of Ennānum.

source_raw: KIŠIB i-ku-pí-a-šur DUMU pì-lá-aḫ-a-šur KIŠIB a-šur-ma-lik DUMU a-lá-ḫi-im KIŠIB {d}MAR.TU-ba-ni DUMU šu-ku-bi4-im KIŠIB i-dí-a-šur DUMU ku-ku-lá-nim KIŠIB lá-qé-pì-im DUMU a-šur-ma-lik KIŠIB a-šur-qá-bi4-a DUMU ku-ra-a-a KIŠIB ni-na-ša DAM a-pì-lá lá-qé-pu-um DUMU a-šur-ma-lik a-šur-qá-bi4-a DUMU ku-ra-a-a ù ni-na-ša DAM a-pì-lá ki-ma a-pì-lá i-na mì-ig-ra-tí-šu-nu iṣ-bu-tù-ni-a-tí-ma a-wa-tí-šu-nu nu-ga-me-er-ma ni-iš a-lim{ki} it-mu-ú-ma ṭup-pá-am ša 2 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫu-bu-ul zu-zu-ba DUMU ku-ut-ba-kà-nim a-bu a-pì-lá ša a-na a-šur-ma-lik DUMU lá-qé-pì-im ḫa-bu-lu a-na ITU.4.KAM i-dí-a-šur DUMU ku-ku-lá-nim ú-ba-lam-ma i-na re-eš15 ṭup-pì-im 2 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ki-ma a-pì-lá ni-na-ša a-ša-sú ú a-šur-qá-bi4-a a-na i-dí-a-šur DUMU ku-ku-lá-nim i-ša-qú-lu 45 ma-na URUDU ma-sí-am lá-qé-pu-um il5-qé šu-ma ṭup-pá-am ša ša-ba-e ša ku-nu-uk a-šur-ma-lik lu ša ší-be-e ša zu-zu-ba a-na a-šur-ma-lik iš-ku-nu-ma KÁ DINGIR iḫ-ri-mu a-pì-lá a-na ITU.4.KAM i-tab-lam 45 ma-na URUDU ma-sí-am i-dí-a-šur DUMU ku-ku-lá-nim a-na ša ki-ma a-pì-lá ú-ta-ar-ma ṭup-pu-um ṭup-pá-am i-ma-ḫa-aṣ ṭup-pu-um iš-tù kà-ni-iš a-dí tur4-ḫu-mì-it a-šu-mì a-pì-lá GÁN-lam e-tí-iq KÙ.BABBAR i qá-qá-ad šál-mì-šu-nu ra-ki-is ITU.KAM ku-zal-li li-mu-um šu-ra-ma a-šur-ma-lik i-ku-pì-a-šur ù {d}MAR.TU-ba-ni a-wi-lu a-ni-ú-tum ga-me-ru a-wa-tim
target_raw:
Seals: Ikuppi-Aššur s. Pilaḫ-Aššur, Aššur-mālik s. Al-aḫum, Amurrum-bāni s. Šū-Kūbum, Idī-Aššur s. Kukkulānum, Lā-qēpum s. Aššur-mālik, Aššur-qābiya s. Kura, Ninašša w. Abela.
Lā-qēpum s. Aššur-mālik, Aššur-qabīya s. Kuraya and Ninašša w. Abela, on behalf of Abela, seized us regarding their agreement.
(The obligation to pay) the silver is bound to whichever one is financially solvent.
Month: Kuzalli.
Eponymate: Šū-rama.
Aššur-mālik, Ikuppī-Aššur and Amurrum-bāni, these men, were the resolvers of the dispute.

source_raw: KIŠIB i-tur4-DINGIR DUMU a-mur-IŠTAR KIŠIB a-lá-bi-im KIŠIB zu-zu ša ḫu-bu-ul zu-zu 5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am ša a-šur-SIPA iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša sú-kà-lí-a ù ú-ra-a a-na 10 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM-im a-na 1 ma-na-im ú-ṣa-áb KIŠIB GAL-a-šur DUMU i-ba-a KIŠIB ku-bi-na-da DUMU bu-kà-na-a-a KIŠIB a-šur-i-mì-tí DUMU a-mur-lu-sú KIŠIB zu-ú-zu DUMU ku-la ša ḫu-bu-ul zu-ú-zu DUMU ku-la 9 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er zu-ú-zu DUMU ku-la me-er-ú a-šur-SIPA i-šu-ú iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša a-šur-na-da ù e-me-me ITU.KAM ḫu-bu-ur li-mu-um šu-ḫu-bu-ur DUMU e-lá-lí 1 ma-na.TA i-na ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA i-na ITU.KAM-im a-na 1 ma-na-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb ta-aḫ-sí-is-tum a-ni-tum me-eḫ-ra-at 2 ṭup-pé-e ḫa-ru-mu-tim ša ḫu-bu-ul zu-zu-ú DUMU ku-la
target_raw:
Seals: Itūr-ilī s. Amur-Ištar, Al-abum, Zuzu.
Regarding the debt of Zuzu, 5 minas refined silver belonging to Aššur-rēʾī.
He will pay 10 ḫamuštum weeks from the ḫamuštum of Sukkalliya and Uraya.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month.
Seals: Rabi-Aššur s. Ibāya, Kubi-nādā s. Buknaya, Aššur-imittī s. Amur-lussu, Zuzu s. Kula.
Regarding the debt of Zuzu s. Kula, Zuzu s. Kula owes the sons of Aššur-rēʾī 9 minas refined silver.
He will pay one mina one year from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-nādā and Ememe, month Ḫubur, eponymate Šū-Ḫubur s. Elālī.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
This memorandum is a copy of two sealed tablets concerning the debt of Zuzu s. Kula.

source_raw: KIŠIB ku-ku-zi DUMU ṣé-eḫ-ri-ì-lí KIŠIB LUGAL-sú-en6 DUMU ma-ni-a KIŠIB na-áb-sú-en6 DUMU šu-be-lim 27 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er na-áb-sú-en6 a-šur-ma-lik i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša i-dí-a-bi4-im ITU.1.KAM ku-zal-lu li-mu-um i-na-a a-na 10 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-im ṣí-ib-tám i ITU.KAM ù-ṣa-áb KÙ.BABBAR ú lu-qú-tum a-šu-mì a-šur-ma-lik GÁN-lam e-tí-iq DAM.GÀR ú-lá i-qí-áp KIŠIB na-zi DUMU a-mu-ru-ba-ni KIŠIB lá-qé-ep DUMU PÚZUR-sa-du-e KIŠIB LUGAL-sú-en6 DUMU ma-ni-a 0.83333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er LUGAL-sú-en6 DUMU ma-ni-a DAM.GÀR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša i-dí-a-bi4-im a-na 4 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-im i ITU.1.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb KIŠIB pì-lá-aḫ-a-šur DUMU a-šur-na-da KIŠIB a-šur-ták-lá-ku DUMU e-na-ni-a KIŠIB šu-a-šur DUMU i-na-aḫ-DINGIR 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am i-ṣé-er šu-a-šur DAM.GÀR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša i-dí-a-bi4-im ITU.1.KAM kán-mar-ta li-mu-um en-na-sú-en6 DUMU IḪ.ME 22 ḫa-am-ša-tim i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-im i ITU.1.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb wa-bi-il5 ṭup-pí-im šu-ut DAM.GÀR me-eḫ-ru-um ša ṭup-pé-e ḫa-ru-mu-tim GÁN-lam e-tí-qú
target_raw:
Seals: Kukuzi s. Ṣeḫr-ilī, Šar-Suen s. Ma-ni-a, Nāb-Suen s. Šū-Bēlim
Nāb-Suen owes Aššur-mālik 27 minas refined silver.
He will pay 10 weeks from the ḫamuštum ša Iddin-abim, month Kuzallu, year Innāya.
If he does not pay he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekel per mina per month.
He will leave the silver and merchandise in the field (in Anatolia) in the name of Assur malik.
The merchant will not entrust (the merchandise to anyone else).
Seals: Nazi s. Amurru-bāni, Lā-qēpum s. Puzur-sadū'ē, ȃr-Suen s. Mania.
Šar-Suen s. Ma-ni-a owes the merchant 5/6 mina refined silver.
He will pay 4 weeks from the ḫamuštum of Iddin-abim.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
Seals of Pilaḫ-Aššur s. Aššur-nādā, Aššur-taklāku s. Ennānīa, Šū-Aššur s. Inaȟ-ilī.
Šū-Aššur owes the merchant 1 mina refined silver.
He will pay 22 weeks from the ˍmußtum of Iddin-abum, month Kanmwarta, eponym of Enna-Suen s. of the priest.
If he does not pay, he will add interest at the rate of 1 1/2 shekels per mina.
The bearer of the tablet is the merchant.
They left a copy of the sealed tablet in the feild (Anatolia).

Seal of the colony of Kanesh.
The colony at Kanesh rendered a verdict:
The Šū-Suen's investors and the sons of Šū-Suen will seize the three partners on their own authority and they will enter the house of Šū-Suen and read his tablets.
Whoever cannot enter or is not able (to be there?), they will set up witnesses for him so that those will enter.

He will pay 13 ḫamuštum weeks from the month Allanātum, eponymate Šudaya s. Ennānum.
If he does not pay (on time) he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
Purchase 5 tables and search.
And purchase 5 tables.
He will give to him 1 šūtum measure of honey and 1 šutum measuer of butter with his silver.
Ḫamuštum of Idī-Aššur and Ašsur-taklāku.
This memorandum is a copy of the sealed tablet of Aššur-dān.

Seals: Lā-qēpum s. Zuzuzānum, Iluwa s. Aššur-šamšī, Aššur-emūqī s. Iddin-Aššur, Iddin-Aššur.
Iddin-Aššur s. Isaliya owes Aḫu-waqar 2 minas 2 1/2 shekels silver.
He will send the silver to the city after Aḫu-waqar.

Month: Kanwarta.
Eponymate: Iddin-abum s. Narbitum.

Seals of Paruawata, Ištaribra, Puta, Išpunaḫšu, Udkaria
Udkaria owes Aššur-mālik 1 1/3 minas 1 shekel silver, 4 bags grain, 2 bags wheat, 20 bread, and 2 sheep.
He will pay one year from the month of ša sarrātim, eponymate after Ikuppi-Ištar.
Išpunaḫšu and Šuppi-uman are the guarantors.

Siegel des Tarikuda; Siegel des Tarḫuni’a; Siegel des Ḫabu’ala; Siegel der Burka; Siegel des Tarḫula; Siegel Šubunaḫšu; Siegel des Hašušman.
Zwei Sack anderthald Krug zur Hälfte Weizen, zur Hälfte Gerste und 3⅔ Seqel 15 Korn Silber hat zu Lasten des Ḫašušman, des Tarḫula und des Šubunaḫšu Addāda gut.
Den Weizen und die Gerste werden sie zur (kommenden) Erntzeit geben.
Das Silber werden sie zur (nächsten) Aussaat zahlen.
Ḫabu’ala und Burka, seine Frau, sind Bürge.
(Betrifft) den Wohlbehaltenen und (Orts)beständigen: er wird zahlen.

Seal of Amurrum-bāni son of Manana, seal of Puzur-saduʾē son of MAŠ, seal of Uṣur-ša-Aššur son of Aššur-mālik, seal of Ilabrat-bāni son of Aššur-mālik, seal of Idī-Ištar son of Aššur-mālik.
Ušur-ša-Aššur, Ilabrat-bāni and Idī-Ištar, sons of Aššur-mālik owe Pūšu-kēn 7 minas silver.

Siege[l des Us]ān[um, des Sohnes des] Ām[ur]aššur; Siegel des Kulumā´ā, [des Sohnes des] Aššurimittī; Sie[gel des Ḫuni’a, des Sohnes des Aššu]rimittī; Siegel des Tāb-ṣillaššur, des Sohnes des A[ššurid]ī; Siegel des Aššurtaklāku des Sohnes des Alâḫum; Siegel des Bēlānu[m, des Sohne]s des Šū’aššur; Siegel des Puzurenna, des Sohnes des Enn[a’aššur]; Siegel des Idī’ašš[ur, des] S]o[hnes des Dā]naššur; Siegel des Aššurpilaḫ, des Komissäars <des Hauses> Āmurištar; Siegel des S[u’i]nrē’ûm, Siegel des Ilībāni, Siegel des Aššurnīsū, Siegel des Š[ūlab]an, der Söhne des Āmurištar; Siegel des Aššurmuttabbil, Siegel des Buzāzu, S[iege]l des Ikuppāša, der Söhne des Pūšukēn; Aššurpilaḫ, [der Kommis]sär des Houses Āmurištar, Su’inrē’ûm, Ilībāni, Aššurnīšu und Šūlaban, in eigener Sache und als Vertreter der Abšalim, der Tochter des Āmurištar, und des Iddinadad, ihres Bruders, Aššurmuttabbil, Buzāzu, Ikuppāša, [die Söhn]e des [Pūšuk]ēn, in ei[gener Sache] und als Vertreter der Aḫāḫā <ihrer Schwester>, [der gubabtu]m-Priesterin, und ihres Bruders S[u’e’a haben auf] ihre gegenseitgigen Verpflichtungen un[s (als Richter) genommen].
Den Eid bei der Stadt haben sie ge[leis]tet.
Dann haben wir ihre Rechtsstreitigkeiten be[en]det: Entweder ist Āmurištar als Vertreter des Pūšukēn ist als Vertreter des Āmurištar aufgetreten; oder der eine hat dem anderen ein Depôt geschickt: Entweder hat der eine des anderen Silber vom Außenstand eingenommen, oder, (was) eine Doppel- urkunde mit den Siegeln (des einen) von ihnen oder das (Gut) der Karawanen beziehungsweise die Vorräte (betrifft), (so) hat der eine (es) dem anderen aufgespeichert, (und zwar) sowohl in Assur als auch in Kappadokien.
Die Söhne des Āmurištar und die Tochter des Āmurištar, die gubabtum-Priesterin, die Söhne des Pūšukēn und die Tochter des Pūšukēn, die gubabtum-Priesterin: Die eine (Partei) wird auf die andere in irgendeiner Sache nicht zurckkommen.
Diese Leute (waren) [die Beender der Rechtssach]en.

Regarding the debt of Šuli s. Merāli.
Šuli s. Merāli owes Ḫunia ? minas refined silver.
He will pay one year from the month Teʾinātim, eponymate Ilī-dān.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1/3 mina per mina (per month) as interest.

Ilī-bāni s. Amur-Ištar owes Ḫunia s. Aššur-imittī 2 minas 10 shekels refined silver.
He will pay twenty weeks from the ḫamuštum of Aššur-taklāku s. Al-aḫim.

target_raw:
Puzur-Aššur and Amur-ilī settled accounts in our presence.
Puzur-Aššur said to Amur-ilī, "I paid 1 mina silver to your name in the City. Deduct the price in the City from my goods which you drove up from the City--14 minas tin-from my remuneration."
Amur-ilī said, " Why did you not bring the letter of my represtative stating that you paid the 1 mina silver?"
Puzur-Aššur said, "If I did not pay 1 mina silver to your representative, let me weigh out 2 minas silver to you. Also, I am .. concerning the 1/6 shekel of the textile."
And regarding the fact that ... 1/6 shekel of the textile, Puzur-Aššur said, "Inquire of Imdī-ilum and I will give it to you."
Also we questioned Šū-Bēlum concerning your packer.
For this matter, the colony at Burušḫaddum gave us permission, (and we settled our accounts before the dagger of Aššur)

target_raw:
Puzur-Ištar, representative of Puzur-Aššur, seized us against Sabasiya:
Puzur-Ištar stated: "I am questioning you on behalf of Puzur-Aššur. From the goods which were concerning Adad the share ... and which Šū-Ištar and Il-wedāku delivered (it) to you and Puzur-Šamaš, 2 minas 36 shekels gold belonging to Adad to your debit was not paid. You paid whatever was on your person.
Sabasiya replied, "It is true. From the goods which were set for the share as Adad and the goods which Puzur-Aššur entrusted to Šū-Ištar and Il-wedāku and which they delivered to myself and Puzur-Šamaš, the goods were converted to gold and I owed 2 minas 36 shekels gold. It was weighed out as the share of Adad. I did not pay anything of myself. I paid from the goods a-mi-tim-ma."
The plenary colony assembly at Waḫšušana set for us (witnesses) concerning this matter and we gave our witnesses before the dagger of Aššur.
Aššur-rabi, Dān-Aššur, Mamaḫer

Puzur-Ištar seized us against Adad-rēʾī:

To Šū-Ḫubur from Pūšu-kēn:
From the 21 kutānum-textiles and 2 šurūtum-textiles as wrappings which Enlil-bāni brought, at the beginning of accountings they called for 10 kutānum-textiles and 4 šurum-textiles, and I purchased 2 šurum-textiles for 15 shekels silver and 10 kutānum-textiles and 4 šurum-textiles -your share ... minas .. kutānum-textiles ...
... x (minas and shekels) silver I deposited to your share.
At 1/3 minas for each kutānum-textile and x shekels for each šurum-textile, separately I put their silver into your share.
After your silver from Enlil-bāni's cargo arrived, (they called?) 15? kutānum-textiles and 5 šurum textiles ...
... they bring you in the cargo of Irma-Aššur.

... to/for the transport of (PN), silver to Ḫu... ... so that he makes purchases.
When they make purchases, you yourselves assist them.
My dear brothers, I myself am ...
Here, I seized Uzuwa s. Aššur-damiq.
I said, "Give the silver."
He said, "Bring me the sealed tablet with witnesses so that you can take your (pl.) silver."
We produced the tablet for him, so, my dear brother, urgent, so that Nūr-Ištar and Uštala bring out the tablets to Tegarama.
Send out 3 shekels silver under my seal to Walḫašna for the tablets.
Nūr-Ištar carries it as a favor for them.
Urgent, urgent, ... silver ... wherever ... 2 minas silver, my dear brother, also come quickly, so that we can satisfy.

source_raw: a-mur-IŠTAR ú tù-ùḫ-ni-iš iṣ-bu-tù-ni-a-tí-ma ni-kà-sí i-sí-ú-ma URUDU lu ša {TÚG}ma-ku-ḫi-šu lu ša {TÚG}ku-ta-ni ša ik-ri-bi-šu 32 GÚ 30 ma-na URUDU ša wa-áš-ḫa-ni-a i li-bi tù-ùḫ-ni-iš a-mur-IŠTAR i-dí URUDU i-na wa-áš-ḫa-ni-a i-ša-qal um-ma tù-ùḫ-ni-iš-ma 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ù 3.75 GÍN KÙ.GI tal-qé um-ma a-mur-IŠTAR-ma a-na zi-ga-an-LUGAL ù TÚG.ḪI.A ša a-dí-na-ku-ni al-qé IGI pu-šu-ke-en6 DUMU sú-e-a <gap> IGI la-qé-pí-im DUMU {d}AB-ba-ni IGI e-na-ZA DUMU en-na-nim a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um kà-ni-iš{ki} i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma IGI GÍR ša {d}a-šur ší-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in
target_raw:
Amu-Ištar and Tuḫniš seized us and made accounts, copper, both yeilding from makuḫi-textiles and from the kutānum-textiles of his ikrībū-account, Amur-īštar deposited 32 talents 30 minas copper from Wašḫaniya.
He will weigh out the copper in Wašḫaniya.
Amur-Ištar said, "I took it for the z and textiles which I sold to you."
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn s. Sueyya, Lā-qēpum s. Nabu-bāni, Enna-ZA s. Ennanum.
For this matter, the colony of Kanesh gave us permission, and we gave our testimonies before the dagger of Aššur.

... he will pay in 42 hamuštum weeks.
Al-aḫum and Šalim-Aššur owe 12 minas 47 shekels less 22 1/2 grains.
He will pay 45 weeks from the ḫauštum week after the ḫamuštum week of Lā-qēpum and Ilī-ālum, month Allanātum, eponymate of Aḫ-marši.
Lulu s. Azua owes 13 2/3 minas.
[He will pay ...] from the hamuštum week of the kaššum official after the week of Lā-qēpum and Ilī-alum.
Ana... owes x minas silver.
He will pay 45 weeks after the hamuštum of Šukkutum and Uraya, month of Qarrātum, year of Aḫ-marši.
PN s. Šū-Ilabrat owes 15 minas 38 1/s shekels.
He will pay 40 hamuštum weeks after the hmuštum week of the kaššum official after the ḫamuštum week of Lā-qēpum and Ilī-ālum, month of Teʾinātum, year of Idī-Aššur.
Asqudum and Ilī-uṣranni owe 32 minas 51+x shekels.
They will pay 20+x weeks after the ḫamuštum of Aššur-imittī and Amur-Ištar, month of Narmak Aššur ša kēnātim, year after the year of Sukkalliya.
Šū-Suen
[He will pay x ḫamuštum weeks?] from the ḫamuštum of [PN and] x-tí-a-x, month [of MN, year LN.]

Why is it that you wrote me (thus)

To Puzur-Anna from Irma-Aššur and Puzur-Šamaš:
Regarding the tablet of .. withesses of the shareas and Ašsur-ṭāb about which you wrote, ... which
... Pilaḫ-Anum ... Pilaḫ-Anum ... to there ... to you.
... which you wrote ... silver ...
He said, "Who is he that he does not know me?"
I don't have even 10 shekels silver for my expenses!
I don't (even) have a donkey to ride!
He will enter the house of my father and I will give about 10 shekels silver for the child's sustenance funds, but not a third of a mina (silver)!
The man is not reliable!
Wherever there are profits (to be made) we will set witnesses and we will seal your tablets and we will clear your tablets and instructions and send them to you.
Here ... with Aššur-imittī ... of the shares of Ennum-Aššur .. to Sabasiya Aššur-imittī ...
... in the (verdict?) ... for the remainder (?) ...

Ikuppiya brought 6 talents tin.
We keep getting reports about the price of tin in Purušḫattum.
Come and send out as much of your tin as (has arrived?) so that you buy a shekel of silver.

To Puzur-Aššur from Sabasiya:
Regarding the tablet (in?) the house of Wašḫuba about which you wrote me, take Ilabrat-bāni with you, open my tablet, and take out the tablet stating 49 minas silver (owed by) Anaḫ-ilī, his sister the gubabtum priestess, and Āl-ṭāb his brother concerning the two houses they entered, --there are these men denoted in the supplementary tablet -- wherever these men are denoted as judges, blot them with water so they are erased.
Let your hand seize them--
As much as you are able, settle the matter.
A tablet with the seal of Ili-ālum s. Sukkalliya stating, "8 minas silver of the 2 minas gold which he took.",inform me as to whether or not the tablet is available.
My dear brother and lord, have you not lost everything that was mine?
My all was handed over to the sons of Lālum.
The tablet recording the decision of the plenary (assembly of the) colony at Waḫšušana regarding the 2/3 minas gold and its interest is in my tablet regarding that which I owe to Adad.
The tablet of the colony, is within my tablet.
There is a second tablet of the colony.
Zupa will pay our the share.
I myself will pay the interest.
The represetative will take it from whatever Zupa has, from the holdings of Adad.

source_raw: a-na PUZUR4-a-šur qí-bi4-ma um-ma bu-za-zu-ma a-na ša-lá-tù-wa-ar a-na té-er-tí-a e-ta-ra-áb ma-ki-ru-ú a-dí-ni lá e-ru-bu-nim-/ma um-ma a-na-ku-ma lá-áš-pur-ma AN.NA iš-tí URUDU-i-ni a ba-ru-im lu-šé-tí-qú-nim a-bi4 a-ta be-lí a-ta i-ḫi-id-ma i {d}UTU-ši ṭup-pì-i ta-ša-me-ú šu-pur-ma AN.NA-ki ma-lá ta-le-e-ú lu-šé-ri-bu-nim-ma pá-ni-a-ma áb-kam a-bi4 a-ta a ma-nim ša-nim a-da-gal i-ḫi-id-ma ṣú-ḫa-ru a-ma-kam u4-ma-kál lá i-bi4-tu ḫa-ra-nam dí-šu-nu-tí a-na-ku a té-er-tí-kà ma-ṣa-ku pè-er-dí ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni i pá-ni-a ú-šé-ra-am a-wa-ti ša DAM GAL sí-sé-e ma-lá tág-mu-ru té-er-ta-kà lí-lí-kam
target_raw:
To Puzur-Aššur from Buzazu:
I arrived at Šalatuwar according to my message.
Buyers have not yet entered (the market).
I said, "Let me send so that I can cause them to leave the tin with my copper."
My dear father and lord, take care so that you you send (it) on the day that you read my letter, send as much of my tin as you can and dispatch them with the first caravan.
My dear father, to whom else can I look?
Take care to not delay the boys there overnight.
Give them the caravan.
As for me, I am mṣ' your instructions.
The animals about which you wrote I will dispatch with the first caravan.
Let your word come as to whether you have come to an agreement about the matter of the wife of the chief of horses.

source_raw: a-na PUZUR4-a-šur qí-bi4-ma um-ma i-kà-a-ma mì-šu ša ta-na-zu-mu-ú ma-nu-um i-na tù-ur-ḫu-mì-it ša AN.NA a-na URUDU lá-mu-nim i-ta-na-dí-nu i-nu-mì sú-e-a i-li-kà-ni AN.NA 9 ma-na.TA ú 10 ma-na.TA i-ta-na-dí-nu a-na-ku 11 ma-na.TA ú-šé-bi-lá-kum a-šur ú DINGIR-lu a-ḫu-tim li-ṭù-lá 5 ma-na AN.NA i-na li-bi AN.NA-ki-kà a-na ḫa-na-nim a-na 30 ma-na.TA URUDU lá-mu-nim a-dí-šu-ma šu-ut-ma i-ta-ba-al 1 GÍN i-na er-bi-im lá i-dí-na-ni a-na ša ku-<gap> mì-šu-um ta-na-zu-um wa-di-i a-sà-kà-am il5-ta-na-qé-ú 55 ma-na URUDU ší-kam 12 ma-na URUDU SIG5 ma-sú-ḫa-am 6 ma-na ki-ib-sà-tim a-na šu-IŠTAR a-dí-in URUDU ša ší-na-ZU ub-lá-ku-ni le-qé-šu a-šu-mì URUDU ša ša-du-ni-ma ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni 6 ma-na KÙ.GI a-na kà-ni-iš ta-ta-ba-al
target_raw:
Zu Puzuraššur sprich! Folgendermaßen Igā’a:
(Was) all das (betrifft), worüber du klagst: Wer ist in Tu[rḫ]umit, der dauernd gegen einen Großkaufmann (?) ein Urteil fällt?
Als Su’e’a herkam, gab man gewöhnlich Zinn zu(m Kurs von) je 9 Minen oder je 10 Minen.
Ich habe je 11 Minen dir geschickt!
Aššur und die Götter der Bruderschaft mögen Zeuge sein!
5 Minen Zinn von deinem Zins habe ich Ḫanānum zu (einem Kurs von) je 30 Minen sehr schlechtes Kupfer gegeben.
D[er] hat (es) bereits weggebracht. Nicht einen (einzigen) Seqel vo[n der A]bgabe hat er mir gegeben.
In Sachen Igmillum (?) ärgerst du dich. Er tut doch wohl dauernd Unrechtes!
Ferner habe ich 55 Minen . . . . . . Kupfer, 12 Minen gutes Bruch- (?) Kupfer— 6 Minen sind Abzüge — dem Šū-ištar gegeben.
Das Kupfer, das Ennasu (?) dir gebracht hat, nimm (es!)
Wegen des Kupfers der Šadunum (?), bezüglich dessen sie mir geschrieben hat: 6 Minen [. . . . ] . . . . an ihren Herren hat sie weggebracht (?).

source_raw: a-na PUZUR4-a-šur qí-bi4-ma um-ma il5-we-da-ku-ma 2 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ni-is-ḫa-sú DIRI ša-du-a-sú ša-bu ša sú-e-a a-na i-dí-lim DUMU DUG-ì-lí i-dí-nu-ma a-na a-lim{ki} a-na ší-a-ma-tim šé-bu-lu-ma DAM.GÀR wa-du-ú DAM.GÀR a-na-ku ṭup-pá-am ḫa-ar-ma-am ša ku-nu-uk sú-e-a ú-kà-al ša KÙ.BABBAR KÙ.BABBAR-pì-ni ù na-áš-pár-tám ša ku-nu-uk sú-e-a a-na i-dí-lim lá-pu-ta-at-ma i-ku-pì-a-šur na-áš-a-kum ki-ma DAM.GÀR a-na-ku-ni a-šur-mu-ta-bi4-il5 DUMU pu-šu-ke-en6 im-ṣí-ma KÙ.BABBAR il5-qé a-na ša lá ḫa-bu-lá-ku-šu-ni-ma KÙ.BABBAR il5-qé-ú ṣa-áb-ta-šu-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR KÙ.GI i-ṣé-ri-šu li-qé-a KÙ.BABBAR ku-nu-uk-ma a-na a-šur-UTU-ši pì-qí-id-ma a-ṣé-er ša ki-ma DAM.GÀR a-na a-lim{ki} lu-bi4-il5 a-bi4 a-ta i-ḫi-id-ma iš-tí TÚG.ḪI.A ša i-dí-a-šur ú ša <gap>-a-šur ša-dí-ma <gap>-ú <gap> ší-ip-kà-tim ma-lá tal-qé-ú ta-aḫ-sí-is-ta-kà i-dí-ma <gap>-ni-ša šé-bi4-lam 3 TÚG i-ku-pì-a-šur ub-lam
target_raw:
To Puzur-Aššur from Ili-wēdāku:
As for the 2 minas of silver, its excise tax paid, its shipping charges satisfied, which Sueyya gave to Idī-ilum son of Ṭāb-ilī and which he transported to the city for purchases and about which informed the merchant, I am the merchant.
I hold the certified tablet with Sueyya's seal.
As for the matter of the silver, our silver and the message with the seal of Sueyya has been written for Idī-ilum and Ikkūppī-Aššur brings it to you.
Aššur-mutabbil son of Pūšu-kēn, instead of the merchant, which is me, checked and received the silver.
Because (?) they received the silver, of which it can be said that I do not owe it, seize him and received from him gold for silver.
Seal the silver and entrust it to Aššur-šamši so that he brings it to the representative of the merchant in the City.
My dear father, take care to cause them to be deposited with the textiles of Idī-Aššur and of ...-Aššur so that ...
Deposit your taḫsistum (concerning šipkātim, as much as they took and send ... .
Ikūppī-Aššur brought 3 textiles.

target_raw:
To Puzur-Aššur, Kulumaya, Amur-Ištar and Amur-ilī: Thus Ilī-nādā:
I heard that you received silver from Dadaya.
I heard that:
You (pl.) received silver from Dadaya.
Get rid of this level sentence
Since you received the silver, travelers have come once or twice.
But you have not sent me the silver.
My dear brothers, take care to send the silver with the first (traveler).
Whether you received the payment in full or it has produced interest, let your full report come immmediately.
If you have allowed the interest to accrue do not overvalue the goods which Arsiah brought to you until you have already __ the silver.
1 mina silver belonging to my ikrībumistherein.
Regarding what I weighed out, send me silver for oil and the price of fine oil soon!

To Puzur-Aššur from Buzāzu:
When I was freed up (deported?) to go to Kanesh, I deposited tin, belonging to both you and I, with Ilī-wēdāku.
After I returned from Kanesh, against the will of the god, the bed seized me.

source_raw: a-na PUZUR4-a-šùr qí-bi-ma um-ma il5-we-da-ku ù bu-za-zu-ma a-šu-mì da-da-a DUMU tí-li-a a-na-kam qá-sú URUDU-kà a-mu-tí-šu-nu i-dí-in-ma KÙ.GI iš-am ni-iṣ-ba-sú-ma um-ma né-nu-ma KÙ.BABBAR lu KÙ.GI šu-qúl-ma lu ni-ik-nu-kà-kum-ma a-ta-ma na-an-ší-šu um-ma šu-ut-ma a-na-ku-ma a-ṣé-ri-šu a-la-ak ù i-ṣé-er um-me-a-ni-a ma-da-tim-ma i-šu a-lá-ak-ma iš-tí-šu a-za-ku-ma KÙ.BABBAR áš-ra-kam ša i-la-qé-ú-šu li-il5-qé a-wi-lúm a-ṣé-ri-kà i-tal-kam a-na-kam sí-ku-šu ITU.1.KAM iš-té-en6 nu-ka-il5 um-ma šu-ut-ma a-ṣé-ri-šu-ma a-lá-ak mì-nam tù-kà-lá-ni ki-ma a-ṣé-ri-kà a-wi-lúm i-lá-kà-ni a ší-a-tí a-wi-lam lá nu-ša-am-ri-iṣ
target_raw:
Zu Puzuraššur sprich! Folgendermaßen Ilīwēdāku und Buzāzu
Was den Dadā’a, betrifft, so hat der hier (ihnen) dein Kupfer gegeben und Gold (dafür) gekauft.
Wir haben ihn vorgeladen; dann (sprachen) wir folgendermaßen: "Silber oder Gold zahle! Dann wollen wir dir eine gesiegelte Urkunde (darüber) ausstellen. Also nimm du es auf dich! (?)"
Folgendermaßen (antwortete) er: "Ich werde zu ihm gehen. Denn bei meinem Geldgeber habe ich viele (Posten) gut. Ich werde gehen und mich dann bei ihm rechtfertigen. Dort möge er das Silber, das er zu nehmen (berechtigt ist), ne[hmen.]"
Der Mann ist zu dir abgereist. Hier haben wir seinen Gewandsaum einen Monat gehalten.
Folgendermaßen (sprach) er: "Zu ihm werde ich gehen. Warum haltet ihr mich (noch) in Haft?"
Da der Mann zu dir gehen wollte, deswegen haben wir den Mann nicht kränken wollen!

target:

target_raw:
Zu Puzuraššur sprich! Folgendermaßen Ilīwēdāku:
Mein Vater bist du! Mein Herr bist du! An dem Tage, an dem du meine Tafel liest, sei achtsam!
Deinen Bedarf notiere in(nerhalb von) fünf Tagen.
Meine Angelegenheit erledige! Dann will ich deinem Bescheid mich beugen und (dementsprechend) meine Reise unternehmen.
1 Mine Silber (sei es nun) meines oder deines, halte ich in Händen.
Einen Monat oder zwei Monate sollst du nicht (darüber) verfügen.
Denn meine Angelegenheien hast du nicht erledigt und soll ich da nicht angesteckt (?) werden.
Was soll ich dir (noch) viel schreiben?
Mit Tafeln und Briefen will ich (es) dir kurz machen! Ḫutka soll nicht einmal übernachten; einen klaren Bescheid von dir soll er mir bringen.
Das Silber, 1 Mine, will ich bereitlegen.
Dann möge er, damit wir das Depot niederlegen können und nicht zuschanden werden, handeln.
10 Seqel Silber, den Preis für den Ring aus Eisen, den Ḫutka mir gebracht hat, mit meinen Siegeln: (damit) ist Ḫutka zu dir unterwegs.

source_raw: a-na a-ba-a-a qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šùr-na-da-ma a ma-lá na-áš-pè-er-tim ša áš-pu-ra-ki-ni a-na-kam i-na-a a-šu-mì-ki ù a-na šu-mì É a-bi-ki tám-kà-ri e-ta-wu a-šu-mì ku-ra li-bu-šu im-ta-ra-aṣ um-ma šu-ut-ma šu-ma lá i-pí-ša ša-ḫu-uz šu-nu i-na a-lim{ki} a-li-ki <gap>-ta-na-mu ù a-ni-um a-na-kam KÙ.BABBAR ú-ṣa-ba-at-ma pu-ru-i iš-ta-na-kán 3 gu5-šu-ri e-ṣí ša É be-tí-ku-nu lá-ma a-na kà-ni-iš a-li-kà-ni ku-ra a ší-mì-im i-dí-in a-ma-kam-ma a-ta-wa-ku-nu-tí um-ma a-na-ku-ma É be-tum a-na-aḫ-IŠTAR e-ṣú-ma gám-ru a-tù-nu ma-la-ku-nu

To Abaya from Aššur-nādā:
In accordance with the message I sent to you, Innaya has here discussed with the tamkāru on your behalf of you and your paternal household.
He became unhappy becasue of Kura.
He said,"If he is not given instructions directly from her, these people will put continual pressure on travelers in the City, and then this one will sieze lots of silver there and constantly cause my reputation harm."
As for the x+3 wooden beamsof your house, Kura had sold them before I arrived at Kanesh.
I discussed that with you in that very place.
I said, "The house of Anaḫ-Ištar is almost finished, and it is you who should ..."
