source_raw: a-na a-bu-ša-lim šu-a-nim šál-ma-a-šur ú e-ni-iš-ru qí-bi-ma um-ma pu-šu-ke-en6-ma a-ša-me-ma ur-kà-at É-bé-tim ma-qí-it 3 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ku-nu-ki a-na e-ni-iš-ru a-bu-ša-lim na-ší a-ḫu-ú-a be-lu-ú-a i ṣú-ḫa-ri-ku-nu iš-té-na a-na e-ni-iš-ru dí-na-ma ú a-tù-nu ú-ma-kál ḫu-ša-ḫa-ku-nu e-zi-ba-ma i-na ma-aḫ-ri-ku-nu lu ni-iz-ku-ma lu gi-mì-lúm GAL i-a-tí ú a-na-ku a-wa-at-ku-nu ša-ma-am a-le-e a-pu-tum iḫ-da a-ḫu-ú-a a-tù-nu e-ni-iš-ru na-ḫi-da-ma É-bé-tám li-ta-ší mì-nam ša iš-pu-ra-am um-ma šu-ut-ma i-na É-bé-tim i-ṭá-na-ru-du-ni a-dí té-er-tí-i lá i-li-kà-šu-ni lá ú-ṣí-i a-na šál-ma-a-šur qí-bi4-ma i-na 0.33333 ma-na LÁ 1 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša PÚZUR-a-šur
target_raw:
To Abu-šalim, Šū-Anum, Šalim-Aššur, and Enišru from Pūšu-kēn:
I heard that after the house fell(?) Abu-šalim brought 3 shekels silver to Enišru under seal.
My dear brothers and lords, he has jipped (?) you.
Give only one to Enišru so that you all this very day are lacking.
Leave him and very soon, whatever you cleared and my great favor I myself will be able to resolver your matter.
Urgent, take care (concerning this matter)!
My dear brothers, pay heed to Enišru so that he ... the house.
What is it that he wrote?
He said, "In constantly approaching the house, until my instructions come, he would not depart."
To Salim-Assur:
From the 19 shekels silver which Puzur-Assur .... (brought?)

To Aduwe from Walawala:
Pūšu-kēn brought 2 shekels silver under my name to you.
Let your message come as to whether or not Pūšu-kēn also brought to you 2 r. for Ištar and Išḫara and 1 k. for Išḫara under my seal.
Pūšu-kēn does not bring a tablet.

To Alāhum and Aššur-nādā from Eddin-Aššur:
In Wašania 260 fleeces, (which are) your goods, are available.
I wrote to my representatives in Wašhania saying: "As long as the fee-paying member appoints Kurara as his agent; Do not release to him his half to dispose over!
He must not write to his representatives there lest they should approach the colony and it should release the fleeces to him.
Stand up and lay claim to the fleeces on my behalf, saying: "Our
Kurara has cheated me here and stood ready to remove your silver.
Until I and Sasia received a tablet from the colony authorities concerning his extradition, he would not come.
Take care that your silver does not get out of reach!

target_raw:
To Alāhum, Aguza, Aššur-nādā and Ilī-alum from Aššur-idī:
5 shekels of iron: Ili-iddinaššu son of Aššur-nādā; 2 shekels of iron: Ikuppia son of Aššur-dān; 6 minas of tin: Ikūnum son of Samaya -- Puzur-Ištar son of Saktaya has given them (this) as an interest-free loan.
He owes 23 1/2 minas of silver to us, and it is us they have satisfied.
My dear brothers, take care to support Aššur-bēl-awātim and Amria, raise the silver and divide it up jointly in shares for you of 4 minas, and send that to me.
Out of the 30 minas of lapis lazuli owned jointly by Šū-Illil, Aššur-malik, myself and Amur-Ištar, i.e. out of all of the lapis lazuli, I checked 5 minas of lapis lazuli and a seal costing 1/3 mina (of silver) was deposited with it.
He said: "I shall sell these either in Kaneš or in Hahhum, and I shall acquire silver for you."
Please, the man must not cheat you, so when you make the division, if he has not brought the lapis lazuli carried by him, has not deposited it in front of you, so that you have not (been able) to send it jointly, or divided it, then do not favour the man with any extras!
The lapis lazuli carried by him is of outstanding quality.

source_raw: a-na a-lá-ḫi-im a-šùr-na-da ù i-dí-sú-en6 qí-bi4-ma um-ma ì-lí-a-lúm-ma a-ša-me-ma lu-qú-tum ša ku-za-zi-im e-li-a-am a-ba-ú-a a-tù-nu KÙ.BABBAR ša-áš-qí-lá 4 ma-na ù 1 ma-na 6 GÍN a-ḫa-ma KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am iš-tù SAG ITU.KAM kà-ra-a-tim 3 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-em i-na ITU.KAM ṣí-ib-tám ù-ṣa-áb li-mu-um šu-da-a DUMU en-na-nim KÙ.BABBAR ú ṣí-ba-sú ša-áš-qí-lá-ma gi-mì-lam i-na ṣé-er a-bi4-ni ù i-a-tí šu-uk-na šu-ma lu-qú-sú a-na KÙ.BABBAR ù ṣí-ba-tim lá i-kà-ša-ad ša lá-qá-em li-iq-a-ma ší-tám a-na 1 ma-na-em 3 GÍN.TA lu-ṣí-ib
target_raw:
To Alāhum Aššur-nādā and Iddin-Suen from Ilī-ālum:
I hear that Kuzāzum's merchandise has come up here.
My dear fathers, have the silver paid.
It is 4 minas plus furthermore 1 mina 6 shekels of refined silver, reckoned from the beginning of the month Qarra' atum, (on which) he will pay interest at the rate 3 shekels per mina per month;
Eponym Sudaya, the son of Ennānum.
Have the silver and its interest paid.
Do our father and me (this) favour.
If his merchandise does not suffice to cover the silver and the interest, then take what there is to take, and let him add interest on the rest at the rate of 3 shekels per mina.

After my account and our father's account had been recorded at 11 1/3 minas 5 shekels and 7 1/3 minas respectively, I have been booked for 18
10 kutānu-textiles and 4 black textiles I left in our guest-house.
Thereof they will deposit 4
The colony has given it to me.
Send me word telling me whether or not he has deposited anything and how much silver has been recorded for our father's account and mine.

I deposited 2 minas of refined silver on my account and on our father's account, so that 28 2/3 minas 7 1/2 shekels of silver is from Pūšu-ken's enterprise:
As to the 1 mina for three textiles from Uršu transported by me, you have written, saying: ''The silver draws interest."
If they have brought the 2
2/3 mina 5 1/6 shekels is owed by Haluli.
1/2 mina is owed by Azu.
Have paid in full out of all this 2 1/2 minas of silver plus interest belonging to Azu.
20+x sickles plus 2 minas ... Have paid 1 mina for grain.

target_raw:
To Alāhum, Ememe and Aššur-taklāku from Aššur-nādā:
I hear that Ṣiriā's son has left for the City.
Why is it that Šalim-Aššur's servants and other travellers have come here twice, but no message from you has arrived?
Ask the maid how much grain has come in.
Send me word.
If you wish to have barley, then buy for up to 1/2 mina of silver.
Send me word about every single shekel of silver which has accrued to you both from Durhumit and from here, where you will entrust it and the month in which it left your hands.
Ninni and Huluba have robbed me, so fill my horn with good quality oil.

target_raw:
To Alāhum, Ememe and Šišahšušar (From Aššur-nādā):
Puzur-Aššur is on his way to you with 1/3 mina 1 shekel of silver under my seal.
Buy fire-wood and reeds, and have the oxen haul as much of what is there as they can; Buy malahum and habašatum utensils for the maziru tin.
Please may everything be ready so I can move quickly as soon as I arrive.
If they brought the textiles belonging to Šū-Suen and myself from Uršu, then sell them there and divide the proceeds.
This one was set aside here.
If Kutubiš asks for any silver then give him some.

To Alāhum and Aššur-taklāku from Aššur-nādā:
Whatever silver and gold they will send to you from Durhumit, add that to what (I owe) on goods entrusted to me, and if Ilī-ālum (is there), then certainly it is agreed.
Hannānum son of Ikuppiya, grandson of Ikua, is on his way with 10 minas of refined silver that belongs to us both.
Deposit its import duty and its transport tariff (with money taken) from my silver or from the price of my textiles, and let the amount go for us both.
Dispatch the servants who have arrived with Saher-ilī.

To Alāhum and Aššur-taklāku from Ilī-ālum:
Why is it that you have given orders to your packer, saying: "I have taken the slave; but if they refuse to receive (him), then take him for our father."
But there you said: "If the price is 8 shekels of silver, then take him and lead him away.
If it is not like that, I shall keep him."
You pointed out your packer; you then gave me 8 shekels of silver belonging to your packer; you refused to give anything to our father's slave.
When you return to Aššur-idī to take decisions about me, I shall say: "Whatever my father's representatives will say, I shall refuse to ask."

To Amur-Ištar from ...
6 talents tin under seal from the city in his debt I opened one sack and I checked 1 talent 3 minas.
išertam itašsu .. 12 kutānum textiles their wrappings 3 donkeys, all this belongs to Šalim-aḫum and myself.
4 talents tin under seal from the city, 8 kutānum textiles their wrappings, ... 2 talents 10 minas tin masram, the tin and the silver marsam I paid out.
In total, 12 talents 10 minas and its uturaúm tin 20 kutanum textiles, and 6 donkeys Iluwa brought to you.
My dear brother, Be a man/ Act like a man and after the tin of the caravan arrives, convert the tin to gold.
Do not release the tin for bali (PN? or purpose?) but idī-Kūbum requested a talent or 2 talents from you.
He said, Llet me exhaust it."
Give it to him.
Urgent, take heed to convert the tin to gold, seal the (silver?) and entrust it to Iluwa and send it 9and him) with the first travelers.
# talents tin ... do not retain
Sell it to him and send their send the gained silver.
The caravan, as soon as the caravan arrives, sell the tin.
Do not retain it.
I gave Iluwa 15 shekels tin for 3 1/3 shekels silver.

To Amur-Ištar and Pūšu-kēn from Kuzallum:
Amur-Aššur brings you 1 mina 37 1/2 shekels silver belonging to Ḫinnaya under my seal, the price of the gold which the colony office lent out.
There, if you need the textiles of Ḫinnaya for smuggling, pay out the silver.
If Ḫinnaya dictated that his goods should be dispatched here, do not in any way send them to me.

target_raw:
To Amur-Ištar and Uṣur-ša-Aššur say: thus (says) Aššur-ṭāb.
You are my fathers, you are my lords.
There is no road anywhere to move forward.
(Regarding) my messenger whom we sent to the (Tuḫpiya) colony, as concerns the opening of the road to Tuḫpiya, take care to report (about the matter): my messenger shoudl be sent to me so that I shall not be delayed here.
Let your report in this respect come here and do inform me.
To Uṣur-ša-Aššur say:
X-tikal should pay 1/2 mina of silver to you.
The merchandise which you sent me has safely arrived.
Until you make the purchases, the silver should remain in safekeeping under your own seal.

To Amur-Ištar and Šū-Anum from Aššur-nādā:
I hear that Dakniš is going to the City.
Regarding the 20 minas of silver which he ... and I hold, let him give you the silver, lay claim to the silver on my behalf and entrust it to an affiliated trader.
1 mina ... let him make you the representatives.
Give the šannu-kettle either to the men from Wahšušana or those from Kaneš as wages, and have them bring it.
To Amur-Ištar: I gave you 1 mina of silver, saying: "Give it to my representatives in the City."
Send me word if you have sent it.
If you have not sent it, now, then send it with travelers, and write ... and seal my copper.

To Amur-Ištar and Šū-Anum from Aššur-nādā:
I hear that Dakniš is going to the City.
Regarding the 20 minas of silver which he ... and I hold, let him give you the silver, lay claim to the silver on my behalf and entrust it to an affiliated trader.
1 mina ... let him make you the representatives.
Give the šannu-kettle either to the men from Wahšušana or those from Kaneš as wages, and have them bring it.
To Amur-Ištar: I gave you 1 mina of silver, saying: "Give it to my representatives in the City."
Send me word if you have sent it.
If you have not sent it, now, then send it with travelers, and write ... and seal my copper.
label: Cuneiform Tablet KTS 1 33a

Zu Addād[a, Buzāzu und] Aššurmu[ttabbil] sprich! [Folgendermaßen. . . . :]
[Meine] Brüder [(seid) ihr! Meine Herren] (seid) ihr! [. . . . . ]
10 sch[warze] Stoff[e. . . . . ] habe ich überlassen [. . . ] . . [. . . . ] dort . . [. . . ] des Kupfers [. . . . . . . . ].
. . . [. . . . ] des Kaufmannes [. . . . . ]. . . [. . . . . ]. . . hast du mir ge<brac>ht.
[. . . . . ]. . . . [. . . . .] und wegen des . . . . [. . . An we]n sonst soll ich mich wenden? Meine Brüder, meine Herren!
Das Silber schickt mir umgehend! Dann will ich (es) dem Kaufmann zurückgeben.
Zu Buzāzu spr[ic]h! Du hast mir geschrieben, folgendermaßen du:
"Einen Kommissär sollt ihr nicht nehmen! Ihr sollt mir (das) nicht schreiben!"
Damit der Stoff <nicht> verderbe, habe ich dir vertraut und entsprechend (dem Wunsche) meines Gatten und Herren habe ich mir einen Kommissär nicht genommen.
Heute war ich entsprechend der Sklavin . . . . . dir nicht und die Genossen (?) für die Sklavin sollst du . . . . . ..
Hier hatte ich eine Schuldverpflichtung (in Händen). Ich habe ihm (einen Teil zurück)-gegeben;
Gold, 1 Mine, ist (der Rest) mein(es) Darlehen(s).
(Betrifft) die Angelegenheiten der Stoffe für mich, die mein Herr mir geschickt hat: Die von dem Anteil des Sohnes des Amā’a (hat) Titatra; 4 gute Stoffe: Buzāzu; 4 gute Stoffe: Asānum; 3 Stoffe: Kūṣum: Š[ūb]ēlum, der Sohn des Zurzur.
Ins[gesa]mt 10 [+ x gute [Stoffe]. Dies ist, (was) ich (mit den Stoffen) getan habe. Mein Darlehen . . . . de[m]
Bēlī’aššur [. . . . ]. . . .. Daß ich das Darleh[en zahlen kann,] habt ihr (das Gold) [nicht] gesiegelt und mir nicht geschickt. Das Gold habt ihr erhalten! Meine Brüder, meine Herren seid ihr!
Umgehend schickt mir (das) Gold! Tut mir doch den Gefallen!

target_raw:
To Aššur-bēl-awātim, Alāhum's and Alili's representatives, Hinnaya and Aššur-nādā from Aššur-bēl-malkim:
About the silver of my working capital, which you sent to the City, Alāhum, Aššur-idī and Alili met concerning it, and they gave me 2 talents of tin under seal, 8 textiles (and) 8 minas of tin for expenses.
In the city gate they made a public statement, saying: ''Now, Uṣur-ša-Aššur is our representative."
Uṣur-ša-Aššur was held back in the City while I left for Uršu with my own enterprise.
A message from Šū-Kūbum reached me at Uršu and in Šū-Kūbum's message (it said): "Convert the tin to textiles!"
Since the textiles had been set aside, I converted the tin to 4 talents of [copper].
My merchandise is scattered around; I shall collect it and arrive at the beginning of spring.

To Aššur-imittī son of Ennānim, thus Pūšu-kēn:
Your awītum valuation is 5 talents 20 minas.
The dātum tax up to Kaneš is 5 2/3 minas at a 5 shekel rate.
A dātum assessment fell at 30 2/3 minas (tin?) .
The head-tax on the drivers was 15 shekels.
The tašiatum of the ŠA.TU was 1/2 mina.
The AŠ-a-dum feed of the drivers was 1/2 mina.
The excise-tax of the palace was 8 minas.
The deficiencies of your packs (and your hand tin?) was 11 1/3 minas.
X from 4 talents 40 minas together with your hand tin- 51 minas 15 shekels was expended.
I deducted it and they cleared 3 talents 48 minas and 45 shekels of your tin.
Thereof, Ilī-ālim took 1 talent.
Lulu took 1 talent 2 minas 15 shekels.
At a 9 shekel rate, your silver was 6 minas 55 shekels.
Thereof, I gave Kurub-Ištar 7 2/3 shekels for the shipping charges.
The remainder of your silver is 6 minas 40 shekels.
Kurub-Ištar brings you 1/3 shekels under my seal.
1 talent 46 minas of tin which they cleared, is in my charge.
From your 10 kutānum textiles, both the excise tariff and the purchases (tithe) tariff, they cleared 9 textiles.
Then 8 šurūtum textiles from the wrappings a deposited at the colony office to your account.
Aššur-rē'ī deposited the remainder.
From the 5 1/2 minas silver which I owe, 2/3 minas is a fine.
Both that of the 6 shekels of your tin and your textiles I paid (to/at) the colony office.
4 2/3 minas 4 shekels (from my own funds?) is the remainder of your silver.
There was a "depositing" at the colony office and (to?) it I withheld your silver.
(I said), "The šuruniʾum will not *drink water from their backs."

To Aššur-imittī son of Ennānim, thus Pūšu-kēn:
Your awītum valuation is 5 talents 20 minas.
The dātum tax up to Kaneš is 5 2/3 minas at a 5 shekel rate.
A dātum assessment fell at 30 2/3 minas (tin?) .
The head-tax on the drivers was 15 shekels.
The tašiatum of the ŠA.TU was 1/2 mina.
The AŠ-a-dum feed of the drivers was 1/2 mina.
The excise-tax of the palace was 8 minas.
The deficiencies of your packs (and your hand tin?) was 11 1/3 minas.
X from 4 talents 40 minas together with your hand tin- 51 minas 15 shekels was expended.
I deducted it and they cleared 3 talents 48 minas and 45 shekels of your tin.
Thereof, Ilī-ālim took 1 talent.
Lulu took 1 talent 2 minas 15 shekels.
At a 9 shekel rate, your silver was 6 minas 55 shekels.
Thereof, I gave Kurub-Ištar 7 2/3 shekels for the shipping charges.
The remainder of your silver is 6 minas 40 shekels.
Kurub-Ištar brings you 1/3 shekels under my seal.
1 talent 46 minas of tin which they cleared, is in my charge.
From your 10 kutānum textiles, both the excise tariff and the purchases (tithe) tariff, they cleared 9 textiles.
Then 8 šurūtum textiles from the wrappings a deposited at the colony office to your account.
Aššur-rē'ī deposited the remainder.
From the 5 1/2 minas silver which I owe, 2/3 minas is a fine.
Both that of the 6 shekels of your tin and your textiles I paid (to/at) the colony office.
4 2/3 minas 4 shekels (from my own funds?) is the remainder of your silver.
There was a "depositing" at the colony office and (to?) it I withheld your silver.
(I said), "The šuruniʾum will not *drink water from their backs."
label: Cuneiform Tablet BIN 4 29 (NBC 1692)

The tablet concerning 17 minas silver which you left in your house is part of our legal affair.
Kill that tablet and release to me the tablet concerning 10 minas silver (owed by) Pūšu-kēn s. Buzāza.
My dear brother assist my brother Ṭāb-Aššur there to kill that tablet and 'make known?' the tablet concernign the 10 minas silver of the debt of Pūšu-kēn s. Šerawa(?), which (is credited to) my name.

target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā from Iddin-Ištar:
as to the silver that you have written about once or twice, may Aššur and Ilabrat, the gods of our fathers, be my witnesses, that since the day you came to Durhumit and we met, people collected goods at a purchase price exceeding 2:1 and at a rate of exchange of 60:1, but within my stipulated term the copper has tripled, so would I envisage losses in copper of maybe a hundred talents?
While Aššur himself and your own god have rejected the man, still silver has fallen into my hands and until this day you have been fully satisfied.
As to the affair of Anninum's son, whom you know, the tin and its overweight stood ready to be shipped with Ṭāb-Aššur, but on the very day that the merchandise came out Ṭāb-Aššur son of Ešu-bāni came in and said: "Within 5 days the man will be disfamed.
Turn back your merchandise!"
So I turned back his merchandise.
The very day I wrote a tablet for you my orders were dispatched to the young man.
If I should personally seize the merchandise both you and I will be happy.

1/3 mina of silver under my seal for our father--money I owe to him--and 5 shekels of silver for your sacrifices Iddin-abum, the son of Iddin-Ištar, is bringing to you.

To Aššur-nādā from Šarrat-Ištar:
Zuba brings [x] textiles to you.
1 belt for you, 1 belt for ...
Sell the 4
The undergarment is for the maid in Kaneš.
2 textiles cost 37 minas of copper.
I sold 1 textile for silver ....
Kurub-Ištar ... is deposited with you.
I have said nothing to him.
Set out and come.
All this Zuba brings to you.
1/2 mina 5 shekels of silver Ennam-Aššur brought me.
5 shekels of silver and 3 shekels of gold Puzur-Ištar brought me.

source_raw: a-na a-šur-na-da qí-bí-ma um-ma pí-lá-ḫi-IŠTAR-ma iš-tù ba-ad-na a-gu5-a ṭá-bi-a-nu-um ú il5-li-ba-ni ú-wa-šé-ru-ni-ma um-ma ṭá-bi-a-nu-ma ba-am iš-tí-ni TÚG.ḪI ú-qá-i-ú iš-tí-šu-nu iš-tù ba-ad-na na-ba-ta-am nu-ṣí-ma iš-tí-šu-nu a-na É wa-áb-ri ú-lá ú-šé-ru-ni a-ḫa-ma É al-pí a bi-it É-tám im-lu-šu-ma 6 TÚG.ḪI i-ta-áb-ku ep-ra-am a-na qá-qí-dí-a áš-pu-KUM-ma a-na ba-ad-na a-na ṣé-er a-lá-bi-im a-tù-ra-ma wa-ba-ar-tum10 ša ba-ad-na a-na ṣé-er ba-ru-li e-li-ú-ma um-ma šu-nu-ma lu ni-iš-e ú šu-ma i-ḫa-li-qú né-nu nu-ma-lá
target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā from Pilah-Ištar:
Agua, Ṭāb-pī-Anum and Ilī-bāni released me from Badna, and Ṭāb-pī-Anum said:
"Come on, together with us."
They were waiting for textiles.
We left Badna together during the night but they did not allow me into the guest-house together with them.
Alone I spent the night in a cow-stable.
They broke into the house and removed 6 textiles.
I poured dust on my head (in despair) for your sake, and I returned to Badna to Alābum;
then (officers of) the trading station at Badna went up to the barullu-officers (of the palace) and said:
"Let us search (for the textiles), and if they are lost then we shall personally give compensation."

To Aššur-nādā and Ištar-pilah from Kuzallum:
When the scribe Kura came in order to levy the transport tariff, after they imposed the tax on what I had sent here, for the merchandise that came with Ištar-pilah . . .
silver . . .
. . . to the colony . . .
. . . to you . . .
on the merchandise in Ištar-pilah's caravan, since the transport tariff had already been levied, then, my dear father and lords, confront the colony there in order that I do not have to pay transport tariff a second time.
(Specifically) to Aššur-nādā:
Did you not personally observe here that they levied the tax?
Check the merchandise in accordance with the memorandum I gave you in Tišmurna.

target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā, Šumma-libbi-Aššur and Alulaya from Adad-ṣulūlī:
I had become indebted to Kudātum for 24 talents 10 minas of fine copper, but in Ulama my representatives paid Kudātum in full, and they produced my tablet concerning my debt and left it with Iddin-Aššur for safe-keeping.
Today Iddin-Aššur refuses to release my tablet, for he says: "They deposited it here in connection with an inheritance."
To prove that they did not deposit it in connection with an inheritance you must there seize 3 affiliated traders (to be witnesses) against Kudātum, and you must say: ''With respect to the 24 talents 10 minas of washed copper that Adad-ṣulūlī owed to you, did Adad-ṣulūlī pay you out Adad-ṣulūlī copper and satisfy you?"
Set witnesses for him and then send me word with his answer as soon as possible.
Alulaya knows the entire business.
Let him inform you there.

To Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur from Pūšu-kēn:
Unfortunately, Idī-Šamaš has died.
A loss of ten minas (of silver?) or more has hit me on the road.
And 5 textiles are lost.
From its remnant which has arised, 1/2 mina of silver, his be'ulātum, and 3 1/3 minas of tin is owed(?) (who owes who here?).
Witnesses: Šū-Suen [x-x]-mātim, Dān-Aššur.
From his four textiles, which he brought up to me, 3 shekels of silver are its excise.
x+6 shekels (of silver) fall to him from the textiles which the palace took for the tithe.
At a 12 shekel rate, the price of the four textiles is 48 shekels.
Thereof, 9 shekels is the excise.
He deducted it from the tithe and the remainder of his silver is 39 shekels.
Thereof, x minas and 1 5/6 shekels (of silver) is its excise, and his shipping charge, [...], I took.
... 3 shekels (of silver?) the assets (?) ... 4 1/6 shekels ...

To Aššur-dān and Agua from Pūšu-kēn:
To Aššur-dān:
x minas silver (with) the representative of Aššur-rē'ī, x minas (silver with) the representative of Iliš-tīkal, x minas (silver with) Lālum, x minas (silver with) Gazia, 1 mina (silver for PN), the losses of the brother of Qarwiya ...
Purchase a very fine table with the 1/2 mina silver, the price of the raqqutum textile you owe.
Sell(?) it when I write my youth.
If you did not give (him it), I will not grant you the parsum and interest as a favor.
Ištar-pilaḫ, the priest of Aššur owes me 35 shekels silver.
His tablet is with my (servant/youth?).
His term is exceeded by a year.
... my instructions will come to you.
The remainder of the silver ...

target_raw:
To Aššur-idī from Aššur-nādā:
My dear father and lord, quickly dispatch the 1 mina of silver which Kurub-Ištar brought you plus whatever goods Ilī-ālum sent to you, so that the goods reach me to be available, and let me receive it en route so it will be heard here and the owner of the goods entrusted to me will not demand a guarantor when I am about to leave, and I shall not be put to shame.
If the goods have not left so it will not be heard here, who then will let himself be noted as my guarantor for the silver in its entirety?
They will say: "Go to your father!
Who knows whether the father is good for his name?"
Things are fine for you, in fact, silver lies in front of you, while I here repeatedly have to pay silver for textiles on your account and my own, and, yes, I constantly establish a reputation for truthfulness; and in this way I am paying the fees.
If you do not send some of your own, then with respect to the silver entrusted to me plus the (silver) belonging to outsiders that Aššur-taklāku sent to you-- clear the goods and dispatch them so it will be heard here, and so that at my departure the owner of the goods entrusted to me will not hold me back and I shall not perish.

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-i-dí qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šùr-na-da-ma a-na šu-mì ni-kà-sí-a a-dí eš15-ri-šu ta-aq-bi-a-am um-ma a-ta-ma i-na KÙ.BABBAR-pì-a šu-qú-ul ù i-na ša-am-ší ú-ṣí-a-ni um-ma a-na-ku-ma lá-áš-ḫu-ut um-ma a-ta-ma lá ta-ša-ḫa-at a-na me-er-a a-na ú-ša-zi-iz ù ki-ma lu-mu-un li-bi4-im a-ni-a-tim ta-ša-pá-ra-am a-šùr ù {d}NIN.ŠUBUR li-ṭù-lá i-na qá-tí-a ú-lá-qí-it-ma 3.5 ma-na 4 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am a-na ni-ip-lá-tí-a ša 16 ma-na.TA lu áš-qú-ul u4-ma-am 16 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR qá-tám i-na KÙ-pì-kà na-ad-a-ku iš-té-en6 ù šé-na ša iš-ḫu-tù-ni ma-lá na-ad-ú-ni i-ti-bi4-il5-ma ù 6 ma-na.TA KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am ša ma-ṣa-tim ša a-šur i-ša-qal-ma i-ša-ḫa-at ma-la-kà 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am ni-is-ḫa-sú DIRI ša-du-a-sú ša-bu a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku na-áš-a-kum AN.NA SIG5 ša-ma šé-bi4-lam
target_raw:
To Aššur-idī from Aššur-nādā:
With respect to my account you told me 10 times: "Pay with my silver."
And the day I left I said: "Let me clear it."
You answered: "Do not clear it!"
For which of my affairs have I personally appointed someone to represent me?
Also, instead of sending these angry messages, let Aššur and Ilabrat be my witnesses that I have scraped together what I had in my possession, and that I did pay 3 1/2 minas 4 shekels of refined silver for my balance payment, amounting to 16 minas each.
Today I am booked for a share of 16 minas of your silver.
As to the one or two who have cleared, what they have deposited has been balanced, and he will have to pay at the rate 6 minas each in refined silver to the treasury of Aššur, and he will then clear it.
It is up to you.
10 minas of refined silver, its import duty added, its transport tariff and export duty paid for, Aššur-taklāku is on his way to you with that.
Buy good quality tin and send it to me.

To Aššur-idī from Aššur-nādā:
The 10 minas of refined silver which is owned jointly by Alāhum and myself, Hannānum, the son of Ikuppiya, has sent to Alāhum and Aššur-taklāku.
I said: "Add the import duty and the transport tariff and send it to our father and Alāhum's representatives."
If you are truly my father and lord, for all the silver and gold
To the City they will go two or three times.

target_raw:
To Aššur-idi from Aššur-nādā:
Aššur-dān is on his way to you with 6 minas of refined silver, its transport tariff paid, from your joint-stock capital and under my seal.
3 shekels of silver, its export-duty, he has been paid.
We wrote to you (in a letter) transported by Šū-Adad, saying: "Within 10 days travelers will come here(?) two or three times, so now we shall set aside for you 5 minas each time."
But after they had left the sikkātum went out and the market is disturbed so we are not in a position to fulfil our promise given when the goods were commissioned.
Silver is hard to get.
Do not expect a lot of silver.
Prices being low, I have sent Ilī-ālum to Wahšušana.
Of the present silver I have received 4 minas of silver from Šū-Anum, and he in turn received a certified tablet with my seal, which says that we have been paid in full for the one mina of gold that was booked in Aššur-emūqi's joint-stock company, and that we shall not raise the matter again.

target:
To Aššur-idi from Aššur-nādā:
Aššur-dān is on his way to you with 6 minas of refined silver, its transport tariff paid, from your joint-stock capital and under my seal.
3 shekels of silver, its export-duty, he has been paid.
We wrote to you (in a letter) transported by Šū-Adad, saying: "Within 10 days travelers will come here(?) two or three times, so now we shall set aside for you 5 minas each time."
But after they had left the sikkātum went out and the market is disturbed so we are not in a position to fulfil our promise given when the goods were commissioned.
Silver is hard to get.
Do not expect a lot of silver.
Prices being low, I have sent Ilī-ālum to Wahšušana.
Of the present silver I have received 4 minas of silver from Šū-Anum, and he in turn received a certified tablet with my seal, which says that we have been paid in full for the one mina of gold that was booked in Aššur-emūqi's joint-stock company, and that we shall not raise the matter again.
label: Cuneiform Tablet CCT 4 10a (BM 115114)

Unfortunately, Puzur-Aššur died.
There is both silver and tablets belonging to Puzur-Aššur.
We seized from Wašḫania, tablets (nom.?) from Waḫšušana and from ...
... about a talent of tin ... mina (silver?) .. him.
When ... (left?) there was very little and I was disappointed.
..... as for myself I retained (?) it.
I said, "Let him ... the goods of the transport of Ennum-Bēlum, or ..., every single minas of silver and make known the silver mastima."
I will bring it myself and divide the silver.
On the day your read the tablet, (with the) 40 minas silver which Ennum-Bēlum brough to you, the excise added, the shipping paid, purchase tin, wrappings, and top pack, and dispatch it with the first departer.
Here, Imdi-ilum regarding the ??? was short.
We said, "Let the prices come up and when the not true he causes to sorrow and every minas silver ...arise and Imdi-ilum bring the silver when he comes and our silver ...."
If anyone for ... sues you (pl.) go? to Imdi-ilum.
You said, "When Imdi-ilum goes/comes ... he ahd brought it, let him gather the silver."
If anyone for ... sues you (pl.) ...
... something which I ... is owed by Assur-ēmūqī s. Idī-Suen.
Our dear fathers and lords, as for you, let him read the very tablet to you and to our presresentatives with you ... and let the x hundred that are written enter.
... the matter.
If Ennum-Bēlum is late, enter and ... in the same way and at the arrival of Ennum-Bēlum sell your .... for silver.
The goods are missing.
...

target_raw:
To Aššur-imittī from Pūšu-kēn:
6 minas 53 shekels silver, which Iliš-tīkal entrusted to Aššur-rēʾī and Ennam-Anum, at the fulfillment of the term Iliš-tīkal collected the silver and thereof I gave 5 5/6 shekels, its šadduʾutum, to Uṣur-ša-Aššur.
The remainder of the silver, 6 minas 48 1/6 shekels silver, Uṣur-ša-Aššur brings to you under my seal.
Sealed tablet for (even?) 1 shekel silver is not in my house nor the house of Aššur-rēʾī nor even the house of Ennam-Anum.
Concerning the matter of Ikūnum about which you wrote to me, I sent Adad-bāni to Ḫaḫḫum and he put his hand on the textiles.
The textiles entered in and he collected the silver according to your instructions.
Your silver is exhausted.

target_raw:
To Aššur-imittī, thus Pūšu-kēn:
I do not have your tablet concerning the transport of Aššur-ṭāb.
I transfered the 1 talent tin from the transport of Aššur-ṭāb son of Uzariya to Itur-ilī.
(He said) "You promised nothing.
And you will not promise 10 minas to me."
I am the man (of favors).
I will do a favor or I will not do a favor.
In the presence of Aššur-imittī son of Ikuppī-Ištar and Ememe son of Azutaya, they brought down your tin and textiles from the palace and they are available.
They are depositing for 1 minas shares (at the?) kārum office and they will set 1½ minas each at accountings.
I paid out (for you) from my own funds.
They will announce/call out/have accountings in four months.
Make your decision.
Because there is much tin and it is fine, we will sold on credit what we could.
For that purpose I deposited tin.
To whom shall your breast turn (other than) to both of us?
You will say, "Give the silver to Pūšu-kēn or let him collect the silver."

To Aššur-imittī and Šū-Ḫubur from Pūšu-kēn:
I wrote 28 minas 48 shekels silver at the colony office.
For this silver I both weighed out your(pl.) silver and sold textiles.
I weighed out 12 minas 49 shekels silver from the silver of Aššur-imittī.
They (the colony office) initiated a pouring in units of 1 mina of silver and I weighed out 2 minas of silver and I placed 1 minas-its
Altogether, 28 minas 48 shekels silver you gave.
The dātum-assessment came to 15 minas each and you will be refunded 1 mina 12 shekels silver.
I will take half from (each of) your (pl.) silver.
To
Why is it that Aššur-imittī wrote me saying: "I will take the silver and its interest from your silver."
As for your half of the silver, your brother is satisfied.
If you do not satisfy (your half), I myself will satisfy (the silver of) Aššur-imittī. With regard to the 15 minas silver, I will take your share, wherever it is, from your silver.
As for myself and Imdī-ilum, we cleared you of obligations at accountings.
We weighed out 6 minas silver, (the amount for) your clearance of obligations, from the price of your tin and textiles.
If you make a deposit, they will assess you a
If you will take responsibility, let your message come and from the day which we weigh out the silver, you will take 1 shekel of silver interest (per mina per month) in the colony office.
Satisfy (the silver) a month. I (already) pressed the colony, saying, "Let them stand for 1 1/4 man."
They did not agree with me. I returned, saying, "Let them stand for a man and a half."
They said, "Are we more 'men' than them?"
On account of these things, I am delaying Uṣur-ša-Aššur.

To Aššur-imittī Šū-Ištar, Šū-Ḫubur, Ennānum and Bubrānum, from Imdī-ilum:
Goods from the transport of Amur-ilī, until this day you have delayed.
Also, the goods of the transport of Āl-aḫm, when Āl-aḫum arrived .. you yourself have delayed until this day!
... the goods of Puzur-Aššur which came down with ..., until now, you have delayed(?) them there.
Also, the which came down with you (pl.) let is go up to here so that I can convert it to silver and return it.
As for you, for three years not you have retained it and ... the tablets.
Also, Āl-āḫum as much as he gave you at accountings, your report has not come whatsoever.
Also, Ḫinnaya went there.
Assist the investors and casue difficulties(?) and raise about a mina of silver.
In the tablets I wrote to you, I said, "Let all the goods, either from the transport of Amur-ilī, or from that of Puzur-Aššur, or from the transport of Āl-āḫum, get to Kanesh to Nāb-Suen so that he can convert them to silver and the caravan may draw close."
But as for you (pl.) you have delayed my merchandise.
Afterward, Puzur-Aššur the transporter of Pūšu-kēn who after he brought my goods down, he(?) exhausted all of my goods.
Pilaḫ-Ištar s. Uzuwa brought (goods) to Mamma (for?) you (s.)(?).

The 21 1/3 shekels gold that Aššur-mālik brought was sold at an 8 5/6 shekel rate.

target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā (and) Uṣur-ša-Aššur from Aššur-taklāku:
Open the tablet containers and give the certified tablet regarding the debt of Aššur-nādā, the son of Ah-šalim, of 1 mina of silver to him.
I have been paid the silver in full here.
If Aššur-nādā has not come, then you and Aššur-nādā must open (for) the tablets and give him his tablet, and after that you both must seal (the container).
If there is a possibility for šitapku-investments of textiles in the colony office, then deposit on our father's account the textiles which I have left.
If there is a possibility for šitapku-investments of silver, then send me word so I can send you silver.

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-in-ma 0.5 ma-na 7.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR {d}MAR.TU-ba-ni ḫa-bu-lam ṭup-pu-šu ḫa-ar-ma-am iš-tù MU.5.ŠÈ e-zi-ba-kum-ma ú-lá KÙ.BABBAR tù-ša-áš-qal ú-lá ṭup-pí tù-ta-ra-am áš-ta-na-me-ma {d}MAR.TU-ba-ni a-na kà-ni-iš e-ta-ar-ba-am ù KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ù 2 ma-na a qá-tí-šu pá-qí-id a-ḫi a-ta ṣa-ba-sú-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša-áš-qí-il5-šu šu-ma i ma-ḫi-ri-im lá i-na-mar DUB.SAR ša kà-ri-im É ša-té-a DUMU.SAL sú-e-ta-ta na-ḫi-id
target_raw:
Zu Aššurnādā sprich! Folgendermaßen Aššuriddin:
½ Mine 7½ Seqel Silber ist Amurrabbāni mir schuldig.
Seine Dopel-urkunde habe ich seit {dem Eponymat} fünf Jahren dir überlassen.
Entweder läßt du ihn das Silber zahlen, oder meine Urkunde gibst du mir zurück.
Wie ich immer wieder höre, ist Amurrubbāni nach Kaniš gekommen.
Silber, 1 oder 2 Minen, ist ihm anvertraut.
Mein Bruder bist du! Lade ihn vor und laß ihn das Silber zahlen!
Wenn er sich auf dem Markte nicht zeigt, beauftrage den Schreiber des Kārum bezüglich des Hauses der Šad’a, der Tochter des Su’eddādā.

To Aššur-nādā from Ennānum:
They sent Aššur-nēmedi, your son, into the countryside, but I fetched him back and it is I who personally feed him 20
Ask Lamašša there (or) Annina your friend.

Now do

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma um-ma ku-ku-la-nu-um-ma 30 ma-na URUDU É DAM.GÀR-ri-im 1 ma-na-um 10 GÍN a-šùr-be-el-a-wa-tim ù a-na-ku ni-zi-iz me-ra-kà ù me-er-ú-a e-ku-lu iš-tù ITU.1.KAM na-ar-ma-ak-a-šur a-pu-tum i-ḫi-id-ma KÙ.BABBAR ù ṣí-ba-sú šé-bi-la-ma-ma DAM.GÀR-ra-am lu nu-ša-bi ù ma-la ṣú-ḫu-ur-kà la i-ba-re-ù-ni KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi-lam <gap> i pá-ni-im-ma ba-tí-qí-im i-ḫi-id-ma KÙ.BABBAR ma-la šé-bu-li-im šé-bi-lam
target_raw:
Aššur-bēl-awātim and I have borrowed 30 minas of copper in a merchant-firm, at the (interest) rate 10 shekels per mina (monthly), and your son and daughters have spent it.
(The loan runs from) the month Narmak-Aššur.
Please, take care to send the silver and the accrued interest so we may pay in full to the merchant.
Also, send silver so that your children do not starve.
Take care and send whatever there is to send with an express messenger!

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi4-ma um-ma a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ak-ma 5 GÚ.TA TÚG.ḪI.A 3 me-at-TA maš-ki a-na qá-ta-tí-ku-nu al-qé 1 ma-na 10 GÍN.TA KÙ.BABBAR i-qá-ta-tí-ku-nu i-ṣa-ḫe-er 31.5 maš-ku 40 ma-na.TA a-na ma-na-em KÙ.BABBAR ša ILLAT-at pu-šu-ke-en6 ik-šu-da-ma 12 GÚ 50 ma-na TÚG.ḪI.A 6 me-at maš-ki al-qé 1 0.16666 GÍN.TA a-na TÚG.ḪI.A KÙ.BABBAR lá-šu maš-ku wa-as-mu a-dí ša a-na kà-ri-im ma-ḫa-ri-im ta-áš-ta-na-pá-ra-ni a-dí eš15-ri-šu a-na a-lá-ḫi-im ù kur-ub-IŠTAR aṭ-ḫi-ma lá na-ṭù-ma lá i-ma-ḫu-ru-ni-ku-um KÙ.BABBAR ša a-bu-zu-ta-a a-pu-lu uk-ta-i-ni ni-kà-sú qú-ur-bu a-na a-ta-al-ki-im ša-áp-ra-ku-nu-tí al-kà-ma a ma-lá ta-aḫ-sí-is-tim li-zu-zu
target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā from Aššur-taklāku:
5 talents of wool for each of you, representing 300 fleeces, I received as your shares.
1 mina 10 shekels of silver will be deducted from each of your shares.
31 1/2 hides or 40 minas (of wool) for each mina of silver (invested) resulted from Pūšu-kēn's enterprise, so I received 10 talents 50 minas of wool representing 600 fleeces.
Even at the rate 1 1/6 shekel for (a mina of) wool there is no silver to be had, although the fleeces are in order.
As to the matter of appealing to the colony authorities, about which you have written so often, at least ten times, I approached Alāhum and Kurub-Ištar, but it was not the right moment to appeal on your behalf.
Buzutaya has confirmed to me the silver which I balanced with him.
The time for the accounting is close.
You had been told by me by letter to leave, so, come and let them divide (the assets) in accordance with the note.

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi4-ma um-ma a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku-ma a-na šé-ni-šu šé-né-en6 pá-tí-té-en6 ṣí-pá-ra-tim ša 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ù ṭup-pá-am ša a-šùr-lá-ma-sí DUMU a-lá-ḫi-im a-na a-šùr-DUG ip-qí-da-ni a-ma-kam i-bi-ší mì-ma ú-nu-tim i-a-tim ù ša ṣú-ḫa-ri ú-na-dí-id-ma lá-šu ṣú-ḫa-ri a-ma-kam ša-il5 ṣí-pá-ra-tim ù ṭup-pá-am a-na a-šùr-DU10 ta-er-ma té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam me-nu-zi-il5-kà ša-al <gap>

To Aššur-nādā from Aššur-taklāku:
Take action for me there with respect to two pairs of open sandals, bronze nails worth 5 shekels of silver plus the tablet that Aššur-lamassī, the son of Alāhum, entrusted to Aššur-tāb.
I searched all my own things and those of the servants, but it is gone.
Ask the servants there and return the bronze nails and the tablet to Aššur-tab, and sent me word here.
Ask Minusilka.

target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā from Iddin-Ištar:
since the land of Burušhaddum as well as Wahšušana is in revolt, for that reason I did not proceed to Wahšušana,and I took no decision about the copper that is stored in Wahšušana.
Within 5 days I shall hear definitive news, and shall proceed to Wahšušana.
Aššur-šamši is on his way to you with x minas of washed copper under my seal.
Pay Alāhum's silver
and buy for the rest of the silver kusītu-textiles from Mamma as well as menuni'ānu textiles and woolly fleeces,
fill up the donkeys so empty donkeys will not come here.
Take care to get a tablet with their testimony as witnesses and send it to me as soon as possible.

source_raw: a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi4-ma um-ma ì-lí-a-lúm-ma i-na ṭup-pì-kà 72 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu lá-áp-tù la-ma né-ru-ba-ni ni-im-nu-šu-nu-ma 71 TÚG 1 TÚG ba-tí-iq ŠÀ.BA 2 {TÚG}a-bar-ni-ú a-šu-mì ni-kà-sí-kà ša-ḫa-tim am-ḫu-ur-šu-nu-ma um-ma šu-nu-ma ú-lá i-ša-ḫa-at i-za-az qá-ta-am li-iš-ku-un mì-šu ša um-ma a-ta-ma a-šar pá-na-i-a a-ša-pá-ra-kum šu-ma a-ta-ma a-na pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim ḫa-ra-kà-ma URUDU-i-ni a-na KÙ.BABBAR tù-ta-ar ù a-na-ku a-na-kam TÚG-tí-kà ku-ba-ri šu-ma a-ma-kam ta-ga-mì-il5 TÚG-tí-kà e-zi-ib-ma a-na pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim URUDU-i-ni ù-šé-ra-áb šu-ma re-eš15 da-áš-e iš-té-en i ba-ri-ni lá e-ru-ub né-ma-lam ú-lá ni-ra-ší 27.33333 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am a-mur-a-šùr i-dí-a-bu-um a-na a-bi4-ni ḫa-bu-lu KÙ.BABBAR ša-áš-qí-il5-ma a-na a-bi4-ni šé-bi4-il5 šu-ma um-ma a-mur-a-šùr-ma qá-tí le-qé lá ta-ma-ḫa-ar-šu KÙ.BABBAR i-na qá-qá-ad šál-mì-šu-nu ra-ki-is šu-ma ma-lá ṭup-pì-šu-nu i-ša-qal le-qé šu-ma lá i-ša-qá-lá-kum um-ma a-ta-ma ma-lá ṭup-pì-ku-nu ṣí-ib-tám ni-ma-nu ší-bi4 šu-ku-šu-um ù ma-lá i-pu-lu-kà šu-up-ra-am TÚG.ḪI.A pá-ni-ú-tum lu ku-wa-ú-tum lu i-a-ú-tum ša a-na e-lá-ma e-zi-bu a-dí-ni i-ba-ší-ú wa-ar-ki-ú-tum sà-sà-am lá-áp-tù
target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā from IIī-ālum:
In your letter 72 kutānu-textiles were mentioned.
Before we entered (the town) we counted them and there were 71 textiles, 1 textile short.
2 of them were abarniu-textiles.
"He cannot settle.
He should guarantee. Let him lay claim."
Why is it that you wrote as follows: "I shall write you from where I find myself."
If your own journey takes you to Burušhaddum and you exchange our copper for silver there, then I personally shall put your textiles up for sale here.
If you are detained there, then I shall leave your textiles and bring our copper into Burušaddum.
Unless one of us two has entered (there) before the beginning of spring, we have enjoyed no profit.
27 1/3 minas 5 shekels of refined (silver) Amur-Aššur and Iddin-abum owe to our father.
Have the silver paid and send it to our father.
Should Amur-Aššur say: "Take my share (of the debt)"- do not oblige him!
They are jointly responsible for the silver.
If he pays in accordance with their tablet, then receive it.
If he does not pay you anything, say to him:
"We shall count interest in accordance with your tablet!"
Set witnesses for him, and then write me what he answered you.
The earlier textile shipments, yours as well as mine, which I left for Elamma, are still available.
The later ones have been infected by moths.

I bring to you 1/2 mina 1 shekel of refined silver under the seal of Aššur-idī about which you wrote to the colony.
My dear brother, buy 6 strong textiles for the household in/of Aru'ar and send them to me.
With respect to the šitapku-investments of textiles that you wrote about, if there is a possibility for such investments, then I shall deposit your father's share.

To Aššur-nādā from Ša-ša-aḫ-šu-šar:
Since you had left they have received 2/3 mina 5 shekels of silver and given that to Uṣur-ša-Aššur son of Aššur-malik.
Uṣur-ša-Aššur has taken 3 kutānu-textiles and deposited them in the port office.
They received 12 shekels of silver from Šarapunuwa and gave that to me.
The house of Kutubiš brought 4 sacks of barley;
I received it here and 1 1/2 sack will be for myself.
The house of Ka'uba brought 3 sacks of wheat.
However, they did not give me anything.
15 shekels of silver from Tarhunu, they owe the rest of the silver;
thereof they have given me 8 1/4 shekels of silver.
However, they did not give me anything (else), and 5 shekels remain owed by Tarhunu.
Also, it was they who took the rest of the silver.
They have received nothing from your outstanding claims.
Make Uṣur-ša-Aššur pay attention to your orders.
The term (for repayment) is overdue by three months.
Even firewood he did not buy for me, so I myself have bought it.
From Habi we have received 1 2/3 mina 5 shekels of silver;
Uṣur-ša-Aššur has taken it.
2/3 mina we got from Haluli;
also, Uṣur-ša-Aššur <has received> 5 shekels of silver.

target_raw:
To Aššur-nādā and Iddin-Suen from Ilī-ālum:
Previously, before you sent me your goods, I wrote to you, (saying): "Do not send any goods to me!"
CHECK TRANSLATION: You drive them verily yourself, so that I do not clear any of my own things.
As your goods arrived, I cleared copper worth 10 or 15 minas of silver for Aššur-taklāku, and kept it back on account of your goods.
Further, I shall make at least 20 talents of copper or more for you.
I
Also, x minas [you received] from my outstanding claims and thus opened my eyes.
You must write to me soon about Šarmama and his partners.
His son is dead.
We were unable to go to him.
You will write to me later.
During the sikkātum it is not feasible for us to live up to the promises with respect to your outstanding claims.
The accounting concerning Abum-ilī's enterprise has not taken place.
I asked Turaya, who said: "I balanced 12 shekels of silver for Iddin-Suen."
Twice I have written to you.
Within 3 months after they have paid the balance (and) the price of the textiles has been received, Abum-ilum must pay the balance to you.
If he does not pay, he must add interest for me.
Why has he not paid in accordance with his contract?
Indeed, Iddin-Suen will receive 12 shekels from Turaya.
Your 5 talents of copper Šalim-beli has brought into Burušhaddum.

To Aššur-nādā and Uṣur-ša-Aššur from Ilī-ālum:
13 minas 11 1/2 shekels of silver (and) 1/3 mina 7 shekels of gold under the seals of my representatives Bēlum-bāni brought to you.
2 1/2 minas of refined silver (and) 10 1/6 shekels of
I wrote to you, (saying): "Pay 15 1/2 minas to Pūšu-kēn and give the rest to Aššur-nādā."
You wrote back, (saying): "Come!"
Against god's will they have held me up in Šaladuwar for 8 months; moreover, merchandise worth some 20 minas of silver has been tied down together with me.
So, if indeed it had been invested in copper, I would have experienced losses of 5 minas of silver; not only was there a deficit from here, but then there Šū-Ištar and his associates hold back on the reparations.
Take care and have the silver paid, so I shall not perish on the journey to the City.
He who has no brothers--I am like him!

To Aššur-nādā and Šū-Aššur from Karubua:
As to Amur-šarrassa, I sent him to Wahšušana, and he has just left to go there.
However, he left the donkeys and they have died.
His working capital is 1/2 mina of silver, and interest accrues to him also on 30 minas of copper;
his terms are overdue by 3 months.
For 42 shekels of silver his maid is held as security, but interest still accrues for him.
He furthermore owes me 10 minas of copper.
My dear brothers, confront the colony, have him brought out so he pays the silver in accordance with this letter;
he should pay to you.
Zuzua brings 22 shekels of silver.
Organise the purchase for him that he wants, do not delay but let him come here.
The donkeys from Hurama Zuzua should send.
The memorandum ...

To Aššur-nādā and Šū-Suen from Pūšu-kēn:
The 3 minas silver which I gave to my kaṣṣārum, Il-kāšid, for purchases, whatever Il-kāšid 'stands', whether donkeys or purchases as much as he purcahsed, entrust the silver to a traveller immediately so that he brings it.
Now, as for him, let him come with his goods.
If he will not come, entrust it to a traveler and let them bring it.
The silver will return to my silver (funds).
If he says anything (against it), give your (pl.) word concerning the gold.

Zu Aššurrēṣī und Aššurmalik sprich! Folgendermaßen Puzuraššur:
Zu Aššurrēṣī sprich! Mit 2 Minen Zinn von dem des Kaufmannes hat man mich bezahlt.
Ich halte mich in Ḫaka auf. Ich weiß nicht (was tun)!
Ich kam her und (sprach) folgendermaßen zu deinen Kollegen: "Die Urkunde gebt mit!"
Folgendermaßen (antworteten) zu dein Kollegen: "Von dem (Zinn) des Kaufmannes bezahle das Zinn!"
Nach Kaniš werden wir es (das Zinn?) bringen und entsprechend (dem Stande) der Angelegenheit werde ich dorthin (eine Reise) unternehmen.
Folgendermaßen (sprach) Šūsu’in: "Die Tafel werde ich nicht (hin)bringen."
Er kam her und der Kaufmann wird (nun) den Besche[id] dir überprüfen (?).
Du sollst mir eiligst scheiben!
Weil ich an diesen Dingen keinen Anteil habe, mein Bruder bist du, möge zu mir nach Zalpa Bescheid kommen.
Dann will ich in Angelegenheiten einer Reise nach der Stadt (d. h. nach Assur) hinkommen und ich will mich dort rechtfertigen

The situation is drastic.
Within one month, he will (be forced to) clear the matter with me.
When I come, will you not do me a favor regarding Ḫaṣabšilum's silver and interest?
Take it on yourself.
Here, the price of
Let your report come as to whether this is the case or not.

Within one month, he will (be forced to) clear the matter with me.
When I come, will you not do me a favor regarding Ḫaṣabšilum's silver and interest?
Take it on yourself.
Here, the price of
Let your report come as to whether this is the case or not.
label: Cuneiform Tablet POAT 29 (L29 588)

target_raw:
To Idī-abum, Aššur-mālik, Aššur-imittī, Šū-Anum, Enna-Suen, and Puzur-ilī, from Pilaḫ-Ištar:
The message of the amtum wife has come to me once or twice.
Regarding Ilī-nādā s. Baziya, why is it that over there he constantly threatens to distrain my amtum-wife, attempts to seal the house and constantly frightens my people?
My father was alive ten years ago, during the plague.
Why did he not seize my father?
Or not deposit memoranda to my father?
Iliš-tikal assembled my father's silver.
Why did he not take from my father's silver, or from the attorney or give a memoranda to Iliš-tīkal?
I and my brothers met five times with him, in Purušḫattum, Ulama, and Waḫšušana!
Why didn't he give us any memoranda?
And how is it that his messages never came?
Nor did he send messages to his representatives with the result that they did not write (anything) down.
Also, his son, Šū-Bēlum, served as caravan driver with me, and he was constantly taking 1 or 2 minas silver in his bundle to him in Ulama and he did not 'ripen' the merchandise in the house of his own father.
Why did he not seize from my silver?
My dear fathers and lords, do you not know that because a robbery occurred when my father died, much silver and my father's tablets were stolen?
My dear fathers, my brothers opened the strongroom five years ago and since then it has been deposed for a matter.
As for my brothers, it is heard they are on the caravans.
But here, I myself have released 1 or 2 minas silver each day at the gate of the market and because of this I am thus made to assemble.
If Ilī-nādā prosecutes the case there, he will claim everything.
My dear fathers and lords, I am the son of a dead man!
I will come to [GN] and we will set [the matter before] the colony so that they will raise [the matter] to the city and [resolve this?].
Here I will clear myself and will come so that we can open my father's strongroom and inspect the tablets and the memoranda and clear the matter for the man, and those that stood at the head of my father here (will) search.
Becasue that silver is his debt, and we wrote him down for that silver, he will not (return)? it to Kalaluwa for him.
A great many of my fathers tablets were stolen!

source_raw: a-na i-dí-a-bi-im ù ku-lu-ma-a qí-bi-ma um-ma PUZUR4-a-šùr-ma šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR a-na SÍG.ḪI.A iš-ta-pu-ku qá-tí qá-at pu-šu-ke-en6 qá-at DINGIR-na-da ù qá-tí i-lá-li-im id-a SÍG.ḪI.A ša É pu-šu-ke-en6 i-ba-ší-ú a-dí a-lá-kà-ni lu ta-áb-ší ta-áš-pu-ra-nim um-ma a-tù-nu-ma KÙ.BABBAR be-lá-num iš-qúl-ni-a-tí-ma na-dí KÙ.BABBAR lá na-dí šu-ma i-dí-a-bu-um i-ḫa-ša-aḫ li-il5-qé šu-ma lá ki-am a-na ṣí-ib-tim dí-na
target_raw:
To Iddin-abum and Kulumaya from Puzur-Aššur:
If they call a pouring silver for wool, deposit the shares--the share of Pūšu-kēn, the share of Ilī-nādā.
The wool which is available in Pūšu-kēn's house, until I arrive, please make available(?)
You (pl.) wrote me.
You (pl.) said, "Bēlānum paid us the silver and it is deposited."
The silver is not deposited.
If Iddin-abum will require it, take.
If it is not so, then take the money on interest.

To Idī-Aššur, Adad-bāni, Aššur-imittī, Ikuppī-Aššur, Puzur-Ištar and Imdī-ilum, from Pūšu-kēn, Puzur-Tiamtum, and Innaya:
With regard to the matter of Šū-Aššur about which you (pl.) wrote, when we read your tablets, we said, "We will not bring in a stranger."
They said, "We will bring someone in."
We remained stuck in these (positions) 10 days, then they went to the colony and they said, "We will bring in the representative(s) of our father."
... tablets ...
... he will pay.
Also(?), they did not deposit anything apart from one talent of silver from the (assets tied up) in short term credit and those with Anatolians (and) smuggled goods of their merchants.
With regard to the goods which came up from the City, we checked his brothers (or partners), and they deposited 9 1/2 talents tin and 150 textiles.
We said, "Where are the letters which you brought from the City?"
They said, "We sent them to him."
They destroyed the copies.
Regarding the wool that he gathered(?) from here, there are no copies.
We made copies of the certified tablets and they were sealed with our seals at the colony office.
We have released the certified tablets with the names of the (merchants?) to his sons.
... 2 piz[..] 7 house servant(s) and the remainder ...
They said, "... ."

To Iddin-Suen, Aššur-nādā and Ennam-Aššur from Kurara:
You borrowed 15 shekels of silver from the house of a merchant.
I paid 12 shekels to Aššur-dān son of Uku, the messenger.
I paid 10 shekels to the son of Puzur-Anna.
In all: you owe 1/2 mina 7 shekels of silver.
Instead of you giving me a corresponding amount, you seize my silver!
If you are truly my father, do not make me angry.
Seal (pl.) the silver.
Send it to me with the first traveler.

To Ikuppia, Ennam-Aššur and Sumi Abiya from Aššur-Nada;
to Ennam-Aššur and Sumi-Abiya:
I entrusted to you 16 half-packs of each 1 talent (and) 7 top-packs of 30 minas, apart from those belonging to Puzur-Ištar, plus 6 black donkeys.
Thereof I sent with you 1 talent 25 minas of copper, that 1 received from Úsur-ša-Aššur, to Ikuppia.
1 talent 12 minas of copper belonging to Ennam-Aššur;
1 talent 20 minas which I balanced with you on behalf of Puzur-Ištar---
the rest of the copper: 15 1/2 talents 3 minas.
Thereof 5 1/2 talents 3 minas of copper belong to the tamkārum.
When the sale has been carried out the tamkārum will pay me 15 112 talents.
And the one who resolved my dispute was Ennam-Aššur, so do not seize anycopper, and without any (authorisation) the tamkārum must not appropriate the 15 1/2 talents ofcopper for which I hold a certified tablet, lest you let it get out of my reach.
As for the rest of the copper, 10 talents from Kunanamit plus the 6 donkeys, I said:
"Come, I shall pay you"-so I trusted you, but you cheated me, saying:
"I shall lay claim to the 10 talents of copper and the 6 donkeys,
turn that into copper of good quality in Durhumit, bring it to Kaneš and your representatives will pay me in full in silver."
I did not payout the copper and the donkeys to you.
Why did you then say:
"I have been paid."
The donkeys belong to Ikuppia!
Release the donkeys to him!
For the rest of your silver I shall personally guarantee for you.
may Aššur and Amurrum, the gods of our fathers, know:
on top of the fact that I guaranteed for your joint-stock capital, it was no stranger who denounced me!
It is you who keep denouncing me in colony after colony!

target_raw:
To Innāya from Šamaš-ṭāb:
On the day that Ikuppīya arrives, he must not stay a single day.
Dispatch him to Waḫšušana.
With regard to my textiles which are there, my dear father and lord, as much the textiles were sold for silver, seal and send the silver to me.
Winter overtook us and the caravan is stalled...
The caravan ...
Your consignment and donkeys are well.
Here the donkeys will dispatch one or two at a time from Ḫaḫḫum to Timelkiya.
... tin ...

source_raw: a-na i-na-a ša ki-ma DAM.GÀR qí-bi-ma um-ma i-ku-num <gap> ù a-šur-lá-ma-sí-ma 20 ma-na LÁ 10 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-šur-ták-lá-ku DUMU i-dí-ku-bi-im ša iš-qú-lá-ni-a-tí-ni a-na té-ga-ra-ma nu-šé-tí-qá-šu-ma i-ba-ší i-na ší-im AN.NA 0.83333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ú-ṣa-ḫe-er um-ma šu-ut-ma i-na kà-ni-iš i-na-a KÙ.BABBAR il5-qé šu-ma tal-qé ki-ma-ma tal-qé-ú-ni tal-qé šu-ma lá tal-qé a-ma-kam i-dí-ku-bu-um li-dí-na-ku-um na-áš-pé-er-tù-šu na-áš-a-ni ša ki-ma 0.83333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ú-ṣa-ḫi-ru li-tí-ik-tám ša KÙ.KI na-áš-a-ni 5 ma-na AN.NA mu-ṭá-e ú-ṣa-ḫe-er mì-li-ik KÙ.BABBAR-pí-kà mì-li-ik
target_raw:
To Innāya, the representative of the merchant, from Ikūnum and Aššur-lamassī:
We sent the 10 minas 50 shekels silver of Aššur-taklāku s. Idī-Kūbum which you weighed out to us off to him in Tegarama and it is there.
He deducted 5/6 mina silver from the price of the tin.
He said, "Innāya took the silver in Kanesh."
If you took it, you took whatever you took.
If you did not take it, make Idī-Kūbum give it to you there.
While his package is being transported, let him ... in compensation for the 5/6 minas silver he deducted.
As for the gold being transported, he deducted 5 minas tin as losses.
Judge the matter of your silver.

target:
To Innāya, the representative of the merchant, from Ikūnum and Aššur-lamassī:
We sent the 10 minas 50 shekels silver of Aššur-taklāku s. Idī-Kūbum which you weighed out to us off to him in Tegarama and it is there.
He deducted 5/6 mina silver from the price of the tin.
He said, "Innāya took the silver in Kanesh."
If you took it, you took whatever you took.
If you did not take it, make Idī-Kūbum give it to you there.
While his package is being transported, let him ... in compensation for the 5/6 minas silver he deducted.
As for the gold being transported, he deducted 5 minas tin as losses.
Judge the matter of your silver.
label: Cuneiform Tablet Prague I 723

To Idnāya, Lā-qēpum, Pilaḫ-Aššur, and Ibni-ilī, from Abela:
I entrusted 15 minas 54 shekels silver under my seal, 16 shekels its shipping charge, to Aššur-šamšī on behalf of Āl-ilī.
There, make sure the representative of Āl-ilī recieves the silver and it's shipping charge.
I gave 5 1/2 minas silver of my own to Aššur-šamšī for purchases.
The seal of the merchant at the opening of the package is marked for Aššur-šamšī.
To Idnāya and Lā-qēpum:
Get the silver and let it come with you within your shipment.
I entrusted 5 shekels silver under my seals for Tatāya my daughter and 3 shekels silver under my seal for Šazua, the daughter of Ennam-Aššur in a package to Aššur-šamšī.
Take the package there and give it to those who go to the city for travel so that they carry the package to the city and give it to Tatāya my daughter and Šazua the daugher of Ennam-Aššur.
Let your message come that your received the silver and the package.

To Imdī-ilum, Pūšu-kēn, Ikūnum, Iddin-abum, and Ḫadāya from Amur-Ištar:
Ikūnum holds 5 minas of my silver for interest.
9 2/3 minas together with its interest he ... to Ḫadāya.
Ḫadāya gave me 4 minas from that amount.
Also, 2 minas (silver) are deposited to Ikūnum and Qarwāya for
Ḫadāya retains the remainder of my silver, 3 2/3 minas.
Though I myself have been looking to him for 3 2/3 minas silver and its interest for 10 years, today I am paying him back 1 minas 10 shekels silver!
As for the matter, as much as Imdi-ilum placed on him, until we see each other, let (Imdī-ilum) (deposit?) it.
Take a sealed document from him.
So that my words are not tread on, gather Qarwiya, Ikūnum and Ḫadāya--all of them--concerning the 2 minas silver.
Let Ikūnum and Qarwiya ... be bound and let each give one mina amongst them (for) 2 minas refined silver and its interest which for 10 years wherever iburani.
My dear brothers and lords do not turn them away.
Do not show them mercy.
Am I not (your) junior?
As for the 10 minas package, m. and š., therein he sold 8 minas to Imdī-ilum.
The remainder is 2 minas.
20 minas metal scraps, 1 sh, x pa-shu-x naqubu - weighing 6 minas, 1 boxwood log, length is 3 in a cubit, and width 1 k., 3 minas of sh, 20 ep from Talḫada, therein,
A weight of 6 minas, 1 (log of)? boxtree.
Its length is within 3 cubits.
Its width is about 1 kabsu.

target:
To Imdī-ilum, Pūšu-kēn, Ikūnum, Iddin-abum, and Ḫadāya from Amur-Ištar:
Ikūnum holds 5 minas of my silver for interest.
9 2/3 minas together with its interest he ... to Ḫadāya.
Ḫadāya gave me 4 minas from that amount.
Also, 2 minas (silver) are deposited to Ikūnum and Qarwāya for
Ḫadāya retains the remainder of my silver, 3 2/3 minas.
Though I myself have been looking to him for 3 2/3 minas silver and its interest for 10 years, today I am paying him back 1 minas 10 shekels silver!
As for the matter, as much as Imdi-ilum placed on him, until we see each other, let (Imdī-ilum) (deposit?) it.
Take a sealed document from him.
So that my words are not tread on, gather Qarwiya, Ikūnum and Ḫadāya--all of them--concerning the 2 minas silver.
Let Ikūnum and Qarwiya ... be bound and let each give one mina amongst them (for) 2 minas refined silver and its interest which for 10 years wherever iburani.
My dear brothers and lords do not turn them away.
Do not show them mercy.
Am I not (your) junior?
As for the 10 minas package, m. and š., therein he sold 8 minas to Imdī-ilum.
The remainder is 2 minas.
20 minas metal scraps, 1 sh, x pa-shu-x naqubu - weighing 6 minas, 1 boxwood log, length is 3 in a cubit, and width 1 k., 3 minas of sh, 20 ep from Talḫada, therein,
A weight of 6 minas, 1 (log of)? boxtree.
Its length is within 3 cubits.
Its width is about 1 kabsu.
Cause Ḫadāya to give all this.

To Imdī-ilum from Uzua:
After I arrived in Kunanamit, and I was far from your breast, may Aššur and Ištar-zat witness that becasue I never recevied heard any of your letters, I (set out) towards you.
I said to myself, " .. so that I may see the eye of my father and lord."
I came down and I gatehred togethre(?) every shekel of silver.
Because I had not heard any of your messages whatsoever, I made my way to the kārum-office.
I am protective of your reputation and wherver I gave a talent for putchases, I am able to raise a 10 shekels rate in silver.
And I have done many favors
Also, I myself here to every shekels of silver which I made I hwl'd
And i will z'b.
My dear father and lord, who (but I) is your true servant?
I will lose from ...
Also, for years(?) I will make every shekel of silver.
When I heard your tablet and Abu-šalim spoke the instructions which you ..., I sent these tablets five time to you so taht you would not return the instructions.
If you desire, you will indeed approach to your breast and I will see your eye.
The packer of Pūšu-kēn which you cleared from being a sāridum hauler, let me set another name before you.
The package worth 10 minas silver, when you sold it on credit to the merchant, give the package worth 10 mians silver and mine so thatI can give from the top of the package which you sold, 10 talents washed copper from Durḫumit to your representative and I will casue the copper to arrive at Purušḫattum and I wil send the silver as much as it fetches to the city and they may make purchases for my package from your house.
Apart from these matters, make amends with me and I every shekel of silver which is in your possession (when I see) as soon as you are deducted, this 'nabritum'' in Kunanamit I will bru it to you your qiptum and whatever I make I will see.
Why is that, with regard to your tablet concerning the transport of Abu-šalim, which I heard/read, 5 talents washed copper toa reliable merchant who is after her every talent of copper which they will weigh out I gave to you concerning this ... I ate.
If these words should have been evil, I would depart for you.
Indeed, let Ištar-pilaḫ hear so that until he is released there I will not klm my bulatia to you.
After he left, ? 10 talents washed copper I opened for you.
15 talents washed copper I will send to you.
Now let him ... you my debt and sell my goods.
And when I see your eye .....
Truly, I am not your servant!
Let Ištar-pilaḫ come with your young man/son, so that I may depart and see your eye.
Why should your eye ... I keep asking.
I hear that my maḫāúm left with Amur-Ištar.
Dispatch him with Ištar-pilaḫ.
The son of Puzur-... ,,,,
Let your message come to the city, to Puzur-Mus and our ladies.

Apart from these matters, make amends with me and I every shekel of silver which is in your possession (when I see) as soon as you are deducted, this 'nabritum'' in Kunanamit I will bru it to you your qiptum and whatever I make I will see.
Why is that, with regard to your tablet concerning the transport of Abu-šalim, which I heard/read, 5 talents washed copper toa reliable merchant who is after her every talent of copper which they will weigh out I gave to you concerning this ... I ate.
If these words should have been evil, I would depart for you.
Indeed, let Ištar-pilaḫ hear so that until he is released there I will not klm my bulatia to you.
After he left, ? 10 talents washed copper I opened for you.
15 talents washed copper I will send to you.
Now let him ... you my debt and sell my goods.
And when I see your eye .....
Truly, I am not your servant!
Let Ištar-pilaḫ come with your young man/son, so that I may depart and see your eye.
Why should your eye ... I keep asking.
I hear that my maḫāúm left with Amur-Ištar.
Dispatch him with Ištar-pilaḫ.
The son of Puzur-... ,,,,
Let your message come to the city, to Puzur-Mus and our ladies.
label: Cuneiform Tablet VS 26 71 (VAT 9301)

target_raw:
To Imdi-ilum and Pūšu-kēn, from Kurūb-Ištar:
As for my merchandise in the transport of Imdī-ilum about which you (pl.) wrote me, you said, "We entrusted what we could. And the remainder, cheap or dear, we will sell and we will send you the silver."
My dear brothers, let you full message come as soon as possible regarding what you gave on credit, and what you sold cheap or dear, (and) as much silver as I have deposited in the colony office.
Ameta brings you 2 1/2 minas exceedingly fine carnelian and a goblet of carnelian under my seal.
If (the going rate) is 6 minas silver or higher, sell the carnelian.
If 1/2 mina pašallum-gold or higher, sell the goblet.
If no sales arise, then do not sell.
As for the carnelian and the goblet, until I come, its heart and tin prices that will make me a little look for(?) and I will borrow an amount around 10 or 15 minas silver and make a purchase.
My brothers either from the previous merchandise or from the price which Ameta brought to you, sell.
Seal the silver and let Būr-Suen bring it at the earliest opportunity.
If Būr-Suen is not available, give it to the first affiliated trader and let him bring it.
If there is not silver from the goods, my dear brothers, give silver from your own funds.
.... let it be a loan to you, and let it be a favor to me.
To Pūšu-kēn:
The one mina carnelian about which you wrote, we received it.
Ameta brings your... to you.

Adad-pilaḫ brought 1 talent 40 minas of tin.
At a 6 shekel rate, their silver is 3 minas 44 1/2 shekels.
The copper was sold for 56 shekels of silver.
Three garments were sold for ... .
In total, your silver ...
... this ... for (?) silver (?) ...

... here ... owes to him(?) ...

To Lipit-Ištar, from Ikkūpiya:
Because of the man you you named for yourself, you yourself gave the young man.
When you dispatched my brothers on the smuggler's route, you entrusted that tin to him.

According to the decision of the plenary assembly of the Kanesh colony, Ennum-Aššur questioned Suen-nādā.
Thus said Suen-nādā to Ennum-Aššur:
I and my servant girl were staying in Kanesh and you went to Durḫumit and acted as though you had authority concerning things that were not owed to you by entering into my guesthouse and pulling out objects and two of my sealed boxes.

In accordance with the tablet stating that the twin brother of Šū-Kūbum owes silver to Aššur-idī, Dagānya will go to Aššur-nādā in Kaneš within a month, and pay.
If he has not paid, he must add interest in accordance with the rule of the colony.
I gave him ...
Witnessed by Alahum, witnessed by Aššur-bēl[- ... ]

target_raw:
Zu Mannumkī’eli’a, Idī’aššur und Zu-mi-ṣa sprich! Folgendermaßen Ilī’ālum: Zu Mannumkī’eli’a sprich!
Was das betrifft, (worüber) ich dich informiert habe, — und du hast mir vertraulich (dazu) geschwiegen — so (sage) ich (jetzt) folgendermaßen: "Silber werde ich kein zweites Mal schuldig werden!"
Zu Mannumkī’eli’a sprich! Wenn er darauf zurückkommt und Aššurmalik irgend etwas, das mich betrifft, sagt, so (sollst) du folgendermaßen (antworten):
"Die Urkunde hat der Sohn des Zuba, der (einer) von den Beisitzern (war), in Verwahrung."
(Hinsichtlich) des (Wertes) des Silbers ist er befreidigt.
Wenn er aber irgend etwas, das mich betrifft, sagt, öffnet meine Urkunde und dann laßt die Urkunde mit dem Siegel des Šūbēlum und des Išūḫum verlesen.
Ferner werde ich ihm über Silber, 1 Seqel, damit er nicht anficht und nicht (Silber) nimmt, bis ich mich mit dem Angestellten des Herren getroffen habe, eine Urkunde ausstellen.
Zu Idī’aššur sprich! Wegen des Silbers des Aššurnāṣir: Das Silber werde ich dir zahlen lassen.
Dein Herz soll nicht (darum) zittern.

To Muza and Pūšu-kēn, to Muza specifically, from Lammaša:
I gave you 3 almonds in bronze, 3 knives, 3 forks, 2 bronze dishes, 2 supānum-bowls, a mamirum, 3 ḫaṣṣinum-axes , a kalappum-ax, 4 arzallum-tools , and a cauldron weighing 20 minas.
My dear sister, entrust them to Pūšu-kēn!
Regarding these goods, they have been with for 8 years.
Separately (there are) 7 thick saddle cloths.
Thereof you gave 3 of the saddlecloths to Peruwa.
The remainder of the saddlecloths went to Arnu-[xxx].
Also, another thing, give the boxes with my seal to Pūšu-kēn!
Give that also (to Pūšu-kēn?).
My dear sister, write to me in your letter to all the ... which you give to him.

silver ... if ... weigh out as a favor to the house of our father.

To Pūšu-kēn, Puzur-Ištar, Ilabrat-bāni, and Idī-īštar from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
We sent the attorney.
And the attorney holds a sealed tablet of the city.
As for the fact that no one must approach anyone either in the city or abroad, they will divide the silver that Šū-Nunu gathered in the city, (here) in the city according to their witnesses.
They will confiscate every shekel received by anyone who makes claims abroad!
We are hearing that you are plundering the men for his goods.
Also, P
You must keep answering the man the mehratim.
Urgent, do not add?take away? anything from the silver!
Let them gather the silver to the city and let your (pl.) representatives take it and wherever there is a single shekels of silver in the city to be sent (?), let your reports be cleared.
To Pūšu-kēn:
As for us, we have no father beside you.
If you are our father, set witnesses on whomever makes a claim on his silver., so that he does (not?) steal silver from him.
Take care to gather the silver and entrust it to the attorney so that he brings it here.
Urgent, do not fear!
Be diligent!
Also, regarding the textiles, the tithe which you credited, which we gave to the son of Šū-Nunu, take care to send me 3 1/3 minas silver in good time(?).
Be diligent!

target:
To Pūšu-kēn, Puzur-Ištar, Ilabrat-bāni, and Idī-īštar from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
We sent the attorney.
And the attorney holds a sealed tablet of the city.
As for the fact that no one must approach anyone either in the city or abroad, they will divide the silver that Šū-Nunu gathered in the city, (here) in the city according to their witnesses.
They will confiscate every shekel received by anyone who makes claims abroad!
We are hearing that you are plundering the men for his goods.
Also, P
You must keep answering the man the mehratim.
Urgent, do not add?take away? anything from the silver!
Let them gather the silver to the city and let your (pl.) representatives take it and wherever there is a single shekels of silver in the city to be sent (?), let your reports be cleared.
To Pūšu-kēn:
As for us, we have no father beside you.
If you are our father, set witnesses on whomever makes a claim on his silver., so that he does (not?) steal silver from him.
Take care to gather the silver and entrust it to the attorney so that he brings it here.
Urgent, do not fear!
Be diligent!
Also, regarding the textiles, the tithe which you credited, which we gave to the son of Šū-Nunu, take care to send me 3 1/3 minas silver in good time(?).
Be diligent!

To Pūšu-kēn, Innāya, and Iliya from Šū-Kūbum; to Innāya
To Innāya:
When you came as
You said, "I have arrived being stretched thin."
You said, "Let me go and see my copies in Kanesh and inspect both mine and Aššur-bāni's šēpum and write you a detailed message. None of your property will be lost."
Set out(?) so that according to the letter which you wrote me, which I hold, the losses, the excise-tax, the tithe which the palace took, and that which you placed on the merchants are credited (to me) and you send the remainder of the textiles, 25 1/2 black textiles.
You wrote me.
You said, "Puzur-Suen and Šū-Suen brought 15 1/2 black textiles to Waḫšušana."
To this day I have been continually writing you, yet you have not responded a word.
Previously, hardly had I myself seized Šū-Suen here, when you wrote me.
You said, "I will receive the silver from Puzur-Suen and send it to you."
silver ... my share ... my share ... I myself brough the silver to you ... you owe
As for you, to whom will you pay the silver?
From the four donkeys you wrote me (in your tablet), "1 donkey died. Three are standing."
Their price is your responsibility, so from the 25 minas tin of my debt, which until Burallim therein, the matter of 15 minas x shekels of silver you gave me.
You said, "I will go and I will have that 10 minas (of silver) paid out and I will send it to you."
You recieved it.
Why did you not send it?
You owe all this: 11 2/3 minas of silver from your previous šēpum.
Ennam-Aššur son of Suen-nādā owes a debt to me.
His term is far past due but when you went (to him), you said, "The man owes me a favor. If he receives anything, then take it as a favor to me."
If it is not so, send as much silver as you can obtain.
If you said, "I didn't receive anything," send a message so that I may sue the man.
My silver, which you took from Zuzu after his term passed, why have you not sent it?
from the (matter?) ... my textiles ... in ...

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn, Ikūppī-Aššur, and Kulumaya from Aššur-mālik:
I wrote to you, "Sell 1 talent of tin and purchase (some) kettles for me."
If you purchased the kettles for me, entrust all the textiles and the kettles to Ilī-wēdāku and let him load the journey to Durḫumit.
Let the tin be deposited in Pūšu-kēn's house.
If you have not yet purchased the kettles, then let the tin - all of it - be deposited in Pūšu-kēn's house.
Until my message arrives, I will look every day toward Purušḫattum.
I will send the silver for the messages with the first traveller.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn, Innāya, and Kilya the yellow one(?) from Šū-Kūbum:
Why is it that you write to me as if I were marked for death and your full report has not come to me for two years?
Previously you wrote to me.
“We received the silver of Apatanānum and since the terms of your merchants draw close, we will give him for interest. We will say, 'Let us send the silver all at once with Šamaš-tappa’ī.'"
The terms of my merchant turned to me already (?).
Indeed, you did send me the silver of Appatanānum with Kiliya.
(But) you did not send the silver of my merchant!
I hear that my textiles from the
He told me here that they sold the textiles in his presence.
Why did you not send the silver?
My dear brothers, take heed to send a detailed message about my assets(?) on credit, in cash, and in my account by express messenger.
I wrote to you (pl.).
I said, "Dispatch my servants as soon as they arrive."
But you (pl.), squandered Maṣi-bēlī and Išar-lu-ballaṭ.
If you are truly my brothers, dispatch Šamaš-tappāʾī, Mannu-kī-abiya and Ataya to me immediately.
Besides them, there is no one to by grain for me.
If you do not dispatch them to me immediately, you will make as angry as if you had lost a talent of silver of mine.
Send me as much as they balanced me at accountings.
Take care to deposit my share.
Will you take pains to make me fear?

target:
To Pūšu-kēn, Innāya, and Kilya the yellow one(?) from Šū-Kūbum:
Why is it that you write to me as if I were marked for death and your full report has not come to me for two years?
Previously you wrote to me.
“We received the silver of Apatanānum and since the terms of your merchants draw close, we will give him for interest. We will say, 'Let us send the silver all at once with Šamaš-tappa’ī.'"
The terms of my merchant turned to me already (?).
Indeed, you did send me the silver of Appatanānum with Kiliya.
(But) you did not send the silver of my merchant!
I hear that my textiles from the
He told me here that they sold the textiles in his presence.
Why did you not send the silver?
My dear brothers, take heed to send a detailed message about my assets(?) on credit, in cash, and in my account by express messenger.
I wrote to you (pl.).
I said, "Dispatch my servants as soon as they arrive."
But you (pl.), squandered Maṣi-bēlī and Išar-lu-ballaṭ.
If you are truly my brothers, dispatch Šamaš-tappāʾī, Mannu-kī-abiya and Ataya to me immediately.
Besides them, there is no one to by grain for me.
If you do not dispatch them to me immediately, you will make as angry as if you had lost a talent of silver of mine.
Send me as much as they balanced me at accountings.
Take care to deposit my share.
Will you take pains to make me fear?
label: Cuneiform Tablet VS 26 67 (VAT 9244)

To Pūšu-kēn, Innāya, and Kiliya from Šū-Kūbum:
My dear brothers, my share from my m. both to accountings and for cash, all that my partners have deposited, you also have deposited and deposit that which is appropriate for accountings for accountings.
Take that which can be taken as cash as cash in silver and send it to me.
My dear brothers, my share from my m. should be deposited together with that of my partners so that no one drinks water off my back.
Take heed of my instructions.
I also am a man of favors.
Wherever needed, I will do a favor for you.

To Pūšu-kēn from Ištar-pilaḫ;
Idī-Aššur brings you 1 mina refined silver.
If the instructions of Bur-Suen come at all, send silver ahead of him.
From the instructions of Bur-Suen; 160 kutānum-textiles, 4 talents tin under seals, 6 Akkadian textiles, he carries.
My dear brother, when you wrote to him himself and they his, then send your own so that they can hide? fine textiles, 50 textiles.
Now, stand at the beginning of the excise so that they bring my goods down from the palace.
Besides you, who do I have?
