target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
You wrote me concerning the 1 mina of amūtum-mineral which Bēlānum and the son of Elāli brought.
You said, "Sell it for gold or silver; do not sell it for copper."
I initiated legal proceedings against Ennam-Aššur in Waḫšušana and I did not sell to him because he would not pay (the?) silver.
I arrived in Puršḫattum and because the muṣium(?) was paying poor quality silver, I said, "Do not sell under any circumstances sell it to just anyone (a commoner?)."
The colony heard and they said, "Until m. do not xxx . Do not sell it to anyone."
There is a verdict of the colony.
I enquired of Ennam-Aššur and Idī-Kūbī.
They said, "Send the amūtam-mineral to Ḫartu."
But you would say "A witness should come to the messengers!" (or?) "He should send it to you!"
I took the amūtum-mineral to the man and afterwards, when he had sold it(?), he said, "Let me refine it."
I said, "I will not give you permission to refine it."
He said, "Let me refine if after you depart so that if it turns for worse, my lord will acquire bad will towards him and me."
He refined the amūtum and 2/3 mina in lumps came up.
Both from the refining and the tithe(?) he/it ... losses of 4 shekels.
I promised you an 8 shekel rate in gold for the rest of the amūtum-mineral.
I said, "It is too little."
...

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur-ma:
My dear father and lord, who else can I trust?
Have the silver paid, as much as I wrote to you in my copy, and cause it to reach me in the City so that it is not your silver (that I need to use(?)).
Have Adada s. Ikuppī-Aššur pay the 5 minas of silver which he promised to pay and also send the 10 minas of silver which you promised.
Dispatch Imgua with the very first traveler.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma PUZUR4-a-šùr-ma a-ma-kam-ma aq-bi-kum um-ma a-na-ku-ma wa-ar-ḫa-am iš-té-en6 lá-as-ḫu-ur-ma ba-ab-tí KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN lu-za-ki um-ma a-ta-ma me-ḫe-er-kà e-ez-ba-ma KÙ.BABBAR ú-ša-áš-qal-ma wa-ar-kà-at-kà ú-šé-ba-lá-kum ù me-eḫ-ra-at URUDU ša É.GAL-lim ù 4 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ṭup-pé-e ḫa-ru-mu-tim 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-na ra-mì-ni-a a-na-dí-ma ú-šé-ba-lá-kum a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta i-ḫi-id-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN ša a-ma-kam e-zi-bu za-ki-ma ú 10 ma-na-e KÙ.BABBAR ša pá-kà ta-dí-na-ni i-dí-ma KÙ.BABBAR ku-nu-uk-ma a-na a-lim{ki} li-ik-šu-da-ma ší-ma-am qá-tí li-iš-a-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN le-li-a-am 5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša a-da-da i-ku-pì-a-šùr ša-qá-lam qá-bi KÙ.BABBAR i-ku-pì-a-šùr ša-áš-qí-il5-šu im-gu5-ú-a lá tù-sà-as-ḫa-ar-šu-ma i pá-né-e-ma a-li-ki-im ṭur4-da-šu-ma li-ik-šu-da-ni É-et-kà ù ṣú-ḫu-ur-kà ša-lim 5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša i li-bi4 i-a-šar a-dí-ú šu-ma lá ú-šé-bi4-lam šu-pur-ma lu-šé-bi4-lá-kum
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
I spoke to you there.
You said, "As for me, I will be delayed one month in order for me to clear every shekel of all my outstanding claims."
You said, "Leave them here for me and I will have the silver paid out and send the silver after you."
Also, I will deposit (arrange)-from my own funds- the proceeds of the copper from the palace and the 4 minas silver from the sealed tablet--10 minas silver and send it to you.
My dear father and lord, take care to clear every (remaining?) shekel of silver there and so deposit the 10 minas silver which you promised me and seal the silver and ship it to the city so that my account can make purchases and I may raise every shekel of silver possible.
5 minas silver which Ikuppi-Aššur promised (subjunctive?) to pay Adada, cause Ikuppi-Aššur to pay the silver.
You must not delay Imgua but dispatch him immediately to the city so that he may catch up to me.
May your house and your little ones be well.
5 minas silver belonging to Ea-šār which I deposited, if he did not send it, write to him so that he sends it.

target:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
I spoke to you there.
You said, "As for me, I will be delayed one month in order for me to clear every shekel of all my outstanding claims."
You said, "Leave them here for me and I will have the silver paid out and send the silver after you."
Also, I will deposit (arrange)-from my own funds- the proceeds of the copper from the palace and the 4 minas silver from the sealed tablet--10 minas silver and send it to you.
My dear father and lord, take care to clear every (remaining?) shekel of silver there and so deposit the 10 minas silver which you promised me and seal the silver and ship it to the city so that my account can make purchases and I may raise every shekel of silver possible.
5 minas silver which Ikuppi-Aššur promised (subjunctive?) to pay Adada, cause Ikuppi-Aššur to pay the silver.
You must not delay Imgua but dispatch him immediately to the city so that he may catch up to me.
May your house and your little ones be well.
5 minas silver belonging to Ea-šār which I deposited, if he did not send it, write to him so that he sends it.
label: Cuneiform Tablet CCT 2 38 (BM 113441)

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma PUZUR4-a-šùr-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR-pí-kà ša šé-ep im-gu5-a AN.NA 16 GÍN.TA ni-iš-a-ma-kum ù me-eḫ-ra-at KÙ.BABBAR ša šé-ep ku-lu-ma-a a-na ṣí-ib-tim ni-il5-qé-ma 16 GÍN.TA AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A ša-mu ANŠE.ḪI.A lá-šu ANŠE.ḪI.A ni-ša-a-ma a-na 5 u4-me šu-IŠTAR ni-ṭá-ra-dam a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta lu-qú-tám ša šé-ep al-be-lí la-ma e-lá-tum e-ru-ba-ni-ni a-li KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN e-li-ú a-ṣé-er ša ki ku-a-tí al-be-lí lu-bi4-il5-ma a-na i-ta-aṭ-lim li-dí-nu a-ki-a-am li-qé lá ú-šu-ru KÙ.BABBAR le-ru-ub-ma AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A lu-uṣ ú 1 GÚ AN.NA ku-nu-ki ù 50 TÚG a-ṣé-er {d}IŠKUR.ANDUL šé-bi4-il5 ù i ṭup-pì-kà <gap>-ra-am šu-pur-šum šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR i ba-áb-tí-a uṣ-a-ma a-na pá-nim šé-bi4-lam ku-lu-ma-a ú-qá-a e-ra-ba-ma a-ta-be-a-ma a-ta-la-kam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
Regarding your silver from the transport of Imgua, we purchased tin for you at a 16 shekel rate.
And(as for?) the corresponding amount, we borrowed silver of Kulumaya's transport on interest and purchased tin at a 16 shekel rate and tin and textiles were purchased, there were no donkeys.
We purchased some donkeys and we will dispatch Šū-Ištar in five days.
My dear father and lord, as for the goods of Āl-Bēlī, before the caravans arrive, wherever a single shekel of silver arises, let the representatives of Āl-Bēlī bring and let them buy with cash.
Receive it (only) in this way.
They must not release.
Let the silver enter in and let tin and textiles go out.
About a talent of tin under my seal and 50 textiles send to Adad-...
Now in your tablet: "Send the ... to him."
If the silver of my outstanding claims comes out, then send it to me immmediatly.
Kulumaya waits.
(When) he comes, I will set out and come.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
On the second day after Ilī-mālik arrived, I wrote you these tablets.
Ilī-mālik brought your silver.
Concerning the tin about which you wrote, tin is at a 13 shekel rate.
Moreover there is not a full package available.
Donkeys and 20 sacks were purchased in the gigamlum.
They will arrive and we will purchase tin and send it to you by the first express.
For silver of my transport, because tin is in short supply, I did not purchase anything.
The tin will arrive, I will make purchases and I will depart with your tin.
Now, as for the purchases of Akkadian-textiles, if (someone) saved some, I will purchase goods worth 1 mina of silver.
About the textiles about which you keep writing me, there is no northern wool.
We purchased 1 heavy textile in the market and we will send it to you.
There are no fine textiles.
I will send to you foodstuffs and ripened dates with Šū-Ištar.
You wrote me.
You said, "Inform me about what you heard from the talk of my investors."
There are no complaints.
Your heart must not bear any concern.
Regarding the matter of Ela: Šū-Ḫubur said, "Why did he send the silver somewhere else?"

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
Instead of sending overly wonderful messages and informing me, you continually send me evasions and curses.
As for me, I will not take Iddin-abum to be an heir.
Also, I will not return a single shekel of silver to him.
Because he will give the tablet to a stranger(?) even I my tablet to my representatives, I give to him.
Today you can see for yourself that the youth is not wise.
I have set it up so that your words may go to his representatives may go (išrat!).
He will not act impudently toward anyone.
I will do so myself.
I wrote to Ikūppī-Aššur.
I took his be
From the silver of Ea-šar that Al-bēlī brought, they want, both for paying the price for purchases and as much as he paid for your share, the silver for the limmum.
I myself gave gold to the limmum.
They made a reckoning with the limmum and they need 3 or 4 minas of silver.
I will pay the silver.
Send silver to your representatives so that they may give it to me.
My dear father and lord, whenever my representatives send you tin or textiles, send them so that they send the silver after you.
Also make sure that Ikūppī-Aššur clears my accounts.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma a-bu-ša-lim-ma a-ša-me-ma a-ma-at a-bi-ni ma-ḫa-i ip-ṭur4 a-na É a-bi-ni e ú-šé-ri-ib-ší-ma ṣa-al-tám É a-bi-ni e iš-ku-un a-bi a-ta a-na É a-bi-ni ú-lá ú-šé-ra-ab-ší a-šar li-bi-šu lu-šé-ší-ib-ší i-na KÁ ḫa-ra-ni-a ú-ra-ni aṣ-ba-at-ma um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-na a-lim{ki} a-lá-ak mì-ma ša ta-da-nim dí-nam um-ma šu-ut-ma ṣú-ḫu-ur-kà ú-ša-kál a-bi a-ta a-na GÉME KÙ.BABBAR 10 GÍN ú 0.33333 ma-na li-dí-in ú a-na-kam a-na-ku a ma-lá té-er-tí-kà ša a-na ša-lim-a-ḫi-im ub-lá-ni e-pu-uš
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Abu-šalim:
I hear that my 'uncle' (maḫā'um) freed the maid of our father.
He must not bring her into the house of our father and cause a quarrel in our father's house.
My dear father, he bring her into the house of our father.
He can send her wherever (else) he likes.
At my departure, I seized Urāni and said to him, "I am going to the City. Give me whatever there is to give."
He said, "I will feed your children."
My dear father, let him give the maid 10 or 20 shekels of silver.
Also, here I have done according to the your message which I brought to Šalim-aḫum.

From the 1 talent 12 minas and 39 shekels of tin which the palace took, 343 minas of copper for one talent went out and they credited to us 410(?) minas.
Thereof, there are 144 minas zalam-ma(??).
The remainder is 168 minas --- 1 2/3 minas of silver at a rate of 6 1/2 shekels.
Total: x minas 10 1/2 shekels (of silver).
Thereof, we deposited 15 shekels (of silver) as the shipping charges.
Dān-Aššur brings you the remainder, 1 mina 56 1/3 shekels of silver under our seals.
I will not sell any silver whatsoever to (the palace) until the exhaustion (of the stockpiles) in the palace.

To Pūšu-kēn from Amur-Ištar:
Ilabrat-bāni sent 4 talents 14 minas of washed copper, 1 talent of Ḫaburatine copper: total - 5 talents 14 minas of copper.
He said, "The copper belongs to Pūšu-kēn."
Thereof, I gave about 5 minas of fine copper to the colony office.
45 shekels were deficient.
The remainder of the copper, 4 talents 8 minas 15 shekel, washed copper, at a rate of 46 shekels silver, its yeilded silver was 5 minas 23 2/3 shekels.
As for the one talent of Haburatāyyum (copper), its silver was 1 mina 5 1/2 shekels.
In total, it was 6 minas 29 1/6 shekels copper.
Thereof, I deposited 6 shekels silver to the accounts of the youth. (?)
As for Adad-bāni, Adad-bāni brings you 6 minas 23 1/6 shekels (of silver).

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma a-mur-IŠTAR-ma a ma-lá na-áš-pí-ir-tí-kà a-na 43 ma-na AN.NA ša na-áš-a-ku-ni ú 9 {TÚG}ku-ta-ni ša iš-tù kà-ni-iš tù-šé-ba-lá-ni um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-wi-lu-um iš-tap-ra-am qá-ta-am lá am-ḫa-aṣ KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ra-mì-ni ak-ta-ba-as-ma ṭup-pí ša 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pí-im IGI a-ší-na DUMU um-me-a-nim al-pu-ut-ma ku-nu-ki-šu ú ku-nu-ki-a šu-lu-ma na-áš-a-kum a-bi a-ta i-ḫi-id-ma TÚG-tí a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum šé-bi-lam a-na-ḫi-id-ma ra-mì-ni ú-za-kà-ma a-na kà-ni-iš a-na ṣé-ri-kà a-lá-kam-ma a-na a-wa-tim ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni i-nu-mì-šu pí-té-a-ší-na ki-ma a-wa-tim a-na ki-tim lá wa-ṣa-i-im mì-ma a-wa-tim ša i na-áš-pé-er-tí-kà lá-áp-ta-ni ú-lá ú-dí-a na-áš-pá-ra-tí-kà ú-kà-al 2 ma-na URUDU a-na qá-tí šu-lu-ma a-dí-in
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Amur-Ištar:
According to your message, regarding the 43 minas of tin which are brought to you and the 9 kutānum-textiles which you sent from Kaneš, I said, "As for me, the man wrote to me. I did not strike(?) the share."
I did my best with about a mina of silver of my own funds and I wrote a tablet which stated '10 minas of refined silver in the presence of Ašina, the affiliated trader' and Šuluma brings it to you under his seal and my seal.
My dear father, take care to send my textiles to Purušḫattum.
I will take care to clear my own funds and come to you in Kanesh so that at that time you will open the tablets referring to the matters about which I wrote you.
Becasue the matters do not 'go out' to justice, I did not inform those matters which were written in your letters.
I hold your messages.
I gave Šuluma 2 minas of copper for expenses.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma a-mur-IŠTAR-ma a-šu-mì AN.NA ša ḫi-na-a ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni DUMU a-wi-lim ú kà-ṣa-ar-šu a-na-kam wa-áš-bu ú-šé-ra-áb-šu-nu-ma ma-aḫ-ri-šu-nu AN.NA ku-nu-ki ša a-lim{ki} ša a-bi4-šu lu-sà-ni-iq-ma ba-tí-iq ú wa-tù-ur a-da-an-ma KÙ.BABBAR ú-šé-ba-lá-kum AN.NA ša É a-šur-ma-lik a-na URUDU ší-ki-im 15 ma-na.TA a-dí-ma URUDU i-ba-ší eb-la-i-ú ma-du-tum i-li-ku-ni-ma URUDU ma-dum i-na É.GAL-lim i-ší-qí-il5-šu-nu-tí-ma KÙ.BABBAR 2.33333 ma-na.TA a-mu-ra-am ú-ta-e-ru a-dí 10 u4-me URUDU-šu i-ga-mu-ru KÙ.BABBAR a-ša-a-ma ú-šé-ba-lam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Amur-Ištar-ma:
Concerning the tin belonging to Ḫinnaya about which you wrote, th(at) affiliated trader and his packer are here.
I deposited the tin belonging to the house of Aššur-mālik for copper at a rate of 15 minas and there was copper available.
But then a great number of Eblaites arrived here and much copper was weighed out to them in the palace and they returned silver at 2 1/2 minas refined.
In 10 days its copper will be exhausted.
I will purchase silver and send it to you.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šur-i-mì-tí ù PUZUR4-a-šur-ma i-na KÙ.BABBAR ša PUZUR4-a-šùr ub-lá-ni 1 me-at 10 TÚG-ta-ni 20 ma-na AN.NA qá-tim 4 ANŠE.ḪI.A ṣa-lá-mu-ú 0.5 ma-na be-ú-lá-tim ša sá-ri-dí-im 2 GÍN lu-bu-šu mì-ma a-nim i-a-šar i-ra-de8-a-kum ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pì-kà nu-za-kà-ma iš-tí wa-ar-ki-ú-tim ni-ša-pá-ra-kum

To Pūšu-kēn from Aššur-imittī and Puzur-Aššur:
From the silver which Puzur-Aššur brought, (we purchased) 110 textiles, 20 minas hand tin, 4 black donkeys, 1/2 mina silver for the be'ulatum of the driver, 2 shekels (silver) for his dressing, and all this Ea-šār transports to you.
We will clear the remainder of your silver and send it to you with the next travellers.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šur-ma-lik-ma i-a-šar ù {d}IŠKUR-ṣú-lu-li-ma URUDU ša PUZUR4-a-šur i li-bi i-a-šar i-šu-ú URUDU ta-áš-bi-ta-am i-dí-ni-a-tí ki-ma URUDU-i-ú i-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum ma-aḫ-ṣú-ni i-ri-qám lá ni-gu5-ur-ma a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum lá nu-šé-ri-ib e-ma-ri-i šé-ri-a-ma URUDU-ú-šu a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum lu nu-šé-ri-ib šu-ma ṭup-pu-šu ša PUZUR4-a-šur a-ṣé-ri-ni i-li-kam ší-ta-me-a-šu-ma a-ni-ša-am šé-bi4-lam i ša-am-ší ṭup-pì-ni ta-ša-me-ú e-ma-ri šé-ri-am
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Aššur-mālik, Ea-šār, and Adad-ṣulūlī:
The copper which Ea-šar owes Puzur-Aššur, he (Ea-šar) has given us the full payment of copper.
Because the (supply of) copper is cut off in Purušḫattum, we were unable to rent a wagon and we were unable to take (the copper) to Purušḫattum.
Send donkeys so that we can take the copper to Purušḫattum.
If Puzur-Aššur's tablet comes for us, read it and send it here.
On the day you read our tablet, send the donkeys.

Uzua son of Idī-Aššur brings you 6 minas lapis in stone block form under my seal.
My dear father and lord, sell it to merchants or to anyone you can and cause the silver to come to the city for me.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Aḫu-waqar:
On the day after Šū-Bēlim son of Šalim-Aššur left, Ennam-Aššur brought my loaded donkeys (back?) from the watchpost.
He said, "Give me the silver, the price of the goods which I entrusted to you so that Pūšu-kēn comes and negotiates/discusses with me."
I said, "The silver which he brought to Kaneš came and you and Pūšu-kēn discuss and if the silver returns to the property of Pūšu-kēn, let him take it, and if it returns to your property, you (will) take it."
He said, "Let Pūšu-kēn come and let him discuss with me."
The man holds my fringe.
And he is repeatedly giving me to the guarantors.
My dear father and lord, when you read my tablet, take a strong tablet from the colony office so that I may get up and come.
I must not be detained in my journey!

Regarding the 8 minas silver and its interest which we owe to the house of Al-āḫum, we borrowed 6 minas silver on interest and we paid it to the son of Qayyātum.
Our dear father, to whom else can we turn?
Cause silver to be sealed and send it here.
Our dear father, to whom else can we turn?
If anyone asks you for silver from my youth, saying, "Give me my possession!", do not give him anything.
Also say to ?Alama?, "We have the tablet of the city."
Fulfill the things which are not yet arrived (but)? owed ???
... give it (f.) ... the cheif of the city ...

Send out 5 good black donkeys which are strong enough for carriage and their gear with Kuṣiya so that I can send ahead of me both the 10 minas copper and k. which I will make so I can reach you while you are still staying there.

On the day that the merchandise exits the palace, dispatch my merchandise with the first traveller.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn, from Ikūnum and Kurub-Ištar:
Concerning the goods of the house of the merchant that we ... to you, you sent the .. of the silver.
We both added interest and deducted silver, and the merchants ... 1 mina 22 1/3 shekels silver in our (accounts?) and our tablet they(?) opened and the silver for interest we recieved and from the month of Kuzalli for the interest will go to us.
Send the 1 minas 22 1/3 minas silver and its interest for the month of Kuzalli.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Kulumaya and Kurub-Ištar
From Kulumaya
As soon as the merchandise of Dān-Aššur ...
I spoke to you.
I said, "Here are the 6 kutānum-textiles for Šāt-Aššur."
The textiles do not belong to Šalim-aḫum's daughter; rather, the textiles belong to Kurub-Ištar.
My name is on the fringe of the textiles.
Ilabrat-bāni is not informed.
They wrote a tablet of the merchant for Dān-Aššur.
His textiles, they are those from Kurub-Ištar.
To Pūšu-kēn from Kurub-Ištar:
My dear father, my dear lord, take heed to give the textiles to Šalim-Aššur.
If he does not want to accept them, speak to him before two witnesses and send your message.
Also, the textiles which Dān-Aššur owes, are to my name.
Send the textiles (brought by) Qarwiya's packer and the tin of Amur-Aššur son of Sukkalliya.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Kulumaya:
1 talent tin and its excess weight, 5 kutānum-textiles, under the seal of Šū-Ištar son of Dadānum, and 1/2 black donkey; all this Irma-Aššur drives to you.
From our awītum assestment whatever dātum payments, food charges and wages of the sāridum he is not informing us.
My dear father and lord, sell my tin, as much as arrives, for silver, and send the silver ahead of me.
If my tin is not sold, send 2 minas silver ahead of me and your message know from what day you sent it.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Kulumāya:
Ikūppīya brought 5 minas silver and 2/3 minas gold under your seal.
Your silver was sold at a rate of 1/3 shekels (silver).
Its silver is 4 minas 53 shekels.
You wrote.
You said, "Kuziziya brings you 15 minas 37 1/2 shekels silver, its excise added. Weigh it out to the price of the houses of Issu-rīk's son and pull out my tablet and put it among my tablets in the storeroom."
Your representatives and Šalim-aḫum opened the package under your seal in front of 5 merchant-class individuals and they took the excise and the cut(?) the remainder of the package and there was 14 minas 37 shekels and 1 minas silver was deducted against your documentation.
There, you must not (disagree?) with the weighing.
Because Ilabrat-bāni issi'd 1 mina silver as losses, and weighed out 16 minas silver to us, and we set witnesses for us for an amount of 17 minas silver and we took it as 17 minas according to the shipment of Ilabrat-bāni, (?) you wrote.
You said, "I will send you 8 minas silver next."
When the maatum caravan u-x-x-ni, we received at the house of the merchant the 7 1/2 minas silver as the equivalent of 8 minas.
Altogether, your silver is 33 minas 24+x shekels.
Thereof, Kulumaya brings 350 kutānum-textiles-they cost 20 1/2 minas silver,14 donkeys - their cost was 3 minas 14 shekels (silver), their feed was 2/3 mina (silver), 1 talent 5 minas hand tin at a rate of 14 shekels - its silver was 4 minas 37 shekels, 5 shekels for sa'itum(?)- 1.5 minas capital payment for 3 packers, 6 shekels for their outfitting, 16 1/2 shekels for the exit tax, 3 shekels losses from the purification, 1 1/2 shekels for the outfitting of Kulumaya, and 10 1/2 minas tin separately
His silver (hire) is 45 shekels.

To Pūšu-kēn from Kuzallim:
Say to P
I owe 20 minas of silver to Enna-Suen son of Šū-Ištar and I set his share at 15 minas of silver to the city.
I owe 20 minas of silver to Enna-Suen son of Šū-Ištar
I set his share at 15 minas of silver to the city
I dispatched copper worth 20 minas of silver two months ago to Purushattum.
At the departure of the silver, I will send his/corresponding silver.
Inquire there if Rab-Aššur will go with the caravan as the transporter of my goods.
Here the colony owes 213 textiles to the palace and they (the colony) ask it daily of us.
26 textiles will fall to Hinnaya and myself.
There, send the 26 textiles for the share from Hinnaya's fund.
Do not send from my own share.
(But) do send (the textiles).
If Enna-Suen's representatives retain my goods, pay them back for the silver.

To Pūšu-kēn from Kurub-Ištar:
Concerning Šalim-Aššur son of Issu-rīk about whom I spoke to you here, Imgua arrived and you did not send anything whatsoever.
Amur-Ištar son of Sukkallīya brought you 10 minas tin.
My aunt(?) brought Akkukum, the packer of Qarwīya, 6 kutānum textiles.
My dear father and lord, do I have another father beside you?
There, inquire of Šalim-Aššur and Wābilanni and send to me as to whether or not they sold it to him, so that at their departure I do not keep opening my mouth.
Concerning the matter of Atata, why do you not inform me?

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
Kulumaya brings you 9 textiles.
Idī-Suen brings you 3 textiles.
Dān-Aššur brought 2 bronze spoons.
Ea-šār brought 1 spoon made of bronze, and a container of bronze.
Idī-Suen brought 2 containers of bronze.
Aḫu-waqar brought 1 container of bronze.
Ikūppiya brought 1 container of silver.
The son of Ibbī-Sua brought 1 supānam of silver.
They brought here cups, spoons, and interest(?).
If you are my lord, do not be angry about the textiles which you wrote that I did not send to you.
Because the girl is growing up, she? took one or two textiles for riding.
And I (must) also make (textiles), for the members of the house and for the little ones.
I was not finished and so did not send the textiles to you.
They will finish as many textiles as we can.
I will send them to you with the next travellers.
As for the house of Abum-ilī, Ikūn-pīya put his wall from the house of Attaya on your wall!
Now as for me, I quarreled (with him) and he said, "I will enter into a partnership with him. Or I will not cooperate with him whatsoever."
Get up and go out!
You yourself speak to him there.
Thus you will say, "Why did you set your wall in my house? You did it when the house was not aširim!'
Also, concerning the beer about which you wrote, "Tell (me) about the beer."
It's made.
It's finished.
Now, the girl has quite grown up.
Get up and come here!
Place her in the lap of Aššur.
Now get going! (lit. Take the foot of your going!)

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma lá-ma-sí-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1.5 ma-na ša ig-ri É-bé-tim ša DINGIR-ma-lik i ba-ab ḫa-ra-ni-kà ta-am-ší-ma i-na ta-aḫ-sí-is-tí-kà ú-lá ta-al-pu-ut mu-zi-zu-kà il5-ta-qé-ú-šu i-na 70 BANEŠ ša li-bi ab-ba-a-im <gap> 20 BANEŠ še-am im-du-ud ší-tí še-im i-ma-da-ad-ma té-er-tum i-lá-kà-kum a-pu-tum i-ḫi-id-ma pu-ùḫ a-ni-ki ša ṣú-ḫa-ri ša ta-áp-ṭù-ru KÙ.GI šé-bi4-lam-ma a-ni-ki lá-áš-ku-šu-nu-ma li-ba-šu-nu lá i-lá-mì-in KÙ.GI ša bu-za-zu ša té-zi-bu ḫi-tí a-na a-ḫa-ḫa e-pu-uš a-na mu-zi-zi-kà lá ta-ša-lá-aṭ ṣú-ḫa-ra-am ṭur4-dam-ma ip-ra-am lu-pá-ḫe-er
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
You forgot the roughly 1 1/2 minas silver for the rent of the house of Ilī-malik at the beginning of your journey and you did not write it in your memorandum.
Your representatives took it.
From the 70 sūtum-measures in the possession of Abbāʾim, he measured out 20 sūtum-measures.
He will measure out the rest of the barley and the message will come to you.
Urgent, take care to send the gold, the yeild of my tin (brought by) the lads which you opened, so that I may set my tin to them and not disappoint them.
The gold belonging to Buzāzu which you left, fashion it into ḫittum-objects for Aḫaḫa.
Do not act high-handedly with your representatives.
Dispatch the lad so that he gathers the grain ration.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma lá-ma-sí-ma li-mu-um šu-da-a KÙ.BABBAR e-ri-ša-ni-ma um-ma šu-ut-ma ša wa-ṣí-tí-šu KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ḫa-bu-ul KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na 10 GÍN ša a-na li-mì-im bu-zu-zu tù-šé-bi-lá-ni a-na li-mì-im šu-da-a <gap> 1 ma-na a-ša-qal 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša tù-šé-bi-lá-ni ú-ra-ni ú-lá i-dí-nam um-ma šu-ut-ma 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-dí-nam a-na pá-šu-ri-im pá-šu-ra-am ú-lá i-dí-nam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
The limmum-official Šudāya requested silver and he said, "He owes about a mina of silver for exit taxes."
I will pay out the 1 minas 10 shekels (silver) which you would pay to the limmum-official Buzuzu to the limmum-official Šudāya as the 1 mina.
Urani did not give the 5 shekels silver which you sent.
He said, "He gave me 5 shekels of silver, but he did not give permission to release it.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma lá-ma-sí-ma ta-ša-me-ma ta-ni-iš-tum il5-té-mì-in a-ḫu-um a-na a-ḫi-im a-na ḫa-lá-tim i-za-az ku-ta-bi-it-ma ù al-kam-ma ku-ur-sí-kà pá-ri-ir ṣú-ḫa-ar-tám a-na sú-un-a-šùr šu-ku-un i-na a-lim{ki} SÍG.ḪI.A wa-aq-ra-at i-nu-mì KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ta-ša-kà-na-ni i-na qé-ra-áb SÍG.ḪI.A šu-uk-nam KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ša wa-ṣí-tí-kà ša tù-šé-bi4-lá-ni mu-ṣí-um e-ri-šu-ni-ma a šu-mì-kà pá-al-ḫa-ku-ma ù-lá a-dí-in um-ma a-na-ku-ma li-mu-um li-ru-ba-ma É-bé-tám li-pu-ug a-ḫa-at-kà <gap> GÉME a-na ší-mì-im ta-dí-in-ma a-na-ku a-na 14 GÍN áp-ṭù-ur-ší ša-lim-a-ḫu-um 2 šé-na É-be-té-en iš-tù a-ta tù-uṣ-ú e-ta-pá-áš né-nu-ma a-na ma-tí né-pá-áš TÚG ša a-šur-ma-lik i-na pá-ni-tim ub-lá-ni KÙ.BABBAR mì-in lá tù-šé-ba-lam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
Each man stands prepared to swallow his neighbor.
Place the girl on the lap of Aššur.
Wool is scarce in the city.
When you set a mina silver for me, put it within the wool.
The mina of silver for your exit tax which you sent me, the exit regulators demanded it, and fearing for your reputation I did not give it.
Your sister sold a maid but I liberated her for 14 shekels.
Šalim-aḫum had built two houses before you left.
As for us, when will we build?
As for the textiles which Aššur-mālik took earlier, why have you not sent the silver?

To Pūšu-kēn from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
Our dear father and lord, the silver of the ikrībū-funds, and that is there, and from the tin which Išar-bēlī owes to you, gather and seal and send it here.
If you will withhold silver from your cargo, when we purchase tin here, we will (have to) borrow four minas silver on interest.
Do not increase (your debt of) silver on interest.
Seal it and send it here.
We would prefer do kindness to you rather than anyone else.

To Pūšu-kēn from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
Concerning the silver of the votive fund, here you saddened Bēlātum.
From the utensils and that among the eṭṭamē, ...
Our dear father and lord, do not release the silver to the youths.
Concerning the mazzāzum the god will do something evil to our fathers's house.
Urgent, if you are our father, gather together as much as you can, and place your name on it so that the youth(s) can in this make a claim against the votive funds.
Urgent, none of the youth should ... nor take a single shekel of silver.
We heard that Išār-bēlī brought you 1 donkey and Uṣur-ša-Aššur departed.
He must not mṣi nor take a single shekel!
If Išār-bēlī had not yet arrived, send word so that whatever goods you own enter before him from the donkeys.
When the textilesare sold, seal and send the silver so that ... altogether ....
Our dear father, without you we have no one.

To Pūšu-kēn from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
The daughters of Puzur-Ištar and Uṣur-ša-Aššur are ill and seem as though they are dying!
We went to the diviners and thus says the god: "You do not respect the votive offerings. ... them."
We are preparing for the harvest!
Ask the youth whether he(?) will ... or not and let your message come so that we can ... them.
Either the price of the tin or of the textiles, seal the silver and send it here.
The ten textiles which we gave to the attorney, have the silver paid and send it here.
We gave 1 textile to Aššur-imittī and Šū-Ištar and the sent it to you in the caravan of Innāya (and) Idī-Suen.
We requested silver of Šū-Išrat and he said, "Idī-Suen gave that textile to Pūšu-kēn."
Send here the silver, the yeild of the one textile.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Adad-bāni:
I siezed Ennam-Aššur and I said to him, "The silver as much as you promised to the man saying, 'We will indeed bring the silver to the man.'"
He said, "Do not worry for another 10 days. Let me settle accounts with my brother so that he sends me my copper so that I may answer the share of Ḫinnaya and the representatives of Dān-Aššur and fulfill it from my own funds."
I said, "At your departure(!) either 5 minas of silver or about 10 talents of silver we will weigh out to the representatives of my investor/principal.
"Why would I write to my principal?"
He said, "About 20 minas of silver, the amount which I promised him, I will go and I will pay."
I said, "Let me write to my principal that you will acquire the silver."
He said, "I will acquire it, write (to him)."
I wait for this ???
As for the copper about which you wrote the merchant, 10 1/2 talents of fine copper were placed in the house of Amur-Ištar.
Aššuriš-tikal took possession of it and he said, "I will depart on my journey in two days."

To Pūšu-kēn from Adad-bāni:
I siezed Ennam-Aššur and I said to him, "The silver as much as you promised to the man saying, 'We will indeed bring the silver to the man.'"
He said, "Do not worry for another 10 days. Let me settle accounts with my brother so that he sends me my copper so that I may answer the share of Ḫinnaya and the representatives of Dān-Aššur and fulfill it from my own funds."
I said, "At your departure(!) either 5 minas of silver or about 10 talents of silver we will weigh out to the representatives of my investor/principal.
"Why would I write to my principal?"
He said, "About 20 minas of silver, the amount which I promised him, I will go and I will pay."
I said, "Let me write to my principal that you will acquire the silver."
He said, "I will acquire it, write (to him)."
I wait for this ???
As for the copper about which you wrote the merchant, 10 1/2 talents of fine copper were placed in the house of Amur-Ištar.
Aššuriš-tikal took possession of it and he said, "I will depart on my journey in two days."

To Pūšu-kēn from Asqudum:
Šamaš-bāni your transporter brings you 3 1/2 minas silver.
My dear father and lord, do you not constantly hear?
Because no purchases for fine copper are available, and the palace is paying silver for mercantile goods, Šū-Ištar s. Luzina acted on behalf of my merchant and sold.
I acted on your behalf.
The colony set our due date for depositing the matter toward there, and our term is one month late.
I will come with the next travelers.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šū-Ištar:
Silver is scarce in Waḫšušana.
I siezed Šū-Kūbum and he sent (some?) to Šalatuwar.
He said, "Let my servants arrive, then I will pay you the silver."
His servants arrived, I caused him to pay (the silver), and I send it to you.
Ea-šār did not want to pay the silver (he owed).
He said, "In ten days I will clear my own funds and I will personally go."
I spoke to him there.
I said , "May I cause you to stand for it after that."
You had said, "Do not allow him to stand later. He does not want to pay the silver."
My dear father and lord, write so that they sell fleeces in Luḫusattiya so that they send the silver ahead of you.
Let your message come as to how much silver they send to you.
Do not give a single shekel of silver to the sons of Amur-Ištar.
They sold my lapis and took the silver.

To Pūšu-kēn, thus Šū-Ištar:
I am setting out from Širmuin.
In addition to the silver of the transport of Kulumaya and Imkuwa, seventeen donkeys go with me: (carrying) 10 talents 50 minas tin under seal and 300 kutānum textiles together with 10 fine textiles.
I will select one hundred textiles in Ḫaḫḫum, fine (ones).
I will exhaust every 'available' shekel of silver and I will arrive.
Let your instructions come quickly to me.
Inform me.
Also, as for myself, my ten fine textiles which I am carrying to you. inform me.
Send two minas silver ahead of me.
I will take care to arrive (in time).

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šū-Kūbum:
My dear brothers, my share in addition to that of Innāya which they deposited--you yourselves deposit my share from my
Do not pay out anything and so disappoint me.
Sell my merchandise wherever it is profitable and send off the silver to me.
Now, as for the silver owed by Ememe, why have you not sent it to me until this day?
Also, clear the son of Ibnili and send me a message.
Why is it that Nab-Suen said, “Šū-Kūbum took my silver.”
Instead of laying claim in the city, I dropped the matter.
Now, the silver which Kiliya(?) set in the share of Nab-Suen—until Kiliya camethe silver was available, then Kiliya came.
Kiliya said to the representatives of Nab-Suen, “If you make a claim, I will go to the city (assembly).”
Kiliya took the silver.
Now, they sealed Saliya’s tablet. (???)
I didn’t receive anything.

target:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šū-Kūbum:
My dear brothers, my share in addition to that of Innāya which they deposited--you yourselves deposit my share from my
Do not pay out anything and so disappoint me.
Sell my merchandise wherever it is profitable and send off the silver to me.
Now, as for the silver owed by Ememe, why have you not sent it to me until this day?
Also, clear the son of Ibnili and send me a message.
Why is it that Nab-Suen said, “Šū-Kūbum took my silver.”
Instead of laying claim in the city, I dropped the matter.
Now, the silver which Kiliya(?) set in the share of Nab-Suen—until Kiliya camethe silver was available, then Kiliya came.
Kiliya said to the representatives of Nab-Suen, “If you make a claim, I will go to the city (assembly).”
Kiliya took the silver.
Now, they sealed Saliya’s tablet. (???)
I didn’t receive anything.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma šu-pu-ul-tum-ma ì-lí-ba-ni iš-tù iš-tí ú-ṣú-ur-ša-a-šùr uš10-bu ar-bi-i-šu ḫa-ra-nam i-li-kam 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-na bé-ú-lá-tí-šu ú-lá al-qé ú-lá šu-ut i-na lá ma-ṣí um-mì-a-ni-šu KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN ú-lá ir-ší ú-lá a-na-ku KÙ.BABBAR 5 GÍN ú-lá áb-lá-aṭ šu-ma a-bi a-ta bé-ú-lá-tí-šu ta-e-er-ma a-na ṣé-ri-kà ṭá-ḫi-šu ṣú-ḫa-ru-um li-il5-qé-šu-ma ṣú-ḫa-ri-kà sí-ni-iq-šu bé-ú-lá-tí-šu a-na qá-tí-šu lá tù-ša-ar i-ga-ma-ar a-dí qá-sú ta-mu-ru-ni šu-ma da-mì-iq ú da-mì-iq-ma a-na ḫu-bu-li-a bé-ú-lá-tù-šu li-tù-ra-am
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šubultum:
Ilī-bāni, after he stayed with Uṣur-ša-Aššur, arrived on four trips.
I did not take even 5 shekels silver from his capital fund.
It was not him.
When it was not sufficient, his investor did not redeem for him even a single shekel of silver.
It was not me.
I did not live on a mere 5 shekels silver
My dear father, return his capital fund and draw him near to you.
Take the youth.
Supervise him as your youth.
Do not return his capital fund to his account.
He will spend it off.
Until you see his account, if it is all well and good, let his capital fund return to my debt.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma um-ma ḫu-sà-ru-um-ma 2 GÚ 11 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ku 40 ku-ta-nu qá-dum li-wi-tim 2 ANŠE.ḪI.A ṣa-lá-mu 17 ma-na AN.NA qá-tim mì-ma a-nim šu-ku-bu-um DUMU a-li-li i-ra-dí-a-kum ki-ma ú-nu-tum i-na É.GAL-lim ur-da-ni u4-ma-kál šu-ku-bu-um lá i-sà-ḫu-ur a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum a-ṣé-er ša ki-ma ku-a-tí ù a-šùr-DUG DUMU a-lá-ḫi-im lu-qú-tí šu-ku-bu-um lu-šé-ri-ib-ma lu-qú-tí ba-tí-iq ù wa-tù-ur a-na i-ta-aṭ-lim li-dí-nu na-ḫi-id-ma a-na DAM.GÀR lá ú-šu-ru šu-ku-bu-um iš-tí-šu-nu li-zi-iz-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR lu-qú-tí lu-ta-i-ru a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta a-lá-nu-kà a-ba-am ú-lá i-šu a-na té-er-tí-a i-ḫi-id-ma KÙ.BABBAR a-na DUMU um-mì-a-nim pí-qí-id-ma šu-ku-bu-am KI KÙ.BABBAR ṭur4-dam i-ṣé-er šu-ku-bi-im en6-kà li-li-ik
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Ḫuṣārum:
2 talents 11 minas tin under seal, 40 kutānum-textiles together with wrappings, two black donkeys, 17 minas hand tin--all this Šū-Kūbum son of Āl-ilī brings to you.
When the merchandise come down from the palace, do not detain Šū-Kūbum for a single day.
Let Šū-Kūbum take my goods to Purušḫattum, to your representative and Aššur-ṭāb son of Al-āḫum, and let them sell my goods-high price or low-for cash.
Take care that they not release it to a merchant.
Let Šū-Kūbum assist them so that they convert my merchandise to to silver.
My dear father and lord, beside you I have no father!
Heed my instructions and entrust the silver to an affiliated trader and dispatch Šū-Kūbum with the silver.
Keep your eye on Šū-Kūbum.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Ḫusārum;
Šū-Kūbum brings to you 26 textiles, 1 donkey and its gear.
As many textiles as they clear to me, thereof, give 10 textiles to Al-ābum.
Give the remainder of the textiles to Kurub-Ištar.
As for the donkey, place it with my previous goods.
My dear father, take care to send the proceeds from my previous goods with the very first traveller.
Now, do not delay Šū-Kubum.
Despatch him.
From my previous textiles, give 1 textile to Šū-Kubum.
Despatch Abī-ṭāb.

To Pūšu-kēn from Ḫusārum:
If you are my father, when he brings my letter to you, give him 3 shekels from my silver and do not delay him 5 days.
Dispatch him with the express caravan.
Sell the textiles and tin, cheap or dear, and send me the silver in the first caravan.
My dear father, besides you I have no other father!

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Ilī-mutabbil and Ennam-Aššur:
From the 4 'talents' tin under seals from the city which Ilī-mutabbil brought, we 'cleared' and he tāru to Abi-ilamdim at a rate of 1 1/2 minas.
Thereof, we weighed out 52 shekels to the colony office as the entry tax.
(As for) the remainder of your tin, your tin cleared 4 talents 5 minas 8 shekels.
Your tin is sold at a rate of 9 shekels 22 1/2 grains (per shekel silver).
Its (yield in) silver was 26 minas 51 5/6 shekels.
From the 6 šurum-textiles and kutānum-textiles that were wrappings, thereof I gave 3 šurum-textiles and x kutānum-textiles to Al-aḫum according to your instructions.
The remainder of your textiles are 2 šurum-textiles.
They are sold for 39 minas copper.
Thereof, I weighed out 32 minas for a kutānum-textile and deposited it in your account in the colony office.
The remainder of your copper, 7 minas copper, are with me.
... Akkadian textiles, which I took .. are sold.
... eribti ... which went to the colony office, we gave.
20 minas liti silver?
The remainder of your copper is with Lulu.
They cleared 18 talents 45 minas.
At a rate of 1 minas 45 shekels, its silver is 10 minas 42 5/6 shekels.
In all, your silver is 38 minas 30+ shekels.
Now, as for the 1 talent 4 minas copper, Aššur-imittī brings to you thereof 10 minas silver.
Ahuqar brings you 11 minas 3 1/3 shekels silver.
Idi-Šamaš brings you 12 minas silver.
Separately 5 minas 38 shekels silver .... x minas silver are in the hands of Idi-Šamaš.
x minas are to the account of Idi-Šamaš.
In sum, 38 minas 45 1/3 shekels silver goes to the two of us.
And 41 minas 40 shekels is with Ilī-mutabbil.

... (lady?) ...
We said, "We will make purchases and I will send to you(f.)."

To Pūšu-kēn from Puzur-Aššur:
Concerning theamūtum-mineral and lapis about which you wrote, the -office will not sell them.
There was nothing at your departure.
You wrote me.
You said, "Take 10 minas of silver from the cargo of Ilī-mālik. Also, take 25 minas of silver from the proceeds of the cargo of Šū-Ištar, your copper and that of the merchants and take the remainder of the silver for that of my account."
Send 10 minas of silver so that I can make a reckoning with your representatives here so that if they took your silver we can deposit in your silver.
You wrote me.
You wrote, (saying) "Add 2 minas of gold belonging to the lady to my account."
In the tablet, may the writing of the lady's name not be retained.
Let your message go to her.
My dear father and lord, clear the silver, tin, and textiles both as to that of Ea-šar and of my outstanding claims and send them to me so that he may make purchases for my account.
If my claims persist as outstanding, send 10 minas of silver so that he may take it when my assets go out, and as much as their term period ...
... know.
... available ...

To Pūšu-kēn from Aššūr-imittī and Puzur-Aššur:
Imgua brings you 12 minas lapis under our seals.
It is worth 6 minas 11 shekels silver.
As for the tithe of the colony office, you said, "When they are sold, give them the silver as tithe. If they do not agree with you, let them assess the stone as broken apart, because it is broken apart."
They will not put a chisel on it.
The stone will go to/with Aḫaya.
If you did not sell it there, send it to Amur-Ištar and Abu-šalim.
Also, inform them that they will asess as broken it at their sale.
Let them send the silver, its revenue to you.

To Pūšu-kēn from Aššur-mālik:
You wrote me.
You said, "The copper from your account has been seized."
I paid to him 14 talents of tin three months ago.
As for myself and Šumi-abiya, on the day that I heard your letter, the man's son paid the certified agent(s) of the man and I said, "Your father is my agent."
As for me, copper and silver as much as their are, 8 talents of fine copper, 4 minas and 45 shekels of silver, we seized and we sealed them with seal of Šumi-abiya and my seal and deposited them in my inn.
He purchased merchandise worth 6 minas silver and (the merchandise) was seized.
They sent it for its release.
Now, they will go and we will seize (the goods).
4 talents of fine copper is deposited in my inn at Ulama.
Now, we will write to them and we will seize him.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi4-ma um-ma a-šùr-ma-lik-ma i-na ḫa-am-ší-im u4-mì-im ša e-ru-bu a-wi-lá-tum ša É a-šùr-ma-lik a-lam im-ḫu-ra-ma a-lúm{ki} dí-nam i-dí-in-ma lu-qú-tám ša 30 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ik-ri-bi4 ša a-šùr-ma-lik ma-ma-an iṣ-bu-tù ú-šar ù ša il5-qé-ú ù-ta-ar lá ša ú-ší-ru ù lá ša ú-ta-e-ru i-ša-ri-iq-šum ṭup-pá-am da-nam ša a-lim{ki} ni-il5-qé ù ra-bi4-ṣa-am né-ḫu-úz kà-ru-um e-mu-uq-šu a-na lá 10 u4-me ḫa-mu-uš-tum ša té-i-na-tim ra-bi4-ṣú-um ù ì-lí-ba-ni ú-ṣú-nim ù a-na šu-mì ša DINGIR-mu-ta-bi4-il5 a-li-ki ak-šu-ud-ma mì-šu iš-tí a-li-ki ú-la i-lá-ak ú-ša-il5-ma a-na tí-me-el-ki-a i-ta-lá-ak iš-tí a-ḫi-šu a-na TÚG.ḪI.A ša tí-me-el-ki-a i-ta-am-gàr šu-ma a-na a-lim{ki} i-tal-kam a-na ma-lá ša tù-na-ḫi-dí-ni a-na-ku-ma a-wa-tám a-da-šum lá ta-na-ḫi-da
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Aššur-mālik:
On the fifth day after I arrived, the ladies of the house of Aššur-mālik approached the city council and the city rendered a verdict that whoever seized the goods worth 30 minas of silver belonging to the votive fund of Aššur-mālik must release them.
Now, whatever he took he will return.
He will have stolen from him whatever he does not release or return.
We received a certified tablet from the city.
And we acquired an attorney.
The colony is his authority.
Within 10 days, during(?) the ḫamuštum-official of Teinātum, the attorney, and Ilī-bāni will depart.
Now, with regard to the matter of Ilī-mutabbil, my traveller has arrived and will he not go with my traveler?
I made inquiry and he departed for Timelkiya.
He agreed with his brother for textiles of Timelkiya.
If he departs for the city, I will give to him whatever you instruct me.
Will you not give me instruction?

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi4-ma um-ma a-ḫu-qar PUZUR4-a-šùr ù dan-a-šùr-ma ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma ša 33 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR AN.NA ša-ma-nim eq-lúm li-iṣ-ba-sú ba-at-qú-um ù wa-tù-ru-um AN.NA ba-ab-šu lá-šu 17 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša DINGIR-ma-lik ub-lá-ni i-na ku-nu-ki-šu i-ba-ší i-na e-ra-ab AN.NA a ma-lá té-er-tí-kà ni-ša-a-ma-ku-um KI ba-tí-qí-im nu-šé-ba-lá-ku-um ni-is-ḫa-tum ù <gap> ša-du-a-tám iṣ-ḫe-er-ma 7.5 ma-na 2 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR KI ga-zi-a ni-il5-qé-ma a-šar a ṣí-ib-tim il5-qé-ú-ni ni-iš-qúl 0.33333 ma-na 7 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣí-ib-tám i-na KÙ-pì-kà ni-iš-qú-ul ra-qá-tám ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni lá-šu
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Aḫu-waqar, Puzur-Aššur and Dān-Aššur:
You wrote. You said, "Purchase 33 minas silver worth of tin for me."
Let the open country (overland journey) seize it!
Cheap or dear, there is was no tin to be had at his departure.
The 17 minas which Ilī-mālik brought is available under his seal.
At the arrival of tin we will purchase tin for you according to your instructions.
We will send it to you with the express caravan.
The excise and shipping fees were deducted and we received 7 minas 32 shekels silver from Gazia, and we will pay out the silver which they received.
We paid out 27 shekels silver interest from your silver.
The raqqutum-textile about which you wrote, is not available.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi4-ma um-ma dan-a-šùr-ma a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta a-na ša-al-ṭim lá ta-ša-ki-ni-a-tí URUDU ša É.GAL-lim a-dí u4-mì-im a-ni-im i-ta-na-pu-lu-ni-a-tí ù i-a-tí mu-ur-ṣú-um im-qú-tám-ma a-dí a-ni a-sú-ḫu-ur ša-al-ma-ku mì-ma li-ba-kà lá i-pá-ri-id a-dí 5 u4-me ú-ta-qá-ma šu-ma URUDU ša É.GAL-lim lá i-ta-ṣa-am kà-ki a-lá-qé-ma a bu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim a-ta-lá-ak
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Dān-Aššur:
My dear father and lord, do not treat me harshly.
Regarding the copper at the palace, until this day they are putting us off.
As for me, an illnes beset me and until now I was stuck (here).
But I am (now) well.
Don't be overly concerned.
I will wait 5 more days, and if the copper of the palace does not come out, I will take my weapon and depart for Purušḫattum.

To Pūšu-kēn from Dān-Aššur:
Sabasiya son of Iliš-tīkal owes 12 minas 34 shekels refined silver
His term is past due 4 ḫamuštum-weeks.
Seize him and make him pay the silver and its interest.
As for the silver which I did not send with Enna-Suen, there are no sales for tin or fine copper and the tin is bad quality, so I released them for one or two months to a trusted investor.

To Pūšu-kēn from Ikūnum, Kurub-Ištar, and Ḫuraṣānum:
Šumi-abiya and Urāni brought 75 kutānum-textiles, their price 6 minas 29 shekels, 15 minas hand-tin, its price 1 mina 4 shekels, 3 donkeys for 44 1/4 shekels, 8 shekels for the donkey's gear, 34 shekels for the be'ulātum of the driver, 4 1/2 shekels for the exit tax--in total 9 minas 3 3/4 shekels silver.
For this silver, we took out (a loan of) 1 mina 10 shekels for you at the house of the merchant.
Send the silver immediately so that the interest does not take you for much and disappoint you.
If we did not take the silver after we read it from your tablet, we would not have on interest in this way ???.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
When you were staying here, I gave 1 heavy textile to Aššur-mālik.
Also, when he returned, I gave to him 1 kamtum textile and 3 kutānum-textiles.
Why is it that the textiles which I keep sending to you, which he (is supposed to) bring, he does not bring to you?
Let your message come and so inform me.
Also, concerning the textiles about which you wrote, you said, "They are too small. They are no good!"
Was it not according to your request that I made them smaller?
Also, when you wrote me(?), you said, "Raise a 1/2 mina each for your textiles."
I raised it.
Also, Urāni did settle the logs to your representative, concerning the beams he confirmed.
I did not exhaust it.
Also, he took the beams belonging to your brother.
The copper which you gave to him for the beams he did not give at all.
Ilī-bāni brought to the youth a namšuḫam-textile belonging to Aḫaḫa.
Later, I will send a nibrarum-textile to him.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi4-ma um-ma lá-ma-sí-ma ṭup-pu-um ta-aḫ-sí-is-tám ša ba-a-ba-tim ša i-na bé-tí-kà té-zi-bu mu-zi-zu-kà ú-šé-ṣí-ú-ma qá-sú-nu-ma ú-kà-al a-na-ku a mì-ma ú-lá aṭ-ḫi ù a-na DAM.GÀR-ri-kà iš-qú-lu la iš-qú-lu mì-ma ú-la i-de8 a-ta ma-lá-kà ù ki-ma áš-pu-ra-ku-ni mì-ma šu-mì lá ta-za-kàr-šu-nu-tí 6.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR KI a-šùr-{d}UTU-ši DUMU lá-qé-ep ŠÀ.BA <gap> ša-du-a-tám ù ni-is-ḫa-tim ú-sà-ḫu-ru 3.6666600000000003 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR KI ú-ṣú-ur-ša-a-šur DUMU ì-lí-a 0.5 ma-na 5 GÍN KI ša-lim-a-šur ŠU.NÍGIN 8.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR a-na a-šur-i-mì-tí DUMU en-na-nim i-ša-qal i-na 4 ma-na KÙ ša a-na ì-lí-ìš-tí-kál ḫa-bu-lá-ku-ni 2 ma-na KÙ KI i-ku-pí-a 2 ma-na a-ḫu-ur 9 GÍN ur-da-na-na <gap> 1.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša DINGIR-ma-lik me-ḫe-er ṭup-pì-im ša té-zi-bu <gap> GÍN a-na wa-ṣí-im šé-bi4-lam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Lamassī:
The 'gates' memorandum tablet which stays in your house, your representatives got it out and they have it in their possession.
As for me, what am I responsible for?
Now, I do not know whether to pay out to your merchants or not!
As for you, make up your mind!
And becasue I write to you must not remember them (or their names).
6 1/2 minas silver is with Aššur-Šamši s. Lā-qēpum.
Thereof, they deducted the shipping charges and excise.
3 minas 45 shekels silver is with Uṣur-ša-Aššur s. Iliya.
35 shekels (silver) is (even) with Šalim-Aššur!
In total he will pay 8 1/2 minas silver to Aššur-imittī s. Ennānum.
From the 4 minas silver which I owe to Iliš-tīkal, 2 minas silver is with Ikūn-pīya.
2 minas (silver) is late.
Warad-Nana (has) 9 shekels.
Send the x+1 1/2 minas silver of Ilī-mālik, the copy of the tablet which you left, and xx shekels for exit-tax.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Ilabrat-bāni:
Šū-Nūnu brought you 17 minas silver.
Also, my instructions went to you.
I said, "Have Šū-Nūnu pay 1 minas 6 shekels silver.
Make Puzur-Aššur pay 1 mina 50 shekels silver and the son of Elī-mēšar pay 33 shekels silver so that you purify the dirty (tin?/copper?) and borrow silver on interest for the balance payment of the 20 minas, its excise tax and its shipping charges, doing according to my instructions, and so do me a favor.
But as for you, your instructions did not arrive.
My dear father and lord, take heed to do according to my instructions.
Let it be a favor to me.
Also, regarding my account, let your message come as to how much Puzur-Aššur deposited at the colony office and how much you desposited and so inform me.
Send frequently to Puzur-Ištar son of Agua so that those men may pay to (you?) the silver.

To Pūšu-kēn from Ilabrat-bāni:
Šū-Nūnu brought you 17 minas silver.
Also, my instructions went to you.
I said, "Have Šū-Nūnu pay 1 minas 6 shekels silver.
Make Puzur-Aššur pay 1 mina 50 shekels silver and the son of Elī-mēšar pay 33 shekels silver so that you purify the dirty (tin?/copper?) and borrow silver on interest for the balance payment of the 20 minas, its excise tax and its shipping charges, doing according to my instructions, and so do me a favor.
But as for you, your instructions did not arrive.
My dear father and lord, take heed to do according to my instructions.
Let it be a favor to me.
Also, regarding my account, let your message come as to how much Puzur-Aššur deposited at the colony office and how much you desposited and so inform me.
Send frequently to Puzur-Ištar son of Agua so that those men may pay to (you?) the silver.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šumi-abiya and Aššur-mālik:
We entrusted 7 talents and 30 minas of fine copper and they took it to Purušḫattum to Amur-Ištar.
4 talents of fine copper are deposited here at the place of Aššur-mālik.
2 talents of fine copper are deposited at the house of Kurara/Karriya in Ulama.
Uṣupiškum brings you 4 minas 45 shekels of silver under our seals.
According to your instructions, we seized the goods of Asqudum.
As for the copper, we stood here for its sale and Asqudum said, "Why do you sell the tin here so that I will see (only) 1 shekel of silver, i.e. losses?"
Asqudum has departed toward you.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bí-ma um-ma sú-e-a-ma DUB.SAR-tám wa-dí lá-am-da-ni e-pá-tá-am a-na um-mì-a-ni-a šé-bi-lam ú ma-tí-ma lá ta-aq-bi um-ma a-ta-ma 1 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-na me-er-i-a lu-šé-bi4-il5 šu-ma a-lá-ak a-ḫu-ra-tí té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam-ma lá-li-kam
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Sueyya:
Teach (me?) the scribal arts!
We are (supposed to be?) learned.
Send anepattum-garment to my teacher.
And (why is it?) you never say, "I shall send a shekel of silver to my son."
If you are delayed, let your message come so that I may come.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn from Šū-Kūbum:
In accordance with the letter which you wrote to me, we gathered your investors and we brokered an agreement.
Now, their sealed tablet they bring to you.
3 fine 'finished' textiles, 7 kutānum-textiles, in all 10 textiles belonging to your wife, she entrusted them to Kulumaya in my presence and he brings them to you.
Aššur-taklāku your servant brings you one bundle of lapis belonging to your wife.
My dear brother, you and Innāya take half of the tin and textiles in Atata's charge and in accordance with (what we discussed) here, send the 'corresponding' silver.
Silver in the charge of [PN] ... (fragmentary) ... take.
Do not disappoint me.
Act in such a way that when you do me a favor, you do not disappoint me.
My dear brother, take heed to deposit my share, silver, textiles, or whatever they will invest so that they balance me in the same way they balance you and do me a favor.
As for the documents which states, 'Concerning the matter of Innāya,' which you brought in your transport, you and Innāya read them and when you have read them, you and Innāya seal them and entrust them to a reliable affiliated trader so that he puts them in protective casing and brings them to me.
Now, take heed concerning the matter of Šū-Suen.
May Aššur (and DN?) witness that Kulumaya ... in Burallum.
He did not want to receive(?) the 10 minas to Ali-...'s representatives and ... silver ...

To Pūšu-kēn, Zupa, Puzur-Aššur, Aššur-tukultī and Aššur-šaduni from Lamassātum;
If you are truly my fathers, to as many tablets of the city, which are in the houses at Kaneš, either maids or tablets or wherever there is a single shekels of silver, take care to seal it and send it to me.
Urgent, Adad-bāni and ... you must not sell the textiles (nominative!) for a price.
Assist the attorney according to the tablet of the city and take/receive the tablet of kārum Kanesh (into?) the tablet of the city and send him to Waḫšušana to Amrīya.
He must not be delayed!

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 ù GAL-a-šur qí-bi-ma um-ma ku-zal-lim-ma ki-ma ù-nu-tum úṣ-a-ni TÚG.ḪI.A-tí ša i-na pá-zu-ur-tim e-ru-bu-ni-ni ú-ma-kál i-na É.GAL-lim uš-ib-ni ṣú-ḫa-ru ù ku-zal-lim ša áb-ri-im li-it-bu-lu-nim lá-ma ku-úṣ-um id-ni-nu ú GAL-a-šur a-dí É kà-ri-im za-kà-im li-ik-lá a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma a-ma-kam TÚG.ḪI.A ma-lá a qá-tí-a ta-ḫa-šu-ḫu ša kà-la-im ki-lá šu-ma ša ki-ma i-na-sú-en6 a-na šu-mì KÙ.BABBAR-pì-šu-nu lu-qú-tám i-kà-lu-ú ma-la 20 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR áp-pu-ší-im TÚG a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum a-bu-uk-ma a-dí KÙ.BABBAR ú-ṣí-a-ni qá-qá-a-kà ma-ḫa-aṣ lu-qú-tám lá i-kà-lu-ú lá ma-qú-ší-im <gap> pu ku-<gap> šu-ma ta-qá-bi4 um-ma a-ta-ma a-na 20 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR qá-qí-dí a-ma-ḫa-aṣ <gap> dan-IR i-ku-nu-um ú dan-a-šur ú i-a-tí li-dí-nu-nim-ma <gap> KÙ.BABBAR i-lá-qé ša É-<gap> ša a-na <gap> i TÚG <gap>
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn and Rabi-Aššur from Kunilum:
When the merchandise come out, the textiles which arrived as smuggled goods, on the very same day they exit the palace, let the lads and Kunilum bring Abrum's property to me.
Until the cold grows severe, Rabi-Aššur should withhold the goods until he has received clearance by the colony office.
To Pūšu-kēn (say):
There, retain the appropriate textiles, as many as you need (of) my share.
If Enna-Suen's representatives withhold (my) merchandise on account of their silver, repay(?) them the amount of 20 minas silver.
I sent the copper (textiles?) to Purušḫattum.
Until the silver matures, reserve it.
He must not retain the merchandise.
...
If you will say, "I will reserve the 20 minas silver."
Let Dān-(warad??) Ikūnum and Dān-Aššur give me also my own so the silver will go.(?)
The matter of the house for ... from the textiles ... (?)

To Pūšu-kēn and Aššur-nīšu, specifically to Pūšu-kēn, from Ikūn-pīya:
With six donkeys and 15 talents fine copper--half washed and half ḫaburataum-style, my sons(/young men) were killed in DÍ-ta-tim.
Also, they carried away my copper.
And I cannot recover(?) the donkeys.
Concerning my copper and the blood money of my sons/young men, I am staying in Tuḫpiya.
I hear that I am summoned to court in the city.
In one (more?) month, the merchant will pay out 10 minas copper.
There is no silver.
A shekel falls to my account.
(But) there is no copper.
Should 10 minas (silver) fall into my account, I will purchase donkesys
My dear father and lord, to whom else can I turn?
Give me 4 or 5 donkeys on long terms which you are wont to let out and rustle the donkeys so they arrive in Purušḫattum so that every shekel of silver may arise and the eye of Aššur may (smile on me?)
And if you have no donkeys yourself, take some from a colleague.
To Kulumaya:
Let your message ...

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn and Ennam-Aššur from Ḫuṣārum:
Regarding the tin and textiles of the šēpum of Āl-ṭāb which the corresponding amount of the goods which Šū-Kūbum brought to you, my dear father, you and Ennam-Aššur sell there as much tin as they clear, cheap or dear and send the silver to me as soon as possible.
Also, send back Šū-Kūbum.
If the tin and textiles are sold, seal the silver and send it to me.
If the prices there are deflated, let my goods go on to Purušḫattum so that they may raise every mina possible.

target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn and Innāya from Šū-Kūbum:
Concerning the matter of accountings in the year which our father died, I was the one who stood in place of our father.
I said, "I will not dishonor the name of my father. Should not the clearing of his obligations go to me?"
Should not the clearing of his obligations go to me?
I hear that they received/declared our father's clearance from obligations.
But I have paid a greivous
It has always been that when I requested (clemency) for my partners and their representatives, they gave it, however you two, you don't request clemency for me.
My dear brothers, take heed to request clemency for me from the colony office.
Now, my lord, ask (clemency) for me so that ... one or two ... our father ... and to me.

source_raw: a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 ù i-na-a qí-bi-ma um-ma šu-ku-bu-um-ma 2 GÚ 20 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ku 35 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu 18 ma-na AN.NA qá-tim 2 ANŠE ṣa-lá-me ù e-nu-sú-nu 0.33333 ma-na <gap> bé-ú-lá-at a-mur-{d}UTU a-na bé-ú-lá-tí-šu 3 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu a-ḫa-ma dar-ku-ú mì-ma a-nim a-ta-ta i-ra-de8-a-ku-nu-ti a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A mì-iš-lá li-qé-a-ma KÙ.BABBAR me-eḫ-ra-tim i ra-mì-ni-ku-nu ki-ma ta-ga-mì-lá-ni-ni id-a-ma KÙ.BABBAR i šé-ep i-na-a li-li-kam li-bi4 lá i-lá-mì-in šu-ma AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A lá ta-lá-qé-a-ma lá ta-ga-mì-lá-ni lu a-na i-ta-aṭ-lim lu a-na u4-me pá-tí-ú-tim lu a-na u4-me qúr-bu-tim dí-na-ma a-li ba-lá-ṭí-a ep-ša-ma ù té-er-ta-ku-nu li-li-kam a-na i-na-a qí-bi4-ma a-ṣé-er na-áš-pá-ra-tim ša i šé-ep pu-šu-ke-en6 ú-lá-pí-ta-ku-ni mì-nam ḫa-bu-lá-ku-ni-ma ša šé-pì-kà wa-ar-ki-tim ší-im e-ma-ri-a ta-ak-lá-šu i šé-ep ì-lí-a lá tù-šé-bi4-lam a-na na-áš-pá-ra-tim ša šé-ep pu-šu-ke-en6 i-ḫi-id i-na a-lá-ki-kà lá ta-qá-bi4 um-ma a-ta-ma mì-šu-um uz-ni lá tap-té
target_raw:
To Pūšu-kēn and Innāya from Šū-Kūbum:
2 talents 20 minas of tin sealed, 35 kutānum-textiles, 18 minas of hand tin, 2 black donkeys, and their gear, 1/3 minas silver--the be'ulātum-loan of Amur-Šamaš, 3 kutānum-textiles for his be'ulātum loan which are packed up separately, all this, Atata drives to you.
My dear brothers, take half of the tin and textiles and deposit the corresponding silver from your own funds as a favor to me and let the silver come in the šēpum of Innāya.
If you will not take the tin and textiles and not do me a favor, sell (them) either for cash or on long or short term credit so that I make a profit and let your message come here.
To Innaya:
In addition to the message which I wrote to you in the caravan of Pūšu-kēn, what do I owe you, seeing as in your last šēpum you withheld the price of my donkeys and you did not send it in Iliya's šēpum?
Pay attention to the letters in Pūšu-kēn's šēpum.
Do not say at your arrival, "Why did you not inform me?"

Aššur-imittī brings you 15 1/2 minas refined silver under my seal.
To Pūšu-kēn:
My dear father and lord, do not let your eye lift a single shekel of silver.
Do not retain my losses.
I am your son!
I will do you a favor.
I will send the 2 minas 10 shekels silver for the price of donkeys to you with my brother.
My dear father and lord, do not retain my tablet.

To Sukalliya, Šū-Anum, and Ikūn-pī-Aššur from Iddin-Suen:
To Ikūn-pī-Aššur:
Aḫ-šalim and Aššur-nādā did not give me the silver.
Let your urgent message go to them.
I owe you 6 minas 24 shekels silver.
You paid 4 minas certified silver to my account.
If they did not cause the silver reach you, but you intend to go, on the day of your departure let Sukkallia and Šū-Anum take the silver for interest and give it to you.
If you do not receive the silver before your departure, (and) the interest does not ..., then send to Aḫ-šalim and Aššur-nādā so that they send your silver to me.

target_raw:
To Elamma's shareholders and Elamma's representatives from Aššur-ṣulūlī, Ikuppia, Aššsur-ṭāb, Ilabrat-bāni, Iddin-Ištar, Ennam-Aššur and Abu-šalim:
You have written, saying: "Open the strong-room in Elamma's house there, and seal both whatever silver is there and the copper there and send it here!"
Before Ilabrat-bāni, Ennam-Aššur and Abu-šalim arrived from Kaneš the sons of Hinnaya had opened the strong-room and taken a certified tablet concerning 21 minas of silver, the debt of Adad-ṣulūlī and Iddin-Ištar son of Aššur-nādā.
They said: "We take responsibility for any complaint by the shareholders."
When they opened the strong-room they let a group of five strangers enter and had them (re-)seal; and they themselves sealed together with them.
Since they opened the sealings of Aššur-ṭāb and Aššur-ṣulūlī we became afraid for your sake, so we have not reopened the strong-room.
We wrote to ask your advice; the very day you hear our letter you should send us word, and then we shall open the strong-room.
The servants must not be delayed.

target:
To Elamma's shareholders and Elamma's representatives from Aššur-ṣulūlī, Ikuppia, Aššsur-ṭāb, Ilabrat-bāni, Iddin-Ištar, Ennam-Aššur and Abu-šalim:
You have written, saying: "Open the strong-room in Elamma's house there, and seal both whatever silver is there and the copper there and send it here!"
Before Ilabrat-bāni, Ennam-Aššur and Abu-šalim arrived from Kaneš the sons of Hinnaya had opened the strong-room and taken a certified tablet concerning 21 minas of silver, the debt of Adad-ṣulūlī and Iddin-Ištar son of Aššur-nādā.
They said: "We take responsibility for any complaint by the shareholders."
When they opened the strong-room they let a group of five strangers enter and had them (re-)seal; and they themselves sealed together with them.
Since they opened the sealings of Aššur-ṭāb and Aššur-ṣulūlī we became afraid for your sake, so we have not reopened the strong-room.
We wrote to ask your advice; the very day you hear our letter you should send us word, and then we shall open the strong-room.
The servants must not be delayed.
label: Cuneiform Tablet CCT 5 3a (BM 120515)

target_raw:
To the investors, Šū-Ḫubur, and my representatives, from Pūšu-kēn:
I requested a 3-year term from the god.
Thus I said, "Let me go."
Let me clear my assets (bûlātum) and I will go out.
Also, let me establish much before you (??).
Šū
Also, you (pl.) were in agreement.
As for me, I did not come before(?) him.
I said, "I will not wait 5 years. Let me go until the term I asked of the god and let me clear my assets."
To my merchants which I 'carry' 2 and 1/3 you did not ...
He wasted much silver.
He is not planted (firm? mature?) and I do not want to name him.
I said (to myself), "As soon as they go, the merchant will release every shekel of silver to them and then they will gather mine as much as possible.
May Aššur and your (pl.) god witness (that) I have been deprived of 2 talents of silver!
My dear lords, if you will, judge how much excise tax the houses of my investors will exact and seal a taḫsistum-tablet stating that my maškattum can continue for 3 more years and so that they may not seize the silver and write to me your kind words through my servant so that I can gather every shekel of my bulātum before the harvest and (...)

To Zukša and Šāt-Aššur say: thus (says) Nab-Suen.
We are well.
Do not worry!
We hear good news from various sides.
As soon as we obtain (a decision in) our legal case, I will send with the first messenger (news about) our well-being.
Do not worry!

source_raw: a-na ša ki-ma a-šur-ma-lik pu-šu-ke-en ù ì-lí-áš-ra-ni qí-bi4-ma um-ma ša-lim-a-ḫu-um-ma 8 GÚ AN.NA ku-nu-ki-a 32 TÚG.ḪI.A ŠÀ.BA 8 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu 4 ANŠE ṣa-al-mu 2 GÚ AN.NA li-wi-sú ù ANŠE a-ṣé-er a-šur-{d}UTU-ši ù ḫi-na-a šé-bi4-lá a-ḫu-ú-a a-tù-nu AN.NA-ki ù ṣú-ba-tù-a lá i-na-dí a-na DAM.GÀR-ri-im ke-nim ša ki-ma qá-qí-dí-ku-nu a-na u4-me dí-na DAM.GÀR-ar-ku-nu lu ke-en6 ITU.KAM-am iš-té-en6 ú šé-na lá tù-šé-qá-ra ù té-er-ta-ku-nu li-li-kam 10 ma-na URUDU li-pá-a ù ta-pá-ú-šu 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR šu-ḫa-bu-ra 9 ma-na a-šur-i-mì-tí 13 ma-na a-bu-um-DINGIR DUMU a-ba 6.5 ma-na a-mur-IŠTAR DUMU i-dí-sú-en6 mì-ma KÙ.BABBAR a-nim u4-mu-šu-nu ma-al-ú ṭup-pì-šu-nu am-ra-ma ša u4-mu-šu-nu e-tí-qú-ni ṣí-ib-tám ša-áš-qí-lá a-ḫu-ú-a a-tù-nu KÙ.BABBAR ša-áš-qí-lá-ma i-na pá-nim-ma wa-ṣí-im ku-un-kà-nim-ma ì-lí-áš-ra-ni ù ku-da-ri-li ṭù-ur-da-nim a-na mì-nim iš-tí a-šur-ma-lik ku-da-ri-lí lá i-li-kam ki-ma ša a-ḫa-am i-na kà-ri-im lá i-šu ú ÌR e-ta-áp-ša-ni šu-ma iš-tí ì-lí-áš-ra-ni a-lá-kam lá i-mu-a i-na ša-ḫa-tí-šu i-zi-za-ma lu-kà-sí-a-šu-ma li-ir-de8-a-šu
target_raw:
To the representatives of Aššur mālik--Pūšu-kēn and Ili-ašranni, thus Šalim-aḫum:
8 'talents' tin under my seal, 32 textiles, therein: 8 kutānum textiles, 4 black donkeys, 2 talents tin, its wrapping, and a donkey send to Aššur-šamšī and Ḫinnaya.
My dear brothers, do not deposit my tin and textiles.
Sell them on credit to a trustworthy merchant, as trustworthy as yourselves.
Do not overvalue one or two months.
Then let your message come.
10 minas copper Libbaya and his partner, 10 minas silver Šū-Ḫabura, 9 minas (silver) Aššur-imittī, 13 minas Abum-ilum s. Abaya, 6 1/2 minas Amur-Ištar s. Iddi-Suen--all this silver there terms are full.
Inspect their tablets and makle them pay the interest according to their days which are past.
My dear brothers, make them pay the silver and seal (and send) it to me with the first departing (traveller).
Dispatch Ili-ašranni and Kudar-ilī to me.
?? To whom with Aššur-mālik Kudar-ilī did not come. ??
There is not anyone like Aḫum(?) in the colony.
Now, the servant which ?was made?, if he does not with to go with Ilī-ašranni, assist him so that he ks' him and he brings it.

To Šalim-aḫum: Lā-qēpum, Iliya, and Pūšu-kēn:
After we deducted the excise tax and the deficiencies, they cleared your tin—122 minas 8 shekels.
27 kūtānum-textiles, 4 textiles as wrappings, all this Son of Erradi turned over to us.
We have not yet cleared accounts with him.
Your donkeys died.
Thereof, 20 kutānum-textiles at 1/2 mina each, 4 šurum-textiles at 15 shekels each, 7 shekels for your tin, we bundled together and 28 minas 27 shekels is placed on the merchant on long terms.
The remainder of your kutānum-textiles is 7 textiles.
They are deposited in your account.
130 minas tin, 4 textiles as wrappings, 5 kutānum-textiles, 1 black donkey (all) of the šēpum of Aššur-mutabbil--Nūr-Ištar drove 26 kutānum-textiles, 1 black donkey here.
One donkey died.
Thereof, 1 1/2 textiles (were) the excise tax.
And we balanced 1 shekel silver.
3 textiles were for 'purchases.'
1/2 textile is with Ilabrat-bāni.
They cleared the remainder of your textiles—26 textiles.
From the 2 talents 10 minas tin from the šēpum of Aššur-mutabbil, 4 minas were the excise tax.
4 minas 54 shekels were lost.
After the hand tin was exhausted, we remitted Aššur-mutabbil 7 minas 27 shekels tin.
They cleared the remainder of your tin—53 minas 39 shekels.
We took 11 minas tin from the caravan of Aššur-mālik at the colony office and placed it with your tin.
The total was 2 talents 4 minas 39 shekels.
Your tin—at a 7 shekel rate, 26 kutānum-textiles—at a 1/2 mina each, a donkey—for 1/2 mina, in all the silver from your tin and textiles and donkey—31 minas 18 1/2 shekels—is placed on a merchant on long terms.
4 textiles of the wrappings are placed in your account.
From the 6 talents 30 minas tin of the šēpum of Aḫ-šalim, 21 minas was the excise tax..
The deficiencies were 3 minas 10 shekels.
The remainder of your tin was 6 talents 5 5/6 minas.
Thereof, 304 minas 40 shekels (tin) at a rate of 8 shekels and 50 kutānum-textiles together with šurum-textiles which were seized, at a rate of 20 shekels (we gathered(?), and) 54 minas 45 shekels silver on long terms are placed on a merchant.

To Šalim-aḫum from Lā-qēpum and Pūšu-kēn:
Pūšu-kēn brought 8 talents tin and its ..., 32 textiles, therein are 10 kutānum-textiles, 32 minas tin sealed, 2 minas tin separately.
In sum, 34 minas hand tin, 4 kusītum-garments.
Therein, the son of Aššur-mālik took 1 kusītum-garment.
They took 1 mṭt kusītum-garment as excise of 5 kutanum-textiles for the palace.
... tin which to ...
.... for ...
...we added ... for 10 shekels silver ... kutānum-textiles and we paid back 12 minas silver to your accounts.
We combined 5 shekels silver which (is deducted ?) from the price of 3 kutānum-textiles, to the total of your taphir of Šarra-mātēn.
Of the 5 talents, the awītum-assessement of the transport of Ili-ašranni, (the segment) from Razama to Burallum set to us 6 minas 51 2/3 shekels tin.
From Burallum to Kanesh, 2 minas 57 1/3 shekels per mina obtain.
For 10 talents, your awītum assessment is 6 minas.
29 minas 49 shekels applies.
1/2 minas tin (for) two heads(?) obtain.
The (fee for) fodder of 4 donkeys and their drivers is 2 minas.
The hire of the drivers is 3 minas.
The tašiātum is 2 minas 34 shekels.
The deficiency of the hand tin is 1/2 mina.
We gave to Ili-ašranni ... of his tin ...

To Šalim-aḫum from Pūšu-kēn:
5 talents 4 minas together with that owned by the shipper, your awītum assessment for the transport of Ennum-Aššur is 5 minas 5 shekels.
The dātum charge assessed 25 minas 45 shekels.
The head tax was 45 shekels.
The feed for the donkeys was 4 1/2 minas.
And also for the packers.
Losses from the hand tin were 20 shekels.
After the 25 minas hand tin were exhausted, we reimbursed Ennum-Aššur 6 1/2 minas tin.
For the 4 minas excise for the tin, we opened your packages and we reimbursed 10 1/2 minas tin, together with its excise.
From the 72 textiles which entered into the palace, you will pay 1 2/3 shekels silver, (in addition to?) the excise of 2 1/3 textiles.
Purchases (the tithe) were 6 textiles.
And you will repay 2 1/4 shekels.
(The palace) deducted a 1/4 textile for the silver which you are paying.

target_raw:
To Šalim-aḫum from Pūšu-kēn:
Aššur-mālik brought 61 textiles, together with the goods of the transporter, and together with the things which you sent to Ilabrat-bāni.
Thereof, the excise was 3 kutānum textiles.
And you will balance the excise of 1 textile.
The purchases was 6 kutānum textiles and they will repay you 3 shekels silver.
They cleared 52 kutānum textiles, and you will receive 2 1/6 shekels.
Thereof, Aššur-mālik deposited 1 kutānum textile to your account according to your instructions.
Your transporter took 3 of your kutānum textiles and your transporter brings 25 shekels silver its excise added and shipping duty paid.
They cleared the remainder of your textiles, 48 textiles.
For your awītum: 2 talents 18 minas tin, the datum set 13 5/6 minas tin for you.
The feed for 2 donkeys and 1 packer was 3 minas 15 shekels.
1 donkey died and he charged you 17 shekels.
He charged you 7 shekels silver for the hire of the handlers from Ḫaḫḫum to Kanesh.
The losses on hand tin were 12 shekels.
After the 12 minas hand tin diminished, you will balance 5 minas 15 shekels tin to the hand tin.
At an 8 shekel rate, the value in silver is 39 1/3 shekels.
The price of the donkey was 17 shekels and the hire of the handler was 7 shekels and I added what coudl be added and in total you will balance 1 minas 3 1/3 shekels silver.
The 2 1/4 shekels silver which they balance to you were deducted and I balanced 1 mina 1 shekels and 15 grains silver to Aššur-mālik.

To Šalim-aḫim and Pūšu-kēn from Al-āḫum, Imdī-ilum and Puzur-Aššur:
Ḫuraṣāum entrusted to us 2 talents, 6 minas tin, 32 textiles together with those for wrappings, and 2 black donkeys.
Amur-Aššur entrusted to us 2 talents 10 minas tin, 30 1/2 kutānum textiles, 4 šurūtum textiles, and 2 black donkeys.
Ilabrat-bāni entrusted to us 23 kutānum (textiles) and 1 black donkey.
From the 4 talents 16 minas of your tin, the excise tax was 8 minas.
We balanced 9 minas 3½ shekels (tin) to Ilabrat-bāni.
We balanced 1 5/6 minas (tin) to Ḫuraṣānum.
The deficiencies were 13 5/6 minas 5½ shekels (tin).
We cleared the remainder of your tin--3 talents, 43⅓ minas, the 85½ kutānum textiles, the 4 šurūtum textiles, and 5 black donkeys.
Therein, we placed 2 talents tin, 32 kutānum textiles, a 10 shekels rate (for) the tin, 15 shekels each for the kutānum textiles--20 minas silver for 25 ḫamuštum weeks.
We placed 1 talent tin, 16 kutānum textiles for 10 minas silver on (another) merchant.
We placed 43⅓ minas tin, 17 kutānum textiles, 2 donkeys for 9 minas 35 shekels (silver) on (another) merchant.
We placed 2 donkeys for 1 mina silver on a merchant.
We placed 1 donkey foal on a merchant for ⅓ minas (silver).
For 3 talents 43⅓ minas tin, 65 textiles and 5 donkeys--40 5/6 (minas) 5 shekels refined silver on trustworthy merchants, not ??the cheating kind??, for 25 ḫamuštum weeks are placed.
Month: Ab-šarrāni (v), Year: Buzuzu (REL 87).
We placed 8 textiles in your account.
Their silver we placed in your account.
1 šurūtum textile (went) into your account.
12 kutānum textiles and 3 šurūtum textiles are the remainder of your textiles.

To Šū-Ištar, the priest of Ištar, this is what Aššur-lamassāi, Nimar-Ištar and Adad-rabi said:
The day the son of Aplum arrived we chased Aššur-rabi and the daughter of Ihan-il out of your house in accordance with your written order; then, we brought your amtum-wife Watniašwe and your son Aššur-imittī into your house and she is in possession of the attributes of priesthood.
Aššur-rabi went to the colony and said: “My father sent me a message to leave the house.
Select for me five independent traders so that I can entrust the goddess and her jewellery and leave.”
The colony selected five independent traders for him and he entrusted the goddess and her jewellery; she is fine.
[...] the jewellery of the [goddess], be it silver or gold [or meteoric iron] or sādum gold ... – the selected men wrote (it on) a tablet and gave that to your amtum-wife.
A second tablet, a copy of it, is at the Office of the Colony.
A dispute arose among your amtum-wife, Nimar-Ištar, Aššur-imitti and Ewrimuša, who are in possession of your house, and the taboo of the goddess has been heard.
They went to the colony and the colony sent the same men as previously selected to take care of the goddess; they checked the jewellery of the goddess (with this result):
12 earrings of meteoric iron; 1 1⁄2 shekel (ca. 12.5 g), a (representation of a) pudendum of gold; 2 beads of gold of two-thirds of a shekel (ca. 5.5 g); half a mina (ca. 249 g) of silver: toggle-pins; 17 stars of silver; a mace of 5/6 shekels (ca. 6.9 g) of silver; (beads of) either sādum gold or lapis lazuli weighing 35 shekels (ca. 290.5 g), (forming) the string of beads on her chest – all this is lost.
The colony cross-examined [them]
and they replied: “Yes, we took (it).”

The man is no good.
For ten years, he has taken me for a deaf and dumb one.
(Even) if you don't allow me to travel, I'll (still) set off and go to (acquire) the merchandise.
Don't be angry!

To Šū-Ištar from Ilī-bāni:
As for the 43 shekels of silver belonging to your daughter which you gave me for purchases, Aššur-taklāku with me and our certified representatives, we purchased for you 2 abarnium-textiles and 1 fine kutānum textile.
They are available under our seals at (the house of?) Dadaya son of the priest of Suen.
As for me, send another on my journey (?) so that he can give the textile to you.

source_raw: a-na šu-a-nim qí-bi-ma um-ma ší-lá-ma-sí-ma ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša ta-ad-mì-iq-tí-ki PUZUR4.IŠTAR li-dí-na-ki-im e-ri-šu-ma um-ma šu-ut-ma mì-ma ú-lá i-dí-in a-ḫi a-ta KÙ.BABBAR ša TÚG.ḪI-tí-a ú ša a-mu-tim ša na-ni-a ub-lá-ku-ni KÙ.BABBAR-pí i pá-ni-tí-ma šé-bi-lam
target_raw:
To Šū-Anum, from Šī-lamassī:
You wrote me.
You said, "Let Puzur-Ištar give you the silverof your tadmiqtum."
He asked him, and he said, "He did not give (the silver)."
My dear brother, send me the silver from my textiles, and from the iron, and that which Naniya brought to you at the earliest opportunity.

target_raw:
To Šu-Anum from Šī-Lamassī:
You wrote me:
"Puzur-Ištar brings to you the yield of your textiles."
I asked Puzur-Ištar about the silver, and he said: "He did not give me anything."
My dear brother, send me my silver.
Puzur-Ištar will bring you 1 (wool) belt as capital for your interest-free loan.

target_raw:
To Šū-Kūbum from Pūšu-kēn and Innāya:
10 minas silver, the installment payment from the goods of Pūšu-kēn's transport has been paid, and 10 shekels, interest, turned out to be silver of inferior quality, so 15 shekels was the compensation.
In total it is 10 minas 25 shekels.
Thereof, we gave 10 shekels as the šaddu'ātum and Aššur-mālik brings you 10 minas 15 shekels of silver.
