source_raw: a-na šu-ku-bi-im ù a-šùr-pí-lá-aḫ a-na šu-ku-bi-im qí-bi-ma um-ma a-šur-na-da-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša a-na tap-ḫi-ri-im ša šar-ma-té-en a-bu-ni i-ḫi-ib-lu a-na-kam iṣ-bu-tù-ni-ma um-ma šu-nu-ma KÙ.BABBAR ḫu-bu-ul a-bi-kà šu-qúl um-ma a-na-ku-ma KÙ.BABBAR tal-ta-qé-a-ma KÙ.BABBAR e-ṣú-ma a-ḫu-ur ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tum ša a-bi-a i-ba-ší-a um-ma šu-nu-ma ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim ša a-bi-kà šé-ṣí-a-ma lu né-mu-ur iš-té-en6 ša ki-ma ku-a-tí ù iš-tí-a a-ḫi-a-am iš-tí-a e-ru-ub-ma ṭup-pí ni-ip-té-ma ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim nu-šé-li-ma ú-kà-li-im-ší-na-ma ú-sà-li-šu-<gap>-im ni-qé-e <gap> ú ša <gap> me-ḫe-er ta-aḫ-sí-sà-tim nu-šé-<gap> kum ṭup-pí ḫa-ar-mu-um ša ba-áb DINGIR-lim <gap>-im ni-a-im <gap> i-na me-eḫ-ri-im ša ṭup-pí-im ḫa-ar-mì-im ša ba-ab DINGIR-lim ša e-li-a-ni sà-ar <gap> ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫu-bu-ul a-lá-ḫi-im a-mur-IŠTAR a-na a-šùr-SIPA iš-qúl um-ma a-šùr-SIPA-ma a-na a-mur-IŠTAR-ma 1 ma-na-kà ma-ṭí-i 1 ma-na-ú-šu ik-ta-ma 1 0.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-dí-šu-um IGI li-ba-a DUMU ú-zu-a IGI en-na-nim sà-be-e-em na-áb-sú-en6 DUMU {d}AB-ba-ni tap-pá-i-ni a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um kà-ni-iš i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma IGI GÍR ša a-šur ší-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in
target_raw:
To Šū-Kūbum and Aššur-pilah, and specifically to Šū-Kūbum, from Aššur-nādā:
With regard to the silver with which our father has become indebted to the fund of divine Šar-mātēn, they seized me here and said: "Pay the silver, your father's debt."
I answered: "You have received silver repeatedly, so little silver is left (of the debt).
There are memoranda of my father's available."
They said: "Produce your father's memoranda and let us see."
One person representing you and one stranger entered (the house) together with me and we opened for the tablets and took out the memoranda and I showed them;
I pleaded with them ... sacrifices ...
and ... we took out the memoranda and copies of the tablets from the Gate of the God concerning our own tablet ...
in the copy of the certified tablet of the Gate of the God which may appear, concerning x mina 5 shekels of silver from Alāhum's debt—(that) Amur-Ištar paid to Aššur-re'i.
Aššur-re’i said as follows to Amur-Ištar:
"Your one-mina weight is too light."
His one-mina weight was checked(?) and he then paid him 1 1/2 shekel of silver.
Witnessed by Libbaya son of Uzua, by Ennānum, the inn-keeper; Nab-Suen son of Illil-bāni was our co-witness.
For this matter, the Kanesh colony gave us permission, and we gave our testimonies before the dagger of Aššur.

To Šū-Ḫubur from Pūšu-kēn:
You wrote me.
(You said,) "Send up the goods of Ik
The man cannot make his thirds.
(Write?) to me the gold, both your own and that of the house of your father, as much falls to you as a share, so that I may take your tablet and take as much as I am able.
Concerning the tin, a talent, and four textiles-I took their price in silver and we ... , and for one talent 3 minas tin at 7 shekels rate, its silver was 9 minas.
Altogether the price of the 4 textiles at 15 shekels each was 1 mina (silver).
10 minas silver, thereof, I took 8 minas.
He will pay me the (remaining) two minas (in?) Zalpa after his return.
From the 5(?) minas silver, I/the representative of Lālum will return x minas silver to the representatives of Lālum/me. (?)
You wrote me.
You said, "Regarding the 1 mina gold I released to Ennam-Aššur ..."
In addition to the mina which you gave to him there, (there is) also 45 shekels silver and 6 minas tin which he owes.
From the silver of Lālum which I received, I took 1 2/3 minas silver, and also gathered 1/3 minas silver from your silver and I gave him 2 minas silver and also 3 minas 55 shekels silver for the 1 minas of gold I added(?) to him.
Dān-Aššur brings to you the remainder of your silver: 6 minas silver.
Regarding Ikūnum, let your instructions come from the city with the first traveller so that I (can bring) as much as I can raise in my cargo.

To Šalim-aḫum from Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding the 30 minas (silver) which Aššur-ṭāb and Uṣur-ša-Aššur owed, 10 shekels were deducted amounts.
I added 1/3 minas silver from my own funds and Šū-Ištar son of Dadānum brings you 30 minas silver under my seal.
You wrote me.
You said, "In addition to this silver, add 10 minas silver and dispatch the boy (Ennam-Aššur) to me.
(As for) the yield of the textiles and the yeild of the 50 minas tin, I will entrust about 1 talent 20 minas silver to Dān-Aššur's transport.
He will arrive in 2 days and I will dispatch him to you.
Also, my complete report will come to you with him.
As for the tin, if (the rate) is 15 shekels or higher, purchase it to the amount of 40 minas silver so that it is deposited until the arrival of Dān-Aššur so that he does not delay there.
Dispatch him to me.
Now, as for my transport, beside the two tablets of Lulu and Aššur-nādā and his son, there is nothing else.
I wrote to your son and I said, "Go with this silver!"
He said to my messenger, "You argue like the son of a dead man."
Before 3 'certified merchants' he refused to go.
What can I say?
In addition to my constantly running around in ... for 5 months ...

As for the activities of shipping, when your instructions come with the ṣuharū-servants of Pūšu-kēn, in your tablet (it said), "Send 12 minas silver from Ea-šār."
For those, both to the man and yours about 10 minas silver which I caused to be siezed, I did not send it late.
I said, "Let aboundant silver arrive and let him be happy."
When I was in Waḫšušana, Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur received 8 1/3 minas tin from the goods of the merchant.
He said, "I took (it) for the price of the donkeys which they took for Dān-Aššur."
I said, "Deduct 2/3 mina silver as the price of your donkey. Give (back) the rest."
He said, "He took my donkeys for 50 shekels silver!"
I said, "Leave it. One could assist your donkey (??)."
He did not see things my way.
There, let him clear for the 8 minas 50 shekels tin.
As for the 20 shekels silver which Ismanum owes to you, I gathered 20 shekels silver from my own funds and Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur paid his son.
20 shekels silver for the hire of Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur in his first transport, he said, "I will purchase tin for you at a 17 shekel rate."
source: translate Akkadian to English: a-na šé-bu-li-ma ki-ma té-er-ta-kà iš-tí ṣú-ḫa-ri ša pu-šu-ke-en6 i-li-kà-ni i-na ṭup-pí-kà 12 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša i-a-ša-ar me-eḫ-ra-tim šé-bi-lam a ší-a-tí lu a-na a-wi-li-im lu ku-a-tí KÙ.BABBAR 10 ma-na ša uš-ta-aṣ-bi4-tù a-ar-ḫi-iš lá ú-šé-bi-lam um-ma a-na-ku-ma KÙ.BABBAR ṭá-aḫ-du-um li-li-ik-šu-ma li-bu-šu li-ḫi-du i-nu-mì i-na wa-aḫ-šu-ša-na wa-áš-ba-ku-ni 8.83333 ma-na AN.NA i ša DAM.GÀR DUG-ṣí-lá-a-šur il5-qé um-ma šu-ut-ma a ší-i-im ANŠE.ḪI.A ša a-na dan-a-šur il5-qé-ú al-qé um-ma a-na-ku-ma 0.66666 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ší-i-im e-ma-ri-kà ṣa-ḫe-er ší-tám dí-na-am um-ma šu-ut-ma a-na 0.83333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR e-ma-ri-a il5-qé-šu-nu um-ma a-na-ku-ma e-zi-ib e-ma-ar-kà iš-té-en6 i-tí-zi-iz šu-ḫu-ut iš-tí-a lá i-na-me-er a-ma-kam a-na 8.83333 ma-na AN.NA iš-tí-kà li-iz-kam a-na ṣé-er 0.33333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša iš-ma-num ḫa-bu-lá-ku-ni 0.33333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i ra-mì-ni-a ú-ra-dí-ma a DUMU-šu DUG-ṣí-lá-a-šur iš-qú-ul 0.33333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ig-ri-a DUG-ṣí-lá-a-šur i-na pá-ni-tim šé-pì-šu ub-lá-šu um-ma šu-ut-ma 17 GÍN.TA AN.NA áš-a-ma-kum
target:
As for the activities of shipping, when your instructions come with the ṣuharū-servants of Pūšu-kēn, in your tablet (it said), "Send 12 minas silver from Ea-šār."
For those, both to the man and yours about 10 minas silver which I caused to be siezed, I did not send it late.
I said, "Let aboundant silver arrive and let him be happy."
When I was in Waḫšušana, Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur received 8 1/3 minas tin from the goods of the merchant.
He said, "I took (it) for the price of the donkeys which they took for Dān-Aššur."
I said, "Deduct 2/3 mina silver as the price of your donkey. Give (back) the rest."
He said, "He took my donkeys for 50 shekels silver!"
I said, "Leave it. One could assist your donkey (??)."
He did not see things my way.
There, let him clear for the 8 minas 50 shekels tin.
As for the 20 shekels silver which Ismanum owes to you, I gathered 20 shekels silver from my own funds and Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur paid his son.
20 shekels silver for the hire of Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur in his first transport, he said, "I will purchase tin for you at a 17 shekel rate."
label: Cuneiform Tablet Kt v/k 121 (AKT 3 39)

target_raw:
To Ḫašušarna from Aššur-taklāku and Idī-Šamaš:
On the day you read our letter, set out and come to Tegarama.
Remain in Tegarama for my instruction.
Before departure, let your report come on the day you depart.
Come and hold on to the silver from Waḫšušana.

target_raw:
Concerning the 30 minas silver that Pūšu-kēn against Kulumaya in the city and the silver went into his
Aḫāḫā, daughter of Pūšu-kēn, Puzur-Aššur, and Buzāzu, her brothers, took 10 minas silver, as their shares.
Buzazu son of Pūšu-kēn took 10 minas silver as his share.
Ikūppaša his brother took 10 minas silver as his share.
Aḫaḫa, Buzāzu and Ikūppaša will not return to Kulumaya or his sons for their shares.
If Aḫaḫa returns to Kulumaya for the 10 minas silver, her share, Puzur-Aššur and Buzāzu will pay atonement for her.
Aḫaḫa and Ikūppaša will not return for the silver of Pūšu-kēn or for his shipmnets(?) or for the matter of Buzāzu for any reason.
And Kulumaya will not return to the family of Pūšu-kēn for any reason.

target_raw:
Aššur-pilah, the attorney of the estate of Amur-Ištar, Suen-rēʾī, Il-bānī, Aššur-nīšu, and Šū-Laban, sons of Amur-Ištar on their own behalf and on behalf of Ab-šalim, daughter of Amur-Ištar and Iddin-Adad their brother, and Aššur-mutabbil, Buzāzu and Ikūppaša, sons of Pūšu-kēn on their own behalf and on behalf of Aḫaḫa their sister the gubabtum priestess and Sueyya their brother Sueyya seized us in their agreement and swore the oath of the city and concluded their case:
Amur-Ištar stood as the legal representative of Pūšu-kēn.
Pūšu-kēn stood as the representative of Amur-Ištar.
One sent accounts to the other.
Each brother accepted silver for the funds of the other.
Sealed tablets with their seals or with witnesses or dividends(?) invested in each other.
Either in the city or abroad, the sons of Amur-ištar and the daughter of the Amur-Ištar, the gubabtum priestess, and the sons of Pūšu-kēn and the daughter of Pūšu-kēn, the gubabtum priestess, will not return to each other for any matter.
The following men were the adjudicators on the matter: Usānum s. Amur-Aššur, Kulumay s. Aššur-imittī, Ḫuniya s. Aššur-imittī, Ṭāb-ṣilla-Aššur s. Aššur-idi, Puzur-enna s. Enna-Aššur, Aššur-taklāku s. Āl-aḫum, Bēlānum s. Šū-Aššur, Idi-Aššur s. Dān-Aššur.

Aššur-nādā and Dān-Aššur seized us and settled accounts in our presence.
We
and they swore the oath by the City to the effect that: either with respect to a certified tablet or a lawsuit or anything else one will not raise claim against the other.
Witnessed by Hinnaya, witnessed by Šū-Nūnu, witnessed by Aššur-rē'ī, witnessed by Hurāṣī, witnessed by Karwaya.
With respect to the words of this tablet I hold a tablet with their seals and with the seal of Aššur-nādā.

target_raw:
Ennasu’in, der Sohn des Ikū’am und Puzurištar, der Sohn des Alâbum, sind für die Frau des Ikuppi’a, die Töchter des Ikuppī’a und die Söhne des Ikuppī’a an deren Stelle eingetreten,
und haben 40 Minen Silber, die Shculd des Ikuppī’a, dem Aššurmuttabbil, dem Sohne des Pūšukēn, und dem Bēlānum, dem Kommissär des Pūšukēn, bezahlt.
Für diese Angelegenheiten hat der Kārum Turḫumit uns (als Richter) bestellt.
Vor dem Dolche des Aššur haben wir unsere Beurkundung gegeben.
Vor Labannādā, dem Sohne des Idīsu’in; vor Ikuppī’aššur, dem Sohne des Pilaẖaššur.
Idīdagān, der Sohn des Sarnikum, und Šalimaḫum, der Sohn des Ṭābilī, (waren) unsere Beisitzer.

Of the former merchandise:
5 talents of copper, 9 minas for expenses, the transport tariff took up 8 minas.
For 117 textiles: their price was 56 1/2 talents 3 minas (of copper); 23 textiles from Šalatuwar, their price was 4 minas 1/2 shekel of silver; 10 textiles, 1 textile: (for) the colony office; in all in copper: 74 talents 20 minas.
Thereof 5 talents 15 minas, the price of a black donkey; he cleared 2 1/2 minas less 1 1/2 shekel of silver for me; the proceeds of 10 talents 20 minas of copper transported by Šalim-bēlī--its value in silver: 5 5/6 minas 1 1/2 shekel; thereof he sent 2 minas of silver to Uršu (to my benefit); Aššur-nādā spent 2 1/3 minas 6 shekels, the value in silver of 1/3 mina 6 shekels of gold; 12 talents 28 minas of copper which Ilī-ālum brought into Šalatuwar, I was charged with its value in silver, 5 1/2 minas 2 shekels; thereof Aššur-nādā acquired 5 minas less 4 shekels of silver; as the proceeds of 2 talents of good quality copper (and) his 4 talents 10 minas of copper Amur-Aššur, the packer of Dān-Aššur, brought you 3 minas 4 shekels of silver, including 5/6 mina less 1 shekel of silver, the price of the gold; 1 mina of silver is owed by Puzur-Anna; his tablet is in Amria's house.

target_raw:
Out of the 2/3 mina 5 shekels of silver of the working capital of Šamaš-tappā'ī and Ilum-malik, which Kurub-Ištar has given, 1/3 mina 2 1/2 shekels of silver, the working capital of Ilum-malik, Illil-bāni must pay to the representatives of Kurub-Ištar, to Aššur-nādā and Aššur-šamšī; he will then depart, and in the City Kurub-Ištar will show the (original) deed to Illil-bāni's representatives.
If he does not show the tablet, then Aššur-nāda son of Aššur-idī and Aššur-šamšī son of Amaya must pay 50 percent (penal interest) on the 1/3 mina 2 1/2 shekels of silver to Illil-bāni.
Kurub-Ištar must sue Šamaš-tappā'ī.
If Šamaš-tappā'ī and Illum-malik, have sold (their working capitals) then they must discuss with his witnesses.
Nannia, Katatum (and) Uṣup-iškum, (these) men are the judges.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum's four donkeys in the caravan of Atata which acame up, the palace took 1 donkey and Agua received its yeild.
One donkey (brought by) the son of Erraya, which Amur-Ištar took to Purušḫattum, stands to be mine(??).
I gave 1 donkey to Lulu.
One donkey died in the
One donkey from the transport of Dān-Aššur they took off to Ilabrat-bāni.
3 shekels silver, the nabrītum of the 3 donkeys.
They consumed 1 1/2 shekels here. (damaged signs?)

target_raw:
From the 6 minas silver which Aššur-mutabbil s. Šū-Anum owes me, 2 1/2 minas silver is late.
Witnesses: Ennānum the soldier (?), Āl-ṭāb s. Anali.
According to his sealed tablet from the hamuštum week of Ikūnum s. Samaya, previously the term was surpassed by a year, interest on the 2 1/2 minas silver you will not read to him.
8 minas 45 shekels refined silver Lipit-Istar s. Maṣi-ilī owes to Imdi-ilum.
Regarding the debt of his father he will pay.
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn s. Sueyya, Kunāya s. Abusa(?), Šū-Bēlum s. Puzur-šaduʾe.
Adad-rēṣī s. Puzur-Anna owes (Imdi-ilum(?)) 4 minas 5 shekels silver.
Witnesses: .. s. Amur-Naḫuš.
From the hamuštum week of Aššur-mālik and Amur-Ištar their term has elapsed, 15 minas silver Aššur-emūqī s. Ikūnum owes.
Witnesses: Idnāya s. Salliya, Ikūnum s. Il-bāni.
I received 8 minas silver from his debt as exchange(?).
Šū-Laban s. Baziya owes 2/3 minas silver.
From the ḫamuštum week of s. Elālī and Šalim-Aššur, he will pay x minas per annum.
Witnesses: Pūšu-kēn s. Sueyya, Amur-Aššur s. Ilī-ālum, Aššur-taklāku s. Šū-Enlil.

From the 7 black textiles which the colony office deposited, Pūšu-kēn deposited 5 textiles.
2 textiles (we purchased?) with the silver of Šalim-aḫum.
From the 9? kutānum-textiles, Pūšu-kēn deposited 5? textiles.
1? textile was classified as šadādum and I deposited one with it from the cargo of Pūšu-kēn.
2 textiles from the cargo of Ilī-ašranni.
All this Šalim-aḫum deposited in the colony office.
Šalim-aḫum is written down for 6 1/3 minas silver in the large tablet.

target_raw:
(Was die (Schuld)urkunde (auf den Namen) des Tarmānā über 8⅓ Minen 1¾ Seqel (betrifft), so ist das Silber und der Zins dazu zu (einer Summe von) 12 Minen angestiegen.
Davon gehören 6 Minen Silber dem Puzuraššur, (und) 6 Minen mir.
8 Minen Silber (betrug) die Schuld des Pferdemeisters, 4 Minen (sind) Zins: Insgesamt 12 Minen Silber hat vom Pferdmeister und dessen Frau er erhalten.
2½ Minen 7½ Seqel Silber: (dieses) Silber, den Preis für Gold, hat er vom ,,Stadtherren‘‘ erhalten.
Einen Dolch (?), eine (Dolch)scheide (?) und ein Halfter aus Silber hat er ihm (dem Stadtherren) (als Geschenk) überlassen.
Vor Aḫûqar.
Den Preis für acht gute Stoffe, den er (P) im Palast erhalten hat, außerdem den Preis für zwei Stoffe vom Zehnten, 1 Mine Silber, hat er (P) im bīt kārim erhalten.
(Was) den Preis für 40 Sack Gerste meines Anteils zu je 4½ Seqel pro Krug (betrifft): 12 Minen Silber ist der Preis dafür.
Dies alles (befindet sich) bei Puzuraššur.

target_raw:
Kuṣiya seized us against Dān-Aššur.
Kuṣiya said to Dān-Aššur, "My dear brother, the three minas silver which you gave to the son of Erraya in our matter and which went to the city and which the son of Erraya consumed the profit of the silver and you took your share, give me the silver so that I can divide it(?) to the son of Erraya. Also, add add about a mina of silver to this silver so that I can pay off our matter with Kudādum."

source_raw: kà-lu-a bé-sú-ú a-na ší-mì-im a-na uš-nu-ma-an a-na 0.5 ma-na 3.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-dí-in šu-ma uš-nu-ma-an a-na a-ba-tim i-tù-ar 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-ša-qal šu-ma ša kà-lu-a lu bé-al ḫu-bu-li-šu lu a-ḫu-šu lu bé-al tù-zi-ni a-na uš-nu-ma-an i-tù-ar 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-ša-qal IGI ša-ḫi-iš-nu-ma-an IGI pé-ru-a IGI du-du
target_raw:
Kalua sold his house to Ušnuman for 33 1/2 shekels of silver.
If Ušnuman makes a claim against Abatum he will pay half a mina of silver.
If a man of Kalua, either his creditor or his brother or the lord of the tuzinnum raises a claim against Ušnuman he will pay half a mina of silver.
Witnesses: Šaḫišnuman, Perua, Dudu.

target_raw:
The colony issued a verdict that Māru the son of Puzur-Ištar son of Attaya and the investors will give (Pūšu-kēn) the tablet stating that 4 minas silver which Puzur-Ištar son of Attaya gave to Pūšu-kēn, and he (Pūšu-kēn) will kill it.
In regard to the one additional mina of silver belonging to Pūšu-kēn which they took--after the taking of the silver Pūšu-kēn will make a statement.
He (?) sealed (?) the seal of the colony of Kanesh and Pūšu-kēn before the dagger of Aššur, 4 minas silver which Puzur-Aššur gave to him for ebuttum, 3 minas silver and 1/3 mina which he gave to him for purchases--the purchases and the ebuttum he( they?) did (?) (Will?), in truthfulness, exhaust.
From this silver there is nothing which I owe.
The following swore (in agreement) in the colony: in place of Māru, Puzur-Ištar, (and) Ennam-Aššur, the sons of Puzur-Ištar--Abu-šalim son of Edinnāya and Ikūn-pīya his brother; in place of Puzur-Ištar--Aḫ-šalim, Aššur-rēʾī, Iliš-tīkal and Nāb-Suen; in place of the investors--Puzur-Ištar.

Muza, daughter(!) of Ispunuman, is the wife of Šu-Bēlum.
Šu-Bēlum can take her along where <he> wishes.
If she wants to go away from him or commits a crime of an improper act, Muza will pay to Šu-Bēlum 1 mina of silver and Šu-Bēlum can go where he wishes.
If she wants to go away from him or commits a crime or an improper act, Muza will pay to Šu-Bēlum 1 mina of silver;
Šu-Bēlum can go where he wishes.
In the presence of Ṣulula, of Inib-Aššur, of Ennum-Aššur, of Perua.

target_raw:
Pūšu-kēn and Adad-šulūlī seized us and rendered accounts in our presence.
Pūšu-kēn gave 5 minas silver owed by Adad-šilūlī.
Month: Ab-šarrānē.
Eponymate: Kubīya.
He will go to Waḫšušana and 4 ḫamuštum weeks will pass, then he will deliver the silver and the silver will cross back and forth and the colony at Kaneš will give (Pūšu-kēn?) the ebuṭṭum loan of Adad-šulūlī for this matter and I will give my witnesses before the dagger of Aššur.
Witness: Puzur-Aššur s. Išar-kitt-Aššur.
Puzur-Šamaš s. Šū-Nunu is my partner.

target_raw:
Witnesses: Irma-Aššur, Aššur-mālik s. Amuraya.
I deposited 2 shekels silver for their offerings.
I brought 2 shekels silver under my seal, a pair of shoes, and a pair of šaḫirum-footwear to Ab-šalim, Nannaya, and Lā-qēpum s. Buzutaya.
I gave 2 minas copper to the son of Maniya as an offering.
I gave 1 1/3 minas copper to Al-aḫum for the house of Adad-ellat.
He entrusted to Kēnum s. Ennānum and Aššur-mutabbil 1 package for Nannaya d. Dudu, 1 package for Waqqurtum d. Pūšu-kēn, 1 package for Šāt-Aššur d. Šalim-aḫum, under the seals of Sueyya, and separately 22 1/2 grains of gold under my seal for Aḫaḫa altogether four shipments.
Witnesses: Irma-Aššur, Aššur-šamšī s. Amāya.
25 shekels silver, its excise added, under my seal to the wife of Šū-Ḫubur, 2 shekels to Aḫaḫa under the seals of the amtum, and a pair of shoes and a pair of šaḫirum-footwear for Nannaya, I entrusted all this to Irnuit.
Witnesses: Ušmiya, Abiya.
Aššur-šamšī delivered 10 shekels silver under my seal.
5 shekels silver from the city he will return to him.
Aššur-idī will pay.
Witnesses: Aššur-mālik, Bēlānum.

Pilaḫ-Išṭar seized us against Enna-Suen:
Enna-Suen said: "The debt is for two years.
I said: "You are the son of a dead man and I am the son of a dead man. Let us not litigate. If you have the tablet refering to (its) payment in full or you have witnesses, arraign them so that I can ..discuss with them..?"
Enna-Suen said: "I do not hold any tablet of (its) payment in full, and I do not have witnesses. I am young and I do not know the seals of my father. Let me establish a witness to his seal and I..."
Month: Ab-šarrānē.
Year: Šū-Suen.

source_raw: pí-lá-aḫ-IŠTAR wa-lá-wa-lá a-ma-sú e-zi-ib e-zi-ib-ta-ša 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša-bu-a-at a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim pí-lá-aḫ-IŠTAR wa-lá-wa-lá ša-at-IŠTAR um-ma-ša nu-nu ú a-mur-a-šùr a-ḫu-ša i ša-ḫa-tí-ša i-zi-zu-ma ni-iš a-šùr ni-iš a-na ni-iš ru-ba-im it-mu-ú-ma a-na pí-lá-aḫ-IŠTAR me-er-e-šu <gap> ú a-na mì-ma šu-mì-šu ú-lá i-tù-ru-šu-um šu-ma i-tù-ru-šu-um 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR i-ša-qú-lu lá-ma-sí me-er-a-sú pí-lá-aḫ-IŠTAR i-nu-mì a-na a-lim{ki} i-lu-ku i-ra-de8-šu ta-ar-bi-tám ú-ku-ul-ta-ša ša-bu-ú mì-ma ú-lá e-ri-šu-šu IGI en-nam-a-šùr IGI en-na-sú-en6 IGI <gap> IGI da-lá-áš IGI a-da-da
target_raw:
Pilaḫ-Ištar has left Walawala his amātum.
She has been paid her settlement, 1 mina silver, in full.
Regarding this matter (between?) Pilaḫ-Ištar( and) Walawala, Šāt-Aššur, Nunu, and Amur-Aššur her brother stood at her side and swore the oath of Aššur, Anna, and the rubāum that they will not return to Pilaḫ-Ištar, his sons, ..., for anything.
If they return to him, they will pay out 10 minas silver.
As for Lamassī his daughter and Pilaḫ-Ištar, when they go to the city, he will take her(!).
The rearing for the child rearing is satisfied.
They will not ask him for anything.
Witnesses: Ennam-Aššur, Ennam-Suen, ..., Dalaš, Adada.

The chief of the city seized us against Ennam-Aššur son of Anninim and the chief of the city said, "Unfortunately Suaeyya has changed. Did Sueyya give to you 40 minas silver under his seal for nabšum or not?"
Before these men, our seed?, the chief of the city myself and the the extensive assets I requested??
Ennam-Aššur said, "you filled? and Ikūn-pīya when Sueyya asked, on that day the governor asked, he established. To the governor and my lord, do not carry my matter concerning Pūšu-kēn, I myself?, to general assembly ...."
Concerning these matters the large and small assembly of Purušḫattum gave to us and we gave our testimonies before the dagger of Aššur.
Winesses: Bēlum-bāni son of Naram-Su(en), Idī-Suen son of Ennānum.

source_raw: um-ma 10-tum ša bu-ru-tim-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en qí-bi-ma 10-tum ša ší-ma-lá iš-tí-ni sá-bi-is a-ḫu-ú-ni a-ta šu-ma mì-ma ta-ki-li <gap> a-na kà-ri-im kà-ni-iš{ki} iš-ta-áp-ru-nim ki-ma ni-a-tí a-wa-tám ta-e-er-ma ù té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam-ma ú-za-ni pì-té a-ḫu-ú-ni a-ta a-na ṣú-ḫa-ri-kà na-aḫ-dá-ni a-na ḫa-ra-nim ta-da-nim
target_raw:
From the council of ten of Buruddum to Pūšu-kēn;
The ten-person council from Šimala is seized in a disagreement with us(?)
Our dear brother, if any of the trusted ones(?) have written to the colony office at Kaneš, return a (good) word on our behalf and let your message come.
Inform us.
Our dear brother, we are taking care to send your youth on his way.

Concerning the matter of Ennam-Aššur son of Šū-Enlil about which you wrote, we .. and he said, "When Pūšu-kēn was there, Iliya and Aššur-imittī stood and added 5 minas silver from Uṣur-ša-Aššur to all the tablets.
He added the 1/3 minas of the house of A-...
3 minas x shekels beloning to PN and 20 minas silver, at a rate of 5 minas per year I will say.
To these (Besides these?) my matters are concluded.
If you(pl.) are in agreement write my tablet.
If you do not swear(?) let (me) go.
Because b. not in your tablet, you did not agree.
Let your message come as to whether it is one or the other.
Regarding the matter of Uṣur-ša-Ištar about which you wrote, their matters with Imdī-ilim are concluded.
We seized him and he said, "Let me clear myself from my father so that I can say one or the other to you (pl.).
We seized Ennāmnum and he said, "Ennum-Aššur did not pay out any silver."
Aššur-nādā his brother ... answered, "Aššur-nādā did not pay.
... did not deposit and ...

From Puzur-Ištar and Idnaya to Ennam-Aššur:
Regarding the copper belonging to Pūšu-kēn, do not come to an agreement with anyone.
From Puzur Ištar:
Let your message come on the (same) day when the copper goes out so that I myself may send the corresponding silver.
From Idnaya:
As for mine, I will send the corresponding silver.

target_raw:
Folgendermaßen Abūšalim an Stelle des Ilī’alum. Zu Aššurimittī sprich!
Folgendermaßen (sprachen) hier die Gläubiger des Aššurištikal und des . . . [. . . . ] . . tili, des Sohnes des Aššurištikal zu Ām[u]raššur, dem Sohne des Sukalli’a:
"10 Minen Silber — die Abgabe war hinzugefügt — , mit seinen Siegeln, hat Aššurištikal, unser Händler, von Purušḫattum nach Kaniš, an das Haus des Ilī’ālum, daß es (dort) zur Bewachung deponiert werde, schicken (wollen) (?) ; [x Min]en 50 (?) Seqel Silber hast du erhalten.
Ferner, [x M]inen [x Seq]el Silber und den Zins dazu war Pilaḫištar, der Sohn des Aššu[rm]alik, auf Grund einer Doppel-urkunde dem Aššurištikal, unserem Händler, schuldig geworden; das Silber und den Zins dazu hast du erhalten.
2 Minen 9 Seqel Silber und den Zins dazu war Tarāmilī, der Sohn des Palšeni (?), auf Grund einer Doppelurkunde dem Aššurištikal, unserem Händler, schuldig geworden; das Si[lber] und den Zins dazu hast du erhalten, 1 ⅓ Minen 2⅓ Seqel Silber, die ,Drittanteile‘ des Aššurištikal, unseres Händlers, hast du vom bīt kārim erhalten.
15 Stoffe hast du in Zalpa von der Ware des Aššurištikal, unseres Händlers, gekauft; Aššurlamassī, der Sohn des Šūkūbum, hat (sie) deiner Hand übergeben.
6 Stoffe vom Palasttore hat Aššurištikal, unser Händler, dir gegeben.
Dies alles, das Silber unseres Händlers, hast du insgesamt erhalten."
Vor diesen beiden leugne oder bestätige (es).
Unser Händler ist tot; doch nach dem Silber unseres Händler haben wir die Suche aufgenommen.
Du sollst uns nicht Mühe machen; viel Silber sollst du uns nicht ausgeben lassen. (Also) bitte!

2 Minen 9 Seqel Silber und den Zins dazu war Tarāmilī, der Sohn des Palšeni (?), auf Grund einer Doppelurkunde dem Aššurištikal, unserem Händler, schuldig geworden; das Si[lber] und den Zins dazu hast du erhalten, 1 ⅓ Minen 2⅓ Seqel Silber, die ,Drittanteile‘ des Aššurištikal, unseres Händlers, hast du vom bīt kārim erhalten.
15 Stoffe hast du in Zalpa von der Ware des Aššurištikal, unseres Händlers, gekauft; Aššurlamassī, der Sohn des Šūkūbum, hat (sie) deiner Hand übergeben.
6 Stoffe vom Palasttore hat Aššurištikal, unser Händler, dir gegeben.
Dies alles, das Silber unseres Händlers, hast du insgesamt erhalten."
Vor diesen beiden leugne oder bestätige (es).
Unser Händler ist tot; doch nach dem Silber unseres Händler haben wir die Suche aufgenommen.
Du sollst uns nicht Mühe machen; viel Silber sollst du uns nicht ausgeben lassen. (Also) bitte!
label: Cuneiform Tablet ATHE 48

20 kutānu-textiles and 1 pirikannu-textile Pithala and Watkam are bringing to you.
Here Nannaya, the son of Ikuhlum, entrusted it to them.
Give them each 1 1/3 shekel of silver as wages.
Then you must mark the hems of the textiles, acting as my representatives.
If you know the men, then question the men from Timelkia there, the earlier messengers who stay there.
If this letter catches up with Šalim-Adad, then seal 5 minas of tiri silver from my stocks of silver, that he may bring it here.
Šalim-Adad must not stay for a single night, but send him.
Ilī-ālum and Inar should come together with him.
If Šalim-Adad has already left or is unwilling to come, then give the 5 minas of silver to a trustworthy affiliated trader, so he may bring it.
If there is no affiliated trader, then give 2 minas of silver to Ilī-ālum, and let him be bound by a contract in Kaneš, so that Inar and he may come overland.
Do give Ilī-ālum instructions there!

To Kurub-Ištar:
Who are you to Aššūr-imittī that you took 2 minas of silver belonging to Imlikaya?
Now, your tablet of the house of Aššur-rēʾī which states 2 minas 15 shekels as your debt, pay the debt and its interest!
And as for the 2 minas silver of Imlikaya which you seized, pay it out!
If A
To P
My dear brothers, hd' to the inventory of Abī-anum.
At your departure, balance my account so that we give 1 minas 7 shekels silver from your account.
Also, take the weapon of Abī-anum and so take the silver he owes to.

You opened the tin and textiles on (your own) authority and you took for yourself.
A 6 shekel rate silver for our tin.

On the day you read (this) tablet, give the silver to Pūšu-kēn and Dān-Aššur.
If you already gave it to Abela, line up your witnesses there, and take it from Abela’s silver, and give it to our representatives.

From Alāhum to Aššur-nādā:
1 package containing 30 minas of silver, its import duty added; 1 package containing 10 minas, its import duty added; 1 package of 11 minas from the votive offerings in all: 51 minas of silver in 3 packages, its import duty added, Puzur-šadue is bringing to you.
If Puzur-šadue is delayed well, if there is a caravan (and) Kulbānum is there, then attach one of my servants to Kulbānum, pack their 30 minas and entrust it to an affiliated trader, so they may bring it to the City.
Give the transport tariff on the silver out of my silver.

Open the tablet containers and send me the tablet concerning 10 minas of silver, Nanna's and Akaya's debt, plus the two shekels of gold which Imlikaya deposited.

From Alāhum to Aššur-nādā and Puzur-Anna:
Open the tablet containers and send me the tablet concerning 10 minas of silver, Nanna's and Akaya's debt, plus the two shekels of gold which Imlikaya deposited.
Further, among the tablets which were put away--Aššur-amārum knows where they were placed--send me the tablet concerning 20 minas of silver relating to Šikmatum
If you have already given the two minas of silver to Ilī-ālum, then send later on with a trustworthy affiliated trader 3 further minas of silver.
label: Cuneiform Tablet TC 2 31

target_raw:
From Alāhum and Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā and Šū-Aššur;
(specifically) to Aššur-nādā:
the same day that you hear the letter Šū-Aššur shall give you 1 talent of tin under the seal of the City, and you must pay him 10 minas of refined silver promptly.
Why is it that you say:
"You have seized my silver"?
Have I removed any of your silver in this way?
Deduct it from the merchandise transported by Ennam-BēIum.
But you yourself, have you not sent the 15 minas that you promised me from Abum to where it pleased you?
As to the silver transported by Izimea and Hinnaya, their terms are overdue.
Send the silver as soon as possible.
With respect to the certified tablet with the testimony of Lā-qēp and Aššur-imittī son of Amur-ilī, before he leaves you must show him the tablet with his testimony and his seal with one of the travellers as witness, and then erase the words on the tablet.
Please, take Alāhum's tin and scatter it among colleagues and friends and pay him his silver.

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Here 'difficulties' (Veenhof) 'procedures'? have arisen concerning the saptinnū and pirikannū textiles and the woollen blankets (?), and many people have been fined.
You also they condemned to pay 10 minas silver.
You may pay in annual installments of 1 mina silver.
We borrowed on your behalf 7 1/2 minas of silver against interest from (the silver) transported by Kulumaya.
Send the silver and its interest.
Urgent, do not deal in
Your house and your servants are well.
The decrees of the City are binding!

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the matter about which you wrote, we gathered your investors for you.
They read your letter and said, "Whatever wages he has received, income he has gained, houses he has bought, who ..."
... Šamaš-taklāku brings ...
Aššur-nādā son of Puzur-Anna and the son of Mešar-rābi did not want to seal the tablet.
They said, "We also (will agree) if our partners are in agreement. (?)
Make your own decision there.
Šū-Ḫubur did not want to give us the 5 1/2 minas silver of the son of Qayyātum.
He said, "To his brother, (I will go and ...) I will give him ...
We will (give?) 2/3 mina silver which ... to your brother. From the silver ... Aššur-imittī ...

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
You sent me 2 minas less 1/2 shekel of silver with Ištar-pilah.
I took 1 mina 1/2 shekel out of what belongs to Šū-Ištar.
In all: 3 minas of silver.
Thereof I paid 7 1/2 shekels as import duty; since the (money for the) transport tariff had been spent, I paid the balance of 1 shekel of silver.
The rest of your silver amounts to 2 5/6 minas 1 1/2 shekel.
I borrowed 8 shekels of silver from the son of Abu-šalim and his partner.
I still had a claim against you of 2 2/3 minas 1 1/4 shekels of silver, the proceeds from the sale of the textiles transported by Ennam-Bēlum.
You said: ''The silver has been taken."
But I forgot to seal it, (and) since I have no silver available to me I have taken it.
I also still had a claim of 16 shekels 15 grains of silver from the goods transported by the son of Irnuid.
In all: 2 5/6 minas 7 1/3 shekels have been deducted, so the rest of your silver is 2 1/6 shekels.
It has been spent for you.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
Alulaya's son gave me his word here, saying:
"As soon as I arrive I shall send you 6 minas of silver."
1 mina of silver is owed by Puzur-Ištar's son, and take care to send Zuzu's silver as well as what he borrowed from you as soon as possible.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
Concerning Iddin-Suen about whom you wrote, I seized Iddin-Suen and in the presence of our colleagues I said:
"You have taken silver from my outstanding claims!
Give me my silver!"
He answered: "I have not taken any of your silver."
So, he cheated me with the account, for he has taken at least 1 or 2 minas of silver at my expense.
"Reckoned from the month Tanbarta in the eponymy of Ibni-Adad son of Baqqunum he is to pay 1 mina per year
He carries goods worth at least 1 or 2 minas of silver.
Seize him there and act as a gentleman.
Please, free yourself of claims, set out and come at harvest time together with Aššur-malik.
If you have taken tin as an investment loan, then give it in commission to agents on fixed terms.
Please, send me Ilī-ālum with the first travelers.

target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
Of the silver from my votive offerings you should make me a sun-disk for divine Assur, weighing 1 mina of gold.
Seal the import duty separately.
Place 6 minas of good quality scrap silver plus its import duty, for divine Ištar, under seal.
Seal then also the rest of the silver and send it to me.
Then you must clear yourself of obligations, set out and come here.
People less important than you administer joint-stock investments, and people less important than me have invested 10 minas of gold.
Come and take over a joint-stock capital and only then leave again.
Also, if anyone there wants to give you anything, then take it!
Come then, for here there is no opportunity for receiving anything.
The god is full of anger towards you because of your journey.
Set out and come!
The 10 minas of silver which you will give me in addition will serve as a substitute for your own life.
As to the votive offerings for divine Assur and Ištar, the textiles from Gasur and for divine Ilabrat--if there is not enough, then have it shipped.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā, Ilī-ālum and Aššur-taklāku:
Both once and twice I have written, saying: "Send me the 30 minas of silver from Ilī-ālum's previous transport!"
Both once and twice travelers have come here.
Why, then, have you not sent the silver?
Please, send 30 minas of silver with the very first traveler.
The men are standing right behind me.
Please, take care and send it to me!

Why have you written me as follows: "Alāhum's son will seize some of your silver"?
To whom do I owe anything, since it was our agreement that Šū-Aššur will place 2 talents of tin under the seal of the City authorities, before you and you will choose one of the two and pay 10 minas of silver promptly.
Moreover, that very tablet Šū-Aššur brought you personally!
Why should he seize any silver?

If indeed I owe anything to his father, then let him write to his father, so that his father can seize me here.
Take what tin you are able to there, and act like a gentleman and give them the silver.
The matter should not be heard there.

From Aššur-imittī to Pūšu-kēn:
I owe you 5 1/2 minas refined silver here.
What is it that you wrote?
You said, "I withheld silver from your account to your accountings, to your sales."
I don't owe money there!
Indeed! You're witholding silver which I owe you here!
Now, if I do not owe silver there, then weigh out (silver) from your own funds and let it be a loan from you for a month or two!
Everything? goes to Kurub-Ištar and you have not collected silver from the son of Al-āḫum or from my merchants whose terms are full, and you have not sent it.
I anticpated 20 minas or more of silver.
You sent (only) 7 minas.
My dear brother, in whom else can I trust there?
Heed my instructions!
Wherever there is a single shekel of silver, collect it and send it!
From my merchandise to my accountings ... my own share/spendable cash.
Enter into the house of Aššūr-rēʾī and take my tablets so that they are in your possession.
At the very moment of his dying, he said something about my name!
He took something of my silver there!
Learn (something) and send me some good news and thus inform me.
Also, his silver of his naruqqum, no one must take anything.
Seal it and send it here.
Concerning the purchase of the son of Šū-Aššur about which you wrote, "If I do not write you anything in the next 10 days, I will clear you."

From Aššur-imittī to Pūšu-kēn:
I owe you 5 1/2 minas refined silver here.
What is it that you wrote?
You said, "I withheld silver from your account to your accountings, to your sales."
I don't owe money there!
Indeed! You're witholding silver which I owe you here!
Now, if I do not owe silver there, then weigh out (silver) from your own funds and let it be a loan from you for a month or two!
Everything? goes to Kurub-Ištar and you have not collected silver from the son of Al-āḫum or from my merchants whose terms are full, and you have not sent it.
I anticpated 20 minas or more of silver.
You sent (only) 7 minas.
My dear brother, in whom else can I trust there?
Heed my instructions!
Wherever there is a single shekel of silver, collect it and send it!
From my merchandise to my accountings ... my own share/spendable cash.
Enter into the house of Aššūr-rēʾī and take my tablets so that they are in your possession.
At the very moment of his dying, he said something about my name!
He took something of my silver there!
Learn (something) and send me some good news and thus inform me.
Also, his silver of his naruqqum, no one must take anything.
Seal it and send it here.
Concerning the purchase of the son of Šū-Aššur about which you wrote, "If I do not write you anything in the next 10 days, I will clear you."
It is lost./not here.
If ..., send so that with you I may be able to šxx.
label: Cuneiform Tablet TC 2 15

From Aššur-imittī to Pūšu-kēn:
I sent out to you your goods, tin and textiles, in the caravan of the son of Šū-Enlil son of Idī-Aššur.
We despatched to you that which (arose) from your silver and the silver which the son of Erraya brought.
If you are my brother, send the silver from your own funds and from my tin immediately.
Concerning the matter of Šū-Aššur son of Al-aḫum about which I wrote you, take care to enter into the man's house and let your message come as to how much he left.
Also, concerning my tin which the son of Ikkupya and Il-bāni gave to him there, turn to his brother and say, "The man is a man of favors. Do not overvalue every shekel!"
Let him give the silver to you.
Send it to me immediately.

Kurara and Idī-Aššur brought to you 22 kutānum-textiles, 1 black donkey.
Idī-Aššur sent to you 1 talent 5 minas tin and you sent 9 2/3 minas silver and then it was lost.

From Aššur-imittī to Pūšu-kēn:
Your message came and on the same day that I heard your letter, I gave 3 minas silver to your (pl?) representatives, and thay purchased tin for you.
My dear brother, from one traveler, "Send my sivler immediately!
But when I weighed out silver, I sent (it) to you and your message has not returned.
Say to Imdi-ilum: "Send as much as can be sent of the profits of my (Aššur-imittī's) wheat grain."

target_raw:
From Aššur-nādā to Šišahšušar:
As to the fact that I wrote to you, saying: "At the beginning of spring I shall write to you, so you can come here"-- do not be angry at me!
The cold weather caught up with us, and consequently the capital that I was expecting reverted to outstanding claims, and that is the reason I did not write to you.
Why do you say: "I cannot manage"?
The capital that I could lay my hands on-- very little could be had, much has been lost.
When I arrive at Kunanamit I shall take what there is to take, and as soon as I leave again I shall write to you so you can come to me.
As you know, my funds he must not touch.
If he comes, do not look the other way and do not give it to him.
But if he asks you for the girl, then let him take Sarna and his children where he wants.

My dear brother, my message has come to you once or twice.
I said, "Take every single shekel of silver for the blood of the youth, as much as Ennum-Aššur son of Qarwiya recieved, and send to me."
As for me, why is it that no message of your has come whatsoever?

Get both the silver that my messenger took and whatever is available from Ennam-Aššur son of Qarwiya, and seal the silver and send it to me.

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Šū-Ḫubur did not want to give us the silver of Qayyātum's son.
He said, "Let him take it there."
So take it there.
As for the silver which you owe to Aššur-imittī, we borrowed (silver) on interest and we paid him.
We borrowed 6 talents of copper for the price of the houses and we paid it out.
Send the silver so that the interest does not become burdensome to you.
Tupezi stood to face the city council and we cleared his obligations(?).
We said, "There inasa (?) your house is spoken(?)"

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
In your letter you wrote, "My hand is placed on the silver."
You said, "This belongs to Puzur-Anna; this belongs to Puzur-Aššur; it is not the property of the father."
In the next (letter) do not write thus.
Do not declare(?) to us the names of the men.
When you send goods and lay your hand, and when you send the silver to the city, declare "the merchant."
Do not declare to us the name of the man.
There, ... your shipping person(?) witnesses (?).
If not man and wife (?)would we be able to .. and take away the silver?

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Šuma-abiya and Mannum-bālum-Aššur arrived
They brought 8 1/2 minas of silver.
The excise-tax was not added.
According to your instructions, we ... the silver (in) the house of the merchant and made purchases for you.
3 donkeys (loaded with) kutānum-textiles, 1 donkey (loaded with) tin and its wrappings are yours. A donkey belonging to the merchant, and separately 1/2 donkey(load) of tin and one donkey (loaded with) kutānum-textiles--all this, purchases made, Šuma-abiya departed five days after Kiliya.
(There are) donkeys belonging to the merchant, and separately 1/2 donkey(load) of tin and one donkey (loaded with)
All this, purchases made, Šuma-abiya departed five days after Kiliya.
Your silver has not yet come.
We will dispatch Mannum-bālum-Aššur with the next travellers.
Concerning the matter of the merchant about which you wrote to us, you said, "Let him raise about 1/2 mina silver for my
He does not want to raise the money.
We borrowed 1 1/2 minas silver and we wrote 3 minas silver with the investors from the assets of the ebuttum-loan in a tablet under the name of the merchant.
All the merchants gave each 4 minas of silver to him.
If you desire, you yourself add 1 mina of silver to him.
Send the 1 1/2 minas of silver which we owe immediately.
We will return it.
From Šalim-Aššur: My dear brother, when Kiliya arrived I enrolled him for service and then dispatched him.
There, they are informed about the man.
Please, assist him so that (he does) according to his instructions.
Now take heed concerning the matter of Azua.
Urgent! Do not set him for dishonesty(?) so that it grieves me here.

From Aššur-bāni and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Šuma-abiya and Mannum-bālum-Aššur arrived
They brought 8 1/2 minas of silver.
The excise-tax was not added.
According to your instructions, we ... the silver (in) the house of the merchant and made purchases for you.
3 donkeys (loaded with) kutānum-textiles, 1 donkey (loaded with) tin and its wrappings are yours. A donkey belonging to the merchant, and separately 1/2 donkey(load) of tin and one donkey (loaded with) kutānum-textiles--all this, purchases made, Šuma-abiya departed five days after Kiliya.
(There are) donkeys belonging to the merchant, and separately 1/2 donkey(load) of tin and one donkey (loaded with)
All this, purchases made, Šuma-abiya departed five days after Kiliya.
Your silver has not yet come.
We will dispatch Mannum-bālum-Aššur with the next travellers.
Concerning the matter of the merchant about which you wrote to us, you said, "Let him raise about 1/2 mina silver for my
He does not want to raise the money.
We borrowed 1 1/2 minas silver and we wrote 3 minas silver with the investors from the assets of the ebuttum-loan in a tablet under the name of the merchant.
All the merchants gave each 4 minas of silver to him.
If you desire, you yourself add 1 mina of silver to him.
Send the 1 1/2 minas of silver which we owe immediately.
We will return it.
From Šalim-Aššur: My dear brother, when Kiliya arrived I enrolled him for service and then dispatched him.
There, they are informed about the man.
Please, assist him so that (he does) according to his instructions.
Now take heed concerning the matter of Azua.
Urgent! Do not set him for dishonesty(?) so that it grieves me here.

Aššur-bēl-malkim has here settled accounts with Aššur-rē'ī, and he must deposit for you 45 minas of tin sealed by Adad-ellat and Aššur-bāni from the second package that bears the seal of the City; then you must have brought 34 minas 15 shekels of tin and take it.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā.:
You have sent me 10 minas of silver.
Specification: 2 talents 10 minas (of tin) under seal at the rate 16 1/2 to 1 - in silver: 7 5/6 minas 2 2/3 shekels.
4 black textiles (and) 5
It diminished 7 shekels during the washing.
17 shekels of silver: the price of one donkey.
5 shekels: its harness.
1/3 mina of silver: the price of 2 black textiles that Aššur-taklāku left with you.
You said (earlier): ''Take that out of the 1 1/3 mina of silver that Ennam-Bē1um is bringing."
I did not take the silver then, (so) I have taken it out of this silver.
12 minas 5 shekels of tin for expenses at the rate 15 to 1 - in silver: 2/3 mina 8 1/3 shekels of silver.
Aššur-taklāku brings all this to you.
(Your money) has been spent for you.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā, Ilī-ālum and Aššur-taklāku:
Alulaya's son owes me 12 minas of silver.
Seize him and make him pay the silver.
If he is absent, then write to him by means of one of my servants to where he is staying, so he can make him pay the silver.
If his term (for repayment) is not up yet, then have him seal the silver in accordance with his contract.
Let him send it to his representatives and to me, so we can purchase goods for him.

From Aššur-idī to Ilī-ālum and Aššur-taklāku; to Ilī-ālum:
You know, twice you did this to me; now you have done it a third time!
Did you respect your lord when you did not send me the silver?
Travelers have come here at least twice, but you had acquired nothing and you have not sent me the 10 minas of silver.
For every mina or half mina they prowl around me.
Ask Usur-ša-Aššur there!
Please, send the rest of my silver with the caravan!
With respect to the 40 minas--I take divine Aššur as witness that I shall not make any concessions to you regarding interest, which runs from the month Mahhur-ili, eponymy Iddin-Aššur, the son of Kūbī-idī.
Clear the goods from your own caravan, set out and come here.
Also, Aššur-taklāku must clear his goods, set out and come here --before harvest.

From Aššur-idī to Puzur-Ištar and Asqūdum:
As to the 1 talent 5 minas of tin under seal which Asqūdum is bringing, as soon as you reach Timelkia you must say as follows to Šū-Ištar, the son of Azuda: "Aššur-idī's orders were that you should take at least 1 talent of tin to do him a favour."
Draw up a certified tablet concerning his debt to run for 25 weeks.
If he does not agree with you, then draw it up for 30 or 35 weeks.
Certify his tablet and entrust it to Aššur-nādā.
Also, make him take as many textiles as an investment as you can.
Bring the rest of the textiles to Aššur-nādā.
If you are truly my brothers, take care and do me this favour.
To Asqūdum: If Puzur-Ištar <is absent>, then hand it over personally to him.
Set 3 witnesses for him.
source: translate Akkadian to English: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na PUZUR4.IŠTAR ù ás-qú-dim qí-bi4-ma 1 GÚ 5 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ki ša ás-qú-dum na-áš-ú a-na-ma tí-me-el-ki-a ta-kà-ša-dá-ni um-ma a-tù-nu-ma a-na šu-IŠTAR DUMU a-zu-da qí-bi4-a-ma té-er-tí a-šùr-i-dí AN.NA 1 GÚ li-qé-ma gi-mì-lam i-na ṣé-ri-šu šu-ku-un ṭup-pu-šu ḫa-ar-ma-am ša ḫu-bu-li-šu lu-up-ta a-na 25 ḫa-am-ša-tim šu-ma lá i-ma-ga-ar-ku-nu a-na 30 lu 35 ḫa-am-ša-tim lu-up-ta ṭup-pu-šu ḫi-ir-ma-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da pì-iq-da ù TÚG.ḪI.A ma-lá ta-le-e-a-ni ad-ma-ta-ni ší-tí TÚG.ḪI.A a-na ṣé-er a-šùr-na-da bi4-il5 šu-ma a-ḫu-a-a a-tù-nu ḫi-da-ma gi-mì-lam i-ṣé-ri-a šu-uk-na a-na ás-qú-dim qí-bi4-ma šu-ma PUZUR4.IŠTAR a-ta-ma té-bi-ir ší-bi4 3 šu-ku-šu-um
target:
From Aššur-idī to Puzur-Ištar and Asqūdum:
As to the 1 talent 5 minas of tin under seal which Asqūdum is bringing, as soon as you reach Timelkia you must say as follows to Šū-Ištar, the son of Azuda: "Aššur-idī's orders were that you should take at least 1 talent of tin to do him a favour."
Draw up a certified tablet concerning his debt to run for 25 weeks.
If he does not agree with you, then draw it up for 30 or 35 weeks.
Certify his tablet and entrust it to Aššur-nādā.
Also, make him take as many textiles as an investment as you can.
Bring the rest of the textiles to Aššur-nādā.
If you are truly my brothers, take care and do me this favour.
To Asqūdum: If Puzur-Ištar <is absent>, then hand it over personally to him.
Set 3 witnesses for him.
label: Cuneiform Tablet TC 3 95

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-la-ḫi-im e-la-ma pu-šu-ke-en6 ù a-šur-ta-ak-la-ku qí-bi-ma a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu iš-tù MU.ŠÈ 30 i-na a-lim{ki} wa-áš-ba-ku-ma ù ni-kà-sí ù-ša-qal kà-ra-am mu-ùḫ-ra-ma iš-tí kà-ri-im e-na-na-tim er-ša-nim-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na-um 3 GÍN la-dí a šu-ma la i-ma-gu5-ru sà-li-a-ma a-mì-šál a-we-el la-zi-iz a-tù-nu la tí-de8-a-a a-i-a-tum ma-áš-kà-na-tù-a e-la ma-la ma-áš-kà-tám la áš-ku-nam 37 ma-na ni-kà-sí áš-qúl gi-mì-lam i-ṣé-ri-a šu-ku-na iḫ-da-ma a-na mì-šál a-we-el la-zi-iz a-ni-tum la a-ni-tum té-er-ta-ak-nu i pá-ni-e-ma li-li-kam iš-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pí-a 4.5 ma-na ša ku-ru-ub-IŠTAR ba-áb-tám e-zi-ba-ku-nu-tí-ni ša-áš-qí-la-ma šé-bi-la-nim
target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Alāhum, Elamma, Pūšu-kēn and Aššur-taklāku:
My dear brothers, for 30 years I have been living in the City and I always pay the accounts.
Appeal to the colony authorities and ask for clemency for me from the colony, so that for the silver I may deposit 3 shekels per mina.
If they do not agree with you, then implore them that I may stand only for half a share of a man.
Are you not aware which are my deposits?
Should I make another deposit on top of that?
I have paid 37 minas on the account!
Do me this favor.
Take care to have me stand only for half a share of a man.
Send me word whether this is so or not as soon as possible.
Together with my other silver I left 4 1/2 minas belonging to Kurub-Ištar as an outstanding claim with you.
Have it paid and send it to me.

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-mur-IŠTAR a-la-ḫi-im ì-lí-a-lim ù a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku qí-bi-ma 2 GÚ 10 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ku 38 TÚG.ḪI.A ú-ṣú-ur-ša-a-šùr ub-lá-ku-nu-tí ig-ri sà-ri-dim ša-bu mì-ma lá ta-da-na-šu-um i-na pá-ni-im ṭup-pì-a áš-pu-ra-ku-nu-tí um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-na ì-lí-a-lim dí-na mì-ma lá ta-da-na-šu-um i-na ša šé-pì-šu AN.NA-kam ma-dam il5-té-qé i-na TÚG.ḪI-tí-a ma-ṭí-ú-tim li-qé-a-ma a-na ni-kà-sí-a qá-tí i-ta-dí-a ší-tí TÚG.ḪI-tí-a ù 2 GÚ AN.NA a-na a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku dí-na-ma a-šar KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN ba-lá-ṭí-a lu-bi-il5 ù šu-ma lá-šu a-šar wa-áš-bu šé-bi4-lá-šu-um šu-ma ḫa-ra-šu a-ni AN.NA-ki ù TÚG.ḪI-a i-na ṣé-er DAM.GÀR ke-nim a-na u4-me id-a DAM.GÀR lu ke-en6 u4-me ITU.KAM ú ITU.KAM lá ta-be-e-lá a-na i-lí-a-lim qí-bi4-ma ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pì-a pá-ni-im 20 ma-na i-na pá-ni-im-ma šé-bi4-lam lu-qú-tám ša šé-pì-kà za-ki-a-ma ti-ib-a-ma a-tal-kam ṭup-pu-um a-num wa-ar-ki
target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Amur-Ištar, Al-āḫum, Ilī-ālum and Aššur-taklāku.
Uṣur-ša-Aššur has brought to you 2 talents 10 minas of tin under seal and 38 textiles.
The wages of the driver are paid.
Do not give him anything.
In my previous letter I wrote to you:
"Give (the goods) to Ilī-ālum."
Do not give him anything.
He took much tin from his own caravan.
Out of my textiles, take those of lesser quality and deposit them as my share on my account.
Give the remainder of the textiles and 2 talents of tin to Aššur-taklāku and let him bring that to wherever there is even a small profit for me.
If he is absent, send (the goods) to him wherever he is staying.
If he is on a journey, well, then entrust my tin and textiles witn a trustworthy agent in commission on fixed terms.
The agent must be reliable!
Do not attach importance to terms of one or two months.
To Ili-ālim: Send me the rest of the previous silver shipment, 2 minas as soon as possible.
Clear the goods from your own caravan, set out and come here.
This tablet is late(?)...

target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā and Aššur-takl
2 talents 10 minas of tin under seal, 34 kutānu-textiles, 2 black donkeys, 2/3 mina of silver and 6 minas of tin for his expenses--he must calculate for you the value of the tin in silver using the rate which is established by the enterprise--as for the donkey-driver, his wages have been paid for the stretch until Hurama--with all this Šuli son of Puzur-Aššur is on his way to you.
Entrust both the tin and the textiles, as many as are cleared in the palace, to Aššur-taklāku and let him bring it to where there is even a single shekel of profit for me.
And do send me word.
If he is not there, then send it to him where he is.

target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
If you are delayed, then divide every single shekel of silver, as much as you carry, into two parts, and send me one half as soon as possible;
then send the rest with the following transport.
While you are delayed I shall make purchases and return that to you, so every single shekel of silver may be available.
If you are en route, then send me nothing, but bring me the silver personally.
Why do you keep doing things that the gods do not allow, so your sin becomes ever heavier?
Have you forgotten the words of the gods?
Why are the words of the gods not important for you?
Until now you have delayed.
As for me, in whom can I trust?
You must not fraudulently keep sending me silver in such a way that the colony authorities do not collect the transport tariff.
Please, send him as soon as possible; also, let him bring the girl.
If his silver is tied up in Kanes I shall restore it to him.
Send him!

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma mì-šu ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni um-ma a-ta-ma i-na KÙ.BABBAR-kà ni-kà-sí-a a-ša-qal pá-kà ta-dí-nam pá-i ú-la a-dí-na-kum ù tù-sà-li-a-ma IGI a-šùr-ka-ší-id 20 {TÚG}ku-ta-ni a-qí-ša-kum iš-tù a-wa-tù-ni gám-ra-ni lu i-ší-im TÚG.ḪI-tí lu i-ší-im ANŠE.ḪI.A KÙ.BABBAR pá-ni-a-ma 10 ma-na šé-bi-lá-ma 2 GÚ AN.NA la-áš-a-ma lu-šé-bi-la-ku-ma né-ma-al-šu a-ku-kúl mì-ma i-na KÙ.BABBAR-pì-a la ta-kál li-ba-kà i-zi-iz li-ba-kà la ta-za-az me-ra-kà ú-ra-bi-ma um-ma šu-ut-ma lá a-bi a-ta it-bi-a-ma i-tal-kam ù me-er-ú-wa-tí-kà ù-ra-bi-ma um-ma ší-na-ma la a-bu-ni a-ta i-na ša-al-ší-ma u4-mì-im it-bi-a-ma i-tal-kà-ku-ma tí-ir-ta-kà lu i-de8
target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
Why have you written to me as follows: "I shall pay my accounts with your silver.
You gave me your word."
I did not give you my word!
However, you begged me, so with Aššur-kāšid as witness I gave you a gift of 20
Since our affairs are concluded, you must send me at least ten minas of silver both from the proceeds of the textiles and from the proceeds of the donkeys, the first silver you may obtain, I shall then buy two talents of tin and send that to you, so you can enjoy the profit.
Do not use any of my silver!
If you wish, then stand!
If you do not wish to, then don't stand.
I have raised your son, but he said to me: "You are not my father."
He got up and left.
Also your daughters have I raised, but they said:
Three days later they got up and left to go to you, so let me know what you think.

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi4-ma a-wa-at DINGIR dan-na a mì-nim a-na ni-qí-ša-tí-kà a-na É en-na-nim ta-tù-nu-ar a-pu-tum lá ta-tù-ar ta-ḫa-li-iq a-šùr-ma-lik šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN a-na e-bu-ṭim i-da-na-kum lá ta-ma-ḫa-ar-šu šu-ma ki-ma šu-wa-tí ú-ša-za-az-kà lá ta-za-az um-ma a-ta-ma ḫa-ra-ni a-na a-lim{ki} a-šu-mì tí-ir-tim ša PUZUR4-a-šùr lá té-ra-áb-ma TÚG.ḪI.A lá ta-ta-na-dí-in qá-dí um-me-a-ni a-ḫi-ta-ma i-zi-iz ù ṣí-it TÚG.ḪI.A ù KÙ.BABBAR du-gu5-ul šu-ma TÚG.ḪI.A i-zu-zu i-zi-iz-ma a-na qá-tí-ni zu-uz iš-tù a-šùr ú-ša-pá-lá-ni a-na ma-tim ki-li-ša IR-du-tám lá ta-ṣa-ba-at <gap>-ma-sú wa-ṣa-am lá i-mu-ú ša i-ta-na-dí-nu li-ta-dí-in a-ta lá ṭá-ḫi
target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
The words of the gods are urgent!
Why do you keep renouncing your obligations towards the house of Ennānum?
Please, do not renounce them!
You will perish!
As to Aššur-malik, if he wants to give you even 1 shekel of silver as an investment loan, don't receive it!
If he appoints you to be his representative, don't do it!
Say instead: "I am on my way to the City."
Because of Puzur-Aššur's message you are not to enter (the house), and do not try to sell the textiles all over the place.
Stand outside together with the shareholders and observe the textiles and the silver that come out.
If they divide up the textiles, then stand by and join in the division for our share.
Since divine Assur would then humble me, do not become a slave of the entire country.
... they refuse to come out, let he who wishes to sell all over the place do so, but you should not be involved.

... they refuse to come out, let he who wishes to sell all over the place do so, but you should not be involved.
label: Cuneiform Tablet CCT 3 2b-3a (BM 113314)

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi4-ma ki-ma šé-ep {d}IŠKUR ra-pì-dim é-tí ra-ḫi-iṣ ù a-ta ta-da-lá-ak a-pu-tum a-wa-at ì-lí ú-ṣú-ur a-na ni-ki-iš-tim ša DINGIR i-ṣí-ra-ku-ni lá ta-tù-ar ta-tù-ar-ma ta-ḫa-li-iq AN.NA ša i-na ṭup-pì-im lá-pu-ta-ku-ni i-na ni-kà-sí le-eš-ku-nu ù a-ta 6 ma-na 8 GÍN AN.NA ni-a-am i-na ni-kà-sí šu-ku-un 2 ma-na 13.33333 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR zu-zu ḫa-bu-la-am 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR iš-tí {d}UTU-ba-ni DUMU PUZUR4.IŠTAR 0.5 ma-na 5 GÍN KÙ iš-tí {d}en-LÍL-ba-ni 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša iḫ-bu-lá-ku-ni 0.5 ma-na ša a-šùr-{d}UTU-ši ub-lá-ku-ni AN.NA-ak-šu 8 ma-na ù 6 ma-na AN.NA ša a-na ILLAT ta-ša-ku-nu le-qé-ma ŠU.NÍGIN 14 ma-na 8 GÍN AN.NA a-na ṣé-er KÙ.BABBAR i-dí-ma i-na a-li-ki-im ku-nu-uk-ma i-na e-ra-bi-kà-ma ki-ma šu-ul-mì-kà šé-bi4-lá-am šu-ma a-ḫi a-ta li-bi4 lá tù-lá-ma-an šu-ma {d}UTU-ba-ni lá-šu 1 ma-na KÙ me-eḫ-ra-tim i-dí-ma šé-bi4-lá-am a-pu-tum qá-tí a-na ni-kà-sí i-ta-dí
target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
As if by the foot of divine Adad in full rush my house is devastated!
But as for you-- you have gone away!
Please, please heed the words of the gods!
Do not renounce the decision that the god has drawn up for you.
If you renounce it, you will perish!
The tin for which I was booked in the tablet, let them claim that at the accounting.
Then you too may claim 6 minas 8 shekels of tin belonging to us at the accounting.
Zuzu owes me 2 minas 13 1/3 shekels of silver.
1 mina of silver is owed by Šamaš-bāni son of Puzur-Ištar.
1/2 minas 5 shekels of silver is owed by Illil-bāni.
The 1 mina of silver which he borrowed from you, the 1/2 mina which Aššur-šamšī has brought to you-in tin it was 8 minas--plus the 6 minas of tin that you will claim for the enterprise--take that, and then add in all
If you are truly my brother, do not make me angry.
If Šamaš-bāni is absent, then make available a corresponding amount of 1 mina of silver and send it to me.
Please, let my share be deposited at the time of the accounting.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā:
In accordance with Šū-Kūbum's tablet according to which on account of the servant he owes silver for a fibula, deduct 8 shekels of silver and make him pay the rest the very day he arrives, and send it to me.
Please, make him pay.
Send me Iddin-Šamaš.

target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā, Ilī-ālum and Aššur-taklāku:
How come that your brother has been ill for 2 years now, but you have held him back?
Please, on the very day you hear this letter you must write and let him come.
Have Ilī-ālum seal 20 minas of silver for me and let him send me furthermore the 8 minas of silver that he owes on the maintenance of the house.
Have also Aššur-nādā seal as much as he makes and send it to me.
Send Aššur-taklāku with the very first travelers.
Let Ilī-ālum leave in the spring.
To Aššur-nādā: Your forgot 11 minas of tin belonging to Alāhum in the safe when you left.
We shall send it with travelers.
40+[x minas of] tin and 2/3 minas of silver ...

target_raw:
From Aššur-idī to Aššur-nādā and Aššur-taklāku:
to Aššur-nādā:
You have sent me 1/2 mina of silver from your votive offerings for divine Aššur.
They are bringing to you 4 kutānu-textiles.
I gave 3 shekels for his expenses.
Let him establish the tin for you at the same rate they use (for others).
Šū-Nūnu brings it to you.
To Aššur-taklāku:
You sent me 1/2 mina 5 shekels of silver from your votive offerings to divine Aššur.
I have deducted its [import] duty and ....
and the rest of the silver
break
.... Clear it and send the silver to me with the first traveler.
To Aššur-taklāku:
If you are truly my brother, clear the remainder of the goods transported by you and send it to me, and let it be shipped (even) while you are delayed so every shekel of silver is active for me.
I wrote you about the later shipment I sent to you, saying:
"I have to pay the money to the City Hall."
Take care and send me the silver.

target_raw:
From Aš[šur-idī] to Aššur-nādā and Ilī-ālum:
When you were about to leave I said to you: "When I have cleared myself of obligations, I shall write to you.'"
I have now cleared myself, and the words of the gods are urgent.
Please, the very day you hear this letter you must offer for sale the houses, both of your slave-girls and both of the grinding-girls, and then send me the silver!
Then you must rent a house and settle there.
Further, should there be no purchaser, you must still leave (the house).
Indeed, you must rent a house and settle there!
Please, please, pay heed to the words of the letter!
Sell everything!
If you have not sold (everything)--as the words of the gods are urgent--you will perish!
Do strive to give me every mina of silver and send word to me as soon as possible.
Also, send my servant to the house of ... Bread ... for ... we shall return to you, and Karriya's son who has paid 1 mina of silver.
My god is the owner of the silver.

From Aššur-idī to Aššur-taklāku:
With respect to my account, send word to your brothers with the travelers to make them stand (as representatives).
Concerning the silver I gave you, every shekel, as well as what I gave to you of your mother's, I have given a corresponding amount to your mother.
The silver has now become mine.
If the colony puts pressure on you, just say: "I shall deal with the silver on behalf of my father."
Do not mention anybody's name, and do not deposit any transport tariff.
If they force you to mention (anyone), then do so, but you will not share in the profit.

From Aššur-idī to Pūšu-kēn:
I gave 2/3 minas of silver and its excise to Puzur-Aššur.
Regarding the tablet of Puzur-Ištar of (his) debt to me, which I hold, why are you silent?
For whom are you waiting every day? (?)
Seize him and Ilabrat-bāni so that he writes me and they entrust the tablet with my seal to your representatives in the City.
Wait this day for a tablet.
But watch very (damn) carefully! (OR) But be very careful!
The house of my father is clearing accounts.
I hear that Ilabrat-bāni has decided to come to the city.

From Aššur-imittī and Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the houses of Ennānum about which you wrote me, "Buy the house."
They asked from us a lot of silver for the house and so we said, "Let's deposit our money together and so suppress the price about a mina."
Šalim-Aššur and Šū-Ḫubur, without consulting us, purchased the houses for you for 16 minas silver.
And they wrote to you.
They said, "We borrowed silver against interest for you."
We did not borrow any silver against interest on your behalf.
Šalim-aḫum paid ten minas silver towards the price of the house out of his own funds.
Make sure to send Šalim-aḫum's silver.
Also, send the remaining balance - 6 minas silver.
Aššur-imittī said, "I gave your silver to Ḫuraṣānum and it's gone, so I didn't pay. Don't be disappointed."
I gave your silver to Ḫuraṣānum and it's gone, so I didn't pay. Don't be disappointed.
I gave your silver to Ḫuraṣānum.
It's gone. (?)
I did not pay.
Don't be disappointed.

You have written as follows: "Binima-ahum has led 52
(The import duty was) 2 1/2
3 kutānu-textiles for pre-emption, and you will receive 1 1/2 shekel of silver in the storehouse; 4 1/3 shekels of silver accrued to you as losses in the sa'utum; for 5 kutānu-textiles you will receive 1 mina 45 shekels at the accounting; 6 kutānu-textiles have been taken from the sa'utum in the name of Elamma and Hinnaya; they will balance it with you at 16 shekels each, whereof we have received 28 shekels of silver; 7 1/2 kutānu-textiles have been taken for the transport tariff; Binima-ahum has received 1 kutānu-textile; 1/2 kutānu-textile was either loss

From Aššur-mālik and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the price of the textiles of the Akkadians about which you wrote, since you left, no Akkadians have come to the City.
Their land is in great turmoil, and if they arrive before winter and the prices are beneficial to you, we will purchase some for you.
We will even make the purchases with silver from our own funds.
Take heed to send the silver.
From Aššur-mālik:
My dear brother, because you took care to take out every single mina of silver of my outstanding claims, you have opened my eyes.
Also, the 10 minas of silver with Puzur-Ištar son of Aššur-mālik, take care to send it with the first departure.
I read your tablet and my heart was gladdened.
From Šalim-Aššur:
I dispatched the youth with Enna-Suen.
Also, Enna-Suen is bound (?) for your ten textiles which I gave to him.
My dear brother, if you wait, it will cause the youth to be sorrowful.
Let the youth live with you.
Enna Suen is bound to silver for my

I received 1 1/2 shekels silver on your behalf from Ennum-Aššur.

Witnesses: Ayyamšil, Ilī-rēʾī, Šaqṣum.

To Pūšu-kēn:
Ilī-mutabbil departed with the caravan.
Also, Adad-bāni and Kašlum departed with the express caravan.
The goods have departed toward you.
4 talents of tin under seals, 20 minas of tin for 'hand-tin', 8 black textiles as wrappings, 10 kutānum-textiles (in) two top-packs, three black donkeys and their gear ...

Also, clear(?) the youth there.
He must not contract any affliction.
Keep an eye on him.
He must not act inappropriately.
Clear your m.
Who ...

Why from ... which exist .. 4 1/2 minas ...

source_raw: um-ma a-šùr-na-da-ma a-na a-gu5-za ú-sú-ur-ša-a-šùr ù ší-ša-aḫ-šu-šar qí-bi-ma 17 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am iš-tù 8 MU.ŠÈ ku-zi-zi-a ḫa-bu-kam i-nu-mu wa-áš-ba-ku-ni aṣ-ba-sú-ma um-ma šu-ut-ma ú-nu-tí a-dí-ni lá e-ru-ba-am a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu ṣa-áb-ta-šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR ù ṣí-ba-sú ma-lá wa-as-mu ša-áš-qí-lá-šu lá ta-ga-mì-lá-šu a-na am-tim dí-na-šu nu-ḫe-en6 a-ša-ma-ma ANŠE ì-GIŠ ma-li-a-ma še-bi4-lá-nim ù ma-la ki-lá-le-ma šé-pé-a

From Aššur-nādā to Aguza, Uṣur-ša-Aššur and Šišahšušar:
7 shekels of refined silver Kuziziya borrowed from me 8 years ago.
When I stayed there I seized him, but he said: ''My merchandise has not arrived yet."
My dear brothers, seize him and make him pay the silver and as much interest as is fitting.
Do not show him any favours!
Give it to the maid.
Buy two leather pouches, load a donkey with oil, and send it to me.
Do send as much as both can contain.

target_raw:
From Aššur-nādā to Aššur-taklāku and Šišahšušar:
Why is it that you say: "Apart from 20 sacks of wheat and 15 sacks of barley that Kutubiš gave me there is nothing else (here)!"
Where are the 40 minas of sickles (and) the 2 1/2 minas of tin which Ilī-išar and Huluba brought to Šana?
Please, put pressure on Azu and Tutuli as well as Ka'uba with respect to the silver of Haluli, and soak 10 sacks of malt and 10 sacks of beer bread to prepare (beer).
If you want any grain then buy it.
If they are not careful with the outstanding claims, send me word.
Send me some oil.

From Aššur-nādā to Iddin-Suen and Šišahšušar:
Kuzuziya has owed me 17 shekels of refined silver, the price of an abarniu-textile, for 10 years.
How come that when the maid went, he insulted her?
The profit accrued on my silver, over which he has disposed for 10 years, make him pay that silver, and give it to the maid.
If he protests in any way, then let him swear by divine Aššur' s
Please, do not show him any favour.
Either he pays the silver or you make him take an oath!
15 shekels of silver is owed by Hartal.
His tin was pledged for it.
Make him pay the silver and have paid x mina 6 shekels of silver belonging to Dudu (and) 1/2 mina owed by Šarapunuwa, and give it to the maid(?).

From Aššur-nādā to Pūšu-kēn, Itūr-Ilī, and Ennam-Aššur:
Concerning the silver, the price of your house, and the house of Dalaš, the city rendered a verdict that the merchants of the house of Dalaš are rapš.
Now after Dalaš died, anyone of the merchants who took silver, maid, servant or consumable grain or anything, will return and will rpš before the dagger of Aššūr and stand for the 'gate' of the your(pl.) silver.
If merchants who took silver after the death of Dalaš return, the you(pl.) must return the price of the houses and take an oath, and stand for the 'gate'of my silver and send it to me.
If they who took (silver after the death of Dalaš) do not return, then you (pl.) do not return even a single shekel.
Let Pūšu-kēn pay so that you seal and send to me so that anyone who ther is(?) may ešu to me.
Let you rmessage come stating whoever it was who took the silver from the caravan of Aššūr-mālik, my kšdt, and 3 minas silver from the caravan of Buṣiya which they promised to pay,-- kutanina-ma(???).
To Pūšu-kēn:
My dear brother, the terms of the silver they mtd(?).
Seal the silver and send it to me.
Do not disappoint me.

From Aššur-nādā to Zuba, [ ... ] and Uṣur-ša-Aššur:
Pay on my behalf at the time of the accounting.
Send me word about how much the fees amounted to, and how much you recorded.
5 kutānu-textiles have been placed in Ennam-Bēlī's sa'utum.
Check the memorandum and add them on top of those on my account.
If Ilī-Anum has sent any silver, then do ship and send me the silver and his tablets.
The proceeds from 1 abarniu-textile is owed by a merchant ... 1 textile ...

target_raw:
From Assur-nada to Ili-alum:
Against the will of the god misfortune has fallen upon me, as they have just written to me in Uršu.
My dear brother, act in such a way that no evildoer may do harm to our father's house!
While I may still be saved-
9 talents 20 minas of my copper that Aššur-taklāku has given on commission to an agent, 8 talents 40 minas less 2/3 mina apart from the transport tariff, which you have bought for me---
out of which you sent 5 talents to Burušhaddum, and whose proceeds I have already received-
1 talent 18 minas of good broken copper, proceeds from the sale of 3 donkeys, which you gave in commission to an agent, of that 10 minas belong to you, 25 minas of good (copper), proceeds from the sale of a donkey, owed by me.
The copies concerning Šalim-bēlī's earlier caravan are not here;
guard the tablet with my instructions!
45 kutānu-textiles (and) 25 minas of sealed tin, apart from what I gave to him for expenses, Šalim-bēlī brought to you earlier.
72 kutānu-textiles, 2 of those abami'u-textiles, 4 black donkeys plus their harness, 1 mina of tin (and) worth 2 2/3 shekels 15 grains of silver of small goods you carried personally.
4 kutānu-textiles are owed by Kurub-Ištar.
The terms for the agent are overdue by 7 months.
My dear brother, send as much silver as falls into your hands to Uśur-ša-Aššur, so he may let it reach me and I shall not be put to shame.
While could still I oblige you I gave 8 shekels of silver for the fodder of 2 donkeys and 1 shekel for your porters.
2 shekels were for the share of Aššur-šamši, witnessed by Šū-Suen the son of Itūr-ilī.
I bound him by contract to pay 1/2 mina per textile, witnessed by Aššur-tāb and Aššur-dān.

They brought 5 textiles within the package of 15 kutānum textiles and 4 1/2 talents of tin for Aššur-nādā.
1 1/2 shekels for the gear for the donkey.
We gave 5 shekels silver to the driver.
We balanced 2 1/2 shekels silver to Šalim-Aššur s. Memenānum.
We paid 4 shekels silver to the son of Nabu-bāni for the fine copper which you owed.
10 shekels silver were lost from the cleaning procedure.
15 shekels silver, the price of the donkey.
2 shekels silver - the exit tax; 4 minas 11 shekels (hand) tin we gave for the share.
The remainder of the silver was 1 mina 9 shekels.
Amur-ilī brought 1 mina tin.
2/3 minas copper was the exit tax.
Your funds are exhausted.
If you come, bring my silver, Hananum's, and that belonging to the son of Abarzizum.
If you do not come, seal the silver altogether and send it to me.
You brought(?) 1 textile for Ikūnum.

From Buzutaya, Ennum-Bēlum and Puzur-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
After we sent the silver from the cargo of Ilī-bāni, Tarmana gave us 2 minas 45 shekels silver.
Dalaš (gave to us) 1 mina (silver).
Aššur-ṭāb took 4 shekels.
There are 8 shekels of poor quality (silver).
7 shekels are deductions.
The remainder of the silver is 5 minas 26 shekels.
From the 2 minas 31 shekels of your silver about which there is agreeing(?), they deducted from us 1 mina 6 shekels on account of Puzur-Aššur.
Let it be released there with you.
We took the remainder of the silver, 1 minas 24 shekels.
Altogether 4 minas 50 shekels.
We weighed out 3 minas 16 shekels for (21-ma?).
Thereof, the remainder of the silver is 1 minas 34 shekels.
Also, Puzur-Aššur gave us 1 minas 13 1/2 shekels silver and concerning 2 minas 37 shekels silver which was in Il-bāni's cargo for the filling of the sack, he said, "That which we took for you on interest, we will weigh out the silver and its interest."

From Buzutaya, Ennum-Bēlum, and Puzur-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
After you departed, the colony office held a pouring for 14 textiles per share and in addition to the 7 textiles which you left for Puzur-Aššur, we purchased 21 textiles for 3 minas 16 shekels and we paid with your silver.
We deposited 20 textiles, as your share and the shares of Aḫu-waqar and Amur-Ištar.
They held the pouring for 3 minas each and Puzur-Aššūr wieghed out 6 minas silver to your shares.
The held a pouring for 1 fine textile each and he paid the price of 2 textiles 42 shekels silver.
Later they held a pouring for 13 textiles and as for the 26 textiles, your shares, at a rate of 9 1/2 shekels each, paid 4 minas 7 shekels silver.
He gave to us 1 mina 13 1/2 shekels silver.
Altogether, Puzur-Aššur paid to your name 12 minas 1/2 shekels silver from his own funds.
You are deposited for 20 minas 35 shekels silver in the colony office.
7 minas 45 shekels belong to Aḫu-waqar.
7 minas 47 1/2 shekels belong to Amur-Ištar.

From Buzutaya and Ennum-bēlum to Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding the 10 minas silver which Imdī-ilum paid to us, Amur-Ištar sent 6 minas 57 shekels from Durḫumit.
We took from your outstanding merchandise 3 minas 3 shekels for payments, 2 minas 53 shekels for Innaya son of Ammurāya, 2 minas 32 shekels belonging to Aššur-imittī, 3 minas checked silver with the 'natives'.
Thereof, 15 shekels silver (went) to Pala and the priest of Tibnum, 3 shekels silver for sickles and the half-pack.
Total: 28 minas 3 2/3 shekels which we took from your outstanding merchandise.
We (still) needed 2 minas 36 shekels and we borrowed it for you on interest.
We will (add) it from the yield of your silver.
In sum, your silver is 30 minas 39 1/6 shekels.
Il-bāni brings you 30 minas silver under our seals.
We gave 1/2 minas silver to his share, 1 shekels to his sacrifice(?), 5 1/3 shekels silver to Kissē.
According to your instructions, we will clear the remainder of your outstanding merchandise and we will send you your silver.

From Buzutāya to Amur-ŠamaS, Aššur-taklāku, Pūšu-kēn, and Suen-nawer:
As for the tin and textiles that Suen-nāwer and Libbaya transported to you (pl.), let your message come as to whether you sent it to Waḫšušana to Kuzizīya, or Suen-nāwer took it, or you sold it on credit to a merchant.
If you do not send it to Kuzizīya, send the remainder of the tin, as you read in my letter, to him.
I am in business relations with the man.
He must not detract any silver for every mina of tin.
Deposit the remainder of my goods on a trustworthy merchant whom I have no reason to worry, and do not overvalue (a short) term or two months.
Now, concerning my share in the colony office, take care to deposit my share and ... and the silver not ... and thus disappoint me.
And if they call for a pouring .. of silver (?), then you yourself will deposit silver from my silver (account?).

And if they call for a pouring .. of silver (?), then you yourself will deposit silver from my silver (account?).
label: Cuneiform Tablet TC 1 17
