From Buzutāya to Amur-Šamaš, Aššur-taklāku and Pūšu-kēn:
My dear lords and brothers, on the day that you read this tablet, enter into the strongroom of my house and read my copy (of the tablet?) so that wherever anyone holds a single mina of silver from my outstanding claims, cause it to be given, and don't do me a favor.
As for the son of ...du, (? having sold it for silver?)
When he weighs the silver, make known the textiles.
There are two tablets.
My dear brothers, heed my instructions
As for you (pl.), don't go abroad tomorrow.
Let your message come if Puzur-Suen does not send you the gold.
Now, if he sends you the gold, he will pay the gold on the same day that he sends you the tin.

Buzutaya to Pūšu-kēn and Aššur-emūqī:
To Pūšu-kēn:
My dear brother!
You must not trust anyone!
If Aššur-taklāku is not available, take care to inspect the silver, both the leftovers(?) and the interest of my list and clear (these things) and not detain Aššur-emūqī.
Send him here.
I am awaiting him.
If they did not send me anything from Purušḫattum, let your message go with my tablet and yours.
If Aššur-emūqī is delayed, send 10 minas or 20 minas silver with the express messenger.

target_raw:
From Elamma to Aššur-nādā, Iddin-abum and Aššur-lamassā:
(specifically) to Aššur-lamassī:
My tin which you brought up here from the City, send me word about where you entrusted it on commission;
also, write the names of your witnesses in the letter.
Then you yourself should write a note about your witnesses, with their names, so that when they convene you can there remind those to whom you entrusted (goods) about the names of your witnesses.
Send me word about who was not and who was present.

Folgendermaßen Enna’aššur. Zu Āmurištar sprich!
Warum hast du mir (nur) bis zu diesem Tage gehorcht?
(Betrifft:) die kurrū (Getreide) für die Stadt dorthin zu schicken: sie (die Stadt) soll nicht hungern!
Bitte! An dem Tage, an dem du meine Tafeln liest, möge meine Urkunden Waza dir geben.
Ferner ist mit den Urkunden des Hauses Absūšalim, des So[hnes des . . . ] . . [. . . , wie] ich geschrieben habe, Aššurtaklāku zu dir unterwegs.
Gemäß meinem Briefe nimm meine Urkunden, die der Waza und die der Frau des Abūšalim und komm schleunigst her.
Deinetwegen habe ich nach Waḫšušana nicht gehen wollen.
Dich erwarte ich! Bitte!
Möglichst keine zwei Tage sollst du dort dich aufhalte!
Dann können die Geldgeber mir nicht zusetzen.
Auch sollst du nicht zugrunde gehen und meine Urkunden sollst du nicht zugrunde richten
Sei achtsam" Waza . . . Šūsu’in ist ihr nahe (?).
Sie möge herausgehen. Hierher möge er sie nicht herausgehen lassen (?).

Folgendermaßen Enna’aššur. Zu Āmurištar sprich!
Warum hast du mir (nur) bis zu diesem Tage gehorcht?
(Betrifft:) die kurrū (Getreide) für die Stadt dorthin zu schicken: sie (die Stadt) soll nicht hungern!
Bitte! An dem Tage, an dem du meine Tafeln liest, möge meine Urkunden Waza dir geben.
Ferner ist mit den Urkunden des Hauses Absūšalim, des So[hnes des . . . ] . . [. . . , wie] ich geschrieben habe, Aššurtaklāku zu dir unterwegs.
Gemäß meinem Briefe nimm meine Urkunden, die der Waza und die der Frau des Abūšalim und komm schleunigst her.
Deinetwegen habe ich nach Waḫšušana nicht gehen wollen.
Dich erwarte ich! Bitte!
Möglichst keine zwei Tage sollst du dort dich aufhalte!
Dann können die Geldgeber mir nicht zusetzen.
Auch sollst du nicht zugrunde gehen und meine Urkunden sollst du nicht zugrunde richten
Sei achtsam" Waza . . . Šūsu’in ist ihr nahe (?).

From Idī-Aššur, Adad-bāni, Aššur-imittī, Ikuppī-Aššur, Puzur-Ištar, and Imdī-ilum to Pūšu-kēn, Zupa, Aššur-ṣulūlī, Innāya and Pilaḫ-Ištar:
We will turn over the joint-stock fund to him.
Entrust all of it to him.
And let your authoritative message come seprately as to how much you entrusted Aššur-mālik.
If Buzutaya is not available, cause two others who will go to the city to enter with with Aššur-rē'ī.
In the presence of the men which you bring in, speak to him.
Except for you, those delineated in the tablet, and the two partners of Al-aḫum, no one else is to enter the house with you.

From Ili-imittī to Aššur-rabi and Aššur-imittī:
To Aššur-rabi:
Against the will of the gods, Ṭāb-Aššur, your brother's son, has died.
Also, I seized about a mina of silver of his assets from a transaction.
Come!
If he is not there, write to him wherever he is!
Dispatch him with Kurub-Ištar.

target_raw:
From Imdī-ilum, Enna-Bēlum and Aššur-ṣulūlī to Puzur-Aššur:
From the 245 textiles and those of the transporters of the transport of Amur-Ištar, therein, 3 textiles of the Akkadian type were left in the hosue of your father-in-law in Ḫaḫḫum.
Now, I caused 3 luḫuppu-textiles to enter (the palace) here.
The remainder of your textiles, 239 textiles entered the palace.
Therein, 12 textiles as excise tax.
The palace took 23 textiles as the (tithe?) for purchases.
They will balance 38 textiles to you in the colony office.
They cleared the remainder of your textiles together with those of the transporters, 166 textiles from the transport of Amur-Ištar and they are available.
From the 100 textiles of the transport of Idi-Adad, which entered the palace, the excise was 5 1/2 textiles and they will balance 5/6 shekel silver to you.
The palace took 10 1/2 textiles for the tithe for purchases.
You will balance 18 1/2 shekels silver as losses.
They will balance you 2 textiles in the colony office.
They cleared the reaminder of your textiles, 92 textiles from the transport of Idi-Adad, combined with the (textiles) of the transporters and they are available.
The son of Erraya (Aššur-mālik?) brought his contraband (into the city) to Pūšu-kēn, but his contraband was seized and the palace seized Pūšu-kēn and placed him in jail.
The guards are strong.
The princess/queen sent to Luḫušattiya, Ḫurrama, Šalaḫšua, and her own land concerning the contraband and ENNATUm is established.
Urgent, do not deal in contraband!
If you departed from Timelkiya, I ... you, whatever you sent out, leave it in a good house in Timelkiya and leave one of your trustworthy ṣuḫārum-servants and you yourself leav it and be counseled here.
Also, if anyone tries to get you to agree for even a single shekel of silver for whatever you have, saying "Sell me tin or textiles so that I can smuggle them and I will be responsible.", do not sell!
There, a hand is set in hand against you.

source_raw: um-ma im-dì-DINGIR-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma i-na ṭup-pì ší-ta-pu-ri-im a-ta-aḫ-da-ar a-na <gap> a-šur-ta-ak-la-ku a-<gap> a-na pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim a-ṣé-er ru-ba-im GAL i-ša-pár-šu-ma iš-ti kà-ri-im e-na-na-tim e-ri-iš-ma um-ma šu-ut-ma a-na a-lim{ki} ḫa-ra-ni e-na-num ù ku-a-tí a-na kà-ri-im ú-ma-ša-aḫ-kà um-ma šu-ut-ma a-šùr-na-da KÙ.BABBAR ma-dam ú-kà-al-ma a-šu-mì na-pá-áš-ti-a e-ṭá-ri lá a-li-a ù a-na kà-ri-im um-ma šu-ut-ma ṭup-pá-am ša ma-lá lu-qú-tám ú-kà-lu lu-šé-ṣí-a-ma am-ra kà-ru-um ú-lá e-ta-na-an-šu-um a-na pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim i-lá-ak i-na ša-am-ší ṭup-pí ta-ša-me-ú iš-tí ì-lí-áš-ra-ni tí-ib-a a-tal-kam-ma a-pí-iš a-ḫu-kà a-na-kam la-šu ú a-šar KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN i-ṣé-ri-kà lá-qá-im lu ni-im-lik-ma ṭé-em-ni lu ni-iṣ-ba-at a-ḫu-kà e-na-šu ik-lá a-sú-ri iš-tù iš-ti ru-ba-im a-wa-sú i-lá-qé-ú a-ma-nu-ma a-na ṣé-ri-a e-ti-qám-ma a-ma-kam e ú-ni bu-lá-tí-kà e it-ba-al e ú-ḫa-li-qá šu-ma lá ta-ḫi-id-ma lá ta-ta-al-kam ḫa-al-qá-a-ti ki-ma a-ḫu-kà ṣa-aḫ-ru-um ú-ni-ú-kà
target_raw:
From Imdī-ilum to Aššur-nādā:
I have had it, constantly writing letters!
Your brother Aššur-taklāku here sends a messenger to Burušhaddum, to the great king and he asks favours of the colony, saying:
"I am on my way to the City, do me a favour!"
so, he will cause you to be robbed in the colony, because he says:
"Aššur-nādā keeps a lot of silver, while I am powerless to even save my own life!"
Then he says to the colony:
"Let me produce the tablet showing how much merchandise he keeps, and examine it!"
Since the colony has not shown him any favours he will go to Burušhaddum.
The very day you hear my letter you must get up together with Ilī-ašrannī and come here,
and given that your brother is not here we must confer about where we may receive a single shekel of silver owed to you, and then make up our minds.
As for your brother, his eyes are dark.
Heaven forbid that after he obtains a decision from the king he will proceed from there to me, lest he cheats you there, removes your working capital and destroys you!
If you do not take care to come, you will be destroyed!
Like En[ ... ] your little brother has cheated you!

target_raw:
From Imdi-ilum to Pūšu-kēn:
Stand so that when they asses the excise on the goods transported by Aḫu-waqar son of Zurzur, the merchandise comes down, and dispatch the goods to Purušḫattum according to my instructions so that the goods are sold for cash.
They are not to be sold on credit.
Do not release them.
Let the silver arrive and let tin and textiles depart!
Also, just as I set witnesses here for myself, you must set witnesses there for you.
Let silver and textiles pile up!
Deposit my share in the colony office.
Finish my share at accountings.

source_raw: um-ma in-bi-IŠTAR-ma a-na lá-qé-pí-im ù ṣí-lá-{d}IŠKUR a-na ṣí-lá-{d}IŠKUR qí-bi-ma áš-pu-ra-kum um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-dí a-šur-ma-lik KÙ.BABBAR TÚG iš-qú-lu lá ta-da-šu-ma ḫa-ra-šu lá i-lá-ak a-ta KÙ.BABBAR lá tù-uš-ta-áš-qí-il5-ma a-wi-lam tù-ta-šé-er ù a-ni ki-a-ma té-pá-áš ki-ma ší-bé-e ta-aḫ-ri-ma-ni iḫ-da-ma KÙ.BABBAR i-ku-pí-a-šur ù É a-šur-ma-lik ša-áš-qí-il5 <gap> ul ša-pá-tim ma-lá a-na KÙ.BABBAR-pì-a lá a-pá-lu-ḫu ša-dí-a-šu-nu áš-pu-ra-kum um-ma a-na-ku-ma TÚG.ḪI.A a-na lu-bu-uš ṣú-ḫa-ri ša iš-tù 0.5 GÍN 0.66666 GÍN 1 GÍN ša-a-ma šé-bi4-lam a-ta pá-li-a-tim ša 1 GÍN.TA ù 1.5 GÍN.TA tù-uš-té-bi4-lam mì-ší-it ṣú-ba-tí pá-ni-ú-tim lá ú-kà-al-ma a-ni-tám té-pá-áš a-dí ta-lá-kam-ma iš-tí-a ta-na-mu-ru TÚG-ba-tù e-ma-ad-kà i-ba-ší ú-lá TÚG-ba-tí lá SÍG.ḪI.A lá ší-ma-am tí-šu i-ḫi-id-ma ra-ma-kà za-ki-ma ar-ḫi-iš a-tal-kam
target_raw:
Folgendermaßen Inbi’ištar. Zu Lāqīpum und Ṣilladad, zu Ṣilladad sprich!
Ich habe dir geschieben, folgendermaßen ich: "Bevor nicht Aššurmalik das Silber für die Stoffe bezahlt hat, sollst du ihm nicht(s) geben und seine Reise soll er nicht antreten."
Du hast ihn das Silber nicht zahlen lassen und hast den Mann freigelassen!
Nun sollst du so handeln: Gemäß den Zeugen hast du . . . . . . . und wir waren achtsam.
Also lasse das Silb[er des E]nbi’aššur und des Hauses [Aššu]rmalik, —Rs um welches mir zustehende Silber entspre[chend] (dem Werte) der Wol[lad]ung ich keine Furcht habe, sie (den Enbi’aššur und den Aššurmalik) deponieren.
Ich habe dir geschrieben, folgendermaßen ich: "Stoffe für die Bekleidung der Diener im Werte von ½ Seqel, ⅔ Seqel (oder) 1 Seqel kaufe mir und schicke (sie) her!"
Du hast pali’ātum-(Stoffe) im Werte von je 1 Seqel oder je 1½ Seqel mir geschickt.
Ein Lager von früher (gelieferten) Stoffen habe ich nicht (in Händen).
Dies also sollst du tun.
Bis du herkommst, um dich mit mir zu treffen sind die Stoffe, (die) du (mir) auferlegt (hast), vorhanden.
Weder Stoffe, noch Wolle, noch einen Kaufpreis (dafür) hast du (bei mir) gut. Sei achtsam!
Folgendermaßen (läßt) Kasāsā (dir sagen) : "Komm schleunigst her!"
source: translate Akkadian to English: um-ma in-bi-IŠTAR-ma a-na lá-qé-pí-im ù ṣí-lá-{d}IŠKUR a-na ṣí-lá-{d}IŠKUR qí-bi-ma áš-pu-ra-kum um-ma a-na-ku-ma a-dí a-šur-ma-lik KÙ.BABBAR TÚG iš-qú-lu lá ta-da-šu-ma ḫa-ra-šu lá i-lá-ak a-ta KÙ.BABBAR lá tù-uš-ta-áš-qí-il5-ma a-wi-lam tù-ta-šé-er ù a-ni ki-a-ma té-pá-áš ki-ma ší-bé-e ta-aḫ-ri-ma-ni iḫ-da-ma KÙ.BABBAR i-ku-pí-a-šur ù É a-šur-ma-lik ša-áš-qí-il5 <gap> ul ša-pá-tim ma-lá a-na KÙ.BABBAR-pì-a lá a-pá-lu-ḫu ša-dí-a-šu-nu áš-pu-ra-kum um-ma a-na-ku-ma TÚG.ḪI.A a-na lu-bu-uš ṣú-ḫa-ri ša iš-tù 0.5 GÍN 0.66666 GÍN 1 GÍN ša-a-ma šé-bi4-lam a-ta pá-li-a-tim ša 1 GÍN.TA ù 1.5 GÍN.TA tù-uš-té-bi4-lam mì-ší-it ṣú-ba-tí pá-ni-ú-tim lá ú-kà-al-ma a-ni-tám té-pá-áš a-dí ta-lá-kam-ma iš-tí-a ta-na-mu-ru TÚG-ba-tù e-ma-ad-kà i-ba-ší ú-lá TÚG-ba-tí lá SÍG.ḪI.A lá ší-ma-am tí-šu i-ḫi-id-ma ra-ma-kà za-ki-ma ar-ḫi-iš a-tal-kam

source_raw: um-ma kur-ub-IŠTAR-ma a-na a-šur-na-da qí-bi-ma ma-lá TÚG.ḪI.A ù AN.NA ù ṭup-pé-e ša DAM.GÀR-re-e ša is-mì-tí-DINGIR ša ip-qí-du-ni-ku-ni té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam úz-ni pí-té-e ù a-na DUG-ṣí-lá-a-šur šu-up-ur-ma um-ma a-ta-ma 60 {TÚG}ku-ta-ni SIG5 wa-at-ru-tim ù 6 {TÚG}a-ba-ar-ni-e na-áš-a-ku-um ù-lá a-ta a-na pá-ni-a ṣí-a-am ù-lá ma <gap> šu-up-ra-ma TÚG.ḪI.A ù ší-tí AN.NA <gap> ù ANŠE.ḪI.A lá-ap-qí-sú-um 20 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ga-am-ra-am ša a-lim{ki} a-na e-li-tí-kà i-dí-ú a-ma-kam a-na DUG-ṣí-lá-a-šur šu-up-ur-ma ma-lá KÙ.BABBAR a-na a-wi-tí-kà i-kà-šu-du-kà-ni a-na pá-ni-kà lu-šé-bi4-lam ku zu a-ni-am 2.5 GÍN ša <gap> ba a lá
target_raw:
From Kurub-Ištar to Aššur-nādā:
Send me word about how many textiles and how much tin and what tablets concerning commission agents of Iṣmid-ilum they have entrusted to you.
Inform me; then write to Ṭāb-ṣill-Aššur, saying: "He is on his way to you with 60 extra-fine kutānu-textiles plus 6 abarniu-textiles."
You should either leave yourself to come to me or send Kura, so I may entrust the textiles plus the rest of the tin and the donkeys to him.
They have imposed on 20 minas of silver expenses imposed by the City on your enterprise.
Write there to Ṭāb-ṣill-Aššur that he should send to you whatever silver will accrue to you for the value of your shipment.
I shall place 12 1/2 shekels of silver within the saddle.

From Kurub-Ištar to Aššur-nādā:
Aššur-bām brought me 2 minas of silver, its import duty added.
I bought for you 30 minas of tin at the rate 15:1; thereof I gave 2 minas of tin for expenses to Pilah-Ištar.
Pilah-Ištar is on his way to you with 28 minas of tin under my seal.

source_raw: um-ma pu-šu-ke-en-ma a-na la-ma-sí qí-bi-ma ší-tí TÚG.ḪI.A-tí-ki pá-ni-ú-tim gám-ra-ki-im 20 TÚG.ḪI.A ša a-na PUZUR4-a-šùr ta-dí-ni-ni 1 TÚG ni-is-ḫa-tum 2 TÚG ší-mu-um ší-tum 17 TÚG.ḪI.A al-qé 6 TÚG a-ḫu-qar ub-lam 6 TÚG i-a-šar ub-lam 2 TÚG i-dí-sú-en6 ub-lam ŠÀ.BA 3 TÚG a-na PUZUR4-a-šùr ú-ra-dí-ma 20 TÚG ú-pì-iq-šum-ma a qá-tí-šu a-dí-in ší-tí TÚG.ḪI-tí-ki 11 TÚG i li-bi-a ŠÀ.BA 1.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ni-is-ḫa-sú DIRI ša-du-a-sú ša-bu ku-nu-ki-a ku-lu-ma-a na-áš-a-ki-im ta-áš-pu-ri-ma um-ma a-tí-ma i li-bi TÚG.ḪI.A ša ú-šé-bi4-lá-ku-ni 2 TÚG ša šu-pu-ul-tim-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša ku-lu-ma-a na-áš-a-ki-ni 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ku-wa-um 0.5 ma-na a-na šu-pu-ul-tim dí-ni ší-im 1 TÚG kab-tim ša šu-pu-ul-tim iš-tù bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum ú-bu-lu-nim-ma 7 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša il5-ba-ni ša DUMU ku-sà-ri-im iš-qú-lá-ni ù i-na ší-im ší-tí TÚG.ḪI.A-tí-ki KÙ.BABBAR uš-ta-ṣa-ba-at-ma <gap> i-dí-sú-en6 ú-šé-ba-lá-ki-im a-na lá-ma-sí qí-bi-ma 5.TA TÚG.ḪI.A SIG5-tim a ṣú-ḫa-ri dí-ni lu-ub-lu-nim li-ik-šu-du-ni mì-šu ša e-ma-am ta-aḫ-dí-ri-ni a-na-ku a-na-kam ki-ma i-qá-bi4-ú-ni i-ṣé-er NINDA-kà a-zu-áb e-na-a e-ra-ba ú-ṣí-a
target_raw:
From Pūšu-kēn to Lamassī:
The remainder of your previous textiles are exhausted to you.
As for the 20 textiles which you gave to Puzur-Aššur, 1 textiles was for the excise and 2 for the purchases, and I received 17 textiles.
Aḫu-waqar brought 6 textiles.
Ea-šar brought 6 textiles.
Idī-Suen brought 2 textiles.
Thereof, I added 3 textiles to Puzur-Aššur and I entrusted 20 textiles to him and I gave them to his account.
The remainder of your textiles, 11 textiles, I owe you.
Thereof 1 1 /2 minas silver, the excise added and shipping satisfied, Kulumaya brings to you under my seal.
You wrote me, "From the tetiles which I sent to you, 2 textiles of Šupūltum and the silver which (you said) Kulumaya brings to you, 1 mina silver kuwaʾum, give a half mina to Šu-ūltum."
The price of 1 heavy textile which Šupūltum brought me from Purušḫattum and 7 shekels silver which Ili-bāni, which the son of Kusārum paid to me, and from teh remainder of the revenues of your textiles I will seize the silver and I will send it to you with Idī-Suen.
To Lamassi:
Give 5 fine textiles to each of the youth.
Let them bring them.
Let them reach me.
Why is it that you have worried about the maḫā'um? Here, I myself will concern myself with what they said about your bread rations.
Innaya will enter and leave.

target_raw:
From Pūšu-kēn, the representative(s) of Puzur-Aššur, and Sueyya to Puzur-Aššur:
We checked the 19 talents of refined copper under seals which Ea-šar (brought), and it was deficient 13 2/3 minas.
Thereof, they brought 1 talent 40 minas of poor quality copper from the previous and former washed copper.
We checked the 7 1/2 talents of poor quality copper under seals which Ea-šar (brought), and it was deficient 10 minas.
All this Ea-šar brought.
3 talents of 'black' copper are placed in his copper.
They brought 2 talents of refined copper which he deposited in Walama and we checked it and it was deficient 1 mina.
20 minas of poor quality (copper) are deposited in the previous caravan-enterprise.
We checked the 10 talents 30 minas of poor quality copper under seals which Kurara and Šū-Bēlum (brought), and it was deficient 12 minas.
Your representatives brought 52 textiles from Waḫšušana.
Thereof, 30 kutānum-textiles are deposited to Pūšu-kēn at a rate of 9 1/4 shekels (of silver) and he has not yet paid the silver.
Idī-Aššur drove here 48 kutānum-textiles.
Total of your textiles is 148 textiles which arose from the transport of Idī-Aššur.
Enna-Suen son of Elāli is bringing you 20 minas silver under our seals.
We will convert to silver the copper, the textiles, and the outstanding claims and assets which you left like gentlemen, and we will clear your silver from the cargo of Dān-Aššur and we will send your silver to you.
According to your instructions your donkeys are put to pasture.
Your servant is well.

From Pūšu-kēn to Lamassī:
1 minas 15 shekels silver, its excise added, its shipping charges paid, under my seal, Dān-Aššur brings to you.
As for the proceeds of the 1 or two xx textiles which you sent to me, your silver is exhausted.
21 minas fine copper, for the ikrībū of Bēlum, 2 s. of bronze, 14 e., x minas their weight, above half heavy half taziki divided, which to Kitaum, 5 e. talḫadiatum, 1/3 minas silver, both a š. and a musarrum belonging to the youth (Dān-Aššūr? or the other youth?) their worth 62.5 shekels, separately his anuqqum, a youth and a maiden, all this Dān-Aššur bring to you.
Urgent, do not release the youth to anyone so that he may grow up among us.
When Dān-Aššur departs from there, Give the š and m. in addition to the 1/3 minas silver which I gave to him so that in total he makes purchases worth about a mina of silver.
Send him back up.

From Pūšu-kēn, Aššur-dān and Ennum-Aššur to Ištar-pilaḫ:
Pūšu-kēn gave us the 1 1/2 minas silver which Abatum brought to him and thereof, we weighed out 26 5/6 shekels silver to Iddin-abum for your payment.
We paid 14 shekels silver for the donkey.
We weighed out 6 shekels silver (tibnim) to Ennum-Aššur.
There, let him pay you in full.
The remainder of your silver, 38 1/3 shekels, is with Pūšu-kēn.
Iddin-abum did not give any tin to Ennum-Aššur.
We will take the tin and send it to you with the next (caravan).

From Kurub-Ištar to Aššur-nādā:
It was here that I gave you instructions about Iṣmid-ilum's textiles.
Write before you (leave) so Ṭāb-ṣill-Aššur may travel on to you, and then give him Iṣmid-ilum's textiles on your own authority.
As for the 2 minas of silver that you sent me, since the trade in Akkadian textiles is affected, I have not sent you any.
If within 10 days the trade in Akkadian textiles does not return to normal, I shall buy you tin and send it to you.
Puzur-Suen son of Ikaya should give you 10 2/3 shekels of silver.
Then check the tablet of Katātum son of Nanaya in Puzur-Suen's house, and have the silver and its interest paid.

The tablet of the verdict of the city concerning the gold which we sent to you
As for the gold, we did not ... a document.
The matter of the previous gold, one sold to another...?
According the word of the stele: no Assyrians will sell any gold to Akkadians, Amorites, or northerners.

target_raw:
From the
You wrote me.
You said, "I seized the 5 minas silver from Asqudum here. Also, I borrowed 10 minas silver on interest and I sent you the silver. The merchandise from Asqudum which I took, I will convert to silver and the silver on interest I will 'convert'and I will send you the rest of your silver."
2 talents 5 minas tin, 30 kutānum-textiles, 2 black donkeys, 5 minas 5 shekels exceedingly fine 'gold', and 11 shekels meteoric iron in lumps--all this my father gave to Asqudum.
I will pray for you in the presence of Aššur and my god.
My son, because you are grieved over every shekel of your silver, take heed so that up to a talent of silver or more goes out so that the silver of the man is seized.
You hold my tablet.
Concerning my tablet saying that it is I who (stands) for your authority which you will seize, the man earned the disdain of my father and my disdain.
Cause him to constantly fret and do me as big a favor as you can.
He ... my two textiles and he will bring/brought your (pl.) silver.
My message went to Timelkiya.
But as for you, send them from there so that they might send my silver with you.
They must not borrow a single shekel of silver.
Išār-bēlī my youth is with Asqudum.
Take care to lead him away immediately.

source_raw: um-ma {d}NIN.ŠUBUR-ba-ni-ma a-na a-mur-DINGIR qí-bi-ma ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma a-šu-mì TÚG.ḪI.A ša ik-ri-bi4-a úz-ni pì-té TÚG.ḪI.A i-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum a-na 10 ḫa-am-ša-tim DAM.GÀR a-qí-ip-ma u4-mu-šu ma-al-ú iš-tù ITU.KAM ṣú-ḫa-ra-am a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum a-ṣé-er ú-ṣur-ša-a-šur áš-ta-pár DAM.GÀR KÙ.BABBAR ú-ša-áš-qal-ma ú-šé-ba-lá-kum ù DAM.GÀR IGI ú-ṣur-ša-IŠTAR a-qí-ip-šu a-ma-kam ša-il5-šu a-ḫi a-ta eb-ri a-ta sà-ḫe-er-tum ša i-na ṭup-pì-a lá-pu-ta-at-ku-ni ù TÚG.ḪI.A a-na li-tab-ší-a šé-bi4-lam ba-lúm TÚG.ḪI.A wa-áš-ba-ku lu-be-er-šu ú ḫu-la-li la-bu-ša-ku <gap> ni-a-šur a-ša-me-ma a-na tur4-ḫu-mì-it ḫa-ra-kà a-pu-tum i-ḫi-id-ma ba-bi4-iš TÚG.ḪI.A ù AN.NA ri-e-šu-nu na-áš-ú-ni lá-ma ma-ma-an e-tí-qá-ni et-qá-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ù 2 ma-na šé-li a-ḫi a-ta 1 mu-sà-ra-am SIG5 a-na qá-áb-li-a šé-bi4-lam e-ri-im-tám i qá-áb-li-a ša-ak-na-ku
target_raw:
From Ilabrat-bāni to Amur-ilī:
You wrote me.
In Purušḫaddum I consigned the textiles on terms of 10 weeks to an agent, and his term is now up.
One month ago I sent a servant to Uṣur-ša-Aššur in Purušḫaddum.
When he has made the agent pay the money I will send it to you.
Also, I entrusted (the textiles) to the agent with Uṣur-ša-Aššur as witness.
Ask him there yourself!
As you are my brother and friend, send me the small things that were booked for you in my tablet as well as textiles for my own clothing.
I live here without any textiles.
I am dressed ... in his garment ... ?
x-x-ni-Aššur is the ... ?
I hear that you are going on a trip to Durḫumit.
Urgent, take care to get the first tin and textiles which they carry at the gate there before anyone else and thus raise an extra mina or two of silver.
As you are my brother, send me a good belt for my waist.
I am fitted out with a wrap on my waist.

source_raw: um-ma {d}NIN.ŠUBUR-ba-ni-ma a-na ú-ṣur-ša-IŠTAR qí-bi4-ma 10 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-na a-ḫa-at a-bi4-ni ku-nu-ki-a ku-ra-ra DUMU a-bu-ša-lim na-ší KÙ.BABBAR ma-aḫ-ri-kà áp-qí-sú-um a-ḫi a-ta eb-ri a-ta a-mur-DINGIR ù a-ta šu-up-ra-šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR a-na ma-ma-an lá i-dan 1 a-bar-ni-a-am a-na li-tab-ší-a lu-šé-li-a-am a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu iḫ-da-ma šu-up-ra-šu-um KÙ.BABBAR a ta-ri-iš-ma-tim kà-i-in mì-šum ta-áš-ta-na-pá-ra-am gi5-im-lá-ni
target_raw:
From Ilabrat-bani to Uṣur-ša-Ištar:
Kurara s. Abu-šalim brings 10 shekels silver to the sister of our father under my seal.
I entrusted the silver of your revenues to him.
My dear brother and friend, you and Amur-ilāi send to him so that he does not give the silver to anyone.
Let him bring up 1 abarnīum for my clothing.
My dear brothers take heed to send to him.
Establish the silver for Tariš-mātum.
Why must I constantly be writing?
Do me a favor.

From Aššur-mālik to Pūšu-kēn, Kuzāziya, and Aššur-idī:
You wrote me.
You said, "The silver of Puzur-Ištar son of Aššur-mālik, which his brother was to pay within 40 days, he will pay all the silver in the city. Have him pay his excise tax and shipping charges."
I questioned Ennamānum and he said, "The silver is deposited for interest."
As for me, I need its interest.
I purchased the houses and I will return my share(?).
Aššur-idī son of Šū-Ḫubur owes (me) 8 minas of silver.
His credit term is one year past due.
Seize him and ...
... to ... to you (sg.) ... silver of ... PN ... of Aššur ...
I paid to Ikūn-pī-(ya?/Aššur?) ..., which ... to him.
Let Zukua go with the silver.
To Pūšu-kēn:
Your house, which we sold to Aššur-ṭāb son of Matāti as a replacement(?), now they have abandoned the house.
Let your message come, concerning whether to (do) this or not.
Request tablets and our yield of Aššur-idī at my departure (at the gate of my journey!) and study them carefully.
Because you did not send the silver, you have dissapointed me.
Now, keep your eye constantly on Aššur-idī.

You wrote me.

18 textiles ... Išme-ilī, ??? Ennam-Aššur?? his account satisfied, brought to you.
Why is that you refused to confront (him) about the textiles?
You've never done me a favor!
My dear brother, do not disappoint me.
Acquire textiles and act so as to do me a favor.
My dear brother, do not disappoint me.
Do me a favor.

target_raw:
Enlil-bāni to Aššur-rabi, Al-aḫum, Ab-šalim, and Šakrilka, and Iddi-Šamaš:
If the perephinalia is deposited and the (entry?/path?) accessible, bring all the perephinalia which is in the house into the new house, and seal the 'palace' and strongroom with your seals so that no one can open (them).
Both flour and wheat, wherever their is grain, bring it in and secure it.
And also, exit from the 'palace' and let the maid depart with Al-aḫum.
As for the kilaritum maid, sell her.
As for Iddi-Šamaš and the senior maid, wherever the house is insecure, let then extract seals of the 'palace (courtyard?)' and strongroom.
Let no one enter into the p. of Šū-Ištar's house.
If you need grain, store more grain within the grain (already there), and ... 3 shekels silver and šitrum textile ...

target_raw:
Šāt-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the attack (of the disease), the entire house is affected.
If you intend to come to the city, sacrifice to the gods!
But the elders do not look after me.
And you yourself, Amunaḫi, do not take care of me ... (who will?)
Beside you, I have no one else!
Because you did not .... from Belet-ekallin (month i) to Kanwarta (month ix), Kutaya ... check MIchel's translation...
Now, they have become angry.
They have provided me with 20 silas rations along with your female slaves.
A slave girl eats 20 silas (of rations).
And I am to eat (only) 20 silas (of rations)?!
Now, they do not respect the name of the youth...?
Here, you must not invoke them.
The travellers will go, but you must not invoke my name.
Who would invoke my name.
Beside you, I have no one else!
not from Inaar ?? (broken section) ... we wait? they gave to me, nikistum...

From Šalim-Aššur, Imdī-ilum, and Pūšu-kēn and Ilī-bāni to Kuziza:
Ilī-bāni brought 18 minas silver under your seal.
6 shekels were lost in the washing process.
We gave 1 shekels for the smith and wood.
3 2/3 shekels 15 grains poor quality silver under our seals is available in the house of Pūšu-kēn.
Your silver was deducted 16 1/2 shekels both deductions and losses.
Also, according to your instructions, in addition to the 18 minas silver which Ilī-bāni brought, we acquired for you 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels refined silver at a 1 shekel (per mina per month) rate and they sealed our tablet.
31 minas refined silver, your debt, we weighed out to Pūšu-kēn.
We asked him about your tablet and he said, "Their terms are two weeks past due. Have them pay the interest so that I may give you (pl.) his tablet. I hold your tablet for interest."
Let Kuzizīya come and we will negotiate about the interest.
Take care to send the 13 minas 16 1/2 shekels silver which we took for interest.

From Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
When you departed, you said, "Should my representatives request of you either silver or copper for Imdī-ilum, do not refuse them. Give it."
We had extended to Imdī-ilum 5 months time.
(The rate was) about 1 mina silver for 5 (months).
Now when they gave the silver, I was abroad.
They weighed that silver out to Ikūnum.
To your investors we extended 5 months and they put him to shame(?).
(The rate was) for 5 months, 1 1/2 minas silver.
He gave 5 minas tin to us.
Altogether, you owe 2 5/6 minas silver.
After Adad-bāni exhausted your 40 minas silver, may Aššur witness that I weighed 1 1/2 minas to your name and it was at (your) disposal for 5 months.
I am returning from being abroad (and) they are not able to dispatch Adad-bāni, and you are writing here in your tablet (that) mine was on my own responsibility(?).
(You are) like a despicable man from 1 hundred
Were I dead, would that you bear my
May Aššur and the gods of my investors witness that your inverstor who has given you money is not as concerned as am I about your
Now you are writing bitter things to me.
.... you are setting me .... of my own ... -im, I will not write down your name. ... I received ...., and you wrote me this matter in the tablet.

From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn and Ilabrat-bāni, specifically to Ilabrat-bāni:
You wrote me.
You said, "Let your instructions come to your representatives so that when they sell me tin and textiles on credit, they sell me (some) totaling about a talent of silver. And in confirmation of these things let a 10 minas silver portion (of the total sale amount) go to you so that you heed my message."
There are 2 talents tin with Ḫurašāum.
There are 2 talents with Amur-Aššur son of Šū-Ištar.
Also, there are 100 kutānum-textiles and fine tin on top of the (bulk) tin.
Now, come to an agreement (for a price) on the textiles on short-term credit and take them.
If you send your silver for purchases, then before your silver enters, let your tablet travel overland so that I can purchase the purchases which you send from my own funds and send them to you.
Let my silver be lent out for a month or two
My dear brother, for my tin, do no go less than a 6 shekel rate.
Send me 5 minas silver and 1/2 mina
Fulfill 1 mina silver for the god from your own funds.
Do not disappoint me!
May the god, the owner of my votive fund, drive you!
My dear brother, send me my silver!

From Šalim-aḫum, Ḫinnaya, Aššur-imittī, Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum to Pūšu-kēn:
According to your instructions, we obtained a tablet of the city and the attorney brings it to you.
There, take care to gather together silver, maids, servants, houses--whatever Šū-Nunu left behind and seal and send to us.
We gave 1/3 mina silver a mina tin to the attorney.
Give to him 1/3 mina silver there so that he is satisfied.
We gave 10 shekels silver and 1 mina tin to Imgua.
According to the tablet of the city: "Concerning the silver of Aššūr-mālik, no one shall make claims on the sons of Aššur-mālik until they have satisfied the debts of the joint-stock fund, the income, the GA-ra-tim, and the šakultum (feasts?)."
It is there.
Show them the tablet.
To Pūšu-kēn from Tariš-mātum and Bēlātum:
Concerning the silver about which you wrote, at the arrival of the silver, we will give the first silver to your representative.

From Šalim-aḫum to Ennam-Aššur:
As for your outstanding claims with both the Anatolians and wherever, which I continue to hear about, clear yourself before the caravan from Ḫaḫḫum arrives.
In addition to the silver which you sent to me with Innaya, send as much silver as you can lay your hands on ahead of you as an installment.
Come home!
Do not leave my assets behind, (i.e.) the silver from the tin in the caravan of Aššur-mālik s. Luzina.
Send the silver from Šāt-Aššur's and Azia's tin ahead of you.
Now, do not (mix) the proceeds of their textiles with that of my goods!
Bring 15 minas of top-quality fine copper from both Kanesh and Ḫaḫḫum.
I shall give you the 16 1/2 shekels gold owed (you) by Li--en in Šimala.
Bring a tabletop one and half cubits in size.
There are legs available here.
Let Agua s. Tāb-Aššur give to you the table which he owes me.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Ennam-Aššur:
You wrote me.
May the silver (problem) turn out well.
A daughter of an investor leans against the wall, and you like a bigshot delay until this day!
Now, as for them, they are not writing you so as to save me face.
Do not worry about the Anatolians.
Let the wind carry them off, together with their silver!
To Dān-Aššur:
Let him arrive here for the accountings.

You (pl.) wrote to me.
You (pl.) said, "From the goods of the šēpum of Aḫ-šalim, we 'placed' 12 minas 35 2/3 shekels silver on long terms on a trustworthy merchant."
As for the 54 minas 45 shekels which are due (thereof, Šū-Suen brought 31 1/2 minas together with the shipping fee; Aššur-mālik brought 21 minas 10 shekels silver), he owes the remainder of the silver, 2 minas 5 shekels.
Aššur-mālik brought 21 minas 10 shekels silver.

Inspect their tablets.
As for those whose term is due, do not listen to the interest.
Seal the previous silver you recieved with its interest and send it with the first (departer).

1/2 mina silver is the
25 shekels silver is the

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Lā-qēpum and Pūšu-kēn
You (pl.) wrote me.
You said, "From the merchandise of Ilī-ašranni's transport, 6 1/3 minas tin and 1/2 textile are with Ilabrat-bāni."
He acted on his own authority and opened my package and took my tin!
Instead of some random thief, he took my tin!
Cause him to pay a six shekel rate for my tin!
I read that Iddin-abum s. Iddin-Ištar owes 19! 1/2 minas [silver] (for) tin from those very same goods, (paying) 1/2 mina silver (per kutānum textile) and a 7 shekel rate (for tin).
He will not pay less than a 6 shekel rate for the tin!
In the presence of Enlil-bāni and Puzur-Aššur son of Elāli, he drove my goods and their goods together from here and the thief (i.e. Ilabrat-bāni) broke up 1 or 2 ‘talents’ (i.e. sacks) of tin in addition to the hand tin which I gave to them.
In this way they who were there ‘informed’ the man so that he took from mine while they took away their merchandise intact.
As for the caravan of Lā-qēpum, they charged feed (expenses of) 45 shekels each.
How is it that they took it out of my own merchandise?
Did he take (anything) from their tin or did he take (anything) from their textiles?
They 'informed' him and he took from mine, and he returned their textiles and tin intact!
Urgent! I shall not lose out!
Let one or two of them stay and do whatever is appropriate.
If they speak harsh words, [go] to the colony.

From Šalim-aḫum to Lā-qēpum and Pūšu-kēn:
2 talents 10 minas tin under my seal, 12 minas 'hand-tin', 4
Bundle the textiles and the black donkey with the tin and sell them on short terms to a trustworthy merchant as responsible as yourselves.
Do not overvalue one or two months.
Your merchants must be trustworthy!
Write down the silver as much as you have placed on the merchant and the term period in your tablet.
The textile for the driver (is laid out?) within my textiles.

Regarding the 54 minas 45 1/2 shekels silver from the šēpum of Aḫ-šalim which you 'entrusted' on long terms, 58 minas 18 1/2 shekels from the šēpum of Pūšu-kēn, 28 minas 27 shekels from Lulu son of Zukuḫūm, 31 1/2 minas which Aššur-šamši and Ḫinnāya 'entrusted' in Durḫumit, 5 minas from Amur-Ištar for my tin, you (two) wrote me.

Kurub-Ištar brought me 18 minas 18 shekels.

From Šalim-aḫum to Lā-qēpum and Pūšu-kēn:
My dear brothers, my tin and textiles should not be stocked up.
Sell (the tin) on short terms at a rate of 9 shekels or higher; otherwise, sell as much as can be sold on long terms.
Let your report come as to how much of my goods—those from the cargo of Erraya’s son (Aššur-mālik), those that Dān-Aššur drove, and those from the caravan of Šū-Enlil’s son—(that is) my tin and textiles, cleared (the palace).
Let your report come regarding the five kusītum-robes, the 1 nibrarum-textile from the cargo of Dān-Aššur, and the price of the donkeys, as much as you bought.
As for the 30 minas 27 shekels of silver on terms about which you wrote me—10 minas 10 shekels of silver of the sons of Aššur-šamšī and 5 minas of silver of Ḫinnaya—have all this paid and send it to me at the first opportunity.
A tablet recording the will of Aššur-imittī is with Šalim-Aššur son of Ennum-Aššur in Ḫurrama.
Send to him to have the tablet brought to you.
Wrap up the tablet in straw, and carefully entrust (it) to a reliable affiliated trader so that he may bring it to me.
Give a shekel of silverfrom the silver to the bearer of the tablet.

From Šalim-aḫum to Lā-qēpum and Aššur-nādā:
Hannānum son of Ubhākum owes me 9 minas of refined silver here.
He is to pay (in installments of) 3 minas per year.
He is to pay me 3 minas of silver in this month of Kuzalli, year of Aḫ-marši.
Of the merchandise that he will lead to you may 4 minas of tin, the nisḫātu-tax on my tin for the palace, may that be charged to the tin for his expenses; should there be any balance, he should deduct it from the money he owes, and then you and Hannānum must seal the rest and send it to me; then his representatives here may open his tablet and deduct his payment (from his debt).

Let Ilabrat-bāni give you the 1/2 mina gold and its excise duty.
Collect the 6 shekels gold from Agua s. Ṭāb-Aššur at your departure.

From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding the tin which you gave to Ennam-Aššur and the textiles, gather up as much as you can so that I can raise every shekel of silver from my textiles.
But let him retain his share.
I heard that ... to(?) Ilabrat-bāni is there.
Approach him and give to him as many tin and textiles as you can on short terms.
Thus you will say, "The instructions of Šalim-aḫum: Do not hand over my tin and textiles to any of your representatives. Let your share be deposited to the merchant so that my heart is gladdened specifically by you bearing the silver in your transport."

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
You wrote to me.
“I sent the tin of Kuzari’s transport to Purusḫattum when he arrived. I expect the silver in 5 days. On the day you read this tablet, purchase 45m silver worth of tin for our common property and let you silver be thus disposed.”
I purchased the tin and Puzur-Aššur transports it to you.
In addition to the silver from Kuzari’s transport, send to me that which you entrusted to Aššur-idi’s son with the first departer.
According to your tablet: “When Dan-Aššur arrives, I will set off to Purushattum myself and the goods, both mine and yours, I will clear and in my transport will deliver (it) to you.”
Apart from the the goods between us and that of Dan-Aššur’s transport, and Kulmaya and Idi-Suen’s transport, from your transport, my dear brother, you did not leave me any silver.
So that I will not be put to shame in the city gate, make some deposits to the colony office in your name (to) my account as previously, so that I will not have to balance anything at accounting time.

Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Ela brought 16 minas silver.
Idi-Suen brought 16 minas silver.
Thereof, 2/3 minas 'inspected' silver, a bag which Idi-Suen brought, I opened in the presence of Kulumaya, and
20 2/3 shekels silver were deficient.
The lumps? were too large.
It must not bear it in the repeated reception.
I gave 3 1/3 shekels for the nisḫat nisḫatum.
I paid 10 minas 54 1/6 shekels for the goods which Aššur-taklāku transported.
130 minas tin under seal, and 10 minas hand-tin, the silver price was 10 minas 4 2/3 shekels.
10 textiles, they were valued at 33 1 /3 shekels (each).
The donkey and its feed together cost 17 shekels.
And (I gave?) 2 shekels silver to Ela(?)
The gear for the donkey was 2 shekels (silver).
The exit tax was 5 shekels.
1 heavy textile apart belongs to Aziya.
Ela brings all this to you under my seal.
The remainder of you silver was 9 minas 36 1/2 shekels.
2 talents 7 minas tin under seal, 9 minas hand-tin, at a rate of 14 5/6 shekels, their (price in) silver was 9 minas 10 shekels.
The price of the donkey was 1/3 minas (silver).
The exit tax was 5 shekels.
The gear for the donkey was 1 shekels (silver).
Kulumaya brings you all this under my seal.
The silver from the transport of Ela and Idī-Suen is exhausted.
I did not give the wages of a driver to Kulumaya.
Kulumaya brings you 1
Ela brought 1/2 minas silver corresponding to Iluwa.
I gave 1 2/3 shekels for the excise.

Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Ela brought 16 minas silver.
Idi-Suen brought 16 minas silver.
Thereof, 2/3 minas 'inspected' silver, a bag which Idi-Suen brought, I opened in the presence of Kulumaya, and
20 2/3 shekels silver were deficient.
The lumps? were too large.
It must not bear it in the repeated reception.
I gave 3 1/3 shekels for the nisḫat nisḫatum.
I paid 10 minas 54 1/6 shekels for the goods which Aššur-taklāku transported.
130 minas tin under seal, and 10 minas hand-tin, the silver price was 10 minas 4 2/3 shekels.
10 textiles, they were valued at 33 1 /3 shekels (each).
The donkey and its feed together cost 17 shekels.
And (I gave?) 2 shekels silver to Ela(?)
The gear for the donkey was 2 shekels (silver).
The exit tax was 5 shekels.
1 heavy textile apart belongs to Aziya.
Ela brings all this to you under my seal.
The remainder of you silver was 9 minas 36 1/2 shekels.
2 talents 7 minas tin under seal, 9 minas hand-tin, at a rate of 14 5/6 shekels, their (price in) silver was 9 minas 10 shekels.
The price of the donkey was 1/3 minas (silver).
The exit tax was 5 shekels.
The gear for the donkey was 1 shekels (silver).
Kulumaya brings you all this under my seal.
The silver from the transport of Ela and Idī-Suen is exhausted.
I did not give the wages of a driver to Kulumaya.
Kulumaya brings you 1
Ela brought 1/2 minas silver corresponding to Iluwa.
I gave 1 2/3 shekels for the excise.
At a rate of 13 2/3 shekels, He put 6 minas 33 shekels tin its package in your sack.
Kulumaya brings this to you.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
2 talents 10 minas under seal, 13 minas hand tin, 13 textiles--wrappings and top pack--1 black donkey.
The wages of the sāridum are paid.
All this Aššur-taklāku brings to you under my seal.
My dear brother and colleague, have I not already written you?
I said, "Take about 1 talent of tin or 10 textiles from my inventory and do me a favor."
My dear brother, now that it is time (for a) favor, do not overvalue every shekels against my tin and textiles.
In addition to the 25 minas silver of Lulu, and the proceeds of my tin and textiles, add the silver and bring it in your transport.
My dear brother, in order that I not be ashamed at the city gate when you arrive, collect every shekel of silver--that which is on terms and the proceeds of my tin and my textiles--so that every shekel of silver will enter my house at your arrival to the benefit of your reputation.
Of the 13 textiles, they should not recognize the dātum of 2 textiles.
Aššur-taklāku bears to you separately 1 heavy textile for travelling from Šāt-Aššur.
In addition to the silver, 30 minas of Aššur-ṭāb and the yeild of textiles for Aššur-mālik's cargo, put up some 10 minas silver yourself and let your silver be disposed for one or two months.
Dispatch Ennam-Aššur.
Sell as much of the goods of Ennam-Aššur's cargo as you are able and take your silver.
If Ennam-Aššur has left, on the day you read my tablet, cause the silver to catch up to him.
Do not disappoint me.

Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the houses about which you wrote me, if we purchase them for you, I will pay as much as is required from my own funds, and my message will come to you.
The men requested a lot of silver.
On this account, we will wait.
Besides you there are no buyers or murri qablītim.
If any sales develop, I will make sure to purchase them for you, and I will pay the silver from my own funds.
The gold which Aššur-mālik's son owes me, why did you return 1 minas 11 shekels silver to him?
On account of this he defaulted and wrote me here.
Sell for cash the 14 minas ma-ZI-ra-am-tin which Dān-Aššur brings on behalf of Agua, and besides the 5 or 4 shekels of gold from Ilī-ašranni, send 1/3 mina gold and 1 mina pašallum-gold immediately.
The

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the matter of Ilabrat-bāni, as for the 29 minas of his silver, I weighed out 8 minas silver for a pouring of the son of Al-āḫum.
I gave 12 1/2 minas to his mother, his sister, and his representatives (and) 17 minas to your representatives about which you wrote.
Dān-Aššur will drive to him 1 talent 10 minas tin, 2 textiles, (and) a black donkey.
We paid 5 minas 25 shekels silver (for that merchandise).
(And paid) 1/2 mina for 'additional expenses' of his silver.
He requested silver from you for management.
Reject him and so that he pays you the remainder of my silver!
As for my tin and my textiles which you entrusted to him, When it is him(?) he paid the silver.
Now, let it be a favor to me!
Every year I have given him silver, I am embroiled in arguments.
Who amoung us here (in Aššur) have sold him tin on interest and got (our) silver?
Now, as for the rest of his silver, there is a tablet or two of a merchant.
The hand is indeed quite slack.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the matter of Ilabrat-bāni in which you entrusted to him, you wrote me and in your message: "I sold the goods in Amurrum and I took my silver."
The remainder of the silver is 51 1/4 shekels.
Regarding the remainder, I wrote to you.
I said, "Let him weigh it out to you and give his tablet to him."
Five or six of your tablets have arrived.
As for these, why did you not write in your tablet that I am paid in full?
6 1/3 minas tin--its price in silver: 1 mina 3 shekels; a mal'aītum kusītum-textile and 1/2 a kutānum-textile--their price in silver: 35 shekels; 3 textiles for my votive fund; and Aššur-mālik son of Azuza brought him 1 textile for the girl along with his textiles.
He must not pay less than 1 mina silver.
If you yourself received the textiles, then let your message come.
When you read the tablet addressed to both of you, confront him with it so that he cannot deny all for which I am written.
Let a tablet from both of you come to me so that I can take my silver here.
As for his silver which is here, I have done him a great favor.
And you know it very well!
He had sent word to me to withdraw his silver.
He should have written at the same time so that I could take my silver here.
If he quarrels, let your message come with the first traveler and I will make my decision about taking my silver myself here.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding the matter of Ilabrat-bāni about which you wrote, "On the day which I am writing the tablet, his term is full. Purchase cleared tin. Let Dān-Aššur depart with Puzur-Aššur to Amurrum and seize the tin on his own authority."
Dān-Aššur was ill and did not go.
Ennum-Aššur brings my instructions to you.
Now, Puzur-Aššur departed with him and Ennam-Aššur seized the tin on his own authority from (lit. in) the goods of (Ilabrat-bāni’s) son, and deposited it with Puzur-Aššur and I received 19 minas 53 5/6 shekels.
Get the remainder of my silver there, 51 1/6 shekels.
Now, wherever he is, write to him (the following):
Let your message come as to whether he send the remainder of the silver to you or not.
12 minas 15 shekels silver which you sent to me, tin is purchased.
Now, I will raise what I can and I will purchase (more) tin and I will dispatch Dān-Aššur and Puzur-Aššur.
As for the 18 fine
Send 5 minas silver so that I can make purchases and can cause (the goods) to reach there while you are still there and maximize profit.
My dear brother, besides you in whom can I trust?
Heed my messages!

target_raw:
Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Why is it that Puzur-Ištar answered you (in that way?).
He (had) said, "At the arrival of my goods I will pay."
Now if the wind carried off his textiles, how am I liable?
You did a favor for Aššur-mālik.
You caused his silver to be paid.
Mine you did not cause to be paid!
My dear brother, on the day which you read this tablet, seize him and case him to pay the gold.
My gold is the excise-tax.
As for his certified tablet, I retain it here.
If he makes excuses (lit. says anything), take a binding agreement for him so that you cause him to pay the gold and send it to me immediately.

Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
Why is it that Puzur-Ištar answered you (in that way?).
He (had) said, "At the arrival of my goods I will pay."
Now if the wind carried off his textiles, how am I liable?
You did a favor for Aššur-mālik.
You caused his silver to be paid.
Mine you did not cause to be paid!
My dear brother, on the day which you read this tablet, seize him and case him to pay the gold.
My gold is the excise-tax.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
If you are inclined, on the day that you read this tablet, send me 13 1/3 minas silver, its excise added, and I, for my part, will deposit double (lit. two shares).
While our shared merchandise travels up toward you, I will make any purchases which you write to me and send them to you.
On the day that Dān-Aššur arrives there, you can take your silver from the first sales you make, so that he (Dān-Aššur) brings my silver to me.
Until much tin goes with the caravan, I say, "Dān-Aššur, at his arrival, let them not travel overland for every shekels of silver, nor sell.
Let the later deposit 'go' so that a 1/2 or 1/3 extra mina arises between us.

source_raw: um-ma ša-lim-a-ḫu-um-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bí-ma ṭup-pá-am ša té-er-tí-a ša a-na a-šùr-ma-lik ù ḫi-na-a a-la-pu-tù šu-ma a-šùr-ma-lik a kà-ni-iš i-ta-al-kam ù šu-ma ḫi-na-a a-na kà-ni-iš{ki} a-dí-ni la i-li-kam ša ki-ma a-šùr-ma-lik ù ša ki-ma ḫi-na-a i-na u4-mì-im ša té-ra-bu ṣa-ba-sú-nu-ma ṭup-pá-am ša té-er-tí-a iš-tí-kà li-iš-ta-me-ú-ma AN.NA-ki ù TÚG.ḪI-a a-na u4-me i-ṣé-er tám-kà-ri ke-nu-tim ša ki-ma qá-qí-dí-ku-nu id-a 1 ù 2 ITU.KAM lá tù-šé-qá-ra tám-kà-ru-ku-nu lu ke-nu 1 GÚ 5 ma-na AN.NA 2 TÚG ša li-wi-tim ù 6 {TÚG}ku-ta-ni ša a-na a-šùr-{d}UTU-ši DUMU ib-ni-lí na-áš-a-tí-ni ki-ma a-na kà-ni-iš té-ru-bu AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A ù ṭup-pí ša té-er-tí-a ša a-na a-šùr-{d}UTU-ši la-pu-tù-ni a-šar wa-áš-bu e-ḫi-id-ma šé-bi-il5-šu-ma ù té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam 2 ri-ik-sà-an ša 5 ma-na.TA AN.NA i-na pá-ni šu-uq-lim na-ad-ú 1 ma-na 15 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫa-bu-la-ku-ni a-ḫi a-ta 8 GÍN.TA dí-šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR-áp-kà šu-ta-bi AN.NA pa-ze-er i-na 8 GÍN.TA e-li la e-li KÙ.BABBAR-áp-kà šu-ta-bi
target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
The tablet of instructions which I wrote to Aššur-mālik and Ḫinnaya, if Aššur-mālik arrived in Kanesh and if Ḫinnaya has not yet arrived in Kanesh, seize the representatives of Aššur-mālik and the representatives of Ḫinnaya on the day that you arrive and cause them to hear the tablet of my instruction and sell my tin and textiles on credit to a trustworthy merchant -as trustworthy as yourselves.
Do not overvalue 1 or 2 months.
Your merchants must be trustworthy.
1 talent 5 minas tin, 2 textiles as wrappings, and 6 kutānum-textiles which you brought to Aššur-šamšī s. Ibni-ilī, when you arrive in Kanesh, take care to send the tin, textiles, and tablets of my te'ertum written to Aššur-šamšī wherever he is.
Also, send you te'ertum.
Two packages of 5 minas tin are placed in the top of the pack.
1 minas 15 shekels silver which I owe you, my dear brother, please sell it at an 8 shekel rate and take your silver.
Smuggle the tin.
At an 8 shekel rate above or below, you can take your silver.

112 kutānum textiles, 18 minas hand tin, four black donkeys and their gear, 1 shekels silver for a textile of saitim, the wage of the drivers satisfied-Idī-Suen brings all this to you (pl.) under my seal.

source_raw: um-ma ša-lim-a-ḫu-um-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 ù dan-a-šur qí-bi-ma AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A ša šé-ep ku-lu-ma-a ù a-gu5-a té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam i-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫu-ra-ṣa-nim ù a-šur-ma-lik ù 7.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša TÚG.ḪI.A ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni 30 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ku-nu-uk-ma i pá-ni-ú-tim-ma wa-ṣí-e šé-bi-lam a-ša-me-ma dan-a-šur a-na bu-ru-uš-ḫa-dum ta-ṭá-ra-ad KÙ.BABBAR ma-lá ú-šé-ṣa-a-ni lu a-ma-kam ša šé-ep ku-lu-ma-a ù a-gu5-a šu-ma mì-ma ta-dí-in lu ša ANŠE.ḪI.A a-li KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN i-ba-ší-ú za-ki-a-ma dan-a-šùr ṭur4-dam a-ḫi a-ta a-na té-er-tí-a i-ḫi-id-ma li-ba-am dí-nam KÙ.BABBAR ša PUZUR4-{d}IŠKUR u en-um-a-šùr šu-ma u4-mu-šu-nu ma-al-ú ša-áš-qí-il5-šu-nu 30 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ku-nu-uk-ma šé-bi-lam šu-ma dan-a-šùr am-tám lá ir-de8-a-am li-bi i-lá-mì-na-kum i-na ṭup-pì-kà um-ma a-ta-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša a-pì-la a-šùr-iš-ta-ki-il5 <gap> KÙ.BABBAR ša-áš-qí-il5-šu
target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn and Dān-Aššur:
Let your message come concerning the tin and textiles in the transport of Kulumaya and Agua.
From the silver pertaining to Ḫuraṣānum and Aššur-mālik and the textiles worth 7 1/2 minas silver about which you wrote, seal 30 minas silver and send it with the first departing travellers.
I hear that you will send Dān-Aššur to Purušḫattum.
As much silver as he sends(?) or is there from the transport of Kulumaya and Agua, if anything is sold, or of donkeys, wherever there is a single shekels of silver, clear it and dispatch Dān-Aššur here.
My dear brother, give heed to my instructions and so make me happy.
As for the silver owed by Puzur-Adad and Ennum-Aššur, if their term is up, have them pay.
Seal 30 minas silver and send it here.
If Dān-Aššur does not bring the maid, I will be very disappointed.
In your tablet you said, "Aššuriš-tikal (has?) the silver of Apila."
Make him pay the silver.

From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn:
2 talents, 10 minas tin under seal, 40 textiles with wrappings--therein let Dān-Aššur take the 1 textile belonging to Šāt-Aššur--2 black donkeys, 15 minas tin as 'hand-tin'--all this Agua drives to you (pl.).
Let Agua pay to you 25 shekels silver and its interest as his capital fund.
2 talents 10 minas tin under seal, 40 textiles together with wrappings, 2 black donkeys, 15 minas 'hand-tin'--all this Kulumaya brings to you (pl.)
The (wages) of the transporters are paid.
Combine the tin and textiles and sell them on short or long term credit
Wherever the price is even a single shekel higher, sell as much as you are able and make known to me the silver and the terms in your tablets.
If there are no merchants as reliable as your surety, do whatever is in my best interests.

source_raw: um-ma ša-lim-a-ḫu-um-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 ù dan-a-šùr qí-bi-ma AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A ša šé-ep dan-a-šùr lu ša a-gu-a ù ku-lu-ma-a ma-lá iz-ku-a-ni té-er-ta-ak-nu li-li-kam i-na KÙ.BABBAR ša ḫu-ra-ṣa-nim ù a-šùr-ma-lik 7.5 ma-na ša TÚG.ḪI.A ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni 30 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ù ni-is-ḫa-sú ku-nu-uk-ma i pá-ni-ú-tí-ma wa-ṣé-e šé-bi-lam šu-ma dan-a-šùr sà-ḫe-er ù ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR lu i ša ANŠE.ḪI.A lu KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN a-šar i-ba-ší-ú 20 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ma-li-ma iš-tí wa-ar-ki-ú-tim šé-bi-lam KÙ.BABBAR ša PUZUR4-{d}IM ù en-um-a-šùr šu-ma u4-mu-šu-nu ma-al-ú ša-áš-qí-il5-šu-nu-ma šé-bi-lam lu lu-qú-tám ša šé-ep dan-a-šùr lu ša a-gu-a ù ku-lu-ma-a za-ki-a-ma dan-a-šur ṭur4-dam i-na KÙ.BABBAR ša tù-ší-ba-la-ni ḫa-nu ú-ul kà-lá-pá-am <gap> šu-ma am-tám dan-a-šùr i-na a-lá-ki-šu lá ir-de8-a-am li-bi4 i-lá-mì-na-kum i-na ṭup-pì-kà um-ma a-ta-ma KÙ.BABBAR ša a-pì-lá a-šùr-iš-tí-kál i-ša-qal
target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn and Dān-Aššur:
Let your message come as to how many tin and textiles from the transport of Dan-Aššur, as much as they clear.
From the silver of Ḫuraṣānum and Aššur-mālik, and the 7 1/2 minas (silver) worth of textiles about which you wrote me, seal 30 minas silver and its excise, and send it with the first departers.
If Dān-Aššur is late, then the remainder of silver, whether from donkeys, or wherever a single shekel is available, fill 20 minas silver and send it with the next travelers.
As for the silver of Puzur-Adad and Ennum-Aššur, if their terms are full make them pay and send it to me.
Clear both the goods from Dān-Aššur's transport and Agua and Kulumaya's transport and dispatch Dān-Aššur here.
From the silver which you will send, Ḫanu must(?) not ...
If Dān-Aššur does not bring the maid at his departure, I will be angry with you.
In your tablet, you said, "Aššuriš-tīkal will pay the silver of Apila."

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn and Dān-Aššur:
On the day that you read this tablet, send the 20 minas silver and its interest about which you wrote with the first departure.
Also, dispatch Ḫanu.
Let Dān-Aššur spend the nabrītum and in addition ot the silver from the transport of Ḫuraṣānum and the 7 1/2 minas silver from my textiles in the transport of the son of Aššur-mālik about which you wrote, add the proceeds of the tin and the textiles from the transport of Dān-Aššur and that from his donkeys, and that from my outstanding claims, the taḫšištum that I gave to you here,and send (in total) 1 talent silver with Dān-Aššur.
As for the 55 textile from the transport of the son of Errāya, why is it that I have written you 30 times?
As for my share of the textiles, gather the silver in addition to that from the transport of Ḫuraṣānum.
As for the merchandise both from the transport of Dān-Ašsur and from that which I am now sending you with the caravan, clear my tin and textiles so that Dān-Aššur can bring eveyr single shekel of silver that you placed on merchants, the cleared amount, in his transport.
Concerning the matter of Dān-Aššur, send silverof your own funds, as much as you can send, and I myself will put down two shares so that I purchase for whatever price you write, so that Dān-Aššur my bring it to you in the first caravan.
In addition to the 6 shekels gold from Panaka and the yield of the textiles which the boy gave to you you add as much gold as you can so that the merchant send the pašallum gold.
Let Dān-Aššur bring the 40 minas of fine copper.

target_raw:
From Šalim-aḫum to Pūšu-kēn and Ilabrat-bāni, specifically to Ilabrat-bāni:
From Lā-qēpum's tablet: "Ilabrat-bāni took 6 1/3 minas tin from your sack and at that time he went to Hattum."
Ilī-ašranni brought a white kusītum robe for my votive offering to Aššur-mālik.
But Aššur-mālik did not arrive.
He had departed.
You took the kusītum robe yourself.
You said, "It is delivered to my father."
You promised 15 shekels silver to Dān-Aššur.
You said, "I will send it after you."
You have held that one since the year of Aḫ-marši (REL 79).
My dispatch came to you five times!
Give Pūšu-kēn the silver for the tin, the kusītum robe, and the half kutānum textile.
Or, send so that I can take from your silver here.
Aššur-mālik son of Azua brings you three textiles belonging to my votive fund (and) one textile for the girl with your textiles.
Give a mina of silver to Pūšu-kēn so that he puts it in my silver.
Are you not constantly hearing that every year, wherever we sell, I must quarrel with groups of five or ten people concerning even a single shekel of your silver?
May Aššur and Ilabrat witness that whenever I give a single shekel of silver, I am bereft of 10 or 20 minas of my silver.
My dear brother, do not anger me!
Give my silver to Pūšu-kēn.
Or send to me so that I may take from your silver here.
Do not anger me!

target_raw:
From Šū-Bēlum to Pūšu-kēn, Puzur-sadue, Ḫuraṣānum, and Mannu-ki-Aššur:
If the colony office ... the house ... do not ... the houses/my house.
Take from my silver wherever it is good and deposit it.
If anyone among my debtors will pay you silver, open the tablets and cause them to weigh out the silver and its interests according to the tablet.
As for Qatata, the silver which he brought, let him 'clear it out' to you (pl.) and seal it in your seals and do not delay a single day.
Dispatch him here.
If you collect any silver, attach(?) Mannum-kī-Aššur to a reliable affiliated trader.
Let him come.
My dear brother, heed my instructions.

target_raw:
From Šū-Ḫubur and the sons of Aššur-imittī to Buzutaya, Pūšu-kēn and Imdī-ilum:
If you come to an agreement there and the man is well, then the man must not harm our father's house.
As much as you are able, lean on him for the silver from the goods so that the silver is bound in the city.
If it is not so, clear the man of it (the obligation).
We keep hearing that the man is ill.
Take care to clear him.
15 minas tin, separately, that are in the midst of his goods, are my ikrībū-funds.
When his house deposited his goods, Aššu-rēʾī, Ḫannunum, and Aššur-imittī son of Ikuwa gave to him 5 minas tin.
Have him pay the price of my tin.

target_raw:
From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding Ikūnum about whom you wrote: Here I will acquire an attorney over his 'thirds' in the land and with the next traveller I will write to you.
As for the silver that you sent, we did not purchase tin because tin cannot be purchased.
We purchased textiles from the proceeds of my silver and I will dispatch them to you with the first departure.
Hoard every 10 minas tin which goes up to you into your tin.
There won't be tin later.
Regarding the matter of the son of Qayyātum, who are you that you did not write me this concerning the matters (??)
I heard that the man is able to speak and he owes about 30 minas silver to Aššūr-Šamšī his brother and his sealed tablet is there.
Inquire and let your complete instructions come.

From Šū-Ḫubur, Aššur-bāni, and Šalim-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Thereof, 177 kutānum-textiles, their cost was 15 minas 4 shekels.
31 textiles and 1/3 of an Akkadian textile, their cost was 5 minas.
8 black donkeys, their cost was 2 minas 35 shekels.
Their gear was 24 shekels.
Their feed was 10 shekels.
The excise tax was 1 minas 15 shekels.
For 3 drivers their clothing was 1 mina 36 shekels.
40 1/2 minas 'hand-tin,' at a 13 1/2 shekels rate, its cost was 3 minas 4 shekels.
The ša saetum was 4 shekels.
The exit-tax was 14 shekels.
The extra costs were 31 1/2 shekels.
At the weighing (the silver) was deficient by 6 1/2 shekels.
All this Ea-šar brings to you.
2 textiles belong to Aššur-bāni.
2 textiles belong to Šalim-Aššur.

From Šū-Ḫubur, A
Urani brought 30 minas silver, its excise tax added.
Thereof, 27 fine šurbuitum-textiles, their price 3 minas 21 2/3 shekels, 63 kutānum-textiles, their price 6 minas 1 1/3 shekels, total 90 kutnum-textiles, their price 9 minas 23 shekels, (then) 15 minas 'hand-tin,' their price at 14 shekels (per shekel of silver) (was) 1 minas 4 1/3 shekels, 2 black donkeys, their price 1 mina 14 shekels, 15 shekels (silver?) for their feed, 12 2/3 shekels for their gear, 21 1/2 shekels add 'added expenses,' 4 shekels for their head-tax--they levied that on your "servant," 6 shekels the exit tax, 3 2/3 (shekels silver) for 'saetum,' 45 shekels from the tithe he 'set.'
In addition to the 10 minas previous copper from Iluwa, which is 'on his account,' 15 1/2 shekels silver we increased to him.
As for the 7 1/3 minas silver which we paid out to Aššur-imittī, 2/3 minas interest we added.
As for the 6 talents of copper which were the price of the houses of Abum-ilī, we added 30 minas (copper) interest.
At a 1 1/2 mina rate, their price (in silver) is 4 2/3 minas.
We paid 4 minas 22 5/6 shekels for the house of Sutiya.
We took the tablet of your debt.
5/6 minas to Tupezi. X shekels for the reeds? and (hire?) for Ziarim, 1 textile belonging to Aššur-bāni, 1 textiles belonging to Šalim-Aššur, all this Urani brings to you.

From Šū-Ḫubur, A
Urani brought 30 minas silver, its excise tax added.
Thereof, 27 fine šurbuitum-textiles, their price 3 minas 21 2/3 shekels, 63 kutānum-textiles, their price 6 minas 1 1/3 shekels, total 90 kutnum-textiles, their price 9 minas 23 shekels, (then) 15 minas 'hand-tin,' their price at 14 shekels (per shekel of silver) (was) 1 minas 4 1/3 shekels, 2 black donkeys, their price 1 mina 14 shekels, 15 shekels (silver?) for their feed, 12 2/3 shekels for their gear, 21 1/2 shekels add 'added expenses,' 4 shekels for their head-tax--they levied that on your "servant," 6 shekels the exit tax, 3 2/3 (shekels silver) for 'saetum,' 45 shekels from the tithe he 'set.'

Your partners who bought tin and textiles along with you sent me silver, the price of the tin and textiles.

From Šū-Ḫubur to Imdī-ilum, Pūšu-kēn, Amur-Šamaš, and Zupa:
To Amur-Šamaš and Zupa:
The instructions of Lālum came to you from Haḫḫum and my representatives gave you (pl.) 1 talent tin.
You took (them) on behalf of Lālum.
Here, the man promised me 10 minas of silver for my tin.
Unfortunately, Lālum died.
Here, I took from his silver and his representatives said, "Send so that whoever received your tin pays you your silver."
My dear brothers, When you read my tablet, take 10 minas of refined silver from the silver of Lālum and pay it to Imdī-ilum and Pūšu-kēn.

Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:

From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning the matter of Puzur-Anna son of Qayatum, here wherever there is a shekel of silver to be taken, it is not there.
There, as much as you are able, make the man stand up and cause him to pay, and give him your word in a bound agreement about the purchases.
If his sealed tablet with his seal here he does not hold, bind him.
Ennum-Aššur son of Šalim-aḫum is going toward you with the caravan.
Here in the house he will marry (my) daughter).
There, honor him on my behalf.
May he not become ill-tempered.
Is not my son-in-law your son-in-law?
Dispatch him very quickly so that he may take away (my) daughter.
If you are my brother, (Is not my daughter and your daughter?) honor him, and send him off.

From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
Concerning that which you wrote, (saying) "The (two) older sons of Lā-qēpum they are fit to bear a
Do not release them.
Here, Aššur-Šamšī promised to bear the 'sack.'
Now, the sack will enter our house.
Regarding Aššur-mālik his brother, a tablet from myself and Aššur-šamšī addressed to Aššur-mālik went to him.
Aššur-šamšī wrote: Here I promised Šū-Ḫubur to carry the sack.
There, do not make promises to anyone and do not shame me.
Let me come and I will clear you with Šū-Ḫubur.

Thus you will say: "Why ... where your father and your father's father in the past ... to whom the god ... so you must there carry the sack ... and be clothed."
Concerning Aššur-ṭāb son of Qarriya about whom you wrote, take your silver there.
... and not from there ... or from his ...

From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
You wrote, (saying) "Ikūnum's investors have gotten out 2 minas of silver for each mina of gold for their gold (shares)."
You wrote.
Ikūnum's investors have realized 2 minas silver on each mina gold for their gold.
If you judge look on him kindly, then he will give you an additional, (i.e.) three mina rate, instead of two mina rate.
Come to an agreement (with him).
If after the 2 minas he does give up to you an addition, write me so that I may impose his 'thirds' here and bind my silver in the city.

Urgent, take heed to send Aššur-mālik and Irma-Aššur, my witnesses that they entrusted the tin, to the representatives of Lālum there.
Wherever there is a single shekel of silver make them pay so that my silver is not lost.
I deposited 15 minas tin, the ikribū of Aššur, into the sack of Ikūnum.
Make him pay.

From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
You wrote me.
You said, "As for the tablets of your naspartum and the goods (arising from) that silver which I sent to you, Iliš-tīkal does not want to release them."
My instructions to you and Iliš-tīkal have come.
I said, "Take out and thoroughly inspect my tablets.
If you are my brothers, when you see my tablet, let your report come quickly so that I may discuss it with my investor.
He has acted highandledly five times and all of my goods will go to Aššur-rēʾī.
I wrote to you.
Why is it that you have not responded to my instructions?
Take heed to look at my tablet and let your complete report come.
I gave 1 talent tin and 1 kusītum robe to Aššur-ṭāb son of Karriya and a tadmiqtum-loan.
I am continually writing to him, but his reports do not return.
There, inquire (of him), and if the matter is progressing, request silver from him.
Request 1 mina 4 1/2 shekels silver from the son of Saḫrutanāʾum and send it here.
He promised the complete payment in the city.
Kurub-Ištar did not give me the 1 1/2 minas silver.
Let your report come so that I can cause him to pay.
source: translate Akkadian to English: um-ma šu-ḫu-bur-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma ṭup-pé-e ša na-áš-pá-ra-tí-kà ù ša šu-ut KÙ.BABBAR uš-té-bi4-lá-ku-ni ì-lí-iš-tí-kál wa-šu-ra-am ù-lá i-mu-a té-er-tí a-na ku-a-tí ú ì-lí-iš-tí-kál i-li-kam um-ma a-na-ku-ma er-ba-ma ṭup-pé-a a-ta-ma-ra šu-ma a-ḫu-ú-a a-tù-nu ki-ma ṭup-pí ta-mu-ra-ni té-er-ta-ku-nu ar-ḫi-iš li-li-kam-ma iš-tí um-mì-a-ni lá-tù-wu a-dí ḫa-am-ší-šu ú-ša-li-iṭ-ma mì-ma lu-qú-tí-a a-na ṣé-er a-šur-SIPA i-ta-lu-ku áš-pu-ra-kum mì-šu ša té-er-ta-kà lá i-tù-ra-ni i-ḫi-id-ma ṭup-pí-a a-mur-ma té-er-ta-kà za-ku-tum li-li-kam 1 GÚ AN.NA ú {TÚG}ku-sí-tám a-na a-šur-DU10 DUMU kàr-ri-a a-na ta-ad-mì-iq-tim a-dí-in áš-ta-na-pár-šu-ma té-er-tù-šu-ma lá i-tù-ra-am a-ma-kam ší-it-al-ma šu-ma a-wa-tum i-lá-ak KÙ.BABBAR e-ri-šu KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na 4.5 GÍN iš-tí DUMU sà-aḫ-ru-ta-na-im e-ri-iš-ma šé-bi-lam šál-ma-am i-na a-lim{ki} ša-qá-lam qá-bi 1.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR kur-ub-IŠTAR ú-lá i-dí-nam té-er-ta-kà li-li-kam-ma lu-ša-áš-qí-il5-šu
target:
From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
You wrote me.
You said, "As for the tablets of your naspartum and the goods (arising from) that silver which I sent to you, Iliš-tīkal does not want to release them."
My instructions to you and Iliš-tīkal have come.
I said, "Take out and thoroughly inspect my tablets.
If you are my brothers, when you see my tablet, let your report come quickly so that I may discuss it with my investor.
He has acted highandledly five times and all of my goods will go to Aššur-rēʾī.
I wrote to you.
Why is it that you have not responded to my instructions?
Take heed to look at my tablet and let your complete report come.
I gave 1 talent tin and 1 kusītum robe to Aššur-ṭāb son of Karriya and a tadmiqtum-loan.
I am continually writing to him, but his reports do not return.
There, inquire (of him), and if the matter is progressing, request silver from him.
Request 1 mina 4 1/2 shekels silver from the son of Saḫrutanāʾum and send it here.
He promised the complete payment in the city.
Kurub-Ištar did not give me the 1 1/2 minas silver.
Let your report come so that I can cause him to pay.
label: Cuneiform Tablet Prague I 472

From Šū-Ḫubur to Pūšu-kēn:
Regarding the house next to yours which the house of Ilī-lmālik sold, (we said), "We will 'bear the
When I myself departed from there, the house was not sold.
Since (I have departed), your representatives and my representatives are working hard to purchase the house for you.
If when I arrive (back) your representatives and my representatives have not yet acquired the house, I myself will acquire it for you at my arrival.
I did not abandon them.
Concerning the 1 talent of tin which Aššur-mālik and Irma-Aššur sold on credit to Lāum in Ḫaḫḫum, here, I seized the house of Lālum regarding this tin and they said, "Gather your witnesses."
Urgent, there, Aššur-mālik and Irma-Aššur must prepare their witnesses that (witnessed) them (when) sold the tin to Lālum so that the oaths seize the 10 minas silver from my holdings there if they do not present it towards here.
Seal and send the silver.
If the representatives of Lālum are there, clear the men of obligations so that they take the silver.
Also, I gave ... to Hawilia.
I gave 1/3 mina silver to Šasarara(?).
10 shekels silver and 2 minas of tin to the son of Uraya, all this I paid out in consequence of the colony.
There, request the silver from the colony and send it.

I wrote to you concerning how many (of) my goods came to Aššur-rēʾī in the transport of Buzua.
My dear brother, enter into the house of Aššur-rēʾī and read my tablets.
[Find out?] how many goods he brought to Idi-Suen and how many [he sold?] for Idi-Suen.
I hold his sealed tablets.
Seize his silver.

source_raw: um-ma ḫi-na-a ša ki-ma ku-a-tí ù PUZUR4-a-šur-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en6 qí-bi-ma i-na pá-ni-tim ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma 2 ma-na KÙ.GI PUZUR4-a-šur i-na ṭup-pí-šu šu-mì wa-qúr-tim li-il5-pu-ut i-na wa-ar-ki-tim ta-áš-pu-ra-am um-ma a-ta-ma 2 ma-na-e KÙ.GI a-na qá-tí-a ṭá-ḫi-a um-ma né-nu-ma 2 ma-na KÙ.GI a-na qá-tí pu-šu-ke-en6 nu-ṭá-ḫa um-ma PUZUR4-a-šur-ma iš-tù-ma šu-mì wa-qúr-tim lá ta-lá-pá-ta-ni ù a-na qá-tí-šu lá nu-ṭá-ḫa-a ni-iš-ta-a-al-ma 2 ma-na KÙ.GI šu-mì DAM.GÀR-ri-im lá-pí-it a-ta a-ma-kam ma-lá-kà
target_raw:
Thus Hinnaya, your representatives, and Puzur-Aššur to Pūšu-kēn:
Previously you wrote me.
You said, "As for the 2 minas gold, let Puzur-Aššur write Waqqurtum's name in his tablet."
Later you wrote me.
(You wrote), "The 2 minas gold are credited to my account."
(We wrote), "We will credit the 2 minas gold to Pūšu-kēn's account."
Puzur-Aššur said, "Do not write Waqqurtum's name afterward", so we cannot associate it to her account.
We will request so that 2 minas gold is written for the name of the merchant.
Make your decision there.

From Hinnaya to Aššur-nādā:
I gave you 5 2/3 minas of silver and you sent it down to the City.
Since you claim that you do not administer a joint-stock capital, you offered the tin and the donkey to Ištar-pilah.
Why have you booked me for transportation costs and a donkey driver?
It is now two years ago but the agreed amount for the silver has not been reached yet;

source_raw: um-ma ḫi-na-a-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR ša a-dí-na-ku-ni ší-ma-am ma-lá ta-áš-a-ma-ni lu AN.NA lu TÚG.ḪI.A ma-lá ta-áš-a-ma-ni a-na šu-ku-bi4-im dí-ma lu-ub-lam šu-ma šu-ku-bu-um i-ta-ṣa-am a-na a-li-ki-im pá-ni-e-ma dí-in-ma lu-ub-lam
target_raw:
From Hinnaya to Aššur-nādā:
As to the silver I gave to you, whatever purchase you make, be it tin or textiles, whatever you buy give that to Šū-Kūbum so he may bring it.
If Šū-Kūbum has already left, then give it to the first caravan so it may bring it.

source_raw: um-ma ḫi-na-a-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da ù dan-a-šur qí-bi-ma šu-ma ku-lu-ma-a i-ta-lá-ak-ma mì-ma i-na AN.NA-ki-a il5-qé té-er-ta-ak-nu li-li-kam ù ṣú-ḫa-ri-a na-ḫi-dá-ma e-ma-ri lu-ša-ki-lu AN.NA i-na ša-qá-lim qá-at-ku-nu-ma li-iš-qú-ul ù a-šar pá-zu-ur-tim ṣú-ba-tí SIG5-tim qá-tí-ma ṣú-ba-tí-ku-nu ep-ša a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu gi5-im-lá-ni
target_raw:
From Hinnaya to Aššur-nādā and Dān-Aššur:
If Kulumaya has left and taken any of my tin, then send me word.
Also, instruct my servants to feed the donkeys.
In paying tin you must pay personally.
Also, where there is a chance of smuggling you must deal with my good textile together with your own.
My dear brothers, do me a favour!

source_raw: um-ma ḫi-na-a-ma a-na pu-šu-ke-en qí-bi-ma a-na-kam-ma a-ta-wa-ku-um i-na ni-kà-sí šu-ḫu-ut iš-tù té-er-tí iš-tí ša ki-ma i-a-tí i-ba-ší-ú a-na ša-ni-ú-tum ni-kà-sí a-ša-qal ú té-er-tí za-ku-tám lá a-ša-me šu-ma ša-du-e-tí i-ḫa-i-ṭù ki-ma té-er-tí pá-ni-at-ni a-wa-tim ta-e-er 4 GÚ AN.NA ù it-ra-sú ša ILLAT-at a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku e-lá-ma a-šur-na-da ú qar-wi-a il5-qé-ú 6 GÍN.TA a-na AN.NA-ki-a ú-šé-bu-lu-nim ú ṭup-pá-áš-nu ša té-er-tí-šu-nu ú-kà-al ša 6 GÍN a-na AN.NA-ki-a lá i-ba-tù-qú a-ḫi a-ta ki-ma ú-mu im-tí-du-ni-ni i-ḫi-id-ma KÙ.BABBAR ṣa-ru-pá-am ša-áš-qí-il5-šu-ma šé-bi4-lam ú a ša ki-ma i-a-tí a-na tur4-ḫu-mì-it ta-ḫi-tám šu-pu-ur-ma té-er-tí lu-za-ki-ú-nim-ma lu-šé-bi4-lu-nim a-na KÙ.BABBAR 8 ma-na ša ú-ṣur-ša-a-šur a-na šu-nu-nu i-dí-nu šu-ma KÙ.BABBAR i-dí-in i ki-iš-dá-tí-šu 2 ma-na KÙ.GI lu-ša-ki-lá-ni a-na DUMU i-sú-ri-ik qí-bi4-ma 8.66666 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ú ṣí-ba-sú ša iš-tù MU.5.ŠÈ lu-še-bi4-lam 16 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša ša-du-e-tim i li-bi4 a-mur-a-šur um-ma šu-ut-ma a-na a-šur-dan a-dí-in a-ma-kam a-šur-dan lu-ša-zi-za-ku-um 1 lu-bu-šu-um ma-sí-ú-um a-mur-a-šur na-áš-a-kum 5 TÚG DUMU dan-na-i-il5 2 TÚG SIG5 a-lá-ḫu-um a-ḫu-ú qar-wi-a na-áš-a-kum dí-šu-nu-ma KÙ.BABBAR-áp-šu-nu a-na dan-a-šur dí-in ú a-ta i ra-mì-ni-kà ša ra-du-e-em ra-dí-ma i ma-qá-at té-er-tí-a KÙ.BABBAR-áp-kà li-qé a ša ú-ṣur-ša-a-šur i-ḫi-id 1 GÚ KÙ.BABBAR i-ta-áp-lá-ni
target_raw:
From Ḫinnāya to Pūšu-kēn:
Here, I have spoken for you.
It is deposited in my account.
After my shipment is with my representatives, I will make accountings a second time.
And I do not hear the full report.
If my šadueti is ḫiatu, when my previous messages, matter.
Elama, Aššur-nādā, and Qarwiya took the 4 talents tin and its tithe from the caravan of Aššur-taklāku.
6 shekels rate for my tin they sent to me.
Now, I hold their instruction tablets.
They must not pay less than a 6 shekel rate for my tin!
My dear brother, because the days become many, take care to have them pay refined silver and send it to me.
Also, send tḫt to my representative to Durhumit so that they clear my instructions and send to me.
For about 8 minas of silver, they sold the the (goods) of Uṣur-ša-Aššur to Šū-Nunu.
If he gave the silver, they should arrive it?
Let him consume(?) 2 minas gold to me!
To the son of Issu-rīk:
Let him/me send 8 2/3 minas silver and its interst which ...
16 shekels from the shipping charges is owed by Amur-Aššur.
He said, "I gave it to Aššur-dān."
Let him cause Aššur-dān to settle with you.
Amur-Aššur brings you a masium garment.
The son of Danna-il (Taqna-il?), Al-aḫum brother of Qarwiya brings you 2 fine textiles.
Sell them and give their silver to Dān-Aššur.
And as for you, from my own funds add as much as you can and take the silver at the arrival of my instructions.
Pay attention to the matter of Uṣur-ša-Aššur.
He repaid me a talent of silver. (?)

From Ḫinnaya to Pūsu-kēn;
Concerning the matter of the son of Lā-qēpum about which you wrote, the investors of Lā-qēpum took their silver.
Also, if Kunilum laid on him anything there, let his tablets be strong so that I can collect the silver here from his 'holdings'.
Take care that when you read my tablet you send your message.
We took the copper and we gave/deposited it for silver against the arrival of Kulumaya. ???
We have done according to your message and we will dispatch your 'youth.'
The son of Dannum-ilum brings you 5 white garments.

target_raw:
Hinnaya to Pūšu-kēn:
Recently, I gave 35 minas tin, 2 kusītum textiles, 1 zakium textile and 2 šulupkā'um textiles to Kulumaya.
1 fine textile to my account is the 40 shekels debt of the daughter of Ištar-pilaḫ. (?)
As for Kurub-Ištar son of Šū-Ḫubur, declare his payings at his arrival.
Have him pay the silver, then seal it and send it to me.
Should they deposit silver or textiles, deposit my full share.
Then I will send to you mine with a traveller.
You spoke to me.
You said, "Aššur-ṭāb stays in Waḫšušana and he brought your tin and they gave it to Lulu at an 8 shekel rate and they sent the silver to Aššur-ṭāb."
If it is short, send to me by my youth or Anaḫ-ilī so that they may request and seal the silver and send it (verb tense).
My dear brother, take heed that your message goes to Kunilim, and the he dispatches the youth to me.
Besides you, whom do I have?

From Ḫinnaya to Pūšu-kēn and Zupa:
To Zupa:
About 10 minas silver has arrived? from Purušḫattum and it is available in your house.
You wrote me.
You said, "Send your hauler to Ikuppiya and let him borrow silver."
Ikuppiya opened from my house and abd he gave (it) to you.
I wrote to you as I was leaving and I said, "Send my silver."
You wrote me.
You said, "Take the silver from the merchant and send so that wherever you received it, the silver and its miqissu(product?) ...."
If the product ... let your message come(??)
Silver to .... evil? ... you .... Ikuppi-Aššūr ... My silver(?) ....
May Aššur know that ... second .. naruqqum .. !
Give the silver and its product to Pūšu-kēn.
To Pūšu-kēn:
My dear father, have him pay the silver and its product.
1 minas silver and its corresponding interest and 2/3 minas silver, the price of two garments, is with Ennānum son of Šū-Hubur.
Make him pay the silver.

Wherever you sell my tin, make them pay and arise and come here.

Wherever my tin he sells, take care to assist him so that he collects the silver and dispatch him.
I sent to you.
