Now, if you reach Purušḫattum, ... and cause him to take your instructions, and let your representatives deposit my share.
My dear brother, as for me, who do I have beside you?

Ennānum son of Šū-Ḫubur the IHME official, has promised to pay you 2/3 mina silver upon his arrival.
Also, separately, cause him to pay 10 shekels per mina per month.

target_raw:
From Ḫinnaya to Qarwiya, Pūšu-kēn, and Aššur-dān:
To Qarwiya and Aššur-dān:
You wrote me.
You said, "We asked Aššur-nādā and the son of Elama for the silver. They said, 'Wherever silver here to Durḫumit we arrive and we will send the silver and seal it and send it to him.'"
After you wrote to me, third year for the taking of the silver -- should Aššur-dān go after after them and he casued them to pay. ???
Bad or good, who in Kaneš will do anything on my behalf?
Now, you wrote me regarding the 15 1/2 minas silver which Elama promised to pay to Lā-qēpum.
You said, "The representative of Elama said, 'Let him bring out his witnesses so that he can take his silver.'"
Of the third my witness tablet at the gate of the gods you did not take!
For the 20 minas silver which I gave to him or at the gate of the Alaḫḫinum, I will not prosecute him in the presence of Aššur-mālik son of Al-āḫum.
I said, "Take heed to send me the silver quickly!"
Why is it that your full message has not come nor have you sent the silver of my tin?

target_raw:
The wabartum settlement of Ulama rendered a verdict that Panaka and Aššur-mutabbil s. Pūšu-kēn will go to Kanesh within two months time and Panaka will return the sealed tablet of his father and according to the tablet sealed by his father, Aššur-mutabbil will repay Panaka.
If Panaka does not go to Kanesh, he will send notice and his representative will set the matter for him.
Month of Kanwarta (ix) eponym of Buzutaya (REL 102).

Zupa seized us against the sons of Pūšu-kēn and Zupa said to the sons of Pūšu-kēn, "You father received the silver of my sealed tablet."
"Why does Suen-rē'ī withhold my tablet?"
The sons of Pūšu-kēn and Suen-rē'ī responded "Our father did receive all the silver of your sealed tablet.
Release it(him?) and we will give the matter to you.
Suen-rēʾī released Zupa in our presence.
For this matter, the colony at Kanesh gave us permission, and we gave our witnesses before the dagger of Aššur.
Witnesses: Ziadādu, Amurrum-bāni

source_raw: zu-pá a-na me-er-e pu-šu-ke-en6 iṣ-ba-at-ni-a-tí-ma um-ma zu-pá-ma a-na me-er-e pu-šu-ke-en6-ma ša ṭup-pí-a ḫa-ar-mì-im KÙ.BABBAR a-bu-ku-nu il5-qé mì-šu-um sú-en6-SIPA i-a-tí ú-kà-lá-ni um-ma ma-ru-ú pu-šu-ke-en6-ma a-na sú-en6-SIPA ša DUB-ṭù-pí-šu ḫa-ar-mì-im ša zu-pá KÙ.BABBAR a-bu-ni il5-qé wa-šé-er-šu-ma né-nu a-wa-tám ni-da-na-ku-um ma-aḫ-ri-ni-ma zu-pá sú-en6-SIPA ú-šé-er a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um kà-ni-iš-ki i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma IGI GÍR ša a-šur ší-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in IGI zi-a-du-du DUMU a-lá-bi4-im IGI MAR.TU ba-ni DUMU šu-IŠTAR
target_raw:
Zupa seized us against the sons of Pūšu-kēn and Zupa said to the sons of Pūšu-kēn, "Your father took the silver stated in my sealed tablets.
Why is it that Suen-rē'ī detains me?
The sons of Pūšu-kēn (responded): "Our father received the silver of the sealed tablet of Zupa. Release it and we will give you the matter."
Suen-rē'ī released Zupa in our presence.
The colony at Kanesh gave us witnesses regarding this matter and we gave our witnesses before the dagger of Aššur.
Witnesses: Ziyadudu s. Āl-abum, Amurrum-bāni s. Šū-Ištar.

Adad-ṣulūlī seized us against Buzāzu:
Adad-ṣulūlī said to Buzāzu: In Purušḫattum in the eponymate of Ikuppi-Ištar, as you were leaving you said, "I owe Ennum-Aššur s. Uzuwa 4 minas silver, the price of the perdum equids and metoric iron."
For these things, the colony at Kanesh gave us permission and we gave our testimonies before the dagger of Aššur.
Witnesses: Aššur-ṭāb s. Puzur-Aššur, Aššur-imittī s. Iddin-Wēr.

Šū-Suen went to the city and the son of Ennamānum seized him and he said, "When your time is expired, the
We repaid the 5 minas silver on behalf of Šū-Suen and when his term expired, the son of Ennamānum came and we paid him about 2 minas silver from teh matter of Šū-Suen and we said, "Take the silver from a merchant house on interest and we ourselves will pay you the silver and its interest."

source_raw: ša 49.66666 ma-na 7 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR lu-qú-tum ša ba-ri-ni e-li-am ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR 10 ma-na 13 GÍN i-na a-lim{ki} a-ḫu-ur iš-tù ni-is-ḫa-tum ù ša ší-mì-im iṣ-ḫi-ru-ni 89.16666 TÚG iz-ku-ú-nim iš-tu ni-is-ḫa-tim ni-dí-nu a-ṣé-er AN.NA qá-tim né-pu-lu 2.TA šu-uq-lá-tim ku-nu-ki ša a-lim{ki} 23 ma-na.TA AN.NA 27 TÚG.TA ku-ta-ni ù 2.TA ANŠE.ḪI.A qá-tám ni-il5-qé 2.16666 TÚG ù 1 ANŠE i li-bi4 im-dí-DINGIR 0.66666 ma-na LÁ 1.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša-al-ša-tim ni-lá-qé 1.66666 ma-na i-na me-ší-it e-lá-lí ša-lim-a-šùr im-dí-DINGIR ù pu-šu-ke-en6 i-lá-qé-ú
target_raw:
Goods owned between us worth 49 minas 47 shekels silver came up.
The remainder of the silver, 10 minas 13 shekels silver, stayed in the city.
After the excise and the tithe purchases were deducted, they cleared 89 1/6 textiles to us.
After we gave the excise, we settled for the hand tin, 2 sacks under the seal of the city 23 minas tin, 27 kutānum textiles and 2 donkeys each we took as share.
Imdī-ilum owed 2 1/6 textiles and 1 donkey.
We will take 38 1/2 shekels silver as thirds.
Šalim-Aššur, Imdī-ilum, and Pūšu-kēn will take 1 2/3 minas from the mišittum of Elāli.

source_raw: šu-ma-li-bi-a-šur ù {d}IŠKUR.DÙL iṣ-bu-tù-ni-a-tí-ma a-wa-tí-šu-nu nu-ga-me-er-ma 1.8333300000000001 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša šu-ma-li-bi-a-šùr a-na {d}IŠKUR.DÙL ḫa-bu-lu KÙ.BABBAR {d}IŠKUR.DÙL ša-bu ṭup-pu-šu ḫa-ar-mu-um ša ki-ma {d}IŠKUR.DÙL a-na šu-ma-li-bi-a-šùr i-du-nu-ma i-du-ak 1 ma-na 1 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ša a-šur-SIG5 i-ṣé-er da-da-a DUMU dí-li-a {d}IŠKUR.DÙL il5-qé-ú šu-ma-li-bi-a-šur KÙ.BABBAR il5-qé šu-ma a-šur-SIG5 a-na 1 ma-na 1 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-na {d}IŠKUR.DÙL i-tù-ar šu-ma-li-bi-a-šùr KÙ.BABBAR ú-ta-ar um-ma {d}IŠKUR.DÙL-ma 7.5 GÚ 0.66666 ma-na URUDU-kà ma-sí-am en-na-sú-en6 DUMU ì-lí-a-lim il5-qé um-ma šu-ma-li-ba-a-šur-ma 5 GÚ LÁ 5 ma-na URUDU il5-qé ITU.KAM ša ke-na-tim li-mu-um šu-sú-en6 DUMU ba-ba-lim a-na ITU.3.KAM ṭup-pá-am ša ší-bi-šu {d}IŠKUR.DÙL ša 7.5 GÚ 0.66666 ma-na i-lá-qé-ma a-na šu-ma-li-bi-a-šur i-da-šu šu-ma ṭup-pá-am ša ší-bi-šu {d}IŠKUR.DÙL lá il5-té-qé 3.5 GÚ 5.66666 ma-na URUDU <gap> URUDU ma-sí-am ḫu-bu-ul {d}IŠKUR.DÙL a-na a-wa-tim a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um wa-aḫ-šu-ša-na ṣa-ḫe-er GAL i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma IGI GÍR ša a-šur ší-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in IGI a-ḫu-qar IGI ú-ṣur-ša-a-šur PUZUR4-a-šur DUMU i-ša-ar-ki-id-a-šur tap-pá-i-ni
target_raw:
Šumma-libbi-Aššur and Adad-ṣulūlī seized us and we brought their dispute to an end:
(as for) the 1 5/6 minas of silver which Šumma-libbi-Aššur owed to Adad-ṣulūlī, Adad-ṣulūlī has been satisfied with the silver;
Adad-ṣulūlī's representatives will give his certified debt-note to Šumma-libbi-Aššur and it will be destroyed.
(As for) the 1 mina 1 shekel of silver which Adad-damiq had as claim on Dadaya son of Tilia, Šumma-libbi-Aššur took that silver.
The plenary assembly in Wahšušana gave us for these words, and we gave our testimony before the dagger of Aššur.
In the presence of Ahu-waqar (and) Uṣur-ša-Aššur, Puzur-Aššur son of Išar-kitti-Aššur was our partner.

The plenary assembly at Waḫšušana permitted us, and we gave our testimonies before the dagger of Aššur.
Sabasiya is our partner.

source_raw: šu-ḫu-bu-ur DUMU PUZUR4.IŠTAR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša a-šùr-iš-tí-kál DUMU sú-kà-lí-a ITU.KAM ḫu-bu-ur li-mu-um a-šur-mì-tí ma-lá-ḫu-um a-na 5 ITU.KAM i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-em i-na wa-ar-ḫi-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb
target_raw:
He will pay five months from the ḫamuštum of Aššuriš-tikal s. Sukkallīya, month ḫubur, eponymate Aššur-imittī the boatman.
If he does not pay (on time), he will add 1 1/2 shekels per mina per month as interest.
source: translate Akkadian to English: šu-ḫu-bu-ur DUMU PUZUR4.IŠTAR i-šu iš-tù ḫa-muš-tim ša a-šùr-iš-tí-kál DUMU sú-kà-lí-a ITU.KAM ḫu-bu-ur li-mu-um a-šur-mì-tí ma-lá-ḫu-um a-na 5 ITU.KAM i-ša-qal šu-ma lá iš-qúl 1.5 GÍN.TA a-na 1 ma-na-em i-na wa-ar-ḫi-im ṣí-ib-tám ú-ṣa-áb

source_raw: ṭup-pá-am ḫa-ar-ma-am ša 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša a-mur-IŠTAR a-na ì-lí-a-lim i-dí-nu-ma ì-lí-a-lúm a-na a-lim{ki} KÙ.BABBAR ú-šé-bi4-lu a-na bu-zu-ta-a ni-ip-qí-id šu-ma lu-qú-tám ša 10 ma-na-e KÙ.BABBAR i pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim ša ki-ma ì-lí-a-lim a-na a-mur-IŠTAR lá i-ta-ad-nu ṭup-pá-am bu-zu-ta-a a-na da-ni dí-ni-šu ú-ba-al IGI a-šùr-ma-lik DUMU lu-zi-na IGI i-dí-a-bi4-im DUMU a-šùr-GAL a-na bu-zu-ta-a ṭup-pá-am ni-ip-qí-id
target_raw:
We entrusted to Buzutaya the sealed tablet stating 10 minas silver which Amur-Ištar gave to Ilī-ālum then Ilī-ālum took the silver to the city.
If the goods worth 10 minas silver minas in Purušḫattum the representatives of Ilī-ālum are not deposited (?) to Amur-Ištar, Buzutaya will bring the tablet to the determiner of his case.?
Witnesses: Aššur-mālik s. Luzina, Idī-abum s. Aššur-rabi.
We entrusted the tablet to Buzutaya.
